Timely payment of social security contributions
Reporting of work accidents to the relevant Health Fund
documentation_required: Proof of registration with Caja de Salud
Incident reports filed with the Health Fund
compliance_risk: Employer becomes directly liable for all medical and disability costs if the employee is not properly registered.
penalty: Fines, interest on late payments, and direct liability for all costs incurred by the employee for medical treatment.

definition: Regulates the entry, transit, stay, and exit of foreign nationals in Bolivia, establishing the specific requirements for obtaining temporary and permanent residence for work purposes.
compliance_requirements: Obtain a specific purpose visa (visa por objeto determinado) before entering the country for work
Register with the General Directorate of Migration (DIGEMIG)
Maintain current legal status throughout the contract duration
Report changes in employment status to authorities
documentation_required: Employment contract approved by the Ministry of Labor
compliance_risk: Deportation of the foreign employee, imposition of fines on the employer, and potential blacklisting of the company for future hiring.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 1,000 to 10,000 UFV, expulsion of the foreign national, and potential suspension of the company's ability to hire foreigners.

law_name: Decreto Supremo Reglamentario a la Ley de Migración
definition: Provides the procedural framework for obtaining work permits for foreign employees, specifically detailing the requirements for the 'Residencia Temporal por Motivo de Trabajo'.
compliance_requirements: Submission of the employment contract to the Ministry of Labor (Ministerio de Trabajo, Empleo y Previsión Social)
Proof of professional qualification for the position
Verification of the 15 percent limit for foreign staff (Ley General del Trabajo)
documentation_required: Academic and professional titles legalized via apostille
compliance_risk: Invalidation of the work permit, labor inspectors can invalidate employment status if quotas are exceeded.
penalty: Fines per non-compliant foreign worker and rejection of residency applications.

law_code: Decreto Ley de 24 de mayo de 1939
definition: Mandates that foreign workers must not exceed 15 percent of the total workforce in any enterprise, ensuring the prioritization of Bolivian labor.
compliance_requirements: Monitoring and maintaining the 85/15 ratio of national vs. foreign staff
Keeping a detailed payroll record available for labor inspection
compliance_risk: Violating the 15 percent quota triggers administrative sanctions and potential forced termination of excess foreign staff.
penalty: Administrative sanctions, fines, and compulsory adjustment of payroll to comply with the 15 percent rule.

definition: Establishes that the State prohibits and punishes all forms of discrimination based on sex, color, age, sexual orientation, origin, culture, nationality, citizenship, language, religious creed, ideology, political or philosophical affiliation, civil status, economic or social condition, occupation, or any other grounds that attempt against human dignity.
compliance_requirements: Ensure equal treatment for all employees
Implement non-discriminatory hiring practices
Maintain workplace environments free of prejudice
documentation_required: Employee records demonstrating objective selection criteria
compliance_risk: Constitutional challenge, human rights violation claims, and judicial intervention.
penalty: Civil and criminal liability depending on the severity; potential annulment of discriminatory acts.

law_name: Ley contra el Racismo y toda forma de Discriminación
definition: Sets mechanisms and procedures for the prevention and sanctioning of acts of racism and discrimination, applicable to the public and private sectors.
compliance_requirements: Prohibit discriminatory language in advertisements
Establish internal protocols to handle discrimination complaints
Prevent harassment based on race or background
compliance_risk: Fines, administrative sanctions, and potential closure of establishment for repeat offenders.
penalty: Fines, suspension of commercial license, and criminal prosecution for hate speech or discriminatory acts.

definition: Provides the foundational framework for labor relations, prohibiting discrimination in remuneration and employment conditions.
compliance_requirements: Ensure equal pay for equal work
Prevent discriminatory termination based on arbitrary grounds
compliance_risk: Labor lawsuits, mandatory reinstatement, and payment of back wages with interest.
penalty: Fines imposed by the Ministry of Labor, compensation for the employee.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo, Empleo y Previsión Social

law_code: Decreto Ley de 24 de mayo de 1939
definition: The obligation of the employer to pay the 'Aguinaldo' (thirteenth-month salary), which is a mandatory bonus equivalent to one month of salary paid at the end of the year.
compliance_requirements: Pay one full month of salary to employees with at least 3 months of service
Pro-rate payment for employees with less than 12 months but more than 3 months of service
Payment must be made by December 20th of each year
documentation_required: Ministry of Labor Aguinaldo payment report (Planilla de Aguinaldo)
compliance_risk: High; legal claims and administrative fines by the Ministry of Labor.
penalty: Fines, mandatory double payment if not paid by the deadline, and possible labor lawsuits.

definition: Mandatory social security contributions to the Long-Term Social Security system (Pension fund) to provide for old age, disability, and death benefits.
compliance_requirements: Deduct social security contributions from employee payroll
Employer contribution of 3% for the 'Aporte Patronal Solidario'
Remit total contributions to the Gestora Pública de la Seguridad Social de Largo Plazo
documentation_required: Monthly social security contribution forms (Formularios FPC)
compliance_risk: High; debt accumulation and potential freezing of corporate bank accounts.
penalty: Interest, fines, and legal action for recovery of funds.

definition: A seniority bonus that increases based on the number of years worked, calculated as a percentage of three minimum national wages.
compliance_requirements: Calculate and pay the bonus based on the applicable percentage scale
Apply the scale for years of service (from 2 years onwards)
Include the payment in the monthly payroll
documentation_required: Payroll records showing the specific line item for the bonus
compliance_risk: Moderate; claims for retroactive payments.
penalty: Back pay obligations and potential administrative fines by the Ministry of Labor.

definition: Mandatory short-term social security including health insurance coverage for the employee and their family dependents.
compliance_requirements: Register the employer at the Caja de Salud (Health Fund)
Register all employees and dependents
Make the mandatory employer contribution (10% of total payroll)
documentation_required: Registration certificates from Caja de Salud
compliance_risk: Critical; denial of health services to employees and legal repercussions.
penalty: Fines, legal penalties, and direct liability for health costs incurred by the employee.
source_reference: Código de Seguridad Social de Bolivia

law_code: Decreto Ley de 24 de mayo de 1939
definition: Establishes the obligation of employers to maintain accurate payroll records including wages, overtime, and social benefits for all employees.
compliance_requirements: Maintain mandatory payroll books (Planillas de Sueldos y Salarios)
Submit monthly payroll reports to the Ministry of Labor
Record all remunerations and deductions
compliance_risk: Fines, legal disputes with workers, and potential labor claims regarding unpaid social benefits.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 1,000 to 10,000 Bolivianos, potential legal liability.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo, Empleo y Previsión Social

definition: Mandates the registration of employers and employees for social security contributions and the reporting of payroll data for tax calculations.
compliance_requirements: Register the company with the Caja de Salud
Submit monthly contributions (Planilla de Aportes)
Report all insurable salaries
documentation_required: Formulario de Declaración Jurada de Aportes
compliance_risk: Accumulation of interest on unpaid debts, administrative penalties, and lack of healthcare access for employees.
penalty: Daily interest and fines, 10% surcharge on outstanding balances.
source_reference: Gestora Pública de la Seguridad Social de Largo Plazo

definition: Governs the RC-IVA (Regimen Complementario al Impuesto al Valor Agregado) applicable to employee salaries, requiring employers to act as withholding agents.
compliance_requirements: Withhold income tax from employee salaries
Manage Form 110 (Tax credits for employees)
Submit monthly declarations of withheld taxes to the Tax Authority (SIN)
compliance_risk: Severe tax audit penalties and criminal liability for tax evasion.
penalty: Multas por incumplimiento a deberes formales (IMF), interest, and tax fines up to 100% of the unpaid tax.

definition: The obligation of the employer to maintain a record of employees and provide information to the Ministry of Labor.
compliance_requirements: Maintenance of an employee register
Submission of labor statistics to the Ministry of Labor
Registration of the employment contract
compliance_risk: Administrative sanctions and legal presumption of employee claims in case of lack of documentation.
penalty: Fines determined by the Ministry of Labor and Employment of Bolivia, potential legal costs in labor trials.

law_name: Reglamento a la Ley General del Trabajo
definition: Requires employers to keep a 'Libro de Sueldos y Jornales' (Payroll and Wage Book) approved by the Ministry of Labor.
compliance_requirements: Approval of the payroll book by the Ministry of Labor
Continuous updating of personnel changes
Registration of employees under the specific format
compliance_risk: Fines for failure to maintain authorized books and inability to prove salary payments.
penalty: Administrative fines and mandatory regularization of records.

law_name: Reglamento del Código de Seguridad Social
definition: The legal obligation to register the employer and the employee with the National Health Fund (CNS) or relevant social security entity.
compliance_requirements: Employer registration with CNS
Employee enrollment (Aviso de alta) within 5 days of hiring
Payment of social security contributions
compliance_risk: Liability for health expenses of the employee and social security surcharges.
penalty: Fines for late registration, interest on unpaid contributions, and civil liability.
source_reference: Código de Seguridad Social y su Reglamento

law_name: Ley del Sistema de Registro Obligatorio de Empleadores
definition: Establishes the obligation to register through the virtual platform of the Ministry of Labor (OEP - Oficina Virtual de Trámites).
compliance_requirements: Digital registration of the company
Annual registration renewal of the employer
Digital uploading of employment contracts
compliance_risk: Blocking of the 'Certificado de Registro Obligatorio de Empleadores' (ROE), which is necessary for legal payroll submission.
penalty: Administrative fines, inability to process labor documents, and administrative obstacles.

definition: The primary legislation governing individual and collective labor relations in Brazil, establishing rights and duties for employers and employees.
compliance_requirements: Formal registration of employees in the CTPS (Carteira de Trabalho)
Adherence to maximum working hours
Payment of minimum wage
Granting of mandatory rest periods
compliance_risk: High risk of labor lawsuits and labor inspection fines.
penalty: Administrative fines, payment of back wages, severance payments, and moral damages.

law_code: Constituição da República Federativa do Brasil
definition: Sets forth the fundamental social rights of workers, including the 13th-month salary, paid vacation, and safety regulations.
compliance_requirements: Ensuring minimum wage
Providing paid vacation with 1/3 bonus
Providing maternity and paternity leave
Granting 13th-month salary
compliance_risk: Constitutional violations leading to severe legal challenges.
penalty: Labor claims and potential criminal liability in extreme cases of slave-like labor.

definition: Regulates the processing of personal data of employees by employers.
compliance_requirements: Obtain consent or legal basis for data processing
Ensure data security for employee personal records
Appoint a Data Protection Officer (DPO)
compliance_risk: Administrative sanctions by ANPD.
penalty: Fines of up to 2% of annual revenue (capped at 50 million BRL per infraction).

definition: Requires employers to deposit 8% of the employee's monthly salary into an individual bank account to form a severance fund.
compliance_requirements: Monthly deposits via SEFIP/eSocial
Deposits must occur by the 7th day of the following month
compliance_risk: Fines, interest, and monetary correction by Caixa Econômica Federal.
penalty: Monetary fines and legal interest.

definition: Governs work performed outside the employer's premises, mostly using information and communication technologies.
compliance_requirements: Formal amendment to employment contract
Clear definition of who pays for equipment and infrastructure
Instruction on health and safety
compliance_risk: Claims of overtime if control is not properly managed.
penalty: Liability for labor claims and lack of adherence to safety norms.

definition: Establishes that employment contracts may be executed in an express or tacit manner, verbal or written, for a definite or indefinite period, or for the performance of specified work.
compliance_requirements: Ensure all verbal or written agreements adhere to the principle of primacy of reality
Document contract terms including job description, salary, and working hours
Register the employee in the CTPS (Carteira de Trabalho e Previdência Social) within the legal deadline
compliance_risk: Reclassification of employment status, unrecognized employment relationship, and labor lawsuits.
penalty: Fines per employee not registered and potential payments for backdated labor claims.

definition: Mandates the digitalization of the Work and Social Security Card (CTPS) for all employees.
compliance_requirements: Register hiring information via the eSocial system
Ensure data transmission within 5 business days of hiring
compliance_risk: Administrative fines for failure to report hiring, inability to prove employment history correctly.
penalty: Administrative fine per unregistered employee as set by the Ministry of Labor.

definition: Regulates the provision of services remotely (telework), stating that it must be expressed in the employment contract.
compliance_requirements: Include remote work provision in the employment contract or addendum
Specify responsibilities for equipment and infrastructure expenses
Ensure compliance with occupational health and safety standards
compliance_risk: Potential claims for reimbursement of utilities and equipment costs not defined in the contract.
penalty: Labor court orders for payment of non-reimbursed costs and damages.

definition: Governs the processing of personal data of employees collected for the execution of the employment contract.
compliance_requirements: Obtain consent or rely on legal obligation/contract execution for data processing
Implement data protection policies for HR
Maintain transparency regarding employee data usage
compliance_risk: Investigation by the National Data Protection Authority (ANPD) and civil liability.
penalty: Fines up to 2% of Brazilian revenue capped at 50 million BRL per infraction.

definition: Establishes the standard work duration of 8 hours per day and 44 hours per week, allowing for compensation of hours and reduction of work hours via collective bargaining agreement.
compliance_requirements: Ensure standard workday does not exceed 8 hours
Ensure standard work week does not exceed 44 hours
Implement overtime compensation or time bank if exceeding standard hours
compliance_risk: Labor litigation and claims for overtime pay at a minimum 50% premium.
penalty: Administrative fines by the Ministry of Labor and Employment and court-ordered retroactive payments.

definition: Regulates the calculation of working hours, overtime premiums, and the legal duration of the workday including mandatory intervals.
compliance_requirements: Maintain accurate time-tracking records for establishments with more than 20 employees
Ensure overtime payment of at least 50% above normal hourly wage
Respect mandatory meal and rest breaks
documentation_required: Electronic or manual time cards (Cartão de Ponto)
compliance_risk: Systemic overtime claims, social security tax (INSS) audit findings on unpaid overtime.
penalty: Fines per affected employee and judicial labor claims.

law_name: Consolidation of Labor Laws - Remote Work Amendment
definition: Defines the application of work hour controls for employees in remote work (teletrabalho) and exceptions regarding time control.
compliance_requirements: Monitor hours if the remote work contract does not specify 'task-based' or 'result-based' work
Register equipment usage and time if the employee is subject to hour tracking
documentation_required: Written agreement on work hours or exemption thereof
compliance_risk: Claims of unpaid overtime for remote employees if controls are absent.
penalty: Payment of overtime with premiums plus social security charges.

law_name: Ministry of Labor and Employment Ordinance
definition: Provides technical guidelines for the Electronic Time Control Systems (REP), specifying the requirements for time attendance software.
compliance_requirements: Use certified REP equipment/software
Provide receipt to the employee for every time stamp
Maintain fiscal integrity of time records
documentation_required: Certificate of conformity of the time registration software
compliance_risk: Invalidation of time records in court, creating a presumption of truth for employee-claimed hours.
penalty: Administrative fines and burden of proof reversal in labor courts.

definition: Defines the components of salary, including the base wage, bonuses, and gratuities, which constitute the basis for social security and labor taxes.
compliance_requirements: Include all mandatory salary components in payroll
Maintain accurate records of hours worked and payments
Issue monthly pay stubs (holerites)
compliance_risk: Labor litigation and claims for unpaid wages or benefits
penalty: Fines, mandatory payment of differences, interest, and penalties under labor law

law_name: Lei de Custeio da Seguridade Social
definition: Establishes the employer's obligation to contribute to Social Security (INSS) based on the total payroll amount.
compliance_requirements: Calculate and withhold social security contributions
Pay employer's share of INSS
File monthly reports via eSocial
compliance_risk: Tax audits, seizure of assets, and inability to obtain a tax clearance certificate (CND)
penalty: Multas de mora, interest, and potential criminal charges for tax evasion

definition: Mandates employer deposits of 8% of the employee's monthly salary into a linked Severance Indemnity Fund (FGTS) account.
compliance_requirements: Monthly deposit of 8% on gross salary
Deposit made until the 7th day of the subsequent month
Report via eSocial/FGTS Digital
compliance_risk: Fines by the Ministry of Labor and interest payments upon employee termination
penalty: Administrative fines and mandatory payment of all missed deposits with monetary correction

definition: Regulates the withholding of Personal Income Tax (IRRF) from employee payroll based on progressive tax brackets.
compliance_requirements: Calculate monthly withholding tax on salaries
Deduct allowed expenses and dependents
Pay the withheld amount to the Federal Revenue Service
compliance_risk: Tax non-compliance penalties and investigation by the Receita Federal
penalty: Significant administrative fines, interest, and potential criminal tax charges

definition: Regulates the withholding of Income Tax on payroll (IRRF) for employees, which must be deducted by the employer and paid to the Federal Revenue Service.
compliance_requirements: Calculate withholding tax based on the progressive monthly table
Deduct authorized amounts such as social security contributions and dependents
Generate and submit the DCTFWeb declaration
Payment of tax via DARF within the legal deadline
compliance_risk: Fines for late payment, interest accumulation, and potential tax assessment by the Receita Federal.
penalty: Fines ranging from 0.33% per day of delay up to 20%, plus interest (SELIC rate) and administrative penalties.

definition: Governs the contributions of companies and employees to the social security system (INSS), mandatory for all formal employment relationships.
compliance_requirements: Withhold employee's social security contribution share from salary
Pay the employer's social security contribution share (CPP)
Report all payroll data through the eSocial system
Pay contributions by the 20th day of the following month
documentation_required: Payment slips (GPS or DARF via DCTFWeb)
compliance_risk: High risk of fiscal audit, inability to obtain tax clearance certificates (CND), and criminal liability for misappropriation of social security funds.
penalty: Heavy administrative fines, tax penalties (up to 75% or 150% in cases of fraud), and potential criminal charges.

definition: Mandatory monthly deposit of 8% of the employee's salary into a restricted bank account held by the government to serve as a severance fund.
compliance_requirements: Monthly deposit of 8% on the gross salary
Compliance with the FGTS Digital platform
Ensure timely payment by the 20th day of the month
compliance_risk: Fiscal liability, interest on late deposits, and mandatory compensation in case of labor claims.
penalty: Monetary correction, interest, and fines based on the duration of the delay, plus social charges.

definition: The right of the employee to rest after 12 months of service (aquisitive period), totaling 30 days, to be taken within the following 12 months (concessive period).
compliance_requirements: Grant 30 days of vacation after 12 months of service
Pay vacation remuneration at least 2 days before the start of the period
Include a one-third (1/3) bonus over the salary
Ensure notice of vacation is provided 30 days in advance
compliance_risk: Financial liability for double payment of vacation and potential labor lawsuits.
penalty: Payment of double the vacation remuneration and administrative fines.

definition: Mandatory paid leave for birth or adoption of a child.
compliance_requirements: Grant 120 days of maternity leave
Grant 5 days of paternity leave (extensible to 20 days under Empresa Cidadã program)
Guarantee job stability from conception until 5 months after childbirth
documentation_required: Company registration under Empresa Cidadã (if applicable)
compliance_risk: Claims for reinstatement or indemnity due to breach of job stability.
penalty: Payment of salaries for the entire stability period and potential moral damages.

law_name: Lei de Benefícios da Previdência Social
definition: Financial benefit provided to employees incapacitated for work for more than 15 consecutive days.
compliance_requirements: Pay the first 15 days of absence due to illness
Direct employee to the Social Security Institute (INSS) for exams after the 15th day
Maintain the employment contract during the suspension period
compliance_risk: Unfair termination of an employee under medical leave status.
penalty: Reinstatement and payment of back wages.

definition: Mandatory employer-provided benefit to cover commuting expenses between home and work.
compliance_requirements: Provide vouchers for public transportation
Deduct a maximum of 6% from the employee's base salary
Ensure the voucher covers the entire commute
documentation_required: Proof of issuance of transportation benefits
compliance_risk: Labor claim for reimbursement of commuting expenses.
penalty: Fines by the Ministry of Labor and repayment of owed transport amounts.

definition: Mandates the formal registration of all employees in the CTPS (Carteira de Trabalho e Previdência Social) and digital systems upon hiring.
compliance_requirements: Register employee within 5 days of hiring
Record start date, salary, and job description
Maintain updated records in eSocial system
compliance_risk: High risk of labor lawsuits for 'irregular work' or 'black work' (trabalho sem registro).
penalty: Fines per unregistered employee ranging from 800 to 3,000 BRL plus back-dated social security contributions.

law_name: Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados (LGPD)
definition: Regulates the processing and storage of employee personal and sensitive data collected during the hiring process.
compliance_requirements: Obtain specific consent for data processing
Implement data protection policies
Ensure data minimization
compliance_risk: Administrative sanctions by ANPD and potential civil lawsuits.
penalty: Fines up to 2% of local revenue, capped at 50 million BRL per infraction.

law_name: Lei de Cotas para Pessoas com Deficiência
definition: Mandates companies with 100 or more employees to hire a quota of persons with disabilities.
compliance_requirements: Reserve 2% to 5% of positions for disabled workers depending on company size
Accessibility in the workplace
compliance_risk: Fines imposed by the Ministry of Labor and Employment.
penalty: Significant administrative fines calculated per missing position.

definition: Requires the identification and management of occupational risks prior to and during employment.
compliance_requirements: Conduct pre-employment medical examinations (ASO)
Implement Risk Management Program (PGR)
compliance_risk: Regulatory shutdowns and labor accident liability.
penalty: Administrative fines based on the number of employees and severity of the violation.

definition: Defines the employment relationship based on the presence of four elements: personality, habitual nature, onerousness, and subordination. If these elements are present, an employment relationship is characterized regardless of the nomenclature given to the contract.
compliance_requirements: Verify absence of subordination (autonomy in work execution)
Ensure no requirement for personal service if the contract is B2B
Avoid continuous control of work schedule for independent contractors
documentation_required: Service Agreement (Contrato de Prestação de Serviços)
Proof of registration as a legal entity (CNPJ)
compliance_risk: High risk of labor claim (Reclamatória Trabalhista) seeking to recognize an employment relationship and the resulting payment of all labor rights and social security taxes.
penalty: Retroactive payment of all employment benefits (13th salary, vacation, severance fund/FGTS, notice period), potential fines for failure to register employee, and civil/labor lawsuit costs.

definition: Establishes principles of economic freedom and reinforces the validity of private contracts, promoting the differentiation between civil/commercial service contracts and employment contracts.
compliance_requirements: Ensure the contract explicitly outlines the autonomous nature of the work
Document the commercial nature of the relationship
documentation_required: Proof of contractor's independent business operations
compliance_risk: Judicial challenge if the substance of the relationship contradicts the written contract.
penalty: Possible disregard of the civil contract by labor courts and imposition of employment labor charges.

definition: Regulates the outsourcing of services, establishing that the company providing the service must be a distinct legal entity responsible for the management and remuneration of its workers.
compliance_requirements: Verify technical and economic capacity of the outsourced service provider
Ensure no personal subordination of outsourced employees to the hiring company
documentation_required: Proof of payment of social security contributions by the provider
Social Contract of the service provider company
compliance_risk: Subsidiary liability of the hiring company for labor and social security debts of the outsourced contractor.
penalty: Payment of outstanding labor debts and social security contributions if the contractor defaults.

definition: Regulates the rights and obligations regarding the termination of employment contracts, including notice periods, severance pay, and documentation settlement.
compliance_requirements: Pay severance within 10 days of the contract termination
Issue the Term of Rescission of Employment Contract (TRCT)
Submit the severance payment proof
Provide documentation for unemployment insurance withdrawal
documentation_required: TRCT (Termo de Rescisão do Contrato de Trabalho)
Updated CTPS (Carteira de Trabalho e Previdência Social)
compliance_risk: High financial risk including litigation, administrative fines, and labor court claims for incorrect severance calculation.
penalty: Fine equivalent to one month's salary for delay in payment (Article 477, § 8º).

definition: Mandates the payment of a 40% penalty on the total balance of the FGTS account in cases of dismissal without cause.
compliance_requirements: Calculate 40% penalty on total FGTS deposits made during employment
Deposit the penalty amount into the employee's FGTS account via the Caixa Econômica Federal portal
compliance_risk: Tax audit from Caixa Econômica and labor lawsuit for unpaid penalty.
penalty: Monetary correction, interest, and administrative fines imposed by the Ministry of Labor.

definition: Establishes the statute of limitations for filing labor claims, which is two years after the termination of the employment contract.
compliance_requirements: Adhere to the two-year deadline for termination-related claims to avoid litigation risks
compliance_risk: Litigation for back-pay and rights up to two years post-employment.
penalty: Loss of right to claim (statute of limitations) or liability in labor court for payment of claims.

definition: Authorizes collective dismissal without the requirement of prior authorization from the labor union or collective bargaining.
compliance_requirements: Execute dismissal process in accordance with non-discrimination and procedural requirements
Ensure equal treatment in severance calculations
compliance_risk: Public civil actions by the Public Prosecutor's Office if dismissal is deemed discriminatory.
penalty: Possible moral damages (dano moral coletivo) if conducted improperly.

definition: Mandates that every employee must be officially registered in the employer's records, including digital systems like eSocial.
compliance_requirements: Register employee details at the start of employment
Maintain current and accurate employment records
compliance_risk: Fines per unregistered employee and potential lawsuits claiming employment relationship.
penalty: Fixed fines per employee (approximately R$ 3,000.00 per employee).

definition: Regulations for the Occupational Health Medical Control Program (PCMSO).
compliance_requirements: Perform admission, periodic, and dismissal medical exams
Maintain up-to-date health records
compliance_risk: Fines from the Ministry of Labor and Labor Prosecution Office investigations.
penalty: Variable fines based on the number of employees and the severity of the infraction.

definition: Obligation to report payroll data, earnings, and social security contributions via eSocial.
compliance_requirements: Transmit monthly payroll events via eSocial
Pay social security taxes (INSS) on time
compliance_risk: Tax audits and inability to obtain tax clearance certificates (CND).
penalty: Fines for late filing or omission of information.

definition: Governs the documentation requirements for employees working remotely, including the need for a written contract amendment.
compliance_requirements: Specify the nature of work
Define responsibility for infrastructure costs
Include remote work provision in the employment contract
compliance_risk: Claims for equipment reimbursement and overtime pay if remote work terms are not documented.
penalty: Civil liability for costs and labor lawsuits.

definition: Establishes basic rights for urban and rural workers, including minimum wage, 13th salary, and overtime protection.
compliance_requirements: Ensure payment of minimum wage
Provide paid weekly rest
Provide notice of termination
compliance_risk: Constitutional violation leading to civil and labor lawsuits.
penalty: Labor lawsuits, indemnification payments, and potential public civil actions.

definition: Regulates the processing of personal data, including employee data, ensuring privacy and security.
compliance_requirements: Implement data protection policies for employees
Ensure lawful basis for processing HR data
Appoint a Data Protection Officer
compliance_risk: Data breaches and unauthorized processing leading to significant administrative sanctions.
penalty: Administrative fines up to 2% of local revenue capped at 50 million BRL per infraction; public warning.

definition: Governs Occupational Risks Management (GRO) and the Risk Assessment Program (PGR).
compliance_requirements: Create and maintain a risk management program
Provide mandatory safety training
Implement preventive measures
compliance_risk: Workplace accidents and Ministry of Labor inspections resulting in closure or fines.
penalty: Administrative fines per regulation breach; criminal liability in case of severe accidents.

definition: Unified system for recording tax, social security, and labor information related to the workforce.
compliance_requirements: Submit events regarding employee hiring, leaves, and payroll
Maintain consistency between internal data and government system
compliance_risk: Inconsistencies leading to tax audits and inability to issue tax clearance certificates.
penalty: Fines for delayed or incorrect reporting of social security and tax information.

definition: Recognizes collective bargaining agreements and conventions as a fundamental labor right, ensuring the validity of negotiated terms between unions and employers.
compliance_requirements: Recognition of collective bargaining agreements and conventions
Participation of unions in collective bargaining
compliance_risk: Invalidation of negotiated benefits or terms if not aligned with constitutional limits.
penalty: Legal disputes, potential labor claims for unpaid benefits, and court orders for non-compliance.

definition: Governs the procedure for collective bargaining, defining the scope of Collective Labor Agreements (ACT) between unions and specific companies, and Collective Bargaining Conventions (CCT) between unions and employer categories.
compliance_requirements: Mandatory participation of trade unions in bargaining
Formal registry of agreements with the Ministry of Labor
Strict adherence to negotiated clauses concerning wages and working conditions
documentation_required: Copy of the registered Collective Bargaining Convention
compliance_risk: Unfair labor practices, strikes, judicial nullification of agreements, and significant financial liability.
penalty: Fines, mandatory payment of retroactive benefits, and legal sanctions for infringing on labor rights.

definition: Establishes the 'legislated over negotiated' principle, allowing collective agreements to override certain legal provisions under specific conditions (e.g., working hours, break times, telework rules).
compliance_requirements: Ensure the negotiated clause is superior to the statutory minimum where required
Registration in the Ministry of Labor system (Mediador)
Adherence to the specific list of negotiable topics in Art 611-A
documentation_required: Document proof of deposit with Ministry of Labor
compliance_risk: Nullity of agreement if it reduces non-negotiable rights (Art 611-B).
penalty: Payment of benefits as per original law, moral damages, and administrative fines.

law_name: Ministry of Labor - Mediador System Regulation
definition: Regulates the mandatory electronic registry system (Mediador) for all collective bargaining instruments in Brazil.
compliance_requirements: Electronic registration of all ACTs and CCTs via the Mediador platform
Transmission of the electronic copy within the legal timeframe
documentation_required: Digital file of the signed agreement
compliance_risk: Lack of legal validity of the agreement for payroll or administrative purposes without proper registry.
penalty: Administrative sanctions by the Ministry of Labor and Labor Prosecution Office.

law_name: Federal Constitution of the Federative Republic of Brazil
definition: Establishes the freedom of professional or trade union association, the principle of a single union per category within a base, and the requirement of union participation in collective labor negotiations.
compliance_requirements: Recognize the freedom of association
Participate in mandatory collective bargaining
Maintain the unicast/singularity principle of unions
compliance_risk: Legal challenges to employment practices not aligned with mandatory collective standards and potential labor court lawsuits.
penalty: Civil and labor liability, nullification of collective agreements, potential labor fines.

definition: Governs the organization, management, and regulation of labor unions in Brazil, including the rights and duties of unions and their interaction with employers.
compliance_requirements: Adherence to collective bargaining results
Observance of union dues (contributions) as defined by the labor reform
Cooperation with union representatives
Recognition of union delegates
documentation_required: Proof of payment of union contributions if mandatory by CCT
Copy of valid Collective Bargaining Agreements
compliance_risk: Fines from the Ministry of Labor and Employment (MTE) and adverse rulings in individual and collective labor lawsuits.
penalty: Administrative fines, potential labor lawsuits for breach of collective terms.

law_name: Consolidation of Labor Laws (CLT) - Collective Bargaining
definition: Establishes the prevalence of negotiated agreements over the law (negotiated prevails over legislated) for specific aspects of labor conditions.
compliance_requirements: Formalizing agreements in writing
Strict adherence to items allowed for negotiation
Ensuring no reduction in constitutional rights
documentation_required: Evidence of registration with the Ministry of Labor
compliance_risk: Invalidation of employment contracts if negotiated terms violate the mandatory non-waivable rights defined in Art. 611-B.
penalty: Financial indemnification, nullification of contractual clauses, civil liability.

definition: Regulates telework (remote work) as the provision of services outside the employer's premises, using information and communication technologies, which by its nature is not an external work.
compliance_requirements: Formalize the telework agreement in the employment contract
Specify the responsibility for the acquisition, maintenance, or supply of technological equipment
Define the infra-structure expenses reimbursement policy
Notify the employee about safety and health guidelines for the home office
documentation_required: Term of commitment regarding IT safety and infrastructure
compliance_risk: Reclassification of remote work status, potential labor lawsuits regarding equipment costs, and liability for occupational diseases or accidents.
penalty: Administrative fines by the Ministry of Labor and Employment, payment of retroactive allowances, and potential damages for labor violations.

definition: Governs the processing of personal data, including employee data accessed or processed in remote environments.
compliance_requirements: Implement technical and administrative measures to protect personal data from unauthorized access
Provide training to remote employees on data security
Update privacy policies to reflect remote work conditions
compliance_risk: Severe financial penalties from the National Data Protection Authority (ANPD) and reputational damage.
penalty: Fines of up to 2% of the company's revenue in Brazil per infraction, limited to R$ 50 million per violation.

definition: Establishes parameters for ergonomics, applicable to remote work environments where the employer must guide the employee on safe working conditions.
compliance_requirements: Provide written and ostensive instructions regarding safety and ergonomics
Ensure the employee signs a liability term confirming receipt of instructions
documentation_required: Liability Statement signed by the employee regarding adherence to safety instructions
compliance_risk: Employer liability for work-related musculoskeletal disorders (LER/DORT) developed while working remotely.
penalty: Administrative fines and liability for professional illness compensation.

law_name: Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados Pessoais (LGPD)
definition: The LGPD establishes the rules for the processing of personal data of individuals in Brazil, including employees, ensuring rights to privacy, access, correction, and deletion of data.
compliance_requirements: Appointment of a Data Protection Officer (DPO)
Maintaining a record of processing activities
Implementing security and technical measures to protect employee data
Ensuring data minimization (collecting only necessary data)
Adopting transparency regarding the purpose of data collection
documentation_required: Data Processing Agreements with third-party payroll providers
compliance_risk: Exposure of sensitive employee data, loss of business reputation, and legal litigation.
penalty: Administrative sanctions including warnings, fines of up to 2% of the company's revenue in Brazil (capped at 50 million BRL per infraction), and public disclosure of the violation.

law_code: Constituição da República Federativa do Brasil
definition: Constitutional protection of the intimate privacy, private life, honor, and image of individuals as a fundamental right.
compliance_requirements: Respect for the private life of employees
Protection of individual intimacy within the workplace
compliance_risk: Civil liability for moral damages caused by violation of privacy.
penalty: Indemnity for moral and material damages in civil courts.

definition: Sets the mandatory standards for occupational health and safety, requiring employers to implement preventive measures to avoid workplace accidents and occupational diseases.
compliance_requirements: Implement internal accident prevention committees (CIPA)
Provide personal protective equipment (EPI) free of charge
Conduct periodic occupational health exams (ASO)
Maintain a safe and hygienic working environment
compliance_risk: Civil, criminal, and administrative liability in case of accidents or health complications.
penalty: Fines, mandatory shutdowns, and potential lawsuits for moral or physical damages.

law_code: Ministry of Labor and Employment Portaria SEPRT No. 6.730
definition: Establishes the obligation for employers to manage occupational risks through a Risk Management Program (PGR).
compliance_requirements: Draft and implement a Risk Management Program (PGR)
Establish an Occupational Hazard Inventory
Implement an Action Plan for risk mitigation
compliance_risk: Non-compliance results in heavy fines and potential work embargoes by the Ministry of Labor.
penalty: Fines variable according to the number of employees and the nature of the infraction (Infracion code).

law_code: Ministry of Labor and Employment Portaria SEPRT No. 6.734
definition: Mandates the Program of Occupational Medical Control (PCMSO) to monitor employee health.
compliance_requirements: Create and maintain a PCMSO based on workplace risks
Schedule admission, periodic, return-to-work, and dismissal medical exams
documentation_required: ASO (Atestado de Saúde Ocupacional) for every employee
compliance_risk: Administrative sanctions and legal vulnerability in labor courts regarding occupational diseases.
penalty: Monetary fines and legal orders to cease operations until health protocols are corrected.

definition: Constitutional right of workers to reduce risks inherent to work through health, hygiene, and safety rules.
compliance_requirements: Ensure compliance with all occupational safety standards as a fundamental right
compliance_risk: Violation of constitutional principles, potentially leading to class action lawsuits.
penalty: Judicial intervention and liability for damages.

definition: Regulates the rights and duties of migrants in Brazil, including temporary and residence visas for professional purposes.
compliance_requirements: Obtain specific work authorization prior to the immigrant's entry
Registration with the Federal Police within 90 days of arrival
Maintenance of consistent job duties with the authorization granted
documentation_required: Work authorization permit from the Ministry of Labor and Employment
compliance_risk: Deportation of the employee, heavy administrative fines for the employer, and inability to hire foreign nationals in the future.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from R$ 1,000 to R$ 1,000,000 per violation.

definition: Detailed regulations for the implementation of the Migration Law (Law 13.445), specifying the procedures for requesting work-related residence permits.
compliance_requirements: Verification of compliance with local social security and tax obligations
Submission of technical or professional documentation to the Ministry of Labor portal
Annual reporting of foreign employees
documentation_required: Proof of legal status in Brazil
compliance_risk: Rejection of residency permits and inability to regularize the employee status.
penalty: Denial of permits and administrative fines under immigration statutes.

law_name: Normative Resolution of the National Immigration Council
definition: Establishes administrative criteria for the granting of temporary residence and work permits for foreign individuals under labor contracts in Brazil.
compliance_requirements: Equal treatment regarding salary and benefits between foreign and local employees
Compliance with mandatory social security payments for foreign workers
Notification to the Ministry of Labor regarding any changes in the employment contract
documentation_required: CNPJ registration of the Brazilian entity
compliance_risk: Administrative sanctions, labor litigation regarding benefits discrimination, and fiscal penalties.
penalty: Fines ranging from R$ 100 to R$ 10,000 per violation.

law_name: Federal Constitution of the Federative Republic of Brazil
definition: Guarantees the inviolability of the right to life, liberty, equality, security and property, prohibiting discrimination of any kind.
compliance_requirements: Ensure equal treatment for all employees
Implement non-discriminatory hiring and promotion practices
Maintain workplace environment free of prejudice
compliance_risk: High - Breach of fundamental constitutional rights and potential civil lawsuits for moral damages.
penalty: Civil and criminal liability; indemnification for moral damages.

definition: Prohibits discriminatory practices in access to employment and maintenance of the employment relationship, particularly against women.
compliance_requirements: Prohibit requiring pregnancy tests for hiring or maintaining employment
Ensure equal pay for equal work regardless of gender
Provide equal opportunities for professional training and promotion
documentation_required: Payroll reports demonstrating gender pay equality
compliance_risk: High - Fines by the Ministry of Labor and Employment; lawsuits for moral and material damages.
penalty: Administrative fines; judicial orders for damages; potential reintegration of the employee.

definition: Defines crimes resulting from discrimination or prejudice of race, color, ethnicity, religion, or national origin.
compliance_requirements: Ensure zero tolerance for racial or religious discrimination in the workplace
Respond appropriately to any reports of discrimination
compliance_risk: Very High - Criminal offense punishable by imprisonment; heavy reputation damage.
penalty: Imprisonment, fines, and legal liability for the company.

law_name: Law of Inclusion of Persons with Disabilities
definition: Establishes the right of persons with disabilities to work in a safe and accessible environment, prohibiting discrimination in all phases of employment.
compliance_requirements: Implement mandatory quotas for hiring PwD
Ensure accessibility to the physical and digital workplace
Provide reasonable accommodation
compliance_risk: High - Inspection by the Ministry of Labor and Employment resulting in heavy fines.
penalty: Administrative fines; potential Public Civil Actions.

definition: Mandates equal pay and equal criteria for remuneration between women and men for work of equal value.
compliance_requirements: Publish mandatory salary transparency reports
Implement internal mechanisms for pay auditing
Promote diversity programs and support for return from parental leave
compliance_risk: High - Regulatory fines and mandatory legal adjustments.
penalty: Administrative fine up to 3% of the company's payroll; reimbursement of the salary difference.

definition: Governs the nature of salary and benefits in-kind provided by the employer, including food, housing, and clothing.
compliance_requirements: Provide benefits essential to the execution of work without salary deduction unless authorized
Ensure benefits do not exceed specific limits defined by labor statutes
compliance_risk: Risk of characterization of benefits as salary (salary substitution) leading to labor lawsuits and increased payroll taxes (INSS/FGTS).
penalty: Payment of social security contributions on the value of benefits plus fines and interest.

definition: The Guarantee Fund for Length of Service (FGTS) is a mandatory savings fund created for the employee's protection upon termination without cause.
compliance_requirements: Deposit 8% of the employee's gross monthly salary into an FGTS account
Make deposits by the 7th day of each month
documentation_required: GFIP (Guia de Recolhimento do FGTS e de Informações à Previdência Social)
compliance_risk: Fines, interest, and the risk of labor claims demanding retroactive payments.
penalty: Monetary correction, interest, and administrative fines imposed by the Ministry of Labor.

definition: Program to provide nutritional support to workers, allowing companies to deduct meal expenses from income tax.
compliance_requirements: Register with the Ministry of Labor to participate in PAT
Ensure nutritional quality of provided meals
compliance_risk: Loss of tax incentives and potential characterization of meal allowance as salary.
penalty: Revocation of registration and payment of back taxes (IRPJ).

definition: Requires employers to provide transportation vouchers for the commute between home and work.
compliance_requirements: Provide vouchers for public transportation
Deduct a maximum of 6% of the employee's base salary for this purpose
Ensure the benefit covers actual commuting costs
compliance_risk: Labor claims for reimbursement of commuting costs paid out of pocket by employees.
penalty: Payment of unpaid transportation costs plus potential administrative fines.

definition: Mandates the requirement for employers to maintain accurate payroll records and register employees in the Digital Labor Book (Carteira de Trabalho Digital).
compliance_requirements: Maintain updated payroll registers
Issue monthly pay stubs (holerites)
Provide timely information to the eSocial system
compliance_risk: Labor lawsuits, fines by the Ministry of Labor, and lack of proof of salary payments.
penalty: Administrative fines per employee and potential labor liabilities.

definition: Obligates employers to report all labor facts and remuneration to the federal revenue authorities via the eSocial platform.
compliance_requirements: Monthly reporting of social security contributions
Report all payments made to employees
Detail the calculation base for INSS
documentation_required: GFIP (Guia de Recolhimento do FGTS e de Informações à Previdência Social)
compliance_risk: Tax audits, tax assessment notices, and inability of employees to access social security benefits.
penalty: Fines based on the value of uncollected or unreported taxes.

definition: Established the Digital System of Tax, Social Security, and Labor Obligations (eSocial), centralizing all payroll reporting.
compliance_requirements: Submission of standardized digital files
Real-time or periodic updates regarding employee status and payroll
Compliance with the layout manuals provided by the government
compliance_risk: Non-compliance prevents issuance of Certidão Negativa de Débitos (CND), hindering business operations.
penalty: Fixed fines for late submission or incorrect information.

definition: Mandates that employers record employment contracts in the Carteira de Trabalho e Previdência Social (CTPS) within 5 working days of the hire date.
compliance_requirements: Record employee personal data
Record hiring date
Record initial salary and contractual conditions
Update records for any changes in position, salary, or function
documentation_required: Carteira de Trabalho e Previdência Social (CTPS)
compliance_risk: Risk of labor lawsuits for failure to register, leading to unrecognized employment relationships (vínculo empregatício) and potential labor claims.
penalty: Administrative fines per unregistered employee and potential retrospective payment of all labor charges and social contributions.

law_name: eSocial Law (Public System of Digital Bookkeeping of Labor, Tax and Social Security Obligations)
definition: Centralized digital platform for employers to report data regarding workers to the government agencies (RFB, MTE, INSS).
compliance_requirements: Submit hiring events (S-2200) within the mandatory deadline
Maintain current data on professional history and benefits
Real-time synchronization with government databases
compliance_risk: Non-compliance leads to suspension of the employer's tax certificate and heavy fines for late or inaccurate reporting.
penalty: Fixed fines per delayed or missing event, varying according to the nature of the obligation.

law_name: Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados (LGPD)
definition: Regulates the processing and collection of personal data of employees during the registration process to ensure privacy and security.
compliance_requirements: Obtain legal basis for data processing (hiring contract)
Maintain transparency on data usage
Restrict access to sensitive HR data
compliance_risk: Administrative sanctions by ANPD and litigation related to data privacy breaches during the onboarding phase.
penalty: Fines of up to 2% of the company's revenue in Brazil, limited to R$ 50 million per violation.

definition: Mandates that employment contracts must be in writing and signed by the parties within 15 days of the start of services (5 days for fixed-term contracts).
compliance_requirements: Provide a written copy of the contract
Include identity of parties, role, workplace, salary, and working hours
Maintain signed originals at the workplace
compliance_risk: Fines, legal presumption that the employer's version of the facts is true
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 1 to 5 UTM per employee

definition: Regulations governing maximum weekly working hours (44 hours as of 2024, transitioning to 40) and overtime payment rules.
compliance_requirements: Maintain a registry of attendance
Cap overtime at 2 hours per day
Pay overtime with a 50% surcharge
documentation_required: Attendance registry (libro de asistencia o registro digital)
compliance_risk: Regulatory sanctions and back-pay claims
penalty: Fines varying by company size

law_name: Ley de Protección de Datos Personales
definition: Regulates the treatment of personal data of employees, requiring consent for processing sensitive information.
compliance_requirements: Obtain consent for data processing
Ensure data security measures
Confidentiality agreements
compliance_risk: Legal liability for unauthorized disclosure or misuse of private data
penalty: Civil damages and administrative penalties

law_name: Ley de Trabajo a Distancia y Teletrabajo
definition: Framework for remote work agreements, including the right to disconnect and employer responsibilities for equipment.
compliance_requirements: Written remote work agreement
Provide health and safety equipment
Respect right to disconnect
documentation_required: Health and safety assessment for remote workplace
compliance_risk: Lawsuits claiming lack of equipment or failure to respect rest times
penalty: Administrative fines by the Labor Inspectorate

law_name: Ley de Accidentes del Trabajo y Enfermedades Profesionales
definition: Mandates employer liability for workplace safety and obligatory affiliation to a mutual insurance fund (Mutual de Seguridad, IST, or ACHS).
compliance_requirements: Affiliate employees to a mutual
Implement internal health and safety regulations
Provide required safety equipment (EPP)
documentation_required: Proof of affiliation to mutual fund
compliance_risk: Criminal and civil liability in case of workplace accidents
penalty: Fines, legal shutdown of operations, indemnity payments

definition: An employment contract is an agreement whereby the employer and the employee mutually bind themselves, the employee to perform personal services under subordination or dependence, and the employer to pay for these services a specific remuneration.
compliance_requirements: Written contract must be drafted within 15 days of hiring (5 days for contracts less than 30 days)
Must be signed in two copies
Contract must include identity details, date, place, nature of services, duration, and remuneration
compliance_risk: Fines, legal presumption that the content stated by the employee is the actual agreement
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 1 to 10 UTM (Unidad Tributaria Mensual) depending on company size
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile

definition: Regulations governing the provision of services remotely, either partially or fully, outside the employer's premises.
compliance_requirements: Must be specified in the employment contract or an annex
Employer must provide necessary equipment and cover connectivity costs
Right to digital disconnection for at least 12 hours between work periods
documentation_required: Employment contract annex for remote work
compliance_risk: Non-compliance with equipment provision or disconnection rights leading to labor lawsuits
penalty: Fines by the Labor Inspectorate
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile

law_name: Ley de Protección de Datos Personales
definition: Regulation of the processing of personal data, including data collected through employment contracts and employee monitoring.
compliance_requirements: Obtaining express consent for data processing if required
Ensuring data collected is for legitimate business purposes only
Limiting data storage to what is necessary for the employment relationship
compliance_risk: Civil liability, damages for unauthorized disclosure
penalty: Indemnification for damages and potential administrative sanctions
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile

definition: Defines working time as the period during which the employee must be at the employer's disposal and perform the work for which they were hired.
compliance_requirements: Ensure employees are at disposal during contracted hours
Maintain accurate records of attendance
Restrict working hours to the legal maximum
documentation_required: Attendance logs (Libro de asistencia or electronic system)
compliance_risk: Fines by the Labor Inspectorate and potential lawsuits for unpaid hours.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 1 to 60 Monthly Tax Units (UTM) depending on company size and infraction severity.
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile - Código del Trabajo

definition: Mandates the gradual reduction of the ordinary working week from 45 to 40 hours.
compliance_requirements: Update employment contracts to reflect hour reductions
Implement shift distribution schedules
Ensure compliance with the new maximum threshold
compliance_risk: Non-compliance with the gradual reduction schedule leading to sanctions.
penalty: Fines from 1 to 60 UTM per affected worker.
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile - Ley 21.561

definition: Defines overtime as the hours worked in excess of the maximum legal workday or agreed contract duration, limited to 2 hours per day.
compliance_requirements: Overtime must be documented in writing
Overtime must serve temporary needs of the company
Pay overtime with a minimum 50% surcharge
compliance_risk: Illegal use of overtime and failure to pay the 50% surcharge.
penalty: Fine and forced payment of withheld overtime wages.
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile - Código del Trabajo

definition: Defines remuneration as the counterpart of the services rendered by the worker, including base salary, overtime, commissions, and bonuses.
compliance_requirements: Payment of remuneration within the periods established in the contract
Issuance of mandatory payslips (liquidación de sueldo)
Deduction of mandatory social security contributions
documentation_required: Proof of bank transfer or payment receipt
compliance_risk: Fines by the Labor Directorate and mandatory payment of interest and indexation for late payments.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 1 to 60 UTM depending on company size and number of employees affected.
source_reference: Dirección del Trabajo - Código del Trabajo

definition: Mandatory contribution to the pension capitalization system (AFP) based on the employee's taxable income.
compliance_requirements: Deduction of pension contributions from gross salary
Transfer of funds to the selected AFP
Submission of the 'Previred' declaration
compliance_risk: Civil liability for the employer for unpaid contributions; prohibition to fire the worker if contributions are pending (Ley Bustos).
penalty: Fines, interest, indexation, and potential criminal charges if contributions are withheld and not paid.

definition: Mandatory health insurance contribution (minimum 7% of taxable income) to either public (FONASA) or private (ISAPRE) health providers.
compliance_requirements: Withholding of 7% (or higher for Isapre) from taxable income
Monthly payment through electronic platforms
Verification of health provider status
compliance_risk: Lack of health coverage for employees during medical emergencies and legal liability for the employer.
penalty: Fines for late payment and interest accrued on the debt.

definition: Mandatory contribution to the Unemployment Insurance Fund (AFC) to provide income protection during periods of unemployment.
compliance_requirements: Deduction of employee portion
Payment of employer portion (for open-ended contracts)
Monthly reporting through Previred
compliance_risk: Non-compliance prevents employees from accessing benefits during contract termination.
penalty: Administrative fines by the Labor Inspectorate.

law_name: Income Tax Law (Impuesto a la Renta)
definition: Requirement to withhold Second Category Tax (Impuesto Único de Segunda Categoría) on monthly wages above certain thresholds.
compliance_requirements: Calculate monthly tax withholding based on current SII tables
Declare and pay withheld amounts to the Treasury (Tesorería General de la República)
Annual affidavit (Declaración Jurada) submission
compliance_risk: Tax evasion audits, fines, and interest penalties from the Internal Revenue Service (SII).
penalty: Monetary fines based on the amount of under-reported or unpaid tax, plus interest.

law_name: Income Tax Law (Ley sobre Impuesto a la Renta)
definition: Regulates the Second Category Tax (Impuesto de Segunda Categoría) applicable to salaries, wages, and other remunerations received by employees.
compliance_requirements: Calculate and withhold income tax monthly based on the income tax table (Tabla de Impuesto Único de Segunda Categoría)
Deduct mandatory social security contributions before calculating tax base
Perform the tax adjustment (reliquidación) if required
compliance_risk: Under-withholding or failure to declare collected taxes results in tax evasion liabilities and back taxes.
penalty: Monetary fines calculated as a percentage of the omitted tax, plus interest and inflation adjustments.

definition: Defines what constitutes remuneration for tax and social security purposes, establishing that all amounts in cash or benefits in kind that originate from the employment contract are subject to tax unless legally exempt.
compliance_requirements: Clearly distinguish between taxable and non-taxable income in the payroll
Apply correct social security and tax withholding percentages to total taxable remuneration
compliance_risk: Misclassification of income as non-taxable to avoid tax contributions.
penalty: Administrative fines by the Inspección del Trabajo and potential tax reassessment by the SII.

definition: Requirement for employers to report monthly payroll information to the Servicio de Impuestos Internos (SII) through electronic statements.
compliance_requirements: File the Affidavit (Declaración Jurada) 1887 regarding salaries annually
Maintain updated digital records of tax withholdings
compliance_risk: Late filing or erroneous reporting of employee tax data leading to automated system flags.
penalty: Fines ranging from 1 to 50 UTA (Unidades Tributarias Anuales) based on the size of the company.

definition: Regulates the right of employees to paid annual leave and special permissions for life events.
compliance_requirements: Provide 15 working days of annual paid leave after one year of service
Provide 7 paid days for death of a child or spouse/partner
Provide 3 paid days for death of a parent or sibling
compliance_risk: Fines by the Labor Inspectorate and potential lawsuits for denied rights.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 1 to 60 UTM depending on company size.
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile

law_name: Ley de Protección a la Maternidad y Paternidad
definition: Mandatory paid maternity and paternity leave, including childcare benefits and job protection (fuero).
compliance_requirements: Grant 6 weeks prenatal and 12 weeks postnatal leave
Grant 5 days of paternity leave
Provide nursing breaks for mothers
documentation_required: Proof of childcare nursery (sala cuna) enrollment
compliance_risk: Termination in violation of protected leave leads to significant reinstatement costs and high fines.
penalty: Fines from 10 to 60 UTM plus labor tribunal penalties.

definition: Compulsory insurance for parents to take time off to care for children with serious health conditions.
compliance_requirements: Allow leave duration as prescribed by medical authority
Maintain salary payment during the social security coverage period
documentation_required: Medical authorization from a specialized commission
compliance_risk: Blocking the use of the benefit results in severe regulatory sanctions.
penalty: Fines up to 60 UTM per violation.

law_name: Ley sobre Accidentes del Trabajo y Enfermedades Profesionales
definition: Regulates benefits and leaves related to work-related injuries or occupational illnesses.
compliance_requirements: Affiliate employees to an Mutual de Seguridad or ISL
Report accidents immediately
Grant leaves ordered by medical professionals
compliance_risk: Liability for civil damages and criminal responsibility if safety protocols are ignored.
penalty: Fines and potential criminal liability under the Penal Code.

definition: Sets out the mandatory minimum requirements for an employment contract in Chile.
compliance_requirements: Include identity details of parties
Specify place and date of contract
State nature of services and city of performance
Define salary and payment schedule
Define duration of the contract
Establish working hours
documentation_required: Signed copy held by both parties
compliance_risk: Presumption of the existence of an employment contract on the terms declared by the employee if a written document is not signed.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 1 to 5 monthly tax units (UTM) per employee affected.
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile

law_name: Ley de Protección de la Vida Privada
definition: Regulates the treatment of personal data of workers during the hiring and employment process.
compliance_requirements: Obtain explicit consent for background checks
Limit data collection to relevant professional information
Maintain confidentiality of employee personal records
compliance_risk: Civil liability for damages and administrative sanctions by the Civil Registry or courts.
penalty: Fines and potential lawsuits for damages to the employee's privacy.
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile

definition: Mandates companies with 100 or more employees to reserve at least 1% of the workforce for people with disabilities.
compliance_requirements: Maintain 1% disability quota
Report annual compliance to the Labor Directorate
Provide reasonable accommodations
documentation_required: Proof of registration with the National Disability Service
compliance_risk: Fines for non-compliance and reputational damage.
penalty: Monthly fines equivalent to the minimum monthly wage per missing worker.
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile

definition: Governs the hiring of foreign workers in Chile, requiring valid work permits or residency.
compliance_requirements: Verify visa/residency status
Register the employment contract with the Foreigner Department
Ensure payment of social security contributions
compliance_risk: Deportation of the worker and heavy fines for the employer.
penalty: Fines ranging from 1 to 10 UTM per infraction.

definition: Establishes the definition of an employer and employee, establishing the existence of a labor relationship based on subordination and dependency.
compliance_requirements: Verify presence of personal service
Verify presence of remuneration
Verify existence of subordination and dependency
compliance_risk: Recharacterization of independent contractors as employees resulting in retroactive labor rights.
penalty: Fines ranging from 1 to 60 monthly tax units (UTM) depending on company size and number of workers involved.
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile

definition: Regulates the work performed under subcontracting and provision of services, defining joint liability of the principal company.
compliance_requirements: Maintain registry of contractors
Verify compliance of contractor with labor and social security obligations
Ensure right to information and retention
documentation_required: Certificate of compliance with labor and social security obligations (F30-1)
compliance_risk: Subsidiary or solidary liability for debts of the contractor toward their employees.
penalty: Administrative fines and liability for unpaid wages or social security contributions of the contractor's employees.
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile

law_name: Ley sobre Impuesto a la Renta
definition: Regulates the tax treatment of independent professional income (Boletas de Honorarios) versus subordinate employee income.
compliance_requirements: Withhold income tax for independent contractors when applicable
Ensure proper issuance of electronic receipts (Boletas de Honorarios)
compliance_risk: Tax evasion audits by the Internal Revenue Service (SII) and potential reclassification by the Labor Directorate.
penalty: Fines, surcharges, and interest on omitted tax payments.

definition: Regulates termination based on the needs of the company, which includes changes in market conditions, economic situation, or technological advancements.
compliance_requirements: Provide written notice to the employee indicating the causal reason
Submit a copy of the termination letter to the local Labor Inspectorate
Pay severance based on years of service
documentation_required: Termination letter sent via certified mail or hand-delivered
Copy of the receipt of notification
Calculation sheet for years of service indemnity
compliance_risk: Lawsuits for unjustified dismissal, payment of extra indemnities, and labor court fines.
penalty: Payment of the severance plus potential surcharges of 30% to 100% determined by a judge if deemed unjustified.
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile

definition: Mandates the requirement for the employer to be up to date with social security contributions at the time of dismissal for the dismissal to be legally valid (Ley Bustos).
compliance_requirements: Verify full payment of social security, pension, and health insurance contributions prior to termination
Include proof of payment in the termination letter
documentation_required: Proof of payment of social security contributions (Planillas de cotizaciones)
compliance_risk: Dismissal is considered null and void if contributions are not fully paid.
penalty: The employer must pay the employee's salary and social security contributions from the date of termination until the date the omission is corrected.
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile

definition: Regulates the formalization of the 'Finiquito', the legal settlement agreement that concludes the employment relationship.
compliance_requirements: Must be signed before a Public Notary or a Labor Inspector
Must detail the concepts covered and the amounts paid
documentation_required: Proof of payment of all owed amounts
compliance_risk: If the Finiquito is not properly ratified, it may not grant full release of future legal claims.
penalty: The document may be challenged in court, leading to additional litigation costs and potential payouts.
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile

definition: The employment contract must be in writing and must contain specific clauses regarding parties, duties, salary, hours, and duration.
compliance_requirements: Sign the contract within 15 days of hiring
Keep a copy in the workplace
Update the contract for any changes in conditions
documentation_required: Record of digital signature if applicable
Proof of delivery to the employee
compliance_risk: Presumption of the existence of a contract under the terms declared by the worker; fines for lack of formalization.
penalty: Administrative fine between 1 to 5 Monthly Tax Units (UTM) per affected worker.
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile

definition: The employer must maintain a registration system for attendance, overtime, and breaks.
compliance_requirements: Maintain a daily register of attendance
Record the start and end of the workday
Ensure the system is approved by the Labor Directorate
compliance_risk: Fines for lack of reliable attendance tracking; presumption of overtime hours claimed by the employee.
penalty: Administrative fine ranging from 1 to 60 UTM based on company size.

definition: Requirement to provide a liquidation of salary (pay slip) that reflects all earnings and deductions.
compliance_requirements: Issue monthly pay slips
Include mandatory social security and tax deductions
Maintain signed copies of pay slips
documentation_required: Proof of payment (Bank transfer or cash receipt)
compliance_risk: Lack of evidence of payment leads to inability to prove compliance in labor lawsuits.
penalty: Fines from 1 to 10 UTM per worker.

law_name: Ley de Protección de la Vida Privada
definition: Regulation of the collection, processing, and storage of personal data, including employee information.
compliance_requirements: Obtain consent for sensitive data processing
Maintain confidentiality of employee records
Use data only for the purpose it was collected
compliance_risk: Civil liability, damages, and administrative sanctions for unauthorized disclosure.
penalty: Fines and compensation for damages.
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile

law_name: Reglamento sobre Trabajo a Distancia y Teletrabajo
definition: Requires specific clauses and documentation for employees working remotely.
compliance_requirements: Specify the modality in the contract
Provide health and safety equipment check
Specify the digital connection and tools provided
documentation_required: Remote Work Annex to the Contract
compliance_risk: Contract invalidity for remote work if not in writing; safety liability.
penalty: Fines from 1 to 10 UTM.

law_name: Ley de Accidentes del Trabajo y Enfermedades Profesionales
definition: Mandates that employers implement and maintain effective preventative measures to protect the health and safety of employees.
compliance_requirements: Establishment of a Joint Hygiene and Safety Committee
Provision of personal protective equipment
Occupational risk assessment
compliance_risk: Criminal liability for employers in case of serious workplace accidents due to negligence.
penalty: Administrative fines up to 1000 UTM and potential criminal charges.
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile

definition: Regulates the rights and obligations of employers and employees in remote work arrangements, focusing on disconnect rights and equipment provision.
compliance_requirements: Formalization of remote work via contract amendment
Coverage of costs for tools and internet
Respect for the right to disconnection
documentation_required: Remote work annex to employment contract
Risk assessment report for remote workspace
compliance_risk: Non-compliance with disconnection rights leading to labor fines and claims for overtime compensation.
penalty: Fines ranging from 1 to 60 UTM per violation.
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile

definition: The legal framework governing the process of negotiation between an employer and one or more trade unions to establish common working and remuneration conditions.
compliance_requirements: Respond to collective bargaining proposals within the statutory deadlines
Engage in good faith negotiations
Provide information necessary for the collective bargaining process
Ensure the presence of designated representatives during negotiation sessions
documentation_required: Proof of notification of proposal receipt
compliance_risk: Unfair labor practices, strikes, legal sanctions, and damage to labor relations.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 1 to 20 UTM depending on the number of workers and the severity of the infringement.
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile - Código del Trabajo

law_name: Constitución Política de la República de Chile
definition: Guarantees the right of workers to participate in collective bargaining and the right of unions to exist, prohibiting arbitrary interference.
compliance_requirements: Respect the freedom of union association
Non-interference with the internal organization of trade unions
Guarantee the right to collective bargaining for all employees
compliance_risk: Constitutional violation claims, labor lawsuits, and administrative investigation by the Labor Directorate (Inspección del Trabajo).
penalty: Judicial orders to cease conduct and potential heavy civil liability for constitutional infringement.
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile - Constitución Política
