law_name: Ley de Modernización del Sistema de Relaciones Laborales
article_number: Artículos varios que modifican el Código del Trabajo
definition: Law that establishes the rules for union power, multi-employer bargaining, and the expansion of subjects for collective bargaining.
compliance_requirements: Provide periodic financial and company information to union representatives
Facilitate bargaining for unionized workers
Adhere to mandatory minimum floors during negotiation
documentation_required: Financial information reports provided to unions
compliance_risk: Legal challenges to collective bargaining processes, strikes, and fines for non-disclosure of mandatory information.
penalty: Administrative fines administered by the Labor Directorate.
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile - Ley 20.940

definition: Governs the formation, operation, and dissolution of labor unions, including rights to organize, freedom of association, and union immunity (fuero sindical).
compliance_requirements: Facilitate the formation of unions
Provide paid time off (time off quota) for union leaders
Provide union office space if the size of the workforce meets legal thresholds
Deduct union fees from employee payroll upon written request
compliance_risk: Litigation for unfair labor practices (prácticas antisindicales), fines, and potential mandatory reinstatement of union leaders.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 1 to 50 Monthly Tax Units (UTM) depending on the number of workers and the severity of the offense.

definition: Establishes the process, timeline, and rules for collective bargaining between employers and union representatives to reach a collective agreement.
compliance_requirements: Engage in collective bargaining when requested by a union
Submit a formal response to the collective bargaining proposal within the legal timeframe
Maintain current working conditions during the negotiation period (legal floor)
Respect the right to strike in accordance with the law
documentation_required: Collective bargaining proposal (Proyecto de contrato colectivo)
compliance_risk: Fines for bad-faith bargaining, disruption of operations, and legal challenges to the validity of the collective agreement.
penalty: Fines of 10 to 50 UTM, plus the obligation to pay benefits derived from the agreement if non-compliance is proven.

law_code: Constitución Política de la República de Chile
definition: Guarantees the freedom to form unions and the right to collective bargaining as a fundamental right of employees.
compliance_requirements: Guarantee non-interference in union activities
Ensure no discrimination against employees due to union affiliation
compliance_risk: Constitutional protection actions (Recurso de Protección) filed by employees or unions, potentially leading to court-ordered cessation of illegal actions.
penalty: Compensation for damages and potential reversal of discriminatory acts or dismissals.
source_reference: Constitución Política de la República de Chile

law_name: Ley de Trabajo a Distancia y Teletrabajo
article_number: Articles 152 quarter A to 152 quarter O
definition: Regulates work performed at a location other than the employer's premises, using technological, computer, or telecommunication means.
compliance_requirements: Include remote work clause in employment contract
Provide equipment and tools
Cover costs of operation and connectivity
Ensure right to disconnect for at least 12 hours between shifts
Conduct risk assessment of the remote workspace
documentation_required: Training records on safety and health for telework
compliance_risk: Fines, legal orders to pay retroactive expenses, and potential nullification of remote work arrangements.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 1 to 60 monthly tax units (UTM) depending on company size and violation severity.
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile

definition: Establishes technical standards for the prevention of occupational risks in telework and remote work environments.
compliance_requirements: Informing employees of health and safety risks
Providing ergonomic guidelines
Conducting virtual or physical safety evaluations of the workspace
documentation_required: Ergonomic checklist signed by the employee
compliance_risk: Employer liability for workplace accidents occurring at home during working hours.
penalty: Fines by the Labor Inspectorate and potential civil/criminal liability for negligence.

definition: Regulates the processing of personal data, including data collected through monitoring software used during remote work.
compliance_requirements: Obtaining explicit consent for data processing
Implementing security measures for remote data access
Ensuring proportionality in monitoring activities
documentation_required: Consent forms for remote monitoring software
compliance_risk: Legal action for invasion of privacy and misuse of employee data.
penalty: Indemnification for damages and potential administrative sanctions.
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile

law_name: Law No. 19.628 on the Protection of Private Life
definition: Regulates the treatment of personal data in public or private files, establishing the rights of data subjects regarding their personal information including collection, processing, and storage.
compliance_requirements: Obtain express and written consent from the employee for data processing
Limit data collection to information relevant to the employment relationship
Implement technical and organizational measures to ensure data security
Provide notice of data processing purposes to the employee
documentation_required: Data processing agreements with third-party payroll providers
compliance_risk: Unauthorized disclosure of sensitive employee data, civil liability for damages, and reputational risk.
penalty: Indemnification for damages caused to the data subject and potential administrative sanctions under general civil law principles.
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile

definition: Regulates the internal rules of the company (Reglamento Interno), which must protect the employee's dignity and privacy rights in the workplace.
compliance_requirements: Incorporate data protection clauses within the Internal Rules of Order, Hygiene, and Safety
Restrict monitoring of employee communications to what is strictly necessary for business operations
Ensure transparency regarding surveillance equipment or monitoring software
documentation_required: Internal Rules of Order, Hygiene, and Safety (Reglamento Interno)
Employee acknowledgment of company privacy policies
compliance_risk: Fines from the Labor Inspectorate, potential lawsuits for violation of constitutional rights or harassment.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 1 to 60 UTM (Unidades Tributarias Mensuales) depending on the size of the company.

law_name: Constitution of the Republic of Chile
definition: Guarantees the respect and protection of the private life and honor of the individual and their family, including the protection of personal data.
compliance_requirements: Maintain the confidentiality of employee medical records
Respect the fundamental right to privacy in all employment-related actions
Prevent misuse of employee biometric data (fingerprints, etc.)
compliance_risk: Constitutional protection lawsuits (Recurso de Protección) seeking immediate cessation of violations.
penalty: Judicial orders to cease activity, payment of moral damages, and legal costs.
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile

definition: General duty of the employer to take all necessary measures to effectively protect the life and health of workers, providing the conditions and implementing safety equipment.
compliance_requirements: Implement necessary safety measures
Provide personal protective equipment
Maintain a safe work environment
documentation_required: Internal Regulations of Order, Hygiene and Safety
Records of delivery of Personal Protective Equipment
compliance_risk: High liability for industrial accidents, workplace diseases, and potential closure of facility.
penalty: Fines ranging from 1 to 60 Monthly Tax Units (UTM) depending on the size of the company and gravity of infringement.
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile

law_name: Law on Occupational Accidents and Occupational Diseases
definition: Mandates companies to establish and maintain Safety Committees (Comité Paritario de Higiene y Seguridad) and provide mandatory accident insurance.
compliance_requirements: Establishment of Parity Committees in companies with more than 25 workers
Affiliation with an Occupational Accident Insurance Mutual
Implementation of prevention programs
compliance_risk: Civil and criminal liability for workplace accidents; significant financial penalties.
penalty: Fines and potential suspension of operations; civil damages for negligence.
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile

law_name: General Regulation of Hygiene and Safety in Industrial and Commercial Workplaces
definition: Technical regulation establishing minimum sanitary and environmental conditions in workplaces, including ventilation, noise control, and ergonomics.
compliance_requirements: Maintain sanitary installations (bathrooms, dining areas)
Control physical and chemical exposure agents
Ensure proper lighting and thermal conditions
Ergonomic management of loads
compliance_risk: Sanitary inspections, severe operational fines, and temporary shutdown by health authorities.
penalty: Administrative fines up to 1000 UTM and site closure.

law_name: Law on Remote Work and Telework
definition: Extends the employer's health and safety obligations to the teleworker's home or chosen workspace, requiring risk assessment.
compliance_requirements: Conduct a risk assessment of the remote workspace
Provide health and safety training for remote employees
Ensure equipment complies with ergonomic standards
documentation_required: Evidence of health and safety training delivery
compliance_risk: Employer responsibility for musculoskeletal disorders or home accidents arising from poorly adapted workspaces.
penalty: Labor inspectorate fines; employer may be held liable for workplace injury compensation.
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile

definition: Regulates the entry, residence, and working conditions of foreign nationals in Chile, establishing the authorization requirements to perform remunerated activities.
compliance_requirements: Obtain specific work authorization prior to commencement of duties
Registration of the employment contract with the Department of Foreigners and Migration
Maintaining updated migration records for all foreign employees
Payment of social security contributions for foreign workers
documentation_required: Work permit visa or temporary residence permit
Signed employment contract specifying duration and duties
Digital identification card for foreigners (Cédula de Identidad para Extranjeros)
compliance_risk: High; hiring undocumented individuals or failing to formalize migration status leads to severe legal and financial repercussions.
penalty: Fines ranging from 10 to 100 UTM per infraction and potential temporary closure of the business.
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile

definition: Establishes that foreign workers must have their labor contracts written in accordance with Chilean law, limiting the percentage of foreign personnel to 15% in companies with more than 25 employees.
compliance_requirements: Include specific clauses in employment contracts regarding mandatory social security and tax withholding
Adhere to the 15% quota restriction for foreign workers
Ensure all documents are in Spanish
Registration of contracts with the Labor Inspectorate
documentation_required: Signed declaration of understanding of Chilean labor laws
compliance_risk: Medium; non-compliance with quota limitations or contract formality requirements.
penalty: Administrative fines imposed by the Dirección del Trabajo, typically 1 to 5 UTM per worker involved.
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile

definition: Establishes measures to prevent and correct acts of discrimination, defining discrimination as any distinction, exclusion, or restriction that lacks reasonable justification and affects the exercise of fundamental rights.
compliance_requirements: Ensure policies do not discriminate based on race, gender, religion, sexual orientation, disability, or social status.
Implement internal mechanisms for reporting discrimination.
Ensure workplace culture promotes equality.
documentation_required: Internal Rules of Order, Hygiene and Safety
Records of discrimination complaints and internal investigations
compliance_risk: Civil lawsuits, judicial fines, and reputational damage.
penalty: Fines ranging from 5 to 50 UTM (Unidad Tributaria Mensual).
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile

law_name: Chilean Labor Code - Principle of Equality
definition: Establishes that employment relations are based on equality of opportunities, explicitly prohibiting acts of discrimination including any distinction, exclusion or preference based on race, color, sex, age, marital status, union status, religion, political opinion, nationality, or disability.
compliance_requirements: Apply equal pay for equal work principles.
Ensure selection processes are merit-based and free from discriminatory bias.
Prohibit practices that impede access to employment or promotion based on protected characteristics.
compliance_risk: Labor inspection sanctions, lawsuit by the employee for moral damages or reinstatement, and public censure.
penalty: Administrative fines issued by the Labor Inspectorate, which can range from 1 to 20 UTM depending on company size and violation severity.
source_reference: Dirección del Trabajo, Gobierno de Chile

definition: Introduces the legal definition of sexual harassment in the workplace as any unwanted conduct of a sexual nature that is felt as offensive or humiliating, and mandates prevention policies.
compliance_requirements: Maintain a work environment free from sexual harassment.
Include mandatory anti-harassment protocols in the Internal Rules of Order (RIHS).
Conduct formal investigations upon receiving complaints.
documentation_required: Internal Rules of Order, Hygiene and Safety (Reglamento Interno)
Documentation of training sessions provided to staff
compliance_risk: Fines from the Labor Directorate and potential civil liability for failure to provide a safe work environment.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 1 to 20 UTM plus potential compensation for damages claimed in civil courts.
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile

definition: Regulates maternity protection, including maternity leave (pre- and post-natal) and the right to breastfeeding breaks.
compliance_requirements: Provide 6 weeks of prenatal leave and 12 weeks of postnatal leave
Grant breastfeeding breaks of one hour per day
Ensure job security during pregnancy and postnatal period
compliance_risk: High; potential for legal action regarding discriminatory dismissal or denial of benefits
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 1 to 60 UTM depending on company size and specific violation

definition: Mandatory monthly contributions for pension and disability insurance managed by AFPs.
compliance_requirements: Withhold and pay pension contributions (10% + AFP commission)
Pay disability and survivorship insurance premiums
Remit payments to the relevant pension fund administrator by the 10th of the following month
compliance_risk: Moderate to High; failure to pay results in legal liability and interest/penalties
penalty: Fines, interest for late payment, and potential criminal liability for misappropriation of funds

definition: Mandatory annual profit sharing (gratuity) payable to employees if the company generates taxable profits.
compliance_requirements: Calculate and pay 30% of annual net profits or pay 25% of the annual salary with a cap
Make payments monthly or annually
compliance_risk: Moderate; wage dispute risks
penalty: Administrative fines and mandatory retroactive payment with indexation

definition: Mandatory health insurance contributions to FONASA (public) or ISAPRE (private).
compliance_requirements: Deduct and remit 7% of taxable income to the designated health provider
Provide timely reporting of health contributions
compliance_risk: High; health coverage gaps result in severe employer liability
penalty: Fines, interest, and liability for unpaid medical expenses

definition: Regulates the obligation of employers to issue a payment slip (liquidación de sueldo) detailing all earnings, deductions, and contributions.
compliance_requirements: Issue a monthly payroll slip
Maintain a signed copy or electronic receipt
Include breakdown of base salary, commissions, bonuses, and statutory deductions
documentation_required: Proof of payment (Voucher or bank transfer record)
compliance_risk: Fines by the Labor Inspectorate and potential lawsuits for lack of evidence regarding salary payments.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 1 to 60 UTM per employee affected.

law_name: Ley sobre Impuesto a la Renta
definition: Establishes the obligation of employers to withhold income tax (Impuesto Único de Segunda Categoría) from employees' monthly wages.
compliance_requirements: Calculate and withhold income tax monthly
Declare the withheld amounts through Form 29
Submit the Annual Employer Sworn Declaration (Declaración Jurada 1887)
compliance_risk: Tax audits, penalties, and interest charges for late or incorrect reporting.
penalty: Percentage-based fines on unpaid taxes, plus re-adjustment and interest for late filing.

definition: Mandates the payment of social security contributions for pensions, health insurance (FONASA/ISAPRE), and unemployment insurance.
compliance_requirements: Deduct social security contributions from gross wages
Pay contributions via Previred
Report monthly changes in employee status
documentation_required: Report of labor accidents and professional illnesses
compliance_risk: Civil and criminal liability for misappropriation of funds and potential denial of employee benefits.
penalty: Interest and indexation fees, plus potential criminal prosecution for misappropriation.

definition: Requires employer contributions to the Unemployment Insurance (AFC) for all employees with permanent contracts.
compliance_requirements: Calculate employer and employee contribution percentages
Declare and pay via the AFC platform or Previred
Keep records of contract start and end dates
documentation_required: Contract termination records for severance payments
compliance_risk: Fines from the Labor Inspectorate and the obligation to retroactively pay contributions with interest.
penalty: Administrative fines and surcharge of unpaid amounts.

law_name: Ley sobre Cotizaciones de Seguridad Social
definition: Requirement to register employees with their respective pension fund (AFP) and health insurance providers (FONASA or ISAPRE) to enable social security contributions.
compliance_requirements: Affiliate new employee to AFP
Declare and pay monthly social security contributions via Previred
Maintain current records of health affiliation
compliance_risk: Employer liability for unpaid contributions and interest penalties.
penalty: Fines, interest, and indexing of owed amounts plus potential legal action by AFP/Previsión Social.

law_name: Ley de Protección de la Vida Privada
definition: Governs the processing and handling of personal data belonging to employees collected for registration and payroll purposes.
compliance_requirements: Obtain employee consent for personal data processing
Limit data collection to strictly necessary information for the employment relationship
Ensure data security and confidentiality
compliance_risk: Legal claims for data misuse or unauthorized disclosure.
penalty: Civil damages and administrative sanctions if applicable.
source_reference: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile

definition: Defines the employment contract as an agreement where a person agrees to provide personal service under the subordination of another in exchange for remuneration.
compliance_requirements: Formalize contract in writing
Specify work conditions
Pay legally mandated minimum wages
compliance_risk: Reclassification of independent contractors as employees (labor reality principle).
penalty: Payment of social security back-pay, indemnification, and potential labor fines by the Ministry of Labor.

definition: Establishes the Integral Social Security System covering health, pension, and occupational risks.
compliance_requirements: Affiliate employees to EPS, AFP, and ARL
Execute monthly payments
Calculate base contribution income (IBC)
documentation_required: Planilla Integrada de Liquidación de Aportes (PILA)
compliance_risk: Legal sanctions for non-payment or late payment of social security contributions.
penalty: Financial interest, administrative fines, and joint liability for workplace accidents if unregistered.
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Colombia

definition: Regulates telework where work is performed outside the office using information and communication technologies.
compliance_requirements: Mutual written agreement
Supply of tools and connectivity
Ensuring health and safety conditions in remote site
compliance_risk: Work-related illness claims due to non-ergonomic home office environments.
penalty: Administrative sanctions by Ministry of Labor and liability for work accidents.
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Colombia

definition: General regime for the protection of personal data of employees processed by the employer.
compliance_requirements: Obtain explicit consent
Implement data processing policies
Register databases with SIC if required
compliance_risk: Superintendence of Industry and Commerce (SIC) investigations and reputational damage.
penalty: Fines up to 2,000 monthly minimum wages and database suspension.

definition: Governs severance pay (cesantías) and indemnification for unjustified dismissal.
compliance_requirements: Annual severance provision (Feb 14)
Calculation of indemnities based on contract term
Timely payment upon termination
compliance_risk: Lawsuits for unjustified dismissal or failure to pay statutory benefits.
penalty: One day of salary for every day of delay (moratory penalty) and back-pay mandates.
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Colombia

definition: Defines the employment contract as an agreement where a person agrees to provide a personal service to another person or entity under continuous subordination and in exchange for remuneration.
compliance_requirements: Ensure subordination exists
Provide personal service
Payment of salary
Formalize in writing or verbal agreement
compliance_risk: Risk of reclassification of independent contractor agreements as employment contracts (labor reality principle).
penalty: Fines from 1 to 5,000 minimum monthly wages; payment of retroactive social security contributions and labor benefits.
source_reference: Ministerio del Trabajo - República de Colombia

definition: The employment contract may be verbal or written. In a verbal contract, the parties must agree on the nature of the work, the site, the salary, and the duration.
compliance_requirements: Define essential elements verbally if not in writing
Maintain evidence of the agreement
documentation_required: Written document is optional but highly recommended
compliance_risk: Lack of clarity in work terms leads to adverse judicial interpretation against the employer.
penalty: Payment of backdated social security and labor benefits as interpreted by the judge.
source_reference: Ministerio del Trabajo - República de Colombia

definition: Specifies the minimum content required in a written employment contract, including identification of parties, place of work, nature of work, salary, and duration.
compliance_requirements: Include identity of parties
Specify place and date of contract
Include salary and payment terms
Define type of contract (fixed term/indefinite)
documentation_required: Formal written employment contract signed by both parties
compliance_risk: Administrative sanctions for missing mandatory clauses in written contracts.
penalty: Fines imposed by the Ministry of Labor.
source_reference: Ministerio del Trabajo - República de Colombia

definition: Regulates telework in Colombia, requiring the formalization of the telework modality in the employment contract.
compliance_requirements: Amend employment contract to reflect telework
Specify tools and equipment provided
Define health and safety conditions
documentation_required: Addendum to the employment contract for telework
Safety and health report of the remote work site
compliance_risk: Non-compliance with remote work specific requirements leads to the modality being declared void or subject to fines.
penalty: Administrative fines by the Ministry of Labor; potential lawsuit for occupational risks.
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Colombia

law_name: Ley de Protección de Datos Personales
definition: Requires express authorization for the processing of personal data, which must be obtained from the employee at the time of hiring.
compliance_requirements: Obtain written consent for data processing
Inform employees of their rights under data protection laws
Implement data privacy policies
compliance_risk: Heavy administrative fines for processing employee data without authorization.
penalty: Fines up to 2,000 monthly minimum wages and potential suspension of data processing activities.
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Colombia

law_name: Codigo Sustantivo del Trabajo (Substantive Labor Code)
definition: Defines the duration of the ordinary working day. Effective July 2023, the maximum weekly working hours are being gradually reduced from 48 to 42 hours.
compliance_requirements: Ensure the workday does not exceed current legal limits
Implement shift schedules that comply with the gradual reduction schedule
Register overtime hours in payroll systems
documentation_required: Internal work regulation document (Reglamento Interno de Trabajo)
compliance_risk: Lawsuits, administrative sanctions, and mandatory payment of unpaid overtime.
penalty: Fines ranging from 1 to 5,000 monthly minimum wages imposed by the Ministry of Labor.
source_reference: Ministerio del Trabajo - Codigo Sustantivo del Trabajo

law_name: Ley de reduccion gradual de la jornada laboral
definition: Establishes the gradual reduction of the maximum weekly working hours to 42 hours by the year 2026 without reducing employee salary.
compliance_requirements: Update labor contracts to reflect new weekly hour limits
Adjust shift schedules annually according to the law's calendar
compliance_risk: Non-compliance with the gradual reduction schedule leading to administrative investigations.
penalty: Monetary fines and potential order to pay overtime wages for hours worked beyond the new legal maximum.
source_reference: Congreso de la Republica de Colombia

law_name: Codigo Sustantivo del Trabajo - Overtime Pay
definition: Regulates supplemental work (overtime), night work, and work on Sundays/holidays, setting specific surcharges (e.g., 25% for daytime overtime, 75% for night work).
compliance_requirements: Calculate and pay overtime with required surcharges
Maintain evidence of prior authorization for overtime
compliance_risk: High risk of labor litigation (claims for unpaid surcharges) and inspections by the Ministry of Labor.
penalty: Payment of backdated salary surcharges plus interest and administrative fines.

definition: Establishes the General System of Social Security in Health and Pensions, mandating employer and employee contributions.
compliance_requirements: Register employees in the General System of Social Security.
Calculate and pay monthly contributions to health, pension, and occupational risks (ARL).
Withhold employee portion of contributions.
documentation_required: Contribution forms (PILA - Planilla Integrada de Liquidación de Aportes)
Affiliation forms to EPS and AFP
compliance_risk: Financial liability, accumulation of debts, and administrative sanctions from UGPP.
penalty: Fines, interest on arrears, and potential legal lawsuits from workers.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Protección Social

definition: Defines mandatory social benefits including Prima de Servicios, Cesantías, and Intereses sobre Cesantías.
compliance_requirements: Payment of Prima de Servicios (biannual).
Accrual and payment of Cesantías into funds (annual).
Payment of interest on Cesantías.
documentation_required: Proof of payment of social benefits
Records of deposits into Cesantías funds
compliance_risk: Labor claims for unpaid benefits and sanctions from the Ministry of Labor.
penalty: Legal indemnification to the employee, potential payment of daily salary for every day of delay (indemnización moratoria).

definition: Regulations governing the exoneration of payroll taxes (aportes parafiscales) for specific employees under certain salary thresholds.
compliance_requirements: Verify salary thresholds for exoneration of SENA, ICBF, and health contributions.
Correct calculation of payroll tax exoneration.
compliance_risk: Tax audits and penalties for improper application of exonerations.
penalty: Tax sanctions, interest, and surcharges from DIAN.

definition: Sets the mandatory Occupational Health and Safety Management System (SG-SST) requirements linked to payroll contributions to the ARL (Administradora de Riesgos Laborales).
compliance_requirements: Pay monthly ARL contributions based on risk classification.
Implement SG-SST protocols.
compliance_risk: Suspension of activities and heavy fines in case of workplace accidents without coverage.
penalty: Heavy administrative fines, legal liability for work-related accidents.

definition: Sets the withholding tax table for income from labor, determining the progressive rates applicable to employees' salaries.
compliance_requirements: Calculate monthly income tax withholding for employees
Apply the correct tax bracket based on monthly earnings in UVT
Perform annual adjustments if required
documentation_required: Formulario 350 (Declaración de Retención en la Fuente)
compliance_risk: Financial penalties, interest charges, and lack of tax deduction for the employer.
penalty: Late payment interest, penalties for inaccurate reporting (Sanción por inexactitud) equivalent to 100% of the difference between the tax paid and the tax due.
source_reference: DIAN (Dirección de Impuestos y Aduanas Nacionales)

definition: Requirement for employers to declare and pay amounts withheld from employees to the national tax authority.
compliance_requirements: Monthly submission of withholding tax declarations
Timely payment of withheld amounts to the Treasury
documentation_required: Declaración de Retención en la Fuente
compliance_risk: Seizure of bank accounts, tax audit, and significant monetary fines.
penalty: Penalty for late filing (Sanción por extemporaneidad) equivalent to 5% of the tax due per month of delay.
source_reference: DIAN (Dirección de Impuestos y Aduanas Nacionales)

definition: Regulates the exemption of employer payroll taxes for entities that meet specific requirements, impacting the total cost of labor.
compliance_requirements: Monitor eligibility for payroll tax exemptions
Ensure accurate calculation of parafiscal contributions (ICBF, SENA, Family Compensation Fund)
documentation_required: Payroll records (Planilla Integrada de Liquidación de Aportes - PILA)
compliance_risk: Assessment of outstanding contributions plus high interest rates.
penalty: Fines by the UGPP (Unidad de Gestión Pensional y Parafiscales) ranging from 10% to 100% of the non-paid amount.
source_reference: Ministerio de Hacienda y Crédito Público

definition: Obligation of the employer to grant leaves for specific purposes including bereavement, suffrage, and union activities.
compliance_requirements: Grant paid bereavement leave of 5 business days for first-degree relatives
Grant paid leave for public service or jury duty
Grant paid leave for exercise of suffrage
compliance_risk: Litigation for non-payment of wages or obstruction of civic rights
penalty: Fines imposed by the Ministry of Labor ranging from 1 to 5,000 monthly minimum wages

definition: Extended paternity leave granting the father 2 weeks of paid leave.
compliance_requirements: Provide 2 weeks of paid leave to the father
Ensure the employee is affiliated with the health system
Include leave in payroll without deduction
compliance_risk: Discriminatory practices and violation of familial rights
penalty: Fines determined by Ministry of Labor inspectors

definition: Extension of maternity leave to 18 weeks paid fully by the EPS (Health Promotor Entity).
compliance_requirements: Grant 18 weeks of maternity leave
Ensure no dismissal during the protection period (fuero de maternidad)
Facilitate return to work
compliance_risk: Reinstatement orders and back-pay mandates by labor courts
penalty: Significant administrative fines and civil liability for discrimination

definition: Mandatory 15 working days of paid vacation per year of service.
compliance_requirements: Grant 15 business days of vacation annually
Pay during the vacation period
Maintain record of vacation days taken
compliance_risk: Accumulation of liabilities and labor claims for unpaid benefits
penalty: Fines for non-compliance with statutory rest periods

definition: Governs the formation, existence, and proof of the labor contract, distinguishing between oral and written contracts, and specifying necessary elements (service, subordination, and remuneration).
compliance_requirements: Clearly define job functions and subordination
Specify the salary amount
Define the duration of the contract
Ensure written contracts for fixed-term agreements
compliance_risk: Risk of the contract being presumed as an indefinite-term contract if not properly documented.
penalty: Fines up to 5,000 monthly minimum wages and potential recognition of labor rights through judicial processes.
source_reference: Ministerio del Trabajo - República de Colombia

law_name: Ley de Protección de Datos Personales
definition: Regulates the collection and processing of personal data, including the mandatory requirement to obtain informed authorization from employees for data handling during hiring.
compliance_requirements: Obtain written informed consent from the candidate/employee
Inform the purpose of data collection
Implement data security protocols
documentation_required: Signed Privacy Authorization Form (Autorización de tratamiento de datos)
compliance_risk: Sanctions by the Superintendency of Industry and Commerce for improper data handling.
penalty: Fines of up to 2,000 monthly minimum wages, temporary suspension of operations, or permanent closure of database.

definition: Regulates the hiring conditions for remote work or work from home, ensuring that employment rights remain identical to on-site work.
compliance_requirements: Update the internal labor regulations
Clearly state the nature of work in the contract
Ensure right to disconnect
Guarantee social security coverage
documentation_required: Remote work addendum to the contract
compliance_risk: Reclassification of the work modality leading to claims for overtime or additional equipment costs.
penalty: Administrative sanctions by the Ministry of Labor and potential payment of retroactive benefits.
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Colombia

definition: Mandates the mandatory affiliation and contribution of all employees to the health, pension, and professional risk systems.
compliance_requirements: Register employee with Health, Pension, and ARL (Professional Risks) providers
Make timely monthly contributions
Ensure correct calculation base (IBC)
documentation_required: Affiliation forms for EPS, AFP, and ARL
compliance_risk: High risk of litigation for work-related accidents without coverage.
penalty: Monetary fines by the UGPP and payment of all accrued benefits and contributions with interest.
source_reference: Ministerio de Salud y Protección Social

definition: Defines the essential elements of an employment relationship: personal service, continuous subordination or dependency, and remuneration.
compliance_requirements: Verify that civil or commercial contracts do not contain elements of subordination.
Ensure the contractor acts autonomously without following the employer's specific directions, schedules, or disciplinary oversight.
documentation_required: Signed Civil or Commercial Service Agreement
Proof of payment of social security contributions by the contractor as an independent worker
Evidence of independent operations such as billing (CUFE/CUDE) and tax filings
compliance_risk: Risk of 'Labor Primacy' (Primacía de la Realidad) where a judge declares an employment relationship despite the existence of a commercial contract.
penalty: Payment of all retroactive labor benefits (social benefits, vacations, bonuses, severance, and interest) plus social security contributions and potential indemnities.
source_reference: Secretaría Jurídica Distrital - Alcaldía Mayor de Bogotá

definition: Establishes the principle of primacy of reality over formal agreements in labor relationships.
compliance_requirements: Ensure organizational structures reflect the actual nature of work provided.
Avoid using service contracts as a disguise for permanent and subordinate labor.
documentation_required: Internal audit reports on contractor tasks
Contracts defining specific deliverables rather than job functions
compliance_risk: Constitutional protection of workers regardless of the contract type signed.
penalty: Nullification of the civil contract and recognition of full labor rights, including fines from the Ministry of Labor.

law_name: Ley de Formalización y Generación de Empleo
definition: Prohibits the use of cooperatives or other entities to disguise subordinate employment relationships (intermediation fraud).
compliance_requirements: Prohibit third-party outsourcing that hides true labor subordination.
Ensure service providers maintain administrative and financial independence.
compliance_risk: Heavy fines from the Ministry of Labor (up to 5,000 monthly minimum wages) for illegal labor intermediation.
penalty: Administrative sanctions and declaration of employment relationship with the beneficiary of the service.
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Colombia

law_name: Código Sustantivo del Trabajo (Substantive Labor Code)
definition: Defines the just causes for unilateral termination of the employment contract by the employer, including performance issues, moral misconduct, or serious lack of discipline.
compliance_requirements: Provide a written communication stating the specific cause for termination
Observe due process by allowing the employee to present their defense
Ensure the causal link between the act and the termination is documented
documentation_required: Evidence of the disciplinary infraction (Disciplinary records, witness statements, logs)
compliance_risk: Lawsuits for wrongful termination leading to court-ordered reinstatement or payment of substantial damages.
penalty: Payment of back wages, indemnification for termination without cause, and potential court-ordered reinstatement.
source_reference: Secretaría Jurídica Distrital - Alcaldía Mayor de Bogotá

law_name: Código Sustantivo del Trabajo (Substantive Labor Code)
definition: Regulates the indemnity payment required when an employer terminates a contract without a just cause defined by law.
compliance_requirements: Calculate indemnity based on the type of contract (fixed term vs. indefinite term)
Process full settlement (liquidación) of social benefits within the statutory period
documentation_required: Proof of payment of settlement (liquidación de prestaciones sociales)
compliance_risk: Financial liability for statutory indemnities and legal interest.
penalty: Indemnity payments calculated on a sliding scale based on the employee's salary and years of service.
source_reference: Secretaría Jurídica Distrital - Alcaldía Mayor de Bogotá

law_name: Ley de Estabilidad Laboral Reforzada (Constitutional Labor Rights)
law_code: Constitución Política de Colombia / Jurisprudencia Corte Constitucional
definition: Protects individuals with health conditions, maternity, or union membership from arbitrary dismissal, requiring administrative authorization for termination.
compliance_requirements: Request prior authorization from the Ministry of Labor before terminating a protected employee
Demonstrate that the termination is not related to the health or protected status of the employee
documentation_required: Ministry of Labor authorization (Resolución de permiso)
Evidence of lack of causal nexus
compliance_risk: Orders for mandatory reinstatement, payment of all back wages from the date of dismissal, and potential fines.
penalty: Nullification of the dismissal, reinstatement, and payment of back salary and 180 days of indemnity.

law_name: Ley de Indemnización por Despido de Trabajadores con Discapacidad
definition: Establishes that no person with a disability may be dismissed due to their condition unless authorized by the Ministry of Labor.
compliance_requirements: Maintain employment unless official authorization is granted
Document reasonable accommodations provided before termination
documentation_required: Approval from the Ministry of Labor
compliance_risk: Severe legal sanctions for discrimination and breach of protected status.
penalty: Payment of 180 days of salary in addition to other statutory severance indemnities.
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Colombia

definition: Obligation to maintain a written employment contract containing specific conditions such as place, nature of work, duration, and salary.
compliance_requirements: Must be in writing
Include salary and payment terms
Specify work schedule and duration
documentation_required: Signed physical or electronic employment contract
compliance_risk: Contract is presumed to exist in favor of the employee if not documented; high risk of labor litigation.
penalty: Fines imposed by the Ministry of Labor ranging from 1 to 5,000 minimum legal monthly wages.
source_reference: Secretaría Jurídica Distrital - Alcaldía Mayor de Bogotá

law_name: Ley de Protección de Datos Personales
definition: The requirement to obtain prior, express, and informed authorization from employees for the processing of their personal data.
compliance_requirements: Obtain written authorization for data processing
Implement internal privacy policy
Register databases with the National Registry of Databases (RNBD)
documentation_required: Signed employee data processing authorization form
compliance_risk: Sanctions from the Superintendence of Industry and Commerce for unauthorized use or data leaks.
penalty: Fines up to 2,000 minimum legal monthly wages and potential database closure.

definition: A mandatory set of rules that employers with more than 5 workers in commercial activities or 10 in industrial must implement to regulate employee conduct.
compliance_requirements: Draft and publish internal work rules
Communicate rules to all employees
File with the Ministry of Labor
compliance_risk: Inability to apply disciplinary sanctions properly due to lack of regulated procedures.
penalty: Fines by the Ministry of Labor.

definition: Legal framework for remote work requiring specific adjustments to employment contracts and safety measures.
compliance_requirements: Modify employment contract to include remote work clause
Ensure ARL (Workplace Risk) coverage for remote location
Provide remote work tools and equipment
compliance_risk: Legal challenges regarding workplace accidents and overtime liability.
penalty: Administrative sanctions by Ministry of Labor.
source_reference: Ministerio de Tecnologías de la Información y las Comunicaciones

definition: Specifies the just causes for termination of employment by the employer, including disciplinary reasons or poor performance.
compliance_requirements: Provide notice of specific charges to the employee
Conduct disciplinary hearings allowing the right to defense
Document termination events in writing
documentation_required: Written termination letter citing specific causes
compliance_risk: Unjustified dismissal claims leading to mandatory reinstatement or heavy indemnity payments.
penalty: Payment of full back wages and damages determined by labor courts.

definition: Mandates employer and employee contributions to pension, health, and professional risk insurance systems.
compliance_requirements: Register employees with EPS, AFP, and ARL
Monthly payment of contributions via PILA platform
Withhold employee contributions from payroll
compliance_risk: Administrative sanctions by UGPP and legal liabilities for coverage gaps in case of accidents.
penalty: Fines reaching up to 100% of the non-paid contributions plus interest.
source_reference: Ministerio de Salud y Protección Social

definition: Regulates the processing of personal data of employees and candidates requiring explicit consent and data handling protocols.
compliance_requirements: Obtain written authorization for data processing
Implement data treatment policy
Register databases with the National Data Protection Registry
compliance_risk: Regulatory investigations by the Superintendency of Industry and Commerce.
penalty: Fines of up to 2,000 legal monthly minimum wages or database suspension.

definition: Regulates the conditions of remote work, ensuring the right to disconnect and the provision of tools.
compliance_requirements: Formalize remote work in employment contracts
Guarantee the right to disconnect
Provide necessary digital tools and equipment
compliance_risk: Claims regarding unpaid overtime due to lack of digital disconnection.
penalty: Labor lawsuits for extra hours and violations of dignity at work.

law_name: Safety and Health at Work Management System
definition: Standardizes the SG-SST protocols for accident prevention and employee welfare.
compliance_requirements: Conduct annual hazard and risk evaluations
Appoint COPASST committee
Provide periodic safety training
compliance_risk: High administrative fines and criminal liability in case of fatal workplace accidents.
penalty: Fines of up to 500 minimum wages and potential site closure.

definition: Guarantees the right of workers and employers to form unions or associations and the right to collective bargaining as a fundamental right.
compliance_requirements: Respect the right of association
Refrain from interfering in union activities
Recognition of union legal personality
documentation_required: Certificate of existence and legal representation of the union
compliance_risk: Constitutional protection claims (Tutela) and international sanctions for violation of fundamental rights.
penalty: Constitutional sanctions and potential international liability for the State.

definition: Establishes the procedure for the presentation of the list of demands (pliego de peticiones) to initiate collective bargaining.
compliance_requirements: Receive the list of demands
Initiate direct settlement negotiations within 5 business days
Negotiate in good faith
documentation_required: Minutes of the direct settlement stage meetings
compliance_risk: Illegal strike declaration, administrative sanctions by the Ministry of Labor.
penalty: Fines ranging from 1 to 5,000 minimum legal monthly wages.

law_code: Decreto Unico Reglamentario del Sector Trabajo
definition: Regulates the procedures for collective bargaining in the public and private sectors, emphasizing the duty of negotiation.
compliance_requirements: Compliance with mandatory timelines for negotiation
Recording negotiation phases
Formalizing collective agreements
documentation_required: Final act of the negotiation process
compliance_risk: Invalidation of the agreement, administrative investigation for bad faith negotiation.
penalty: Administrative fines issued by the Ministry of Labor.

definition: Sets the legal framework for the right of association, the formation of unions, collective bargaining, and the right to strike.
compliance_requirements: Respect the right of employees to organize and join trade unions.
Refrain from acts of anti-union discrimination.
Negotiate collective bargaining agreements in good faith.
Grant trade union leave for union officials.
documentation_required: Union registration certificates issued by the Ministry of Labor
compliance_risk: Sanctions for union busting, interference in union activities, or refusal to bargain.
penalty: Fines ranging from 1 to 5,000 monthly legal minimum wages (SMLMV) depending on the severity and company size.

definition: Guarantees workers and employers the right to form trade unions or associations freely.
compliance_requirements: Guarantee free exercise of association without state interference
Recognition of the union's legal status upon registration with the Ministry of Labor
documentation_required: Registration documents with the Ministry of Labor
compliance_risk: Constitutional lawsuits (Tutela) filed by employees for violation of fundamental rights.
penalty: Court-ordered remedies including reinstatement of unionized employees and damages.

definition: Prohibits specific actions by employers that impede the free exercise of union activities, including direct negotiation when a union exists.
compliance_requirements: Refrain from direct negotiation of individual employment contracts that contradict existing collective bargaining agreements.
Prevent any discriminatory treatment due to union affiliation.
documentation_required: Records showing equal treatment of unionized and non-unionized staff
compliance_risk: Severe administrative fines and potential nullification of discriminatory labor practices.
penalty: Fines from 50 to 100 SMLMV per incident.

definition: Regulates remote work in the private and public sector when circumstances, force majeure or other reasons require the use of technologies for the performance of work duties.
compliance_requirements: Sign an agreement (either in contract or addendum) specifying the mode, working hours, and tools provided
Maintain the employee's same employment rights
Provide equipment and necessary tools unless otherwise agreed
Ensure the right to digital disconnection
documentation_required: Safety and health at work remote inspection document
compliance_risk: Risk of being classified as regular employment without benefits, labor lawsuits regarding disconnection, and workplace accident liability.
penalty: Fines from the Ministry of Labor ranging from 1 to 5,000 monthly minimum wages (SMMLV) depending on the violation severity.

definition: Grants employees the right to not be contacted by the employer outside of working hours, excluding high-responsibility roles or force majeure.
compliance_requirements: Establish a clear policy on digital disconnection
Include disconnection clauses in internal labor regulations
Ensure no retaliatory actions for exercising this right
documentation_required: Internal Labor Regulation (Reglamento Interno de Trabajo)
compliance_risk: Labor litigation for overtime pay claims and harassment complaints.
penalty: Administrative sanctions by the Ministry of Labor and potential compensation for moral damages.

definition: Regulates a form of labor organization consisting of performing paid activities using information and communication technologies (ICT) outside the employer's premises.
compliance_requirements: Must be voluntary and reversible
Specific contract addendum required
Inclusion of teleworking in the mandatory social security and occupational risk insurance (ARL)
Employer must guarantee support and maintenance for equipment
documentation_required: ARL classification of the home office space
Health and safety protocols for home office
compliance_risk: Lack of proper ARL coverage for home accidents can result in total liability for the employer.
penalty: Administrative fines and judicial mandates to provide compensation for damages.

definition: Governs the processing of personal data for all employees, especially critical when handling remote work data flows.
compliance_requirements: Obtain authorization for data collection
Implement a privacy policy for employees
Register databases with the National Registry of Databases (RNBD)
documentation_required: Data processing agreements for third-party platforms used remotely
compliance_risk: High administrative fines from the Superintendencia de Industria y Comercio (SIC) and reputation damage.
penalty: Fines up to 2,000 SMMLV and potential temporary suspension of database activities.

definition: It establishes the general regime for the protection of personal data in Colombia, ensuring the right of individuals to know, update, and rectify the information collected about them in databases or files.
compliance_requirements: Obtain prior, express, and informed authorization from employees for data processing
Implement a Data Processing Policy (Manual de Tratamiento de Datos)
Appoint a data protection officer or team
Register databases with the National Database Registry (RNBD) if applicable
Guarantee the rights of data subjects (access, update, rectification, deletion)
Ensure data security and confidentiality
documentation_required: Data protection agreements in employment contracts
compliance_risk: Reputational damage, loss of trust, and intervention by the Superintendency of Industry and Commerce (SIC) leading to operational shutdowns of data processing systems.
penalty: Fines of up to 2,000 legal monthly minimum wages, suspension of activities, or closure of databases.
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Colombia - Ley 1581 de 2012

definition: Provides specific guidelines on the implementation of the Habeas Data law, detailing how to obtain consent and the scope of data processing responsibilities.
compliance_requirements: Publication of privacy notices
Standardization of authorization requests for HR processes
Compliance with data security protocols for electronic employee files
compliance_risk: Regulatory investigations due to lack of proof of authorization for processing sensitive or personal employee data.
penalty: Administrative sanctions by the SIC, including financial penalties and corrective orders.
source_reference: Presidencia de la República de Colombia - Decreto 1377 de 2013

definition: Establishes the fundamental right to personal and family privacy, and the right to know, update, and rectify information collected about oneself.
compliance_requirements: Respect for the dignity and privacy of employees
Restriction on unauthorized disclosure of employee personal information
documentation_required: Internal labor regulations respecting constitutional rights
compliance_risk: Lawsuits (Tutela) filed by employees for violation of fundamental rights.
penalty: Judicial orders to cease and desist, and potential compensation for damages.

law_name: Sistema de Gestión de la Seguridad y Salud en el Trabajo (SG-SST)
definition: The legal framework mandating that all employers, regardless of size or sector, implement a management system to anticipate, recognize, evaluate, and control risks that may affect the safety and health of workers.
compliance_requirements: Implementation of a SG-SST policy
Hazard identification and risk assessment (IPEV)
Annual work plan
Assigning roles and responsibilities
Provision of resources
Monitoring and measurement of results
compliance_risk: High operational and legal risk, including government inspections and potential work stoppage orders.
penalty: Fines ranging from 1 to 1000 minimum monthly legal wages depending on company size and violation gravity.
keywords: Seguridad y Salud en el Trabajo
source_reference: Ministerio del Trabajo - Decreto Único Reglamentario del Sector Trabajo

law_name: Ley de Seguridad y Salud en el Trabajo
definition: Law that modifies the Occupational Risk System and incorporates the concept of Safety and Health at Work (SST) in replacement of the traditional Occupational Health approach.
compliance_requirements: Affiliation of workers to the ARL (Administrator of Occupational Risks)
Reporting of work-related accidents and diseases
Formation of the COPASST (Occupational Health and Safety Committee)
compliance_risk: Legal liability in case of workplace accidents and loss of protection for the employer against civil lawsuits.
penalty: Administrative sanctions by the Ministry of Labor and civil liability for damages.
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Colombia

definition: Sets the minimum standards for the implementation of the SG-SST for employers and contractors based on company size and risk level.
compliance_requirements: Self-assessment of minimum standards
Improvement plan based on self-assessment
Specific compliance thresholds (7, 21, or 60 standards depending on criteria)
compliance_risk: Fines for failure to comply with minimum mandatory protocols.
penalty: Closure of the establishment and heavy financial penalties as per Decree 1072.

law_code: Regulatory Decree of the Migration Sector
definition: Regulates the requirements for the entry, stay, and work authorization of foreign nationals in Colombia, establishing the categories of visas (V, M, R) and the necessity for a Work Visa when engaged in professional or labor activities.
compliance_requirements: Obtain the appropriate visa category before the foreigner commences work
Register the foreign worker with the Special Administrative Unit of Migration Colombia (SIRE)
Ensure the foreign worker obtains a Foreigner ID (Cédula de Extranjería) if stay exceeds 90 days
Verify visa validity matches the specific employer contract
documentation_required: Employment contract signed by both parties
Certificate of existence and legal representation of the company
Proof of registration in the SIRE system
compliance_risk: Risk of deportation, administrative fines for the company, and denial of future visa sponsorships.
penalty: Fines ranging from 5 to 40 monthly legal minimum wages for employers hiring undocumented workers; potential cancellation of business visa sponsorship privileges.

definition: Defines the specific requirements for 'M' (Migrant) and 'V' (Visitor) visas, specifically those that allow for work activities, outlining the criteria for labor-based visa applications.
compliance_requirements: Submit evidence of a formal labor contract or professional services agreement
Provide professional certification or resume verifying the foreign national's qualifications
Maintain current company payroll and social security registration for the foreign worker
documentation_required: Letter of sponsorship from the Colombian entity
compliance_risk: Visa rejection or cancellation due to lack of proof of specialized skills or non-compliance with economic activities stipulated in the visa.
penalty: Administrative sanctions by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and potential blacklisting of the company's visa sponsorship account.
source_reference: Ministerio de Relaciones Exteriores de Colombia

law_code: Law for the Return of Colombians
definition: Governs aspects related to the labor integration of returning migrants and the specific documentation required for non-nationals who may be beneficiaries of international cooperation agreements regarding labor mobility.
compliance_requirements: Maintain official registry of foreign workforce demographics
Comply with reporting obligations to the Ministry of Labor regarding foreign personnel
compliance_risk: Regulatory scrutiny from the Ministry of Labor during site inspections if the ratio of foreign to national employees (if applicable under specific labor quotas) is exceeded.
penalty: Heavy administrative fines and potential labor lawsuits for unequal treatment or lack of proper social security contributions.
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Colombia

definition: Establishes that all persons are born free and equal before the law, receiving the same protection and treatment from authorities, and enjoying the same rights, freedoms, and opportunities without discrimination on grounds of sex, race, national or family origin, language, religion, political or philosophical opinion.
compliance_requirements: Implement non-discriminatory hiring practices
Ensure equal pay for equal work
Prevent harassment based on protected characteristics
compliance_risk: High; violation of fundamental rights can lead to tutela actions (constitutional protection appeals).
penalty: Orders for reinstatement, payment of back wages, and potential administrative sanctions.

definition: Mandates that all workers have the same rights, and all employers have the same obligations, prohibiting any discrimination based on gender, race, political opinion, or religious belief in the employment relationship.
compliance_requirements: Eliminate gender-based salary gaps
Prohibit discrimination in the promotion or termination of employees
compliance_risk: Moderate to High; risk of labor lawsuits and fines by the Ministry of Labor.
penalty: Fines ranging from 1 to 5,000 monthly minimum wages, depending on the severity and recurrence.

definition: Criminalizes acts of discrimination that arbitrarily impede, obstruct, or restrict the full exercise of rights of persons based on race, ethnicity, religion, nationality, ideology, sex, or sexual orientation.
compliance_requirements: Ensure workplace policies explicitly prohibit discriminatory conduct
Train staff to recognize and prevent discriminatory behavior
compliance_risk: Very High; leads to criminal prosecution of individuals and potential corporate liability.
penalty: Imprisonment from 12 to 36 months and fines ranging from 10 to 15 monthly minimum wages.

definition: Defines labor harassment as any persistent and demonstrable behavior aimed at instilling fear, intimidation, or inducing resignation, which often manifests as discriminatory treatment.
compliance_requirements: Establishment of a Coexistence Committee (Comité de Convivencia Laboral)
Implementation of a prevention protocol for labor harassment
compliance_risk: Moderate; includes liability for psychological damages and administrative fines.
penalty: Fines from 2 to 10 monthly minimum wages; obligation for employers to pay treatment costs for the affected employee.

definition: Employers must pay a severance allowance (Cesantías) to employees equal to one month of salary for each year of service.
compliance_requirements: Payment of one month's salary per year of service
Deposit of funds into an approved Severance Fund (Fondo de Cesantías) by February 15th of each year
documentation_required: Proof of deposit in the selected Severance Fund
compliance_risk: Lawsuits for unpaid benefits and fines by the Ministry of Labor.
penalty: Payment of one day of salary for each day of delay after the legal deadline.
source_reference: Ministerio del Trabajo - Código Sustantivo del Trabajo

definition: The 'Prima de Servicios' is a mandatory bonus equivalent to 15 days of salary, payable in two installments (June and December).
compliance_requirements: Payment of 15 days of salary per semester (half of the yearly bonus each semester)
Payments must be made in June and December
compliance_risk: Fines from the Ministry of Labor and claims by employees for unpaid service bonuses.
penalty: Indemnification penalties based on judicial rulings plus interest.
source_reference: Ministerio del Trabajo - Código Sustantivo del Trabajo

definition: Employer obligation to contribute to the General System of Social Security, covering health, pension, and professional risks.
compliance_requirements: Employer payment of 8.5% for health, company-specific percentage for ARP (Professional Risk Insurance), and 12% for pension (with employee paying 4% each)
Monthly payment through the PILA system
documentation_required: Employee registration with ARL and EPS
compliance_risk: Severe fines from the UGPP (Unidad de Gestión Pensional y Parafiscales) and inability to provide benefits.
penalty: Late payment interests and massive fines up to 200% of the unpaid amount.
source_reference: Congreso de la República - Ley 100 de 1993

definition: Mandatory 15 working days of paid vacation per year of service.
compliance_requirements: Granting 15 business days of vacation annually
Payment of salary during vacation period
documentation_required: Vacation schedule or signed request forms
Payroll records showing payment during leave
compliance_risk: Legal sanctions and accumulation of liabilities.
penalty: Requirement to pay double in case of non-compliance or failure to grant leave.

definition: Sets out the special obligations of the employer, specifically concerning the payment of wages and the keeping of payroll records.
compliance_requirements: Maintain accurate payroll registries
Issuance of payment receipts
Timely payment of wages and benefits
compliance_risk: Fines, legal disputes regarding non-payment, and claims for back-pay plus indexation.
penalty: Administrative fines imposed by the Ministry of Labor ranging from 1 to 5,000 monthly minimum wages.
source_reference: Ministerio del Trabajo - Registro Único Empresarial

definition: Mandates the submission of magnetic information (Información Exógena) including payments made to employees and withholdings applied.
compliance_requirements: Submission of annual report of payments to employees
Detail of withholdings on labor income
Electronic transmission via DIAN portal
compliance_risk: High risk of fiscal penalties and interest charges from the tax authority.
penalty: Penalty for late filing or inaccurate information, calculated as a percentage of the value of the information not reported.
source_reference: DIAN - Dirección de Impuestos y Aduanas Nacionales

definition: Regulates the employer's obligation to contribute and report social security payments for health, pension, and occupational risks.
compliance_requirements: Monthly reporting through the PILA system (Planilla Integrada de Liquidación de Aportes)
Accurate calculation of base contributions
Timely payment to the integrated system
documentation_required: Affiliation forms for EPS, AFP, and ARL
compliance_risk: Liability for health costs and pension gaps in case of non-contribution, plus interest charges.
penalty: Administrative sanctions by the UGPP (Unidad de Gestión Pensional y Parafiscales) up to 200% of the value not paid or not reported.
source_reference: Ministerio de Salud y Protección Social

definition: Mandatory requirement for employers to transmit payroll data to the DIAN in XML format for tax deduction purposes.
compliance_requirements: Monthly electronic transmission of payroll supporting documents
Validation by DIAN
Maintaining logs of transmitted files
compliance_risk: Non-deductibility of payroll expenses for corporate income tax purposes.
penalty: Rejection of expenses in income tax returns leading to higher tax liabilities.

definition: The mandatory requirement for employers to register employees with the Integrated Social Security Contribution Plan (PILA) for health, pension, and professional risk insurance (ARL).
compliance_requirements: Register employee with an ARL prior to the start of work
Affiliate employee to a Health Promotion Entity (EPS)
Affiliate employee to a Pension Fund (AFP)
documentation_required: Affiliation forms for EPS, AFP, and ARL
compliance_risk: Exposure to liability for medical costs in case of work accidents and severe fines by the UGPP.
penalty: Financial sanctions, retroactive payments with interest, and closure orders for non-compliance.
source_reference: Ministerio de Salud y Protección Social

law_name: Ley de Protección de Datos Personales
definition: Requirements regarding the collection, storage, and processing of personal employee data during the onboarding process.
compliance_requirements: Obtain prior and informed consent from the employee for data processing
Implement a privacy notice at the point of data collection
Register databases with the National Registry of Databases (RNBD) if applicable
compliance_risk: Sanctions from the Superintendence of Industry and Commerce for improper handling of sensitive employee data.
penalty: Fines up to 2,000 legal minimum monthly wages and potential temporary suspension of operations.

law_name: Registro de Empleados en nómina electrónica
definition: Requirement to report all payroll payments to the DIAN in electronic format for tax deductibility purposes.
compliance_requirements: Generate electronic payroll documents for every employee
Transmit information to DIAN within the first 10 days of the following month
compliance_risk: Non-deductibility of payroll expenses for corporate income tax purposes.
penalty: Disallowance of costs in tax returns and penalties for late or incorrect filing.

definition: The Labor Code defines an employment contract as any agreement where a person provides services to another under subordination and in exchange for payment.
compliance_requirements: Written contract for indefinite term contracts
Registration of the contract if applicable
Compliance with minimum wage laws
Subordination must exist
compliance_risk: Reclassification of independent contractors as employees leading to back taxes and social security payments.
penalty: Payment of backdated social security contributions plus fines.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Seguridad Social (MTSS)

law_name: Ley Constitutiva de la Caja Costarricense de Seguro Social
definition: Compulsory insurance for all workers under the CCSS (Caja) covering health and pension benefits.
compliance_requirements: Register employee with CCSS within 8 days of hiring
Report accurate salary amounts
Pay employer and employee contributions monthly
compliance_risk: Fines, legal action for evasion, and inability to register future employees.
penalty: Surplus charges (recargos), fines, and criminal charges for significant evasion.

law_name: Ley de Protección de la Persona frente al Tratamiento de sus Datos Personales
definition: Protects the fundamental rights of individuals regarding the processing of their personal data, including employee records.
compliance_requirements: Obtain consent for data processing
Implement security measures for HR files
Ensure data is used only for labor purposes
compliance_risk: Administrative fines by PRODHAB and lawsuits for damages.
penalty: Monetary fines ranging from light to very serious infractions.
source_reference: Agencia de Protección de los Habitantes (PRODHAB)

definition: Regulates the modality of performing work outside the employer's premises using ICT.
compliance_requirements: Formal written addendum to the contract
Explicit definition of tools, equipment, and costs
Right to disconnect
documentation_required: Health and safety risk assessment of home office
compliance_risk: Labor lawsuits for failure to provide equipment or violation of working hours.
penalty: Labor court claims for indemnity and back pay.

definition: Obligatory insurance coverage for workplace accidents and occupational diseases provided by the National Insurance Institute (INS).
compliance_requirements: Purchase INS insurance policy
Declare accurate payroll for policy adjustment
Provide PPE to employees
compliance_risk: Direct liability for medical costs and indemnities in the event of an accident if not insured.
penalty: Payment of all medical costs plus penalties to the State.

law_name: Código de Trabajo de la República de Costa Rica
definition: Defines the employment contract as the agreement whereby a person commits to provide services or perform a work under the subordination and dependency of another, in exchange for a remuneration.
compliance_requirements: Written form for specific contracts (fixed-term, seasonal)
Formalize terms of employment within the first 15 days
Maintain official payroll records
compliance_risk: High; oral contracts are presumed to have employer-favorable terms, but lack of written records complicates legal defense in labor disputes.
penalty: Fines ranging from 1 to 23 base salaries, and payment of back wages/damages if proven that oral terms were violated.

definition: Regulates the voluntary agreement to perform work remotely, which must be formalized in an addendum or specific contract clause.
compliance_requirements: Written agreement detailing remote work conditions
Provision of equipment and internet cost coverage policies
Health and safety assessment of the home workplace
compliance_risk: Medium; risk of blurring lines between working hours and personal time, potential liability for workplace accidents at home.
penalty: Labor inspection sanctions and potential payment of claims for non-covered expenses.

law_name: Ley de Protección de la Persona frente al Tratamiento de sus Datos Personales
definition: Regulates the collection and processing of sensitive employee data, including the requirement for informed consent in employment records.
compliance_requirements: Provide privacy notice to employees upon hiring
Obtain written consent for non-essential data processing
Maintain security measures for employee files
compliance_risk: High; administrative fines from PRODHAB and potential civil lawsuits for data breaches.
penalty: Fines ranging from 1 to 20 base salaries.
source_reference: Agencia de Protección de Datos de los Habitantes (PRODHAB)

law_name: Código de Trabajo de Costa Rica
definition: Establishes that the effective workday is 8 hours during the day, 6 hours at night, and 48 hours per week.
compliance_requirements: Maintain a daily log of hours worked
Ensure workday limits are not exceeded unless authorized
Guarantee rest periods within the shift
compliance_risk: Fines, legal claims for unpaid overtime, and constructive dismissal claims.
penalty: Administrative fines imposed by the Ministry of Labor and Social Security (MTSS).

law_name: Código de Trabajo de Costa Rica
definition: Defines overtime as the work performed beyond the ordinary workday and requires additional compensation.
compliance_requirements: Pay at least 50% more than the standard hourly rate for overtime
Document mutual agreement between employer and employee for overtime
Adhere to maximum daily limit including overtime
compliance_risk: Back pay claims with interest, labor inspections by MTSS.
penalty: Mandatory payment of owed overtime plus potential fines for labor infractions.

definition: Regulates the working hours for employees under the telework modality, ensuring the right to disconnect.
compliance_requirements: Establish clear communication channels
Respect the 'right to disconnect' outside working hours
Specify the schedule in the telework addendum
documentation_required: Telework addendum to the employment contract
compliance_risk: Claims of unpaid overtime due to lack of disconnection, potential labor inspection.
penalty: Administrative sanctions and compensation for damages due to labor code violations.

law_name: Ley Constitutiva de la Caja Costarricense de Seguro Social
definition: Mandates the compulsory registration and contribution of all employees to the national social security system for health and pension coverage.
compliance_requirements: Register all employees with the CCSS within 8 days of hiring
Deduct and remit employer and employee social security contributions
Report monthly payroll information through the SICERE system
compliance_risk: High. Potential for audits, heavy fines, and personal liability for company directors.
penalty: Fines calculated based on percentage of omitted wages plus interest and surcharges; potential criminal charges for tax evasion.
source_reference: Caja Costarricense de Seguro Social (CCSS) Official Portal

law_name: Ley del Impuesto sobre la Renta
definition: Regulates the withholding tax on salaries, regular income, and other labor-related compensation for employees.
compliance_requirements: Withhold income tax on monthly salaries exceeding the legal exempt threshold
Declare and pay withheld amounts to the tax authority
File annual tax statements regarding employee compensation
compliance_risk: High. Mandatory oversight by the Ministry of Finance.
penalty: Administrative fines based on the Base Salary (Salario Base) and interest for late payments.

law_name: Código de Trabajo de Costa Rica
definition: Establishes mandatory payments such as the Christmas bonus (Aguinaldo) and severance payments, which are subject to specific calculation rules.
compliance_requirements: Payment of Aguinaldo (13th month salary) between Dec 1st and 20th
Calculation of severance pay (Cesantía) upon termination
Proper record keeping of payment receipts
compliance_risk: Moderate to High. Non-payment leads to labor lawsuits.
penalty: Payment of outstanding amounts plus statutory interest and potential legal fees.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Seguridad Social

definition: Mandates the contribution to the Labor Capitalization Fund (FCL) and mandatory pension funds (ROP).
compliance_requirements: Register employees with an OPC (Operadora de Pensiones)
Contribute 3% of the monthly salary to the FCL
Submit timely payments to the chosen pension operator
documentation_required: Employee registration status with pension fund
Proof of payment to pension fund
compliance_risk: Moderate. Failure to register results in legal disputes with workers.
penalty: Payment of outstanding contributions plus interest and applicable fines.

law_name: Ley del Impuesto sobre la Renta
definition: The law governing income tax on salaries, services, and other remunerations, requiring employers to withhold income tax from employee wages (Impuesto sobre el salario).
compliance_requirements: Calculate withholding based on updated tax brackets issued by the Ministry of Finance
Deduct the tax from the employee's gross monthly salary
Remit withheld taxes to the Ministry of Finance within the first 15 days of the following month
documentation_required: Payroll records indicating gross salary and deductions
Proof of tax filing and payment via ATV platform
Employee income tax declaration forms (if applicable)
compliance_risk: Financial penalties, interest on late payments, and potential criminal tax fraud investigations.
penalty: Interest at the official rate and fines ranging from 50% to 150% of the unpaid tax amount depending on the severity and intent.

law_name: Ley Constitutiva de la Caja Costarricense de Seguro Social
definition: Mandates employers to register employees and contribute to the social security system (CCSS) for health and disability benefits.
compliance_requirements: Register the company and all employees in the CCSS system
Calculate employer and employee contributions based on gross payroll
Pay social security contributions by the designated deadlines
compliance_risk: Employer liability for all medical costs if an employee is unregistered, plus hefty fines and potential business closure.
penalty: Surcharges for late payment, fines based on the minimum wage unit (base contributiva), and criminal liability for failure to remit deductions.

definition: Requires employer contributions to the Supplementary Pension Fund (Fondo de Capitalización Laboral) to support employee retirement and severance.
compliance_requirements: Contribute the mandatory percentage to the chosen pension operator (OPC)
Ensure timely monthly payments alongside CCSS payroll
