compliance_risk: Legal claims from employees for unpaid contributions and administrative sanctions by the SUPEN (Superintendency of Pensions).
penalty: Monetary fines and interest on unpaid contributions; potential administrative intervention.

definition: Workers have the right to a minimum of two weeks of paid vacation for every 50 weeks of continuous work with the same employer.
compliance_requirements: Provide 14 days of paid vacation for every 50 weeks of continuous service
Vacation period must be granted within 15 weeks after the completion of the 50-week period
Prohibit compensating vacation with money except upon termination
documentation_required: Proof of payment for vacation pay
compliance_risk: Lawsuits for unpaid vacation time or administrative fines for failure to grant mandatory rest.
penalty: Administrative fines issued by the Ministry of Labor and Social Security (MTSS).
source_reference: Asamblea Legislativa de la República de Costa Rica

law_code: Ley Constitutiva de la Caja Costarricense de Seguro Social
definition: Mandatory social security coverage for sickness and maternity benefits managed by the CCSS.
compliance_requirements: Register employees with the CCSS
Make monthly contributions based on gross salary
Accept CCSS medical leave certificates as valid justification for absence
compliance_risk: Fines from CCSS for evasion, obligation to pay full salary if registration is missing.
penalty: Monetary fines, interest on arrears, and potential criminal charges for employer evasion.

definition: Protection of pregnant workers providing 4 months of maternity leave (1 month pre-natal, 3 months post-natal).
compliance_requirements: Grant 4 months of paid leave
Ensure payment is covered by CCSS (50%) and Employer (50%)
Guarantee stability of employment during pregnancy and breastfeeding
compliance_risk: Discriminatory dismissal lawsuit, mandatory reinstatement, and payment of back wages.
penalty: Significant labor court damages and administrative sanctions by MTSS.
source_reference: Asamblea Legislativa de la República de Costa Rica

definition: Mandatory additional month's salary (Aguinaldo) paid to all employees, calculated based on the average salary earned from December 1st of the previous year to November 30th of the current year.
compliance_requirements: Calculate aguinaldo by totaling all gross earnings within the period and dividing by 12
Pay no later than December 20th
Issue payment to all employees regardless of duration of contract
documentation_required: Payroll records for the period Dec 1 - Nov 30
compliance_risk: Mandatory payment with interest if delayed, administrative fines.
penalty: Administrative fines imposed by the Ministry of Labor.
source_reference: Asamblea Legislativa de la República de Costa Rica

definition: Mandatory severance payment (auxilio de cesantía) upon termination without just cause, based on years of service.
compliance_requirements: Calculate based on years of service with a maximum cap of 8 years
Pay within a reasonable timeframe after termination
Ensure calculation reflects the average salary of the last 6 months
compliance_risk: Labor litigation for incorrect severance calculations.
penalty: Payment of interest on outstanding amounts and potential legal costs.
source_reference: Asamblea Legislativa de la República de Costa Rica

law_name: Código de Trabajo de la República de Costa Rica
definition: Regulates the essential elements of an employment contract, including subordination, personal service, and remuneration.
compliance_requirements: Execution of a written employment contract
Registration of the employee within the payroll system
Verification of legal working age
Formalizing terms of employment in Spanish
documentation_required: Signed physical or digital employment contract
Copy of identification document (Cedula or DIMEX)
Proof of educational qualifications if required for the role
compliance_risk: Reclassification of independent contractors as employees, leading to back taxes and social security debts.
penalty: Fines ranging from 1 to 23 base salaries depending on the severity and company size.
source_reference: Asamblea Legislativa de la República de Costa Rica

law_name: Ley Constitutiva de la Caja Costarricense de Seguro Social
definition: Mandates the mandatory insurance of all employees under the universal health and pension system.
compliance_requirements: Employer registration with CCSS
Inclusion of employee in the employer's planilla (payroll)
Monthly reporting of wages to CCSS
compliance_risk: Incurring civil and criminal liability for failure to register employees.
penalty: Fixed fines, interest on unpaid contributions, and prohibition from bidding on public contracts.

law_name: Ley de Protección de la Persona frente al Tratamiento de sus Datos Personales
definition: Regulates the collection, storage, and processing of sensitive employee personal data during the hiring process.
compliance_requirements: Obtaining informed consent from applicants
Limiting data collection to what is necessary for employment purposes
Ensuring data security protocols
documentation_required: Signed consent forms for background checks
compliance_risk: Administrative and judicial sanctions for data breaches or unauthorized use of personal sensitive info.
penalty: Fines classified as minor, serious, and very serious; potential damages for emotional distress.
source_reference: Agencia de Protección de los Habitantes de los Datos Personales (PRODHAB)

definition: Governs the legal framework for hiring or transitioning employees to remote work modalities.
compliance_requirements: Written amendment to the employment contract defining remote work
Agreement on the provision of tools and equipment
Health and safety risk assessment of the remote work environment
documentation_required: Telework annex to the employment contract
compliance_risk: Labor disputes regarding equipment costs, overtime accountability, and workplace accidents.
penalty: Administrative sanctions by the Ministry of Labor and Social Security (MTSS).
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Seguridad Social

law_name: Código de Trabajo de la República de Costa Rica
definition: Establishes that a labor relationship exists when there is a personal provision of services, subordination to the employer, and payment of salary, regardless of the contract title used by the parties (Principle of Primacy of Reality).
compliance_requirements: Ensure that independent contractors maintain true autonomy.
Avoid imposing schedules, strict supervision, or disciplinary powers over contractors.
Ensure contractors provide their own tools and bear economic risk.
documentation_required: Service provision agreement (Contrato de Servicios Profesionales)
Invoices issued by the contractor (Facturas electrónicas)
Contractor's registration with tax authorities as an independent professional
compliance_risk: High: Risk of 'laborization' where authorities reclassify the contractor as an employee, triggering retrospective obligations for social security, severance, and vacation pay.
penalty: Payment of all unpaid social security contributions (CCSS) plus interest and fines, back-pay for statutory benefits, and potential labor litigation.
source_reference: Código de Trabajo, Asamblea Legislativa de Costa Rica

law_name: Ley del Impuesto sobre la Renta
definition: Defines taxable income and differentiates between income from dependent employment (subject to payroll tax) and independent professional income (subject to corporate/income tax).
compliance_requirements: Verify that payments to contractors are not subject to standard payroll tax (deductions) but are handled as commercial payments for services.
Ensure tax compliance of independent contractors to avoid fiscal audit triggers.
documentation_required: Tax identification documents of the contractor
compliance_risk: Tax authorities (Ministerio de Hacienda) may determine that payments made were wages in disguise to avoid payroll withholding taxes.
penalty: Monetary sanctions, interest on unpaid payroll taxes, and potential tax evasion charges.
source_reference: Ley del Impuesto sobre la Renta, Ley No. 7092

law_name: Código de Trabajo de la República de Costa Rica
definition: Governs the rules for the termination of employment contracts, specifically regarding 'preaviso' (notice period) and 'cesantía' (severance pay) for employees dismissed without just cause.
compliance_requirements: Provide formal written notice of termination based on seniority
Calculate and pay severance indemnity based on years of service
Provide a certification of employment and work certificate
Ensure full payment of accrued benefits including proportional vacation and bonus
documentation_required: Proof of payment of severance and accrued benefits
Final settlement (Liquidación) document signed by both parties
compliance_risk: High. Failure to pay severance or notice can lead to labor court lawsuits, mandatory legal interest, and payment of court costs.
penalty: Payment of unpaid severance plus legal interest and attorney fees; potential fines from the Ministry of Labor.
source_reference: Asamblea Legislativa de la República de Costa Rica

definition: Regulates the mandatory employer contribution to the 'Fondo de Capitalización Laboral' (FCL) which serves as a savings account for the employee, accessible upon termination of the employment relationship.
compliance_requirements: Maintain monthly contributions to the FCL/ROPC
Facilitate employee access to their FCL funds upon separation
Correct reporting of employee salaries to the operator
documentation_required: Proof of monthly contributions to the pension operator
Exit notice sent to the pension operator
compliance_risk: Moderate. Potential for administrative fines from the Superintendencia de Pensiones (SUPEN) and employee lawsuits for unpaid contributions.
penalty: Fines, interest on unpaid contributions, and social security audit sanctions.

law_name: Código de Trabajo de la República de Costa Rica (Despido con Responsabilidad Patronal)
definition: Defines the conditions under which an employer terminates a contract without just cause, triggering the obligation to pay full severance and statutory benefits.
compliance_requirements: Proper classification of termination reason
Full settlement calculation within legal timeframes
Issuance of mandatory employment certifications
documentation_required: Calculation breakdown of the severance indemnity
Acknowledgement of receipt of final payment
compliance_risk: High. Incorrect classification of 'cause' can lead to 'despido injustificado' claims resulting in severe financial penalties.
penalty: Payment of total severance amount, legal interest, and possible reinstatement if discrimination is proven.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Seguridad Social (MTSS)

definition: Mandatory requirement for a written employment contract if it is not verbal.
compliance_requirements: Formalize the employment relationship in writing for specific contract types
Maintain signed copies of employment agreements
Provide a copy to the worker
compliance_risk: Presumption of employer's version of facts if no written contract exists
penalty: Administrative fines by the Ministry of Labor and Social Security (MTSS)
source_reference: Asamblea Legislativa de la República de Costa Rica

definition: Regulations requiring formal documentation for remote work arrangements.
compliance_requirements: Execute written agreement for teleworking
Define schedule and scope of duties
Establish tools and equipment responsibilities
compliance_risk: Ambiguity in work hours or liability for work-related accidents
penalty: Administrative sanctions by MTSS

definition: Requirement to keep accurate records of vacation, wages, and working hours.
compliance_requirements: Maintain a vacation registry
Keep payroll receipts (planillas)
Record hours worked including overtime
documentation_required: Payment receipts signed by the worker
compliance_risk: Labor lawsuits where the employer fails to prove payments made
penalty: Fines and mandatory payment of all disputed benefits
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Seguridad Social

law_name: Código de Trabajo de Costa Rica
definition: Regulates the rights and obligations of employers and employees, including the calculation of severance pay (cesantía) and pre-notice (preaviso) upon termination without just cause.
compliance_requirements: Payment of accrued benefits within the legal timeframe
Proper documentation of termination cause if applicable
Issuance of work certificate upon request
documentation_required: Proof of payment of accrued benefits
compliance_risk: Lawsuits for unjustified dismissal and claims for unpaid benefits.
penalty: Payment of back wages, legal interest, damages, and legal fees.

law_name: Ley Constitutiva de la Caja Costarricense de Seguro Social
definition: Mandates the registration of all employees and the employer's obligation to contribute a percentage of wages to the CCSS for health and pension coverage.
compliance_requirements: Timely registration of employees before the start date
Accurate reporting of gross wages
Monthly payment of employer and employee contributions
compliance_risk: Fines, legal action, and potential criminal charges for non-payment or under-reporting of wages.
penalty: Daily interest, fines for evasion, and closure of establishment in extreme cases.

definition: Defines the legal framework for remote work, emphasizing that teleworkers retain the same rights as on-site employees.
compliance_requirements: Formal written agreement for telework
Provision of equipment and security tools
Adherence to occupational health standards
documentation_required: Occupational risk assessment for home office
compliance_risk: Claims of inequality in benefits or violation of work-life balance rights.
penalty: Labor sanctions and civil claims under general labor laws.

law_name: Ley de Impuesto sobre la Renta
definition: Obligation to withhold income tax (impuesto sobre salarios) from employee compensation and remit it to the tax administration.
compliance_requirements: Correct calculation of tax brackets
Monthly submission of withholding tax declarations
Issuance of annual tax certificates
compliance_risk: Tax audits, penalties for late submission, and interest on unpaid taxes.
penalty: Administrative fines, daily interest, and possible tax evasion prosecution.

law_name: Constitution of the Republic of Costa Rica
definition: Recognizes the right of employers and workers to enter into collective bargaining agreements to regulate labor relations, granting these agreements the force of law.
compliance_requirements: Respect the autonomy of collective bargaining
Engage in good faith negotiations when required
Adhere to the legally binding nature of ratified agreements
documentation_required: Proof of filing with the Ministry of Labor
compliance_risk: Constitutional violation leading to legal challenges and labor unrest.
penalty: Possible nullification of individual contracts contrary to the agreement; potential administrative sanctions.
source_reference: Asamblea Legislativa de la República de Costa Rica

definition: Governs the legal framework for collective labor agreements, regulating the conditions under which employers and unions reach binding pacts regarding working conditions.
compliance_requirements: The agreement must be in writing
Must be filed with the Ministry of Labor and Social Security
Must benefit at least one-third of the workers if signed with an unorganized group, or apply to the whole union if signed with a union
documentation_required: Official copy of the collective agreement
Certificate of deposit from the Ministry of Labor
compliance_risk: Labor litigation, strikes, and failure to apply standardized benefits.
penalty: Fines ranging from 3 to 23 times the minimum base wage, depending on the severity and size of the company.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Seguridad Social

law_name: Law on the Promotion of Social Dialogue
definition: Establishes mechanisms to promote social dialogue between the state, employers, and labor organizations to maintain peaceful labor relations.
compliance_requirements: Participation in social dialogue forums if summoned
Transparency in sharing economic data relevant to negotiations
compliance_risk: Damage to public reputation and industrial disputes.
penalty: Reprimands and administrative oversight by the Ministry of Labor.

definition: Regulates the formation, registration, and functioning of labor unions, as well as the protection of trade union freedom and collective bargaining rights.
compliance_requirements: Recognition of the right to organize without employer interference
Protection of union leaders from arbitrary dismissal (Fuero Sindical)
Deduction of union dues from employee payroll when authorized
Negotiation in good faith for collective bargaining agreements
documentation_required: Union registration certificates issued by the Ministry of Labor
Proof of notification of union leadership
compliance_risk: Unfair labor practices, anti-union discrimination lawsuits, and administrative sanctions.
penalty: Fines ranging from 1 to 23 times the minimum wage depending on the severity of the violation and the company size.
source_reference: Código de Trabajo de Costa Rica, Articles 332-379

law_name: Political Constitution of the Republic of Costa Rica
definition: Establishes the fundamental right of employers and workers to organize freely for the exclusive purpose of obtaining, developing, and preserving economic, social, or professional benefits.
compliance_requirements: Guaranteeing the right to organize without restriction
Prohibition of anti-union acts
Recognition of union autonomy
documentation_required: Bylaws of the union registered with the Ministry of Labor
compliance_risk: Constitutional protection claims (Recurso de Amparo) if rights are restricted.
penalty: Constitutional damages and potential criminal investigation if rights are obstructed.
source_reference: Constitución Política de Costa Rica, Article 60

definition: Regulates Labor Associations (Asociaciones Solidaristas) which coexist with unions, establishing the rules for their administration and employer contributions.
compliance_requirements: Management of mandatory payroll deductions for Solidarismo
Employer contribution of 5.33% to the association
Non-interference in the internal affairs of the association
compliance_risk: Litigation regarding mismanagement of funds or interference with association elections.
penalty: Legal claims for back-pay of employer contributions plus interest.

definition: Regulates the modality of telework as an effective instrument for the modernization of the organization of work in the public and private sectors, through the use of information and communication technologies.
compliance_requirements: Requirement of a written agreement or contract amendment
Guarantee of voluntary nature for both parties
Guarantee of equal rights compared to in-office workers
Provision of equipment and tools by the employer unless otherwise agreed
Compliance with Occupational Health and Safety standards
documentation_required: Signed occupational health and safety assessment
compliance_risk: Reclassification of the employment relationship, potential claims for breach of equality principle, and labor inspection sanctions.
penalty: Fines ranging from 1 to 23 base salaries depending on the severity and number of affected employees as determined by the Ministry of Labor and Social Security.
source_reference: La Gaceta, Diario Oficial No. 182, September 2019

law_name: Reglamento a la Ley para regular el teletrabajo
definition: Provides the specific technical and administrative procedures to apply the provisions established in Law 9738 regarding the implementation of remote work in the private sector.
compliance_requirements: Implementation of a formal policy for telework
Ensuring the right to disconnect
Establishment of reporting and productivity metrics
Validation of the workspace suitability by the employer
compliance_risk: Non-compliance with digital disconnection rights and health risks due to inadequate remote ergonomic setups.
penalty: Administrative sanctions by the Labor Inspectorate, potential lawsuits for workplace stress or injuries.
source_reference: La Gaceta, Diario Oficial No. 238, December 2019

law_name: Ley de Protección de la Persona frente al Tratamiento de sus Datos Personales
definition: Regulates the protection of individual privacy regarding data processing, which is critical in remote work environments where corporate data is handled off-site.
compliance_requirements: Implementation of cybersecurity measures for remote access
Confidentiality clauses in employment contracts
Data protection training for remote staff
Protection of sensitive information transmitted via home networks
compliance_risk: Legal liability for data breaches, administrative fines, and loss of business reputation.
penalty: Administrative fines imposed by PRODHAB; potential civil damages claims by affected parties.
source_reference: La Gaceta, Diario Oficial No. 177, September 2011

law_name: Law for the Protection of Individuals with regard to the Processing of their Personal Data
definition: The legal framework regulating the processing of personal data to protect the fundamental rights of individuals, specifically their privacy and informational self-determination.
compliance_requirements: Obtain informed, express, and written consent from employees before processing personal data.
Implement technical and organizational measures to ensure data security and integrity.
Maintain a registry of databases with the Data Protection Agency (PRODHAB).
Guarantee employees' ARCO rights (Access, Rectification, Cancellation, and Opposition).
Inform employees about the purpose, storage duration, and use of their collected data.
documentation_required: Proof of Database Registration with PRODHAB
compliance_risk: High risk of administrative sanctions, reputational damage, and legal liability for unauthorized disclosure or misuse of sensitive employee information.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 1 to 20 base salaries, suspension of data processing activities, and potential civil or criminal liability for data breaches.

law_name: Regulation to the Law for the Protection of Individuals with regard to the Processing of their Personal Data
definition: The specific regulatory requirements detailing how employers must fulfill their duties under Law 8968 regarding the protection of personal data in the workplace.
compliance_requirements: Define clear roles for data controllers and processors.
Ensure data is used only for the purpose for which it was collected.
Apply the principle of data minimization.
Provide mechanisms for employees to exercise their rights regarding their employment records.
compliance_risk: Failure to comply with regulatory specifics can lead to rejection of database registrations and scrutiny by the PRODHAB authority.
penalty: Sanctions aligned with Law 8968, including fines and orders to rectify data processing practices.

definition: Sets the regulatory framework for occupational health and safety in workplaces, establishing the rights of workers and obligations of employers to ensure a safe environment.
compliance_requirements: Implement internal occupational health and safety committees
Provide personal protective equipment (PPE)
Conduct risk assessments in the workplace
Ensure proper ventilation, lighting, and workspace ergonomics
compliance_risk: Civil liability in case of work-related accidents, potential work stoppage orders, and inspection fines.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 1 to 23 basic salaries depending on the severity and size of the company.

law_name: Reglamento General de Seguridad e Higiene de Trabajo
definition: Detailed regulatory requirements for specific workplace safety conditions including building structure, fire prevention, and electrical safety.
compliance_requirements: Compliance with fire safety standards
Regular maintenance of equipment
Signage for emergency exits
Sanitation and hygiene protocols
compliance_risk: Closure of premises by the Ministry of Health or Ministry of Labor.
penalty: Fixed fines and legal closure of the establishment until compliance is met.

definition: Mandatory insurance coverage for all employees to cover medical, rehabilitative, and economic damages resulting from work accidents or occupational diseases.
compliance_requirements: Mandatory registration of all employees with the National Insurance Institute (INS)
Payment of monthly insurance premiums
Immediate reporting of any workplace accident
compliance_risk: Employer becomes personally liable for all costs if a worker is injured and not covered by the insurance policy.
penalty: Full responsibility for civil and medical damages, plus potential criminal investigation if gross negligence is proven.

law_name: Ley General de Migración y Extranjería
definition: The overarching legal framework governing the entry, stay, and work authorization of foreign nationals within Costa Rican territory.
compliance_requirements: Employers must verify the legal status of foreign workers before hiring.
Employers must notify the General Directorate of Migration and Foreigners (DGME) regarding changes in employment status of foreign staff.
Foreigners must possess a valid residency or temporary work permit category (e.g., 'Residencia Temporal' or 'Permiso de Trabajo').
documentation_required: DIMEX (Documento de Identidad Migratoria para Extranjeros)
Registration with the Costa Rican Social Security Fund (CCSS)
compliance_risk: High; employment of unauthorized workers leads to significant fines and potential deportation of the employee.
penalty: Fines ranging from 2 to 12 base salaries for employers, and potential cancellation of business operation licenses.
source_reference: Dirección General de Migración y Extranjería (DGME)

definition: Regulatory body providing specific procedural guidelines for processing residency and work permit applications for foreign nationals.
compliance_requirements: Adherence to specific visa category requirements (e.g., specialized technicians, investors, or company executives).
Submission of police clearance records for the country of origin.
Compliance with mandatory registration periods for temporary stay.
compliance_risk: Moderate; procedural non-compliance results in the denial of permit renewal or legal status revocation.
penalty: Administrative sanctions, denial of permit, and forced exit from the country.

law_name: Ley de Protección a la Persona frente al Tratamiento de sus Datos Personales
definition: Regulates the collection and processing of personal data, including the biometric and sensitive data required by immigration authorities.
compliance_requirements: Protection of sensitive immigration data collected by employers.
Ensuring data confidentiality during the permit application process.
Limiting the use of personal identification data solely for its intended purpose.
compliance_risk: Moderate to High; data breaches involving sensitive foreign national information can lead to litigation.
penalty: Administrative fines categorized as minor, serious, or very serious (up to 20 base salaries).
source_reference: PRODHAB (Agencia de Protección de Datos de los Habitantes)

law_name: Constitution of the Republic of Costa Rica
definition: Establishes that all persons are equal before the law and there shall be no discrimination contrary to human dignity.
compliance_requirements: Implement non-discriminatory hiring practices
Ensure equal pay for equal work
Maintain fair treatment protocols for all employees
compliance_risk: Constitutional challenge or lawsuits for human rights violations.
penalty: Nullification of discriminatory acts and potential civil liability.
source_reference: Asamblea Legislativa de la República de Costa Rica

definition: Prohibits employers from discriminating against workers based on race, gender, age, disability, or political opinion.
compliance_requirements: Prohibit discrimination in the workplace
Apply equal conditions for equivalent work
Prevent harassment and hostile work environments
documentation_required: Internal work regulations (Reglamento Interior de Trabajo)
compliance_risk: Labor lawsuits, administrative sanctions by the Ministry of Labor, and reputational damage.
penalty: Administrative fines up to 23 base salaries, payment of back wages, and potential reinstatement.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Seguridad Social

law_name: Law for the Promotion of Personal Autonomy of People with Disabilities
definition: Mandates equal opportunities and prohibits discrimination in employment based on disability, requiring reasonable accommodations.
compliance_requirements: Provide reasonable accommodations for disabled employees
Ensure accessibility in the physical workspace
Eliminate barriers in recruitment and promotion
documentation_required: Training logs for staff on disability inclusion
compliance_risk: Legal action for discriminatory practices and failure to accommodate.
penalty: Fines and mandatory compliance orders to rectify discrimination.

law_name: Law for the Prevention of Sexual Harassment in the Workplace and Education
definition: Defines and penalizes sexual harassment in the workplace, requiring the implementation of prevention and internal resolution mechanisms.
compliance_requirements: Establish an internal committee for harassment prevention
Implement a formal anti-harassment policy
Conduct mandatory training for all staff
compliance_risk: Liability for failure to prevent or address harassment complaints.
penalty: Fines up to 12 base salaries and compensation to the victim.

definition: The legal obligation of employers to pay a mandatory annual bonus known as the Aguinaldo, equivalent to one-twelfth of the total ordinary and extraordinary wages earned during the period from December 1st of the previous year to November 30th of the current year.
compliance_requirements: Calculate the total annual earnings from Dec 1 to Nov 30
Divide total earnings by 12
Payment must be made within the first 20 days of December
compliance_risk: Employer liability for payment of interest and potential legal sanctions for failure to pay the legally mandated bonus.
penalty: Administrative fines imposed by the Ministry of Labor and Social Security; employer remains obligated to pay the full amount due.
source_reference: Codigo de Trabajo de Costa Rica, Article 153

definition: Mandatory insurance coverage for all workers against occupational hazards (work-related accidents and illnesses) provided exclusively by the National Insurance Institute (INS).
compliance_requirements: Register all employees with INS
Pay monthly insurance premiums based on declared payroll
Report all workplace accidents to INS within 48 hours
compliance_risk: Employer assumes full liability for all medical costs, disability payments, and indemnities if insurance is not in place or premiums are in arrears.
penalty: Civil and criminal liability for workplace accidents; fines and suspension of operations by labor inspectors.
source_reference: Ley sobre Riesgos del Trabajo No. 6727

law_name: Constitutive Law of the Costa Rican Social Security Fund (CCSS)
definition: Compulsory affiliation and contribution of all employees to the CCSS for sickness, maternity, disability, old age, and death benefits.
compliance_requirements: Enroll employee within 8 days of hiring
Withhold employee contributions from payroll
Pay employer contributions to CCSS
File monthly payroll reports (Planilla)
compliance_risk: Significant interest and surcharges on unpaid contributions; potential criminal charges for misappropriation of funds.
penalty: Closure of business; high financial penalties; inability to participate in public tenders.
source_reference: Ley Constitutiva de la Caja Costarricense de Seguro Social

definition: Compensation due to an employee when the employment contract is terminated by the employer without just cause.
compliance_requirements: Calculate seniority based on length of service
Apply the scale established in Article 29 for payout
Calculate payment based on average salary of the last 6 months
documentation_required: Proof of wire transfer or payment receipt
compliance_risk: Labor lawsuits for unpaid severance, damages, and legal costs.
penalty: Ordering of back-pay payments plus legal interest until the debt is settled.
source_reference: Codigo de Trabajo de Costa Rica, Article 29

law_name: Código de Trabajo de Costa Rica
definition: Establishes the obligation of the employer to maintain accurate payroll records (planilla interna) and payment documentation.
compliance_requirements: Maintain a payroll registry (Libro de Planillas)
Provide pay slips (recibos de pago) with legally required details
Keep records for at least 5 years
documentation_required: Internal payroll book or digital equivalent
compliance_risk: Sanctions by the Ministry of Labor and Social Security (MTSS) during labor inspections.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 1 to 23 base salaries depending on severity.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Seguridad Social

definition: Regulates the mandatory employer contribution to the Mandatory Pension Fund (ROPC) and the Labor Capitalization Fund (FCL).
compliance_requirements: Deduct and remit contributions to the FCL
Remit employer contributions to the ROPC
Ensure payroll systems reflect these deductions accurately
documentation_required: Contribution payment vouchers to OPC (Operadoras de Pensiones)
compliance_risk: Direct liability for missing contributions and legal claims by employees.
penalty: Payment of outstanding contributions plus interest, and administrative sanctions.

law_name: Código de Trabajo de Costa Rica
definition: Establishes the obligation to formalize the employment relationship, acknowledging the link between employer and employee, which serves as the basis for legal rights and obligations.
compliance_requirements: Execute a written employment contract
Ensure the contract details working hours, duties, and remuneration
Keep a copy of the contract signed by both parties
documentation_required: Evidence of payment (Payroll receipts/Boletas de pago)
Internal work regulations (Reglamento Interior de Trabajo)
compliance_risk: Legal presumption that all verbal agreements favor the employee in labor disputes; lack of documentation leads to inability to prove compliance with labor standards.
penalty: Sanctions by the Ministry of Labor (MTSS) for non-compliance with labor registry requirements.
source_reference: Código de Trabajo de la República de Costa Rica

definition: Requires the registration and documentation of remote work arrangements specifically, modifying the standard employment relationship requirements.
compliance_requirements: Addendum to the employment contract defining remote work
Registration of the remote working conditions
Guarantee of labor rights in the remote environment
documentation_required: Health and Safety assessment for the remote workstation
compliance_risk: Lack of clear remote work agreements can lead to claims regarding overtime and workplace accidents being considered occupational hazards.
penalty: Administrative inspections and potential liability for occupational injuries not covered due to lack of proper registration.
source_reference: Ley para Regular el Teletrabajo No. 9738

law_name: Labor Code of the Dominican Republic
definition: The primary legislation governing the relationship between employers and employees, establishing rights, obligations, and the legal framework for labor relations.
compliance_requirements: Execution of written employment contracts
Registration of contracts with the Ministry of Labor within 48 hours
Payment of minimum wage
Adherence to maximum working hours
documentation_required: Registration receipt from Ministry of Labor
compliance_risk: High. Potential for labor lawsuits, administrative fines, and mandated severance payments.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 1 to 7 times the minimum wage; potential liability for unpaid wages and benefits.

definition: Law creating the Dominican Social Security System (SDSS), mandating employer and employee contributions for health, pension, and occupational risk insurance.
compliance_requirements: Register employer and employees with TSS (Tesorería de la Seguridad Social)
Monthly calculation and payment of contributions
Reporting of salary updates
compliance_risk: High. Failure to register or pay triggers penalties and employer liability for medical expenses or pension shortfalls.
penalty: Surcharges of 5% per month on unpaid contributions, potential judicial prosecution for fraud.

law_name: Tax Code of the Dominican Republic
definition: Regulates the withholding and payment of income tax (ISR) for employees (Rentax) and fiscal reporting obligations.
compliance_requirements: Withhold income tax from employee salaries
File monthly IR-4 reports
Annual reconciliation of salary income (IR-13)
compliance_risk: High. Tax evasion penalties and interest on late payments.
penalty: Fines based on the amount due, interest charges, and potential tax audit.

definition: Regulates the collection, processing, and storage of personal data, including employee information maintained by employers.
compliance_requirements: Obtain employee consent for data collection
Implement security measures for HR databases
Ensure data accuracy and limitation of use
compliance_risk: Moderate to High. Legal actions for misuse of employee information.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 10 to 500 minimum wages depending on the severity of the violation.

law_name: Regulation for the Application of the Labor Code
definition: Administrative regulations detailing the technical application of the Labor Code provisions, including procedures for internal work regulations.
compliance_requirements: Registration of internal labor regulations with the Ministry of Labor
Compliance with health and safety committee formation
compliance_risk: Moderate. Failure to register rules renders them unenforceable against employees.
penalty: Administrative sanctions for non-compliance with reporting obligations.

law_name: Labor Code of the Dominican Republic
definition: Regulates the nature, form, and content of individual employment contracts, establishing them as an agreement where a person binds themselves to provide services to another under their direction and supervision in exchange for remuneration.
compliance_requirements: Contracts must be in writing if for a definite term or for a specific work/service
Probationary period cannot exceed 90 days
Oral contracts are valid for indefinite term relationships
Must specify service nature, salary, and duration
compliance_risk: Reclassification of temporary contracts as indefinite, liability for severance payments if not properly terminated, and fines from the Ministry of Labor.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 1 to 7 times the minimum wage; potential lawsuit for severance pay.
source_reference: Ministry of Labor (Ministerio de Trabajo) - Labor Code Law 16-92

definition: Mandates the mandatory affiliation of all employees to the Dominican Social Security System (SDSS), regardless of contract type.
compliance_requirements: Register employee with TSS (Tesorería de la Seguridad Social) within 3 days of hiring
Make timely monthly contributions
Report all salary changes
compliance_risk: Fines for late registration, interest on unpaid contributions, and liability for medical/pension benefits if the employer fails to register the worker.
penalty: Surcharges of 10% for the first month and 5% for each subsequent month of delay in payments; administrative sanctions.

definition: Regulates employment relationships conducted outside the employer's premises using information and communication technologies.
compliance_requirements: Must be formalized via a written addendum to the employment contract
Employer must guarantee the same rights as on-site workers
Right to disconnect must be respected
Work tools and equipment costs must be defined
compliance_risk: Risk of being treated as standard work without proper safeguards, claims regarding extra hours or lack of equipment.
penalty: Standard labor fines for contract non-compliance under Law 16-92.

law_name: Law on Protection of Personal Data
definition: Governs the processing of personal data, including employee information collected during the hiring process and throughout the employment contract.
compliance_requirements: Obtain consent for sensitive data processing
Maintain confidentiality of employee records
Implement security measures to prevent data breaches
Notify employees of data usage
compliance_risk: Legal action from the employee for breach of privacy, administrative sanctions from data protection authorities.
penalty: Fines and potential civil damages depending on the severity of the privacy violation.
source_reference: Pro-Consumidor / General Law on Personal Data Protection

law_name: Labor Code of the Dominican Republic
definition: Regulates the maximum duration of the workday, which is set at 8 hours per day and 44 hours per week, with mandatory rest periods and provisions for extra hours.
compliance_requirements: Ensure workday does not exceed 8 hours daily
Ensure workweek does not exceed 44 hours
Provide a minimum of 36 hours of continuous weekly rest
Pay overtime for hours worked beyond the standard limits at an increased rate (minimum 35% for the first extra hour and 100% for additional hours)
Maintain an official record of working hours
documentation_required: Daily attendance logs or electronic time-keeping records
Payroll records showing overtime calculations and payments
compliance_risk: Fines, labor lawsuits, back-pay claims for unpaid overtime, and administrative sanctions by the Ministry of Labor.
penalty: Fines ranging from 3 to 12 times the minimum wage depending on the severity and recurrence, as established in the Labor Code.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo de la República Dominicana

law_name: Regulation for the Application of the Labor Code
definition: Provides procedural clarity on the implementation of work schedules, shifts, and the specific calculation of overtime compensation including nocturnal work shifts.
compliance_requirements: Proper classification of night shifts (between 9:00 PM and 7:00 AM)
Applying a 15% surcharge for night shift work
Posting of work schedules in a visible location for employees
documentation_required: Notices of work schedules filed with the Ministry of Labor
compliance_risk: Administrative penalties for failing to register schedules and miscalculation of night shift premiums.
penalty: Administrative fines established under the Labor Code and associated decrees.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo de la República Dominicana

law_name: Labor Code of the Dominican Republic
definition: Regulates the payment of the Christmas salary (Salario de Navidad), which is the 13th-month salary equivalent to one-twelfth of the ordinary salary earned during the calendar year.
compliance_requirements: Payment must be made no later than December 20th
The amount must equal 1/12 of the total ordinary salary earned in the year
It is exempt from income tax (ISR), social security contributions, and seizure
compliance_risk: Financial penalties, labor lawsuits for wage theft, and potential administrative fines.
penalty: Fines ranging from 3 to 10 minimum wages depending on company size and number of affected employees.

definition: Establishes the Dominican Social Security System (SDSS) requiring mandatory contributions from both employers and employees to the Family Health Insurance (SFS), Pension Fund (AFP), and Occupational Risk Insurance (SRL).
compliance_requirements: Register the employer and employees with the TSS (Treasury of Social Security)
Calculate and deduct employee contributions via the TSS portal
Pay employer and employee contributions monthly
Report all new hires and terminations within 3 days
compliance_risk: Accrual of interest, surcharges for late payments, inability to access health services for employees, and criminal liability for withholding funds.
penalty: Monthly surcharge of 5% on unpaid amounts, administrative fines, and criminal penalties for embezzlement of employee contributions.

law_name: Tax Code of the Dominican Republic
definition: Regulates the withholding and payment of Income Tax (ISR) applicable to salaries, wages, and other employee compensation.
compliance_requirements: Calculate monthly withholding tax based on DGII tax brackets
File monthly withholding tax return (Form IR-17)
Submit annual informative declaration (Form IR-13)
compliance_risk: Tax evasion audits, severe monetary fines, and administrative closure.
penalty: Surcharges for late payment (10% first month, 4% thereafter) and interest on unpaid taxes plus administrative fines.

definition: Obligation of the employer to grant employees a share of the company's annual profits (Participación en los Beneficios).
compliance_requirements: Distribute 10% of annual net profits among employees
Payment must be made within 90 to 120 days after the fiscal year closes
Calculation is based on the ordinary salary earned during the year
compliance_risk: Labor inspections and demands for payment by employees, potential legal sanctions.
penalty: Payment of outstanding amounts plus interest, possible administrative fines.

definition: Establishes the mandatory contributions to the Family Health Insurance (SFS), Pension Fund (AFP), and Occupational Risk Insurance (SRL) for all employees.
compliance_requirements: Register employees with the TSS
Calculate and deduct employee contributions
Pay employer and employee contributions via the TSS portal
Update employee status changes within 3 days
compliance_risk: Loss of health coverage for employees; penalties for delayed payments; inability to issue certificates of good standing.
penalty: Monthly surcharge of 2% of the amount owed, plus interest and potential legal action for evasion.

definition: Mandates the payment of the Christmas Bonus (Salario de Navidad), which is exempt from Income Tax up to a specific limit, requiring formal reporting.
compliance_requirements: Pay the equivalent of 1/12th of total annual salary
Make payment between December 1st and 20th
Submit proof of payment to the Ministry of Labor
documentation_required: Proof of payment for Christmas Bonus
compliance_risk: Claims of unpaid wages, labor inspections, and potential administrative fines by the Ministry of Labor.
penalty: Fines ranging from 3 to 10 minimum wages depending on the size of the company.

law_name: Labor Code of the Dominican Republic
definition: Mandatory annual paid vacation entitlement for employees after one year of continuous service.
compliance_requirements: Provide 14 working days of paid vacation after 1 year of service
Provide 18 working days of paid vacation after 5 years of service
Pay vacation salary prior to the start of the leave period
Maintain registry of vacation periods
documentation_required: Proof of payment of vacation salary
compliance_risk: Litigation for unpaid vacation days, fines, and potential constructive dismissal claims.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 1 to 7 times the minimum wage; liability for accumulated vacation pay upon termination.

law_name: Labor Code of the Dominican Republic
definition: Mandatory Christmas Bonus (Salario de Navidad) equivalent to one-twelfth of the ordinary salary earned during the calendar year.
compliance_requirements: Pay the bonus no later than December 20th
Include all ordinary salary earned from January 1st to December 31st
documentation_required: Proof of payment to the Ministry of Labor
compliance_risk: Fines from the Ministry of Labor and legal action by employees for collection.
penalty: Administrative fines and mandatory payment of interest on delayed amounts.

definition: Mandatory coverage for illness and maternity benefits, including paid leave during maternity periods.
compliance_requirements: Register employees with the TSS (Tesorería de la Seguridad Social)
Contribute the employer's portion for Family Health Insurance
Guarantee 14 weeks of paid maternity leave (split between employer and social security)
compliance_risk: High administrative fines, inability to process health benefits, and criminal liability for lack of registration.
penalty: Fines based on the amount of evasion and potential surcharges on unpaid contributions.

law_name: Labor Code of the Dominican Republic
definition: Establishes that the employment contract is the agreement by which one person is bound to provide services to another for a wage under the authority of the employer.
compliance_requirements: Ensure all employment contracts are formal or agreed upon
Maintain evidence of the employment relationship
Register the contract with the Ministry of Labor
documentation_required: Copy of the registration at the Ministry of Labor (SIRLA)
compliance_risk: Lack of proof of the employment relationship may lead to legal presumptions in favor of the employee.
penalty: Fines ranging from 1 to 7 times the minimum wage depending on the severity and company size.
source_reference: Ministry of Labor of the Dominican Republic (Ministerio de Trabajo)

definition: Mandates that at least 80 percent of the employees of an employer must be Dominican nationals.
compliance_requirements: Verify nationality of staff
Maintain the 80/20 ratio for Dominican vs. Foreign employees
Submit payroll documentation verifying ratios
compliance_risk: Violating the 80 percent nationalization threshold can lead to severe administrative sanctions and revocation of work permits.
penalty: Fines and potential mandatory dismissal of excess foreign staff.

definition: Mandates the registration of all employees in the Dominican Social Security System (TSS).
compliance_requirements: Register the employee in the TSS system within the first days of hiring
Contribute the employer's portion of social security
Maintain updated payroll records
compliance_risk: Failure to register leads to financial responsibility for medical costs and pension liabilities.
penalty: Fines, surcharges on late payments, and legal liability for healthcare costs.

law_name: Regulation for the Application of the Labor Code
definition: Details the procedural steps for the formalization and registration of labor contracts and workplace reporting.
compliance_requirements: Register the company and employees through the SIRLA (Integrated Labor Registration System)
Provide notification of labor contract start dates
documentation_required: Digital upload of employment contract terms
compliance_risk: Administrative fines for failure to report hiring within the legal window.
penalty: Fixed administrative fines per employee not registered.

definition: Governs the treatment of personal data collected during the hiring process and throughout employment.
compliance_requirements: Obtain consent for sensitive data collection
Ensure confidentiality of personnel files
Restrict access to employee background check data
compliance_risk: Civil liability for damages and administrative sanctions for data breaches.
penalty: Fines ranging based on the severity of the data mishandling.

law_name: Labor Code of the Dominican Republic
definition: Defines the employment relationship based on the existence of subordination, payment of salary, and personal provision of services, regardless of the name given to the contract by the parties.
compliance_requirements: Assess actual working conditions to identify elements of subordination
Avoid contractual labels that contradict the reality of the work relationship
Register employees under the TSS system if subordination exists
documentation_required: Tax identification documents of the contractor
compliance_risk: High risk of reclassification of independent contractors as employees, triggering retroactive labor liabilities.
penalty: Payment of all accrued social benefits, retroactive social security contributions, and potential fines by the Ministry of Labor.
source_reference: Ministry of Labor of the Dominican Republic

definition: Mandates affiliation to the Dominican Social Security System for all workers performing services under a contract of employment.
compliance_requirements: Identify if the relationship triggers mandatory affiliation
Ensure proper reporting to the Treasury of Social Security (TSS)
compliance_risk: Fines for lack of registration and interest payments on delinquent social security contributions.
penalty: Administrative sanctions, interest, and surcharges calculated by the TSS.

law_name: Tax Code of the Dominican Republic
definition: Governs the tax treatment of professional services versus wages, stipulating the withholding obligations for dependent versus independent personnel.
compliance_requirements: Apply correct tax withholding rates (ISR) based on classification
Issue proper tax documentation (e-CF or equivalent)
compliance_risk: Tax audit findings leading to penalties for improper withholding or non-payment of taxes.
penalty: Fines, interest, and surcharges imposed by the DGII (General Directorate of Internal Taxes).

law_name: Labor Code of the Dominican Republic
definition: Regulates the termination of employment contracts with or without responsibility on the part of the employer, including dismissal (despido), resignation (dimisión), and termination by mutual consent or economic causes.
compliance_requirements: Provide formal notification of termination for cause
Calculate and pay severance (auxilio de cesantía) based on tenure
Pay proportional vacations and Christmas bonus (sueldo 13)
Submit termination report to the Ministry of Labor
documentation_required: Ministry of Labor termination filing form
compliance_risk: High risk of labor lawsuits for unjustified dismissal claims and legal costs
penalty: Payment of double severance or indemnity for unjustified dismissal; potential fines by Ministry of Labor
source_reference: Código de Trabajo de la República Dominicana, Ley 16-92

definition: Mandatory notice periods that must be granted to the employee prior to termination when there is no just cause, proportional to the length of service.
compliance_requirements: Provide 7 days notice for 3-6 months tenure
Provide 14 days notice for 6 months-1 year tenure
Provide 28 days notice for 1+ year tenure
compliance_risk: Payment of indemnity equivalent to the notice period salary if notice is not given
penalty: Additional financial liability equivalent to salary during the mandatory notice period
source_reference: Código de Trabajo de la República Dominicana, Ley 16-92

law_name: Regulation for the Application of the Labor Code
definition: Specific procedural regulations for the notification and execution of termination within the Dominican jurisdiction.
compliance_requirements: Notification to the Ministry of Labor within 48 hours of termination
Documentation of the causes for dismissal for cause (despido)
compliance_risk: Fines by the Ministry of Labor and difficulties in proving just cause in court
penalty: Administrative fines imposed by the Ministry of Labor
source_reference: Reglamento para la Aplicación del Código de Trabajo, Decreto 258-93

definition: Requirement to register employees in the Dominican Social Security System (SDSS).
compliance_requirements: Register employees with TSS (Tesorería de la Seguridad Social)
Submit monthly payroll reports
compliance_risk: Fines, surcharges, and liability for benefits coverage if not registered.
penalty: Surcharges of 5% per month of late payment plus interest.

law_name: Tax Code of the Dominican Republic
definition: Requirement to document and withhold income tax (ISR) from employee salaries.
compliance_requirements: Submit Form IR-4
Maintain salary records for tax audit
Report annual tax withholding
compliance_risk: Tax evasion penalties and audit adjustments.
penalty: Monetary fines and interest on unpaid withholding.

definition: Specific documentation requirements for remote work arrangements.
compliance_requirements: Include remote work clause in contract
Document safety equipment provided
compliance_risk: Reclassification of the employment relationship or claims of health risks.
penalty: Administrative sanctions by the Ministry of Labor.

definition: Regulates the contribution to the Family Health Insurance (SFS), Pension Fund (AFP), and Occupational Risk Insurance (SRL).
compliance_requirements: Register all employees with the TSS (Tesorería de la Seguridad Social)
Monthly submission of Novedades
Accurate payment of employer and employee contributions
compliance_risk: High surcharges on late payments and inability of employees to access healthcare or pension benefits.
penalty: Interest surcharges (1.10% monthly) and administrative fines for non-registration.

law_name: Law on Protection of Personal Data
definition: Governs the processing and protection of personal data of employees collected by employers.
compliance_requirements: Obtain consent for data processing
Implement security measures to protect sensitive HR files
Limit data collection to employment purposes only
compliance_risk: Legal actions from employees and potential administrative sanctions from the Pro Consumidor or relevant oversight bodies.
penalty: Fines ranging from 10 to 500 minimum wages based on the severity of the data breach.
source_reference: General Archive of the Nation / Legal Registry

law_name: Regulation for the Application of the Labor Code
definition: Provides detailed administrative procedures for the application of the provisions found in the Labor Code (Law 16-92).
compliance_requirements: Detailed record-keeping of worker registers
Proper notification to the Ministry of Labor regarding contract terminations
Compliance with worker registration procedures
compliance_risk: Inability to prove employer compliance during Ministry of Labor inspections.
penalty: Administrative fines as specified in the Labor Code.

definition: Recognizes the right of workers and employers to form unions and organizations for the defense and promotion of their respective interests, and the right to collective bargaining.
compliance_requirements: Respect the freedom of association of employees
Acknowledge the legal existence of trade unions
Refrain from practices that obstruct collective bargaining
compliance_risk: Constitutional violation leading to potential judicial intervention and nullification of retaliatory employment actions.
penalty: Constitutional protection suits (Amparo) and potential civil liability.

law_name: Labor Code of the Dominican Republic
definition: Governs the collective bargaining agreement (Convenio Colectivo), which is the written agreement between one or more unions and one or more employers to set employment conditions.
compliance_requirements: Negotiate in good faith with the recognized union
Sign the collective agreement in triplicate
Deposit the agreement with the Ministry of Labor for verification
Apply the terms to all employees within the scope of the agreement
compliance_risk: Legal challenge to employment conditions, declaration of illegal labor practices, or union-led industrial action.
penalty: Fines ranging from 3 to 12 times the minimum wage per violation under Article 720.
source_reference: Código de Trabajo de la República Dominicana

law_name: Labor Code of the Dominican Republic - Union Regulations
definition: Rules governing the formation, operation, and dissolution of labor unions, providing the framework for the entities authorized to engage in collective bargaining.
compliance_requirements: Recognize the union's legal personality upon registration
Provide reasonable access to union representatives
Negotiate conditions of work with authorized representatives
documentation_required: Registration Certificate from the Ministry of Labor
compliance_risk: Unfair labor practices complaint (Práctica Desleal) before the Ministry of Labor.
penalty: Fines and potential compensation for damages caused by the obstruction of union activities.
source_reference: Código de Trabajo de la República Dominicana

law_name: Labor Code of the Dominican Republic
definition: Regulates the formation, operation, and protection of labor unions, and the right to collective bargaining and strike action.
compliance_requirements: Permit employees to form, join, or lead trade unions without interference.
Recognize unions with legal personality.
Engage in collective bargaining when requested by a union representing at least 20% of workers.
Ensure no discriminatory practices against unionized employees.
documentation_required: Internal labor regulations registered with the Ministry of Labor.
Proof of absence of anti-union discrimination.
compliance_risk: High risk of legal challenges for unfair labor practices, labor strikes, and administrative fines.
penalty: Fines ranging from 3 to 12 minimum wages depending on the severity of the violation.
source_reference: Ley 16-92 que promulga el Código de Trabajo de la República Dominicana

definition: Guarantees the freedom of association and the right of employees to organize and strike.
compliance_requirements: Uphold the fundamental right of employees to form unions without prior authorization.
Respect the right to collective labor disputes.
documentation_required: Official company policy aligning with constitutional protections.
compliance_risk: Constitutional litigation and invalidation of employment terminations linked to union activity.
penalty: Constitutional lawsuits leading to potential reinstatement and damages.

definition: Regulates the conditions for telework (remote work) in the private sector, establishing it as a voluntary agreement between employer and employee where tasks are performed primarily outside the employer's premises using ICTs.
compliance_requirements: Must be formalized through an addendum to the employment contract or a new contract.
The right to disconnect must be explicitly guaranteed.
Employer must provide the necessary equipment unless otherwise agreed.
Employer must cover communication and electricity costs incurred by teleworking.
Equal treatment and rights for teleworkers as compared to on-site employees.
documentation_required: Data protection and cybersecurity policy acknowledgment
compliance_risk: Reclassification of the contract status and potential labor litigation regarding unpaid expenses or discriminatory treatment.
penalty: Administrative fines imposed by the Ministry of Labor ranging from 1 to 7 minimum wages depending on the severity and company size.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo de la República Dominicana

law_name: Labor Code of the Dominican Republic
definition: The overarching labor law that governs all employment relationships in the Dominican Republic, ensuring protection of workers regardless of the location of work.
compliance_requirements: Registration of the employment contract with the Ministry of Labor (SIRLA).
Adherence to maximum working hours even when performed remotely.
Compliance with mandatory social security and payroll deductions.
Maintenance of subordinate status and employer-employee relationship.
compliance_risk: High risk of labor claims regarding overtime, severance, and social security benefit calculations if the remote arrangement is not properly documented.
penalty: Fines according to the Labor Code and potential legal orders for back-payment of social security and benefits.
source_reference: Código de Trabajo de la República Dominicana, Ley 16-92

law_name: Law on Protection of Personal Data
definition: Regulates the processing of personal data, which is critical for remote workers accessing sensitive company information from home environments.
compliance_requirements: Implementation of security measures to protect employee and company data.
Ensuring data confidentiality in remote work environments.
Informing employees of data processing policies.
compliance_risk: Data breaches and civil or administrative liability for failure to protect sensitive data accessible remotely.
penalty: Administrative sanctions and potential civil damages.
source_reference: Ley 172-13 sobre Protección de Datos de Carácter Personal

definition: Establishes the fundamental right to personal intimacy, honor, and the protection of personal data, including the right to know and access data collected by public or private entities.
compliance_requirements: Ensure individual data privacy rights
Provide access to personal data upon request
Maintain confidentiality of sensitive employee information
compliance_risk: Constitutional challenge or litigation regarding violation of fundamental rights.
penalty: Constitutional remedies (Amparo), civil damages, and potential administrative sanctions for rights infringement.

law_name: Law on the Protection of Personal Data of a Personal Nature
definition: Governs the integral protection of personal data recorded in physical, electronic, or any other medium, applicable to both public and private entities, including employer-employee databases.
compliance_requirements: Obtain informed and explicit consent for data processing
Implement technical and organizational security measures
Appoint a data protection officer if applicable
Allow employees to exercise ARCO rights (Access, Rectification, Cancellation, Opposition)
Maintain data accuracy and confidentiality
compliance_risk: Significant legal liability, loss of reputation, and regulatory enforcement actions.
penalty: Fines ranging from 1 to 500 minimum wages, depending on the severity of the violation as categorized by ProConsumidor and judicial authorities.
source_reference: Congreso Nacional de la Republica Dominicana

definition: Requires employers to maintain strict confidentiality regarding employee employment records and personal health information disclosed during the course of employment.
compliance_requirements: Store employee records securely
Limit access to HR and Payroll personnel
Prevent unauthorized disclosure of medical or disciplinary history
documentation_required: Internal HR protocols for file access
compliance_risk: Violation of privacy leads to labor lawsuits and claims for moral damages.
penalty: Compensation for damages and potential administrative fines by the Ministry of Labor.

law_name: Labor Code of the Dominican Republic
definition: Sets the general framework for occupational health and safety, requiring employers to take all necessary measures to protect the health, safety, and hygiene of workers.
compliance_requirements: Implement necessary measures for hygiene and safety at work
Adapt facilities to minimize workplace risks
Provide appropriate safety equipment and protective gear
documentation_required: Proof of distribution of personal protective equipment
compliance_risk: Potential for fines, work stoppages, and civil liability in the event of workplace accidents.
penalty: Fines ranging from 3 to 12 minimum wages depending on the severity of the violation.

law_name: Regulation 522-06 on Safety and Health at Work
definition: Detailed regulation defining specific technical standards, risk management, and the obligation to create Health and Safety Committees in companies with more than 15 employees.
compliance_requirements: Establishment of an Occupational Health and Safety Committee
Risk identification and evaluation processes
Periodic training programs for employees
Designation of a safety officer
documentation_required: Occupational Safety and Health Policy document
compliance_risk: Sanctions from the Ministry of Labor during inspections, increased liability in litigation.
penalty: Administrative fines and potential mandatory closure of unsafe work areas.

definition: Covers occupational risks (Seguro de Riesgos Laborales) that protect the worker against work-related accidents and occupational diseases.
compliance_requirements: Mandatory enrollment of all employees in the Occupational Risk Insurance (SRL)
Timely payment of social security contributions
Reporting workplace accidents to the ARSS within specified timeframes
documentation_required: Proof of payment to the Treasury of Social Security (TSS)
Accident reports sent to the SRL administration
compliance_risk: Heavy surcharges and interest on late payments, loss of coverage benefits, and liability to pay medical expenses directly.
penalty: Surcharges of 5% of the debt for the first month and 1% for subsequent months, plus criminal/civil responsibility.

law_name: Labor Code of the Dominican Republic
definition: Regulates the mandatory percentage of Dominican nationals versus foreign workers within an enterprise (80/20 rule).
compliance_requirements: Ensure at least 80% of employees are Dominican nationals.
Ensure that wages paid to Dominicans account for at least 80% of the total payroll.
Obtain specific authorization from the Ministry of Labor for foreign workers.
documentation_required: Copies of national ID (Cédula) for Dominican staff
compliance_risk: Fines, mandatory replacement of foreign staff with local staff, and reputational damage.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 1 to 7 times the minimum wage; potential cancellation of work permits.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo de la República Dominicana

definition: Governs the entry, stay, and work authorization of foreign nationals in the Dominican Republic.
compliance_requirements: Requirement for foreign employees to obtain a Temporary Residence Permit for work purposes.
Sponsorship by a local employer legally registered in the DR.
Payment of required government processing fees.
documentation_required: Work contract certified by the Ministry of Labor
compliance_risk: Deportation of employees, inability to legally employ foreign staff, and closure of operations.
penalty: Fines for the employer and deportation/entry ban for the non-compliant employee.

law_name: Regulation of the General Law on Migration
definition: Provides specific procedural guidance for obtaining work permits for foreign personnel employed by local entities.
compliance_requirements: Filing application with the Migration Department (DGM) after Ministry of Labor approval.
Maintenance of an updated registry of foreign employees.
Submission of biannual reports to the labor authorities.
compliance_risk: Revocation of work authorization status and imposition of financial penalties.
penalty: Daily fines for non-compliance with reporting and administrative sanctions.

definition: Right to equality before the law, prohibiting any discrimination based on gender, color, age, disability, nationality, family ties, language, religion, political or philosophical opinion, social or personal status.
compliance_requirements: Ensure all employment policies are neutral and non-discriminatory.
Prohibit arbitrary exclusions in hiring or promotion processes.
compliance_risk: Constitutional challenge or lawsuit regarding fundamental rights.
penalty: Nullification of discriminatory acts, potential indemnity for moral and material damages.
source_reference: Tribunal Constitucional de la República Dominicana

law_name: Labor Code of the Dominican Republic
definition: Prohibition of discrimination, exclusion, or preference based on race, sex, age, color, civil status, parental status, political opinion, or religious conviction in the workplace.
compliance_requirements: Maintain fair hiring practices.
Equal pay for equal work performance.
Refrain from termination based on discriminatory grounds.
compliance_risk: Labor lawsuits for unjustified dismissal and discrimination claims.
penalty: Fine between 1 to 7 times the minimum wage; potential payment of severance and legal compensation.

law_name: Regulation for the Application of the Labor Code
definition: Provisions ensuring that hiring practices and working conditions are free from discrimination as per the principles set in the Labor Code.
compliance_requirements: Ensure job advertisements do not contain restrictive or discriminatory requirements.
Conduct objective performance evaluations.
compliance_risk: Sanctions by the Ministry of Labor inspectorate.
penalty: Administrative fines determined by the Ministry of Labor.

law_name: Labor Code of the Dominican Republic
definition: The obligation for employers to pay an annual bonus (Salario de Navidad) equivalent to the twelfth part of the ordinary salary earned by the employee during the calendar year.
compliance_requirements: Payment must be made no later than December 20th
It is exempt from income tax, social security, and seizure
It is proportional to the time worked if the employee worked less than a full year
documentation_required: Proof of payment receipt signed by the employee
Internal accounting records for the Ministry of Labor
compliance_risk: Legal claims for non-payment, administrative fines by the Ministry of Labor, and potential labor lawsuits.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 1 to 7 times the minimum wage; legal liability for payment plus interest.
source_reference: Código de Trabajo de la República Dominicana, Ley 16-92

definition: Mandatory employer-employee contribution scheme to the Family Health Insurance (SFS), Pension Fund (AFP), and Occupational Risk Insurance (SRL).
compliance_requirements: Registration of the company and employees with the TSS (Tesorería de la Seguridad Social)
Monthly submission of the Unified Payroll (Planilla de Personal)
Employer must pay their percentage portion of the contributions
compliance_risk: Fines for late payment, inability to provide health coverage, exclusion from government contracts, and criminal liability for evasion.
penalty: Surcharges of 10% for the first month and 5% for each subsequent month of delay, plus legal penalties.
source_reference: Ley 87-01 que crea el Sistema Dominicano de Seguridad Social

definition: Requirement for companies to distribute 10% of their net annual profits to employees who have worked for a period not less than a minimum defined by law.
compliance_requirements: Calculation based on 10% of the net fiscal profit
Verification of the fiscal statement by the Tax Authority (DGII)
Proportional distribution among eligible employees based on salary
documentation_required: Annual Corporate Income Tax Return (Declaración Jurada de IR-2)
compliance_risk: Audits by the Ministry of Labor and the Tax Authority (DGII), mandatory adjustment payments with interest.
penalty: Administrative fines and mandatory payment of the participation share plus potential interest.
source_reference: Código de Trabajo de la República Dominicana, Ley 16-92

definition: Mandatory annual paid vacation leave for employees after completing a year of continuous service.
compliance_requirements: 14 working days for 1-5 years of service
18 working days for over 5 years of service
Vacation must be paid at the start of the period
compliance_risk: Fines by the Ministry of Labor and employee legal claims for accrued vacation time.
penalty: Fines imposed by the Ministry of Labor inspectorate.
source_reference: Código de Trabajo de la República Dominicana, Ley 16-92

law_name: Labor Code of the Dominican Republic
definition: Requires employers to keep a payroll register of employees indicating wages, hours worked, and other relevant information.
compliance_requirements: Maintain a registry of personnel
Record daily attendance
Document salary payments and deductions
compliance_risk: Fines and inability to prove compliance in labor disputes.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 1 to 7 times the minimum wage.

definition: Mandates the registration of employees with the Treasury of Social Security (TSS) and monthly payroll reporting for contributions.
compliance_requirements: Register employer with TSS
Submit monthly payroll via TSS platform
Deduct and remit contributions
compliance_risk: Interest, surcharges, and potential criminal liability for withholding funds.
penalty: Surcharges of 5% per month on late payments and legal action for evasion.

definition: Regulates the processing of personal data, including sensitive payroll and employment information, requiring secure handling.
compliance_requirements: Maintain confidentiality of employee financial data
Limit data processing to employment purposes
Implement technical security measures
compliance_risk: Civil liability and administrative sanctions for data breaches.
penalty: Fines depending on the severity of the violation as determined by the Data Protection Authority.

law_name: Labor Code of the Dominican Republic
definition: Obligation of the employer to register the start and termination of labor contracts and maintain accurate employment records for all personnel.
compliance_requirements: Register employment contracts with the Ministry of Labor
Maintain a payroll register
Notify the Ministry of Labor of terminations within the legal timeframe
compliance_risk: Fines, legal disputes regarding employment start dates, and inability to prove compliance during inspections.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 1 to 7 times the minimum wage per violation.
source_reference: Ministry of Labor - Dominican Republic (Ministerio de Trabajo)

definition: Mandatory requirement to register all employees in the Dominican Social Security System (SDSS) through the Treasury of Social Security (TSS).
compliance_requirements: Enroll employees in the Family Health Insurance (SFS)
Enroll employees in the Pension Fund (AFP)
Enroll employees in the Occupational Risk Insurance (SRL)
compliance_risk: Financial penalties for late enrollment, social security coverage gaps for employees, and investigation by the TSS.
penalty: Administrative fines, surcharges on late payments, and joint liability for medical costs if the employee is not registered.

law_name: Tax Code of the Dominican Republic
definition: Requirement for employers to act as withholding agents and register employees with the Tax Administration (DGII) for Income Tax (ISR) purposes.
compliance_requirements: Obtain Tax ID (RNC) for the employee
File Form IR-13 for new employees
Withhold income tax on monthly payroll
compliance_risk: Tax audits, penalties for non-filing, and inability to deduct payroll expenses for corporate tax purposes.
penalty: Fixed fines per infraction and percentage-based surcharges for late filing or withholding.

definition: Regulates the rights and obligations of employers and employees in a contractual employment relationship.
compliance_requirements: Formalize the contract in writing
Register the contract with the Ministry of Labor within 30 days
Adhere to minimum wage requirements
documentation_required: Registration confirmation from SUT (Sistema Único de Trabajo)
compliance_risk: High; considered an automatic indefinite contract if not registered or written.
penalty: Administrative fines by the Ministry of Labor and potential payment of backdated benefits.
source_reference: Registro Oficial Suplemento 167 de 16 de diciembre de 2005

definition: Mandatory affiliation of all employees to the IESS (Instituto Ecuatoriano de Seguridad Social) from the first day of work.
compliance_requirements: Affiliate employee to IESS
Pay monthly social security contributions (employer + employee share)
Submit notice of entry (Aviso de Entrada) within 15 days
compliance_risk: Severe; potential criminal liability and heavy fines for lack of affiliation.
penalty: Fines, mandatory payment of contributions plus interest, and indemnification responsibilities.
source_reference: Registro Oficial 465 de 30 de noviembre de 2001
