law_code: Ley Orgánica para la Optimización y Eficiencia de Trámites Administrativos
definition: Governs the conditions for remote work arrangements, ensuring the right to disconnect and equality of benefits.
compliance_requirements: Include remote work clause in the contract
Ensure equal rights as on-site employees
Respect the right to digital disconnection
documentation_required: Health and safety risk assessment of the home office
compliance_risk: Moderate; risk of claims regarding working hours and overtime.
penalty: Administrative sanctions by the Ministry of Labor.
source_reference: Registro Oficial Suplemento 353 de 23 de octubre de 2018

law_name: Ley Orgánica de Protección de Datos Personales
definition: Regulates the processing of personal data, including employee personal records held by employers.
compliance_requirements: Implement technical measures for data protection
Obtain consent for data processing where necessary
Appoint a Data Protection Officer if required
compliance_risk: High; significant financial penalties for data breaches.
penalty: Fines of up to 1% of annual turnover.
source_reference: Registro Oficial Suplemento 459 de 26 de mayo de 2021

definition: The formal agreement between employer and employee where services are rendered under dependency in exchange for remuneration.
compliance_requirements: Written contract required for indefinite, fixed-term, and part-time contracts
Registration of the contract with the Ministry of Labor within 30 days of signing
Compliance with mandatory minimum wage and social benefits
compliance_risk: Reclassification of the relationship as indefinite if formal requirements are missing or if the trial period is misused.
penalty: Fines by the Ministry of Labor, potential payment of indemnifications, and mandatory payment of social security contributions with interest.

definition: Legal framework regulating the transformation of labor relations and protection of indefinite contracts.
compliance_requirements: Elimination of fixed-term contracts for tasks that are inherently indefinite
Strict adherence to trial period durations (maximum 90 days)
compliance_risk: Contract invalidation leading to indefinite contract status by default.
penalty: Labor lawsuits, back-pay requirements, and administrative fines.

law_name: Reglamento para el Trabajo a Distancia
definition: Regulates the conditions under which remote work (teletrabajo) must be formalized via written contract addendums.
compliance_requirements: Execution of a written amendment specifying remote work arrangements
Defined working hours and disconnection rights
Provision of equipment and internet connectivity
documentation_required: Health and safety assessment for home workstation
compliance_risk: Labor claims regarding unpaid overtime due to lack of disconnection and unauthorized equipment use.
penalty: Administrative fines by the Ministry of Labor and potential breach of contract lawsuits.

law_name: Ley Orgánica de Protección de Datos Personales
definition: Requires explicit consent for the processing of employee sensitive data collected during contract execution.
compliance_requirements: Obtaining informed consent for data processing
Implementing security protocols for employee files
Designating a data protection officer
compliance_risk: Heavy administrative fines based on company turnover.
penalty: Fines up to 1% of total revenue depending on severity.

definition: Sets the maximum regular working day at 8 hours, for a total of 40 hours per week, Monday through Friday, excluding rest time.
compliance_requirements: Adhere to 8-hour workday limit
Ensure 40-hour work week
Grant at least 2 days of rest per week
compliance_risk: Fines from the Ministry of Labor and payment of unpaid overtime wages.
penalty: Administrative fines up to 20 basic unified salaries or legal proceedings for back wages.

definition: Governs the surcharge for work performed beyond the regular hours or on weekends/holidays, setting surcharges of 50% or 100% depending on the case.
compliance_requirements: Apply 50% surcharge for hours between 06:00 and 24:00 outside regular time
Apply 100% surcharge for hours between 24:00 and 06:00
Apply 100% surcharge for weekends and holidays
compliance_risk: Lawsuits for unpaid overtime and labor inspections resulting in penalties.
penalty: Payment of outstanding surcharges plus interest and potential administrative sanctions.

law_name: Ley Orgánica para la Justicia Laboral y Reconocimiento del Trabajo en el Hogar
definition: Defines night shifts as work performed between 19:00 and 06:00, requiring a 25% surcharge if the night shift is part of the regular schedule.
compliance_requirements: Identify employees on night shifts
Calculate and pay the 25% night shift premium
Update employment contracts regarding shift schedules
compliance_risk: Underpayment of salary and breach of contract.
penalty: Monetary fines and retroactive payment of premiums with legal interest.

definition: Establishes the mandatory obligations of the employer regarding the payment of salaries, contributions, and benefits to the social security system.
compliance_requirements: Register employees at the IESS within 15 days of hiring
Make monthly contributions to the IESS
Pay legal bonuses such as Décimo Tercero and Décimo Cuarto
documentation_required: Proof of payment of social security contributions
compliance_risk: High risk of administrative sanctions, lawsuits, and compulsory social security debt enforcement.
penalty: Fines ranging from 3 to 20 basic unified salaries, plus interest on unpaid social security contributions.

definition: Governs the mandatory affiliation and payment of contributions for all workers in a dependency relationship to the Ecuadorian Social Security Institute (IESS).
compliance_requirements: Deduction of the employee's personal contribution share
Payment of the employer's contribution share
Timely submission of payroll via the IESS website
compliance_risk: Civil liability for lack of insurance and criminal risk if contributions are deducted but not paid to the IESS.
penalty: Interest for late payment, fines, and potential criminal charges for employer misappropriation of funds.

definition: Regulations concerning the withholding of Income Tax (Impuesto a la Renta) from employee salaries.
compliance_requirements: Calculate income tax withholdings based on the employee's annual earnings
Submit withholding declarations to the SRI (Servicio de Rentas Internas)
Issue tax certificates to employees
compliance_risk: Fines from the tax authority (SRI) for incorrect withholding calculations or failure to declare.
penalty: Fines for late filing, interest, and surcharges of up to 100% of the unpaid tax.

law_name: Reglamento para la Aplicación de la Ley de Régimen Tributario Interno
definition: Specific procedural regulations for the calculation and submission of income tax on employment income.
compliance_requirements: Annual reconciliation of income taxes
Electronic submission of reports to SRI
compliance_risk: Audit risk and tax penalties.
penalty: Penalties defined under the Tax Code based on the magnitude of the tax omission.

definition: Regulates the income tax withholdings that employers must perform on employee salaries and wages.
compliance_requirements: Calculate monthly income tax withholdings for employees
Retain the corresponding tax amount from the payroll
Declare and pay withheld amounts to the SRI
Submit annual tax reconciliation information
documentation_required: Formulario 107 (Retenciones en la fuente de Impuesto a la Renta)
Proyección de gastos personales del empleado
compliance_risk: Imposition of fines and interest for late or incorrect reporting and tax evasion penalties.
penalty: Monetary fines based on the value of the tax not withheld or not declared, plus interest and potential legal action.
source_reference: Servicio de Rentas Internas (SRI) - Ley de Régimen Tributario Interno

definition: Legal mandate requiring employers to withhold and deposit taxes and social security contributions from employee earnings.
compliance_requirements: Withhold social security (IESS) contributions
Withhold applicable income taxes
Ensure timely transfer of funds to state institutions
compliance_risk: Labor lawsuits, administrative sanctions by the Ministry of Labor, and inability to register new personnel.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 3 to 20 basic unified salaries, plus legal liability for non-payment of social security.
source_reference: Ministerio del Trabajo - Código del Trabajo

definition: Governs the employer's obligation to contribute and withhold percentages for the Ecuadorian Social Security Institute (IESS).
compliance_requirements: Register employees within 15 days of hiring
Monthly payment of employer and employee social security contributions
Report all changes in salary or termination
compliance_risk: Loss of civil rights, denial of public tenders, and mandatory interest on delayed contributions.
penalty: Fines, surcharges (mora patronal), and potential criminal charges if employee deductions are withheld but not transferred.

definition: Provides for 15 days of consecutive annual vacation leave, including non-working days, for employees who have completed one year of service with the same employer.
compliance_requirements: Grant 15 days of continuous vacation after one year of service
Pay for vacation time at the regular salary rate
Increase vacation days based on seniority beyond 5 years of service
documentation_required: Employee signature on vacation request/notice forms
Payroll records proving payment of vacation leave
compliance_risk: Imposition of fines by the Ministry of Labor and potential lawsuits for unpaid benefits.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 3 to 20 basic unified salaries.
source_reference: Ministerio del Trabajo - Codigo del Trabajo

definition: Mandates the payment of the Thirteenth Month Salary (Decimo Tercer Sueldo), equivalent to one-twelfth of total income received during the calculation period (December 1 to November 30).
compliance_requirements: Calculate and pay the benefit before December 24 of each year
Ensure the payment is reflected in payroll records
Allow employees to opt for monthly accumulation if requested in writing
documentation_required: Written request for monthly accumulation (if applicable)
compliance_risk: Labor inspection sanctions and potential claims for interest on delayed payments.
penalty: Administrative fines and mandatory payment of interest arrears.
source_reference: Ministerio del Trabajo - Codigo del Trabajo

definition: Mandates the payment of the Fourteenth Month Salary (Decimo Cuarto Sueldo), equivalent to one basic unified salary, paid once a year for education expenses.
compliance_requirements: Pay one full basic unified salary to all employees regardless of position
Payment periods depend on the region (Costa/Insular or Sierra/Amazonia)
Allow accumulation of the benefit if requested by the employee
documentation_required: Application forms for accumulation of benefits
compliance_risk: Sanctions for failure to pay within the legal deadline for the specific region.
penalty: Fines determined by the Ministry of Labor under the labor inspection regulation.
source_reference: Ministerio del Trabajo - Codigo del Trabajo

definition: Regulates maternity and paternity leave, including medical leave periods covered by the IESS (Ecuadorian Social Security Institute).
compliance_requirements: Grant 12 weeks of maternity leave
Grant 10 days of paternity leave
Ensure job security during the leave period
Process leaves through the IESS platform
documentation_required: Internal record of leave start and end dates
compliance_risk: Discriminatory firing claims and heavy fines for violating protection of pregnant workers.
penalty: Legal reincorporation of the employee and payment of indemnity and fines.

law_name: Reglamento para el Trabajo a Distancia
definition: Regulates the hiring and conditions for employees working remotely, ensuring rights remain equal to on-site employees.
compliance_requirements: Include a remote work clause in the contract
Define tools and equipment provisions
Ensure the right to disconnect
documentation_required: Remote work addendum to the contract
compliance_risk: Legal challenges regarding working hours and overtime payments for remote staff.
penalty: Administrative fines from the Ministry of Labor.

law_name: Ley Orgánica de Protección de Datos Personales
definition: Regulates the collection and processing of personal data of employees during the hiring and employment lifecycle.
compliance_requirements: Obtain informed consent for data processing
Limit data collection to strictly professional requirements
Implement security measures to protect employee records
compliance_risk: Heavy administrative fines for mishandling sensitive employee personal information.
penalty: Fines up to 1% of total turnover of the previous year.

definition: Establishes the presumption of an employment relationship whenever there is subordination, personal service, and remuneration, regardless of the contract title.
compliance_requirements: Ensure the reality of the work relationship matches the contract type.
Avoid using civil service contracts for roles that require permanent subordination.
documentation_required: Proof of payment of social security
compliance_risk: Reclassification of contractor as employee by labor authorities or judiciary, leading to retroactive benefits and penalties.
penalty: Payment of all unpaid social security contributions, statutory benefits, fines, and legal costs.

definition: Mandates compulsory affiliation for all individuals performing work under a relationship of dependency.
compliance_requirements: Register all personnel under labor dependency with the IESS (Instituto Ecuatoriano de Seguridad Social).
Distinguish between independent contractors (Service Provider - Prestación de Servicios) and employees.
documentation_required: Service contracts (Contratos de Prestación de Servicios)
RUC (Registro Único de Contribuyentes) of the contractor
compliance_risk: Audits by IESS resulting in mandatory affiliation and fines for failing to pay employer contributions.
penalty: Payment of retroactive contributions plus interest and surcharges (mora patronal).

definition: Regulates the forms of labor contracts and establishes criteria for professional service agreements that do not constitute employment.
compliance_requirements: Strictly avoid fixed schedules for independent contractors.
Ensure contractors use their own tools and assets.
Avoid exercising direct supervision over the contractor's internal processes.
documentation_required: Specific service provision contracts defining deliverables rather than hours.
Tax invoices compliant with SRI (Servicio de Rentas Internas) regulations.
compliance_risk: Ministry of Labor inspection finding disguised employment (Simulación Laboral).
penalty: Administrative fines imposed by the Ministry of Labor and potential lawsuits for worker compensation.

definition: Specifies the legal causes for the termination of an employment contract, including expiration, mutual agreement, death, liquidation, or employer decision.
compliance_requirements: Notify Ministry of Labor
Sign final settlement
Pay proportional benefits
documentation_required: Ministry of Labor registration of termination
compliance_risk: Lawsuits for unfair dismissal
penalty: Indemnity payments for unfair dismissal ranging from 3 to 20 months of salary

definition: Mandates the payment of severance (desahucio) when the employer terminates the contract or the employee resigns.
compliance_requirements: Pay 25% of the last monthly salary per year of service to the employee
Calculate proportional time for fractions of a year
compliance_risk: Fines by labor authorities for non-payment
penalty: Payment of outstanding amounts plus interest and potential legal action

definition: Provides specific indemnity rules for workers terminated by the employer without just cause.
compliance_requirements: Payment of legal indemnification based on years of service
compliance_risk: High economic risk due to penalty indemnities
penalty: Indemnity equal to 25% of the last remuneration for each year of service

definition: Employer obligation to report the end of the employment relationship to the IESS (Ecuadorian Social Security Institute).
compliance_requirements: Register the 'Aviso de Salida' in the IESS system within 3 days of termination
documentation_required: Proof of payment of social security contributions
compliance_risk: Accumulation of debt with IESS
penalty: Fines and interest charges for late registration or failure to pay

definition: Requirement to maintain written employment contracts for all workers, excluding specific cases allowed by law.
compliance_requirements: Register the employment contract with the Ministry of Labor (SUT platform)
Maintain a physical or digital copy of the signed contract
Keep the contract available for labor inspection
compliance_risk: Fines, legal presumption of an indefinite-term contract, and potential claims of labor instability.
penalty: Administrative fines imposed by the Ministry of Labor ranging from 3 to 20 basic salaries.

law_name: Reglamento para la Aplicación de la Ley de Régimen Tributario Interno
definition: Requirement to maintain payroll documentation and tax withholding records for employees subject to Income Tax (Impuesto a la Renta).
compliance_requirements: Maintain records of tax withholdings
Issue Form 107 (Annual tax certification) to employees
File tax withholding reports to SRI
compliance_risk: Tax evasion audits, fines from the tax authority (SRI), and disallowance of tax expenses.
penalty: Monetary fines based on the severity and duration of the infraction as defined by the Tax Code.

law_name: Reglamento de Seguridad y Salud de los Trabajadores
definition: Obligation to maintain comprehensive workplace health and safety documentation and risk prevention records.
compliance_requirements: Appoint a safety officer or committee
Draft and register the Health and Safety Internal Regulations
Maintain incident and accident registers
compliance_risk: Heavy administrative fines and potential closure of workplaces if safety standards are not met.
penalty: Fines ranging from 3 to 20 basic unified salaries per infraction.

definition: Regulates the legal relationship between employer and employee, establishing the types of contracts (indefinite, fixed-term, etc.).
compliance_requirements: Written contract registration with the Ministry of Labor
Probation period limits
Adherence to maximum working hours
documentation_required: SUT (Sistema Unico de Trabajo) registration records
compliance_risk: Reclassification of temporary contracts into indefinite contracts
penalty: Fines up to 20 basic salaries and mandatory payment of all social benefits

law_name: Ley Organica de Proteccion de Datos Personales
definition: Regulates the processing and handling of personal data of employees collected by employers.
compliance_requirements: Implementation of data security policies
Obtaining explicit consent for non-essential data processing
Appointment of a data protection officer
compliance_risk: Sanctions for data breaches or unauthorized disclosure of employee data
penalty: Fines ranging up to 1% of the annual turnover

law_name: Ley Organica de Trabajo a Domicilio y Teletrabajo
definition: Governs the conditions and rights for employees performing remote work or telework.
compliance_requirements: Registration of telework contract in SUT
Provision of equipment and internet reimbursement
Right to disconnect
documentation_required: Evidence of digital work tools provided
compliance_risk: Litigation regarding 'Right to Disconnect' violations
penalty: Administrative sanctions by the Ministry of Labor

law_name: Constitucion de la Republica del Ecuador
definition: Guarantees the right to work, fair compensation, and non-discrimination.
compliance_requirements: Zero discrimination in hiring or promotion
Fair wages
Right to unionize
compliance_risk: Constitutional protective actions (Acciones de Proteccion)
penalty: Reparation orders, public apologies, and damages

law_name: Constitution of the Republic of Ecuador
definition: Recognizes the right to collective bargaining and the right to strike as fundamental labor rights.
compliance_requirements: Guarantee the right of workers to organize and negotiate working conditions
Recognize collective bargaining agreements as binding legal instruments
compliance_risk: Violation of constitutional rights, risk of constitutional litigation and nullification of labor policies.
penalty: Civil liability, administrative sanctions, and potential international labor complaints.

definition: Regulates collective agreements, specifying procedures for negotiation, content, and the legal effects of agreements between employers and worker associations.
compliance_requirements: Submit collective agreement to the Ministry of Labor for registration
Negotiate in good faith with representative unions
Ensure agreement does not diminish minimum legal labor benefits
documentation_required: Registration certificate issued by the Ministry of Labor
compliance_risk: Imposition of fines by the Ministry of Labor, labor strikes, and legal invalidity of non-registered agreements.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 3 to 20 unified basic salaries (SBUB).

law_name: Ministry of Labor - Regulations on Collective Bargaining
definition: Defines technical requirements and procedures for the registration and digital filing of collective agreements with the Ministry of Labor.
compliance_requirements: Use the Ministry's online system (SUT) for registry
Submit the agreement within 15 days of signing
Verify union representation via official channels
documentation_required: Pdf copy of the signed collective contract
Certificate of the labor union's current board
compliance_risk: Rejection of the collective agreement by the labor authorities and loss of tax deductibility for certain benefits.
penalty: Refusal to register which renders the agreement unenforceable against the employer or state authorities.

definition: Regulates the formation, operation, and legal protection of industrial unions and workers associations.
compliance_requirements: Provide paid leave for union leaders
Do not dismiss union founders without judicial approval
Negotiate collective bargaining agreements in good faith
documentation_required: Lists of protected union board members
compliance_risk: Lawsuits for anti-union practices and illegal dismissal of protected union representatives.
penalty: Fines from the Ministry of Labor and mandatory reinstatement of unionized workers.

definition: Sets the procedure for registration and deposit of collective bargaining agreements at the Ministry of Labor.
compliance_requirements: Register collective agreements within 15 days of signing
Update employee lists for union representation
Apply agreed-upon wage increases
documentation_required: Minutes of approval by the Ministry
compliance_risk: Non-recognition of benefits by the Ministry and risk of labor strikes.
penalty: Administrative fines from the Ministry of Labor.

definition: Regulates the teleworking modality as a form of labor performance where the worker does not need to be physically present at the employer's premises.
compliance_requirements: Formalize the change to remote work via an addendum to the employment contract
Provide necessary equipment and tools for the performance of duties
Ensure the right to disconnect for the employee
Maintain the same working conditions and benefits as onsite employees
documentation_required: Written agreement/contract addendum specifying the remote arrangement
compliance_risk: Reclassification of remote work to standard employment with potential claims for overtime or unrecognized allowances.
penalty: Fines ranging from 3 to 20 unified basic salaries (SBU) as per the Labor Code for contractual violations.

definition: Technical regulation for the implementation of teleworking in the public and private sector to regulate rights and obligations of the parties.
compliance_requirements: Definition of working hours and communication channels
Establishment of cybersecurity protocols for remote access
Assessment of ergonomic and safety conditions in the employee's home
Proportionate payment of expenses incurred by the worker
documentation_required: Teleworking agreement registered with the Ministry of Labor
compliance_risk: Sanctions for failing to register the teleworking agreement with the Ministry of Labor system (SUT).
penalty: Administrative fines imposed by the Ministry of Labor and potential Labor Inspectorate audit penalties.

law_name: Ley Orgánica de Protección de Datos Personales
definition: Regulates the treatment of personal data of employees processed through digital means in a remote work environment.
compliance_requirements: Implement technical measures to protect employee personal data on remote devices
Provide privacy notices regarding monitoring software or performance tracking
Ensure data processing is limited to professional purposes
compliance_risk: Exposure of sensitive corporate or employee data; violation of constitutional privacy rights.
penalty: Fines of up to 1% of the annual turnover of the company for serious infractions.

law_name: Ley Orgánica de Protección de Datos Personales
definition: The law regulating the processing of personal data, establishing rights for data subjects and obligations for data controllers and processors, including the management of employee records.
compliance_requirements: Obtain explicit and informed consent from employees for data processing
Implement technical and organizational security measures
Perform Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIA)
Appoint a Data Protection Officer (DPO) if required
Maintain a registry of data processing activities
documentation_required: Contracts with third-party service providers (data processors)
compliance_risk: Unauthorized access, data leaks, improper storage of sensitive employee financial or health information.
penalty: Administrative fines up to 1% of the annual turnover of the previous fiscal year; reputational damage; suspension of data processing activities.

law_name: Constitución de la República del Ecuador
definition: Constitutional guarantee of the right to personal data protection, including access to and the decision on information and data of a personal nature.
compliance_requirements: Respect the fundamental right of employees to control their personal data
Ensure data is collected for specific, legitimate purposes
Guarantee the accuracy and confidentiality of employee information
documentation_required: Internal privacy guidelines aligning with constitutional mandates
compliance_risk: Constitutional lawsuits (Acción de Protección) filed by employees.
penalty: Constitutional remedies, nullification of acts, potential indemnification for damages.
source_reference: Constitución de la República del Ecuador

definition: Requirements regarding the confidentiality and protection of information gained by the employer during the employment relationship.
compliance_requirements: Protect sensitive employee information contained in personnel files
Ensure payroll data and social security information is handled securely
Limit data access to authorized HR personnel only
documentation_required: Internal labor regulations detailing data handling
compliance_risk: Violation of the duty of custody regarding employee personal files.
penalty: Labor fines, potential claims for moral damages, breach of contract sanctions.

law_name: Instrumento Andino de Seguridad y Salud en el Trabajo
definition: A regional framework establishing basic guidelines for the prevention of occupational hazards and the promotion of health and safety in the workplace for Andean Community member countries.
compliance_requirements: Implement a comprehensive occupational health and safety system
Perform periodic risk assessments
Provide appropriate personal protective equipment
compliance_risk: Legal sanctions and suspension of operations in cases of serious risk to worker integrity.
penalty: Administrative fines determined by national authorities based on the severity of the violation.

law_name: Reglamento de Seguridad y Salud de los Trabajadores y Mejoramiento del Medio Ambiente de Trabajo
definition: The primary regulatory framework in Ecuador governing the technical and administrative requirements for safety, industrial hygiene, and health in the workplace.
compliance_requirements: Establishment of a Joint Health and Safety Committee (Comité Paritario)
Appointment of a Health and Safety Manager
Registration of Health and Safety Regulation with the Ministry of Labor
documentation_required: Internal Regulation of Health and Safety
compliance_risk: High risk of administrative penalties and civil liability in the event of workplace accidents.
penalty: Fines ranging from 3 to 20 Basic Unified Salaries depending on the severity and recurrence of the infraction.

definition: Sets the foundational obligation for employers to ensure safe working conditions and protect the life and health of workers.
compliance_requirements: Maintain adequate safety equipment
Ensure ventilation and lighting standards
Provide first aid services
compliance_risk: Employers held responsible for occupational accidents; potential criminal liability in case of negligence.
penalty: Fines and potential indemnity payments for professional risks and disabilities.

law_name: Resolución del IESS - Seguro General de Riesgos del Trabajo
definition: Regulations governing the mandatory insurance coverage for work-related accidents and occupational diseases provided by the Ecuadorian Social Security Institute (IESS).
compliance_requirements: Report all workplace accidents to IESS within 10 days
Monthly payment of employer contributions for occupational risk coverage
compliance_risk: Fines for failing to report accidents; liability for medical costs if the employer fails to register the risk.
penalty: Economic fines imposed by IESS and potential litigation for damages.

definition: Regulates the entry, stay, and work activities of foreign nationals in Ecuador, requiring specific visa categories for employment-based activities.
compliance_requirements: Obtain proper work visa (Visa de Residencia Temporal de Trabajo) before commencing employment
Maintain registration with the Ministry of Labor
Ensure foreign employee holds valid documentation throughout the contract period
Notify the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Human Mobility of any change in employment status
documentation_required: Contract of employment approved by the Ministry of Labor
Criminal record certificate from country of origin
Affiliation certificate to the Ecuadorian Social Security Institute (IESS)
compliance_risk: Imposition of heavy fines, deportation of the foreign employee, and potential blacklisting of the employer entity for future visa sponsorships.
penalty: Economic fines ranging from 1 to 5 Unified Basic Salaries per violation; potential suspension of business activities.

definition: Mandates that employers must ensure foreign employees hold the necessary authorizations to work in Ecuador to comply with domestic labor protection laws.
compliance_requirements: Verify visa status prior to signing the employment contract
Register the contract with the Ministry of Labor via the SUT (Sistema Único de Trabajo)
Comply with the quota limit (maximum 20% of the total workforce must be foreign nationals)
documentation_required: Ministry of Labor contract registration certificate
compliance_risk: Nullification of the employment contract, inability to deduct salary for tax purposes, and penalties for illegal hiring of unauthorized labor.
penalty: Administrative fines by the Ministry of Labor and mandatory regularization of the worker.

law_name: Reglamento a la Ley Orgánica de Movilidad Humana
definition: Specifies the administrative procedures for the issuance of temporary and permanent residence visas for work purposes.
compliance_requirements: Sponsorship of the employee's visa application by the local employer
Commitment to report employment termination to the Migration Authority within 30 days
Financial solvency proof for the sponsoring entity
documentation_required: Letter of sponsorship from the employer
Proof of registration of the employer with the Superintendency of Companies or equivalent
compliance_risk: Incomplete immigration records leading to administrative hold on company transactions or corporate visa sponsorships.
penalty: Administrative sanction for the legal representative and potential permanent refusal of visa issuance for that company.

law_name: Constitution of the Republic of Ecuador
law_code: Constitucion de la Republica del Ecuador
definition: Prohibits all forms of discrimination, whether based on ethnicity, place of birth, age, gender, sexual orientation, identity, religion, political affiliation, or disability.
compliance_requirements: Implement non-discriminatory hiring practices
Ensure equal pay for equal work
Maintain a workspace free from harassment
compliance_risk: Constitutional violation leading to legal invalidation of company policies and potential public litigation.
penalty: Constitutional protection actions (Acción de Protección) and damages.

definition: Obligation of the employer to pay the employee the salaries and indemnities that are due, and to treat the employee with the necessary consideration, abstaining from mistreatment of words or deeds.
compliance_requirements: Prohibition of physical or verbal mistreatment
Ensuring professional respect in the workplace
documentation_required: Internal Regulation of Labor (Reglamento Interno de Trabajo)
Proof of training on workplace harassment prevention
compliance_risk: Constructive dismissal claims due to discriminatory behavior or workplace abuse.
penalty: Payment of severance and indemnities for unfair dismissal, plus fines from the Ministry of Labor.
source_reference: Registro Oficial Suplemento 167 de 16 de diciembre de 2005

law_name: Organic Law on Labor Justice and Recognition of Household Work
law_code: Ley Organica para la Justicia Laboral y Reconocimiento del Trabajo del Hogar
definition: Strict prohibition of discriminatory practices in hiring and labor relations based on personal characteristics or family status.
compliance_requirements: Standardized recruitment processes
Removal of discriminatory job advertisement requirements
compliance_risk: Administrative fines imposed by the Ministry of Labor for discriminatory practices.
penalty: Fines ranging from 3 to 20 basic unified salaries.
source_reference: Registro Oficial Suplemento 483, 20 de abril de 2015

definition: A mandatory additional remuneration equal to one-twelfth of the total remuneration received by the worker during the calendar year.
compliance_requirements: Payment must be made no later than December 24th of each year.
Proportional calculation for employees who worked less than a full year.
documentation_required: Proof of payment (transfer receipts or signed payroll sheets)
compliance_risk: Fines by the Ministry of Labor and mandatory payment of interest on late sums.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 3 to 20 basic unified salaries.

definition: A mandatory bonus equivalent to one unified basic salary for workers in the Costa/Galápagos region or Sierra/Amazonia region depending on the school calendar.
compliance_requirements: Payment based on the current unified basic salary.
Must be paid in full unless the employee requests monthly pro-rated payment.
documentation_required: Written request for monthly payment if applicable
compliance_risk: Sanctions for failing to pay the bonus in the designated month for each region.
penalty: Administrative fines imposed by the Ministry of Labor.

law_name: Labor Code - Participation of Workers in Profits
definition: The requirement for companies to share 15% of their net profits with all employees.
compliance_requirements: 10% distributed directly to workers.
5% distributed based on the number of dependents (spouse/children).
Must be paid within 15 days of the date the income tax declaration is due.
compliance_risk: Legal action by the Ministry of Labor and potential tax audits.
penalty: Fines and mandatory payment of the outstanding 15% plus legal interest.

definition: Mandatory employer contributions to the IESS (Ecuadorian Social Security Institute) to fund health, pension, and disability benefits.
compliance_requirements: Register employee with IESS from day one.
Calculate and pay contributions monthly based on the employee's salary.
Upload payroll and payment notices to the IESS portal.
compliance_risk: Employer liability for all medical and pension costs if not registered.
penalty: Heavy fines, interest on late payments, and potential legal lawsuits from employees.

definition: Mandates employer obligations regarding the payment of salaries, social security contributions, and maintenance of employment records.
compliance_requirements: Pay employees at least monthly
Maintain current payroll records
Report salary changes to the Ministry of Labor
compliance_risk: High; labor disputes and administrative sanctions.
penalty: Fines from 3 to 20 unified basic salaries (SBU) or potential lawsuits.

definition: Regulates the mandatory registration of employees with the IESS (Instituto Ecuatoriano de Seguridad Social) and the payment of monthly contributions based on total remuneration.
compliance_requirements: Register employees within 15 days of hiring
Report monthly payroll contributions (Aportes)
Submit 'Planillas' via IESS portal
compliance_risk: Extreme; includes interest, fines, and legal liability for unpaid benefits.
penalty: Fines up to 50% of the contribution amount, late interest, and potential criminal charges.

definition: Governs the tax withholding obligations of employers on employee salaries and the reporting requirements to the SRI (Servicio de Rentas Internas).
compliance_requirements: Calculate income tax withholding (Impuesto a la Renta)
Submit monthly withholding tax returns (Formulario 103)
Provide annual tax certification (Formulario 107)
compliance_risk: High; tax evasion penalties and interest.
penalty: Fines based on late filing, audit discrepancies, and interest on unpaid tax.

definition: Specific requirements for reporting payroll information through the Ministry of Labor's SUT (Sistema Único de Trabajo) platform.
compliance_requirements: Upload contracts to SUT
Register payroll changes in the SUT system
Submit the annual workers' profit sharing report (Utilidades)
compliance_risk: Moderate; administrative fines for failing to update the digital system.
penalty: Administrative fines up to 20 SBU.

definition: Mandates the obligation of the employer to keep a register of employees and notify the Ministry of Labor of all contracts and their modifications.
compliance_requirements: Register employment contracts via the SUT (Sistema Único de Trabajo)
Update the SUT platform within 30 days of any change
Maintain current employment records
compliance_risk: Sanctions for non-registration, labor administrative fines, and presumption of indefinite contract.
penalty: Fines ranging from 3 to 20 unified basic salaries depending on the severity and number of affected employees.

law_name: Ley Orgánica de Protección de Datos Personales
definition: Regulates the processing and storage of employee personal data during the registration process to ensure privacy rights are respected.
compliance_requirements: Obtain informed consent for data processing
Implement security measures for employee records
Appoint a data protection officer
compliance_risk: Significant administrative fines based on a percentage of the company's annual turnover.
penalty: Fines up to 1% of the annual turnover, reputational damage, and mandatory remediation.
source_reference: Autoridad de Protección de Datos Personales

law_name: Reglamento para el Registro de Contratos
definition: Specific regulatory framework governing the mandatory use of the SUT platform for the registration of labor relations.
compliance_requirements: Upload all types of labor contracts to the SUT
Register termination of contracts
Register collective bargaining agreements
compliance_risk: Administrative closure of employer account on SUT, resulting in inability to process labor legalities.
penalty: Monetary fines and administrative suspension from the SUT platform.

law_name: Constitution of the Republic of El Salvador
definition: Establishes the fundamental rights of workers, including the right to work, fair wages, limits on working hours, and the right to form unions.
compliance_requirements: Guarantee fair wages.
Respect the right to form trade unions.
Adhere to maximum working hours.
compliance_risk: Constitutional violation, potential lawsuits, and nullification of labor agreements.
penalty: Civil damages, constitutional injunctions, and state-mandated corrections.

definition: The primary legislation governing individual and collective labor relations in the private sector.
compliance_requirements: Execute written employment contracts.
Maintain internal labor regulations.
Provide mandatory rest periods and vacations.
compliance_risk: Fines by the Ministry of Labor, labor claims for severance, and back pay.
penalty: Monetary fines based on minimum wage, requirement to pay back wages, potential closure of business.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Previsión Social

definition: Governs the mandatory social security contributions for health, disability, and retirement coverage for workers.
compliance_requirements: Register employer and employees with the ISSS.
Make monthly contributions (employer/employee split).
Report all changes in personnel.
compliance_risk: Accumulated debt with the social security institute, fines, and denial of employee benefits.
penalty: Surcharges on unpaid contributions, potential legal action for debt recovery.

law_code: Ley del Sistema de Ahorro para Pensiones
definition: Regulates the mandatory individual pension savings accounts for employees.
compliance_requirements: Withhold pension contributions from salaries.
Pay employer portion of contributions.
Submit monthly pension reports to AFPs.
compliance_risk: Accusations of misappropriation of funds and administrative penalties.
penalty: Fines for late payment and mandatory interest accrual.

law_code: Ley del Impuesto sobre la Renta
definition: Regulates the withholding and payment of income tax on employee salaries.
compliance_requirements: Withhold income tax (ISR) from employees earning above taxable thresholds.
File annual tax withholding statements.
compliance_risk: Tax evasion audits, severe monetary penalties.
penalty: Fines calculated as a percentage of unpaid taxes, interest, and legal investigation.

definition: Regulates the conditions for remote work arrangements between employer and employee.
compliance_requirements: Written agreement for telework.
Specify tools and equipment provided by the employer.
Ensure data privacy and security.
compliance_risk: Violation of health and safety standards in the home office, labor disputes regarding working hours.
penalty: Labor fines or nullification of the telework agreement.

definition: Regulates the nature, form, and conditions of individual employment contracts, establishing that a contract exists when there is a provision of service under subordination for a salary.
compliance_requirements: Formalize the contract in writing in at least two copies
Specify the nature of the services
Define the working hours and workplace
Stipulate the salary, method of payment, and periodicity
Include the duration of the contract (definite or indefinite)
compliance_risk: Presumption that all conditions claimed by the employee are true if no written contract exists.
penalty: Fines ranging from $57.14 to $571.43 per worker depending on the severity of the infringement.
source_reference: Asamblea Legislativa de El Salvador - Código de Trabajo

law_name: Ley Reguladora del Trabajo a Domicilio
definition: Governs employment relationships where work is performed outside the employer's premises, often at the employee's home or a remote location.
compliance_requirements: Must formalize specific remote working conditions in writing
Maintain the same labor rights as office-based employees
Define tools and resources provided by the employer
documentation_required: Remote work addendum to the employment contract
compliance_risk: Reclassification of remote workers as independent contractors if subordination criteria are not met, leading to labor lawsuits.
penalty: Administrative fines imposed by the Ministry of Labor and Social Welfare (MTPS).

law_name: Constitución de la República de El Salvador
definition: Establishes that labor is a social function and protects the rights of workers to enter into employment contracts, receive fair wages, and form unions.
compliance_requirements: Ensure equal pay for equal work
Guarantee a minimum wage
Respect the freedom of association
compliance_risk: Constitutional violations can lead to nullification of contract clauses and heavy litigation.
penalty: Sanctions derived from civil and labor liability.
source_reference: Constitución de la República de El Salvador

law_name: Ley de Protección de Datos Personales
definition: Governs the collection, processing, and storage of personal information of employees during the employment lifecycle.
compliance_requirements: Obtain explicit consent for processing employee data
Maintain confidentiality of employee health and financial records
Implement security measures for HR databases
compliance_risk: Data breaches and unlawful disclosure of sensitive employee information.
penalty: Administrative sanctions and potential civil damages.

definition: Mandates the registration of employees in the ISSS (Instituto Salvadoreño del Seguro Social) starting from the date of the employment contract.
compliance_requirements: Register all new employees within 5 days of hiring
Deduct and remit mandatory social security contributions
Report all changes in employee status
compliance_risk: Fines for lack of registration and liability for medical costs if an unregistered employee suffers an accident.
penalty: Fixed fines per employee plus surcharges for late payments.

definition: Sets the maximum daily and weekly working hours, defining ordinary work vs. overtime and the mandatory rest periods.
compliance_requirements: Maximum daytime working hours set at 8 per day and 44 per week
Maximum nighttime working hours set at 7 per day and 39 per week
Payment of overtime at a premium rate of 100% (double) of the hourly rate
Provision of a minimum 45-minute meal break during the shift
documentation_required: Payroll receipts showing itemized overtime payments
Internal work regulations approved by the Ministry of Labor
compliance_risk: High; labor inspection audits often focus on overtime payments and record-keeping.
penalty: Fines ranging from 1 to 3 minimum monthly wages per affected worker, depending on the severity and company size.
source_reference: Asamblea Legislativa de El Salvador, Código de Trabajo

law_name: Constitución de la República de El Salvador
definition: Establishes the constitutional right of workers to a maximum work day and rest periods.
compliance_requirements: Respect the limits of the workday
Ensure mandatory weekly rest periods
Provide paid holidays
documentation_required: Evidence of compliance with Labor Code standards
compliance_risk: Constitutional labor rights violations can lead to nullification of employment clauses and legal claims.
penalty: Damages, legal settlements, and potential administrative sanctions by the Ministry of Labor.
source_reference: Constitución de la República de El Salvador

law_name: Ley Reguladora del Trabajo a Domicilio y Teletrabajo
definition: Governs working hours and connectivity rights for employees performing remote work.
compliance_requirements: Respect the right to digital disconnection outside of agreed working hours
Maintain clear time-tracking mechanisms for remote tasks
Agreement on work schedules between employer and teleworker
documentation_required: Attendance records for remote login times
compliance_risk: Risk of claims regarding unpaid overtime if disconnection rights are violated or hours are poorly tracked.
penalty: Administrative fines under the Labor Code and potential civil liability.

definition: Mandatory social security regime covering sickness, maternity, disability, old age, and death benefits for workers.
compliance_requirements: Register employer with ISSS
Register all employees within 5 days of hiring
Withhold and remit employer and employee contributions monthly
Maintain current payroll records
compliance_risk: Fines, surcharges on late payments, and legal liability for healthcare costs if registration is omitted.
penalty: Monetary fines ranging from 20% to 100% of the owed contribution, plus interest.

law_name: Ley del Sistema de Ahorro para Pensiones
definition: Regulates the mandatory pension savings system where employers and employees contribute to individual accounts managed by Pension Fund Administrators (AFP).
compliance_requirements: Withhold employee pension contribution from salary
Pay employer pension contribution percentage
Remit total funds to designated AFP by the 10th of each month
compliance_risk: Civil and criminal liability for misappropriation of funds; significant fines.
penalty: Surcharges of 5% per month of delay, up to 100% of the total debt, plus potential criminal investigation.

definition: Governs wages, salary payments, bonuses (Aguinaldo), and mandatory payroll calculations.
compliance_requirements: Payment of salary at agreed intervals (maximum 15 days for manual workers)
Proper calculation of overtime, night shifts, and bonuses
Issue payroll receipts signed by the employee
compliance_risk: Labor lawsuits, fines by the Ministry of Labor, and mandatory back-pay penalties.
penalty: Fines per violation, subject to the Labor Inspectorate's discretion.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Previsión Social (MTPS)

law_name: Ley del Impuesto sobre la Renta
definition: Establishes the obligation to withhold income tax (Impuesto sobre la Renta) from employee salaries based on the established progressive tax tables.
compliance_requirements: Calculate withholding tax on gross salary
Declare and pay withheld taxes to the Ministry of Finance monthly
Issue annual income tax certificates (constancia de retenciones) to employees
compliance_risk: Tax audits, tax evasion penalties, and interest on unpaid amounts.
penalty: Monetary fines based on tax debt percentage, plus interest and potential legal action.
source_reference: Ministerio de Hacienda (Dirección General de Impuestos Internos)

law_name: Ley del Impuesto sobre la Renta
definition: Regulates the income tax applicable to individuals and legal entities, including withholding obligations for employers regarding employee salaries.
compliance_requirements: Calculate, withhold, and pay income tax on salaries.
Submit annual income tax statements.
Provide employees with withholding certificates.
compliance_risk: Fines, interest, surcharges, and potential legal prosecution for tax evasion.
penalty: Monetary fines based on a percentage of the omitted tax, plus interest for late payments.
source_reference: Ministerio de Hacienda de El Salvador

definition: Governs the obligation of employers to report and withhold social security and related mandatory payroll contributions.
compliance_requirements: Report all employees to the ISSS (Social Security) and AFP (Pension Fund).
Deduct mandatory employee contributions from payroll.
Pay employer portion of contributions.
compliance_risk: Heavy sanctions by the Ministry of Labor and the Social Security Institute (ISSS).
penalty: Daily fines per affected employee and mandatory payment of social security debt plus surcharges.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Previsión Social

law_name: Ley del Sistema de Ahorro para Pensiones
definition: Mandates the contribution to individual pension accounts for employees, which must be deducted at the source by the employer.
compliance_requirements: Deduct pension contributions from employee gross salary.
Remit employee and employer portions to the Pension Fund Administrator (AFP).
Submit monthly payroll reports to the AFP.
compliance_risk: Civil and criminal liability for failure to remit collected employee pension funds.
penalty: Monetary fines and possible prison sentence for misappropriation of funds.

definition: The right of an employee to a paid vacation period after one year of continuous service.
compliance_requirements: Grant 15 days of paid vacation after one year of service
Pay vacation pay in advance of the leave period
Calculate vacation based on the average salary earned during the year
compliance_risk: Fines by the Ministry of Labor and mandatory payment of accrued benefits.
penalty: Fines ranging from 1 to 3 minimum wages per affected worker.

definition: Mandatory paid non-working days designated by law for all employees.
compliance_requirements: Provide paid leave on national holidays
Pay double time if the employee is required to work on these days
compliance_risk: Litigation for unpaid wages and administrative sanctions.
penalty: Fine calculated per worker as established by the Ministry of Labor.

definition: Provision of medical coverage and subsidy payments for maternity and illness covered by the Salvadoran Social Security Institute (ISSS).
compliance_requirements: Register employees with the ISSS
Report leaves to the ISSS
Ensure maternity leave of 16 weeks
compliance_risk: Employer assumes the full cost of medical subsidies if employee is not registered correctly.
penalty: Financial liability for medical costs and fines for non-registration.

definition: An annual mandatory payment provided to employees based on seniority.
compliance_requirements: Pay the bonus between December 12th and 20th
Calculation based on time served in the calendar year
documentation_required: Aguinaldo receipt signed by the employee
compliance_risk: Administrative fines for late or non-payment.
penalty: Fines determined by labor inspectors.

definition: Mandates the mandatory registration of all employees with the Salvadoran Social Security Institute (ISSS) to provide health coverage and pension benefits.
compliance_requirements: Register employer with ISSS
Enroll all new hires within the first 5 days of employment
Submit monthly payroll reports
compliance_risk: Liability for all medical expenses incurred by an employee if they are not registered at the time of illness or accident.
penalty: Financial fines and potential criminal liability for withholding contributions not reported.

definition: Governs the legal framework for remote work, requiring specific clauses in employment contracts when the work is performed outside employer facilities.
compliance_requirements: Inclusion of remote work modality in the contract
Defining communication and digital supervision methods
Provision of equipment and internet connectivity guidelines
documentation_required: Remote work addendum or specific contract
Safety and health assessment of the home office
compliance_risk: Reclassification of independent contractors as employees if specific remote work conditions are not documented.
penalty: Administrative fines as stipulated by the Ministry of Labor.

law_name: Código de Trabajo - Prohibition of Discrimination
definition: Prohibits discrimination in employment based on age, gender, race, religion, or political affiliation during the hiring process.
compliance_requirements: Neutral hiring practices
Objective job descriptions
Maintaining non-discriminatory interview protocols
compliance_risk: Legal challenges and claims for discriminatory dismissal or refusal to hire.
penalty: Labor lawsuits for damages and administrative fines.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Previsión Social

definition: Establishes the presumption of an employment relationship when there is subordination, even if the parties sign a civil or commercial services contract.
compliance_requirements: Verify the nature of the relationship avoiding subordination
Avoid providing tools or workspace for independent contractors
Refrain from imposing schedules on independent service providers
documentation_required: Tax registration documents of the contractor
compliance_risk: High risk of reclassification of independent contractors as employees (labor fraud).
penalty: Payment of backdated social security contributions, unpaid benefits, and statutory fines per affected worker.

definition: Defines who is subject to mandatory social security, focusing on those who provide services under dependency or subordination.
compliance_requirements: Register all employees under the ISSS system
Distinguish between independent professional services and dependent labor
compliance_risk: Claims by ISSS for unpaid employer contributions if contractors are deemed employees.
penalty: Fines, surcharges, and interest on omitted employer contributions.

definition: Regulates the withholding and payment of income tax, requiring distinction between salaries and professional fees.
compliance_requirements: Apply correct tax withholding rates for employees versus contractors
Report professional services payments in annual filings
compliance_risk: Fiscal penalties for incorrect classification leading to lower tax withholdings.
penalty: Fiscal fines ranging from 10% to 100% of the omitted tax amount plus interest.

definition: The right of an employee to receive compensation when terminated without just cause by the employer.
compliance_requirements: Payment of severance proportional to years of service if the dismissal is without just cause
Calculation based on the average salary of the last year
Mandatory payment within 15 days following the termination
documentation_required: Calculation breakdown signed by employee and employer
compliance_risk: Lawsuits for unjustified dismissal leading to claims for back wages and additional damages.
penalty: Payment of full severance plus potential payment of salaries for the time the legal process lasts up to a limit.

definition: List of specific grounds under which an employer may terminate a contract without liability to pay severance.
compliance_requirements: Documented evidence of the violation
Strict adherence to procedural requirements for disciplinary action
Notification to the Ministry of Labor within the statutory period if applicable
documentation_required: Formal termination notice detailing the Article 48 cause
compliance_risk: Reclassification of termination as unjustified if the evidence is insufficient or procedural timelines were missed.
penalty: Obligation to pay full severance and potentially damages for unfair dismissal.

law_name: Ley Reguladora de la Prestación Económica por Renuncia Voluntaria
definition: Regulates the right of workers to receive a partial severance payment upon voluntary resignation after a certain period of service.
compliance_requirements: Employee must have at least 2 years of continuous service
Calculation of 50% of the normal severance rate
Written resignation provided by the employee
compliance_risk: Non-payment or incorrect calculation of the legal percentage leads to administrative fines.
penalty: Administrative fines imposed by the Ministry of Labor and Labor and Social Security and payment of outstanding balances.

definition: The requirement for notice prior to termination of an indefinite contract.
compliance_requirements: Provide advance notice in writing to the other party
Ensure notice periods comply with tenure-based requirements (30 days or more depending on time served)
compliance_risk: Incomplete notice period results in mandatory payment of the salary equivalent to the omitted notice days.
penalty: Payment of wages in lieu of notice.

definition: Regulates the mandatory form and content of individual labor contracts, which must be in writing and signed in duplicate.
compliance_requirements: Drafting written contracts for all employees
Maintaining updated copies for internal audits
Ensuring contract contents meet minimum legal standards
compliance_risk: Presumption of the truth of the facts stated by the worker in case of dispute.
penalty: Fines ranging from 1 to 3 monthly minimum wages depending on the number of workers.
source_reference: Asamblea Legislativa de El Salvador - Código de Trabajo

definition: Mandates the registration of all employees with the ISSS for health and pension coverage.
compliance_requirements: Register employer with ISSS
Affiliate employees to the system within 5 days of hiring
Report monthly payroll and contributions
compliance_risk: Fines, surcharges on unpaid contributions, and liability for medical costs if the worker is not registered.
penalty: Surcharges up to 20% of the unpaid debt plus administrative fines.

law_name: Ley del Sistema de Ahorro para Pensiones
definition: Obligation to withhold and report pension contributions for employees to their respective AFP.
compliance_requirements: Withhold pension contributions
Make employer contributions
Submit monthly pension reports
compliance_risk: Civil and criminal liability for misappropriation of funds if withholdings are not paid.
penalty: Interest, fines, and potential legal prosecution for retention of funds.

law_name: Reglamento de Seguridad y Salud Ocupacional
definition: Requirements to maintain safety committees and internal health protocols.
compliance_requirements: Formation of a Health and Safety Committee
Execution of a risk assessment
Maintaining a safety manual
compliance_risk: Sanctions by the Ministry of Labor during inspections and increased workplace accident liability.
penalty: Fines according to the Labor Code scale per violation.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Previsión Social

definition: Governs the specific documentation requirements for remote employment arrangements.
compliance_requirements: Addendum to the labor contract detailing equipment and costs
Defining remote work hours and connectivity obligations
compliance_risk: Reclassification of remote workers if documentation is absent, creating rights to overtime or office-based benefits.
penalty: General Labor Code sanctions.

law_name: Código de Trabajo de El Salvador
definition: The requirement to pay wages in legal tender within the established periods and at the place of work or immediate vicinity.
compliance_requirements: Payment must be made in legal tender (Colones or US Dollars)
Payment must be made during working hours
Record payment in payroll books
compliance_risk: Risk of claims for unpaid wages and administrative fines by the Ministry of Labor.
penalty: Fines calculated based on the number of workers affected and the gravity of the infraction, as per Articles 624-627.

law_name: Ley de Protección de Datos Personales
law_code: Anteproyecto de Ley (Constitutional interpretation of privacy rights)
definition: Protection of personal information handled by employers, including payroll data and sensitive medical information.
compliance_requirements: Obtain consent for data processing
Maintain confidentiality of employee health records
Implement data security measures
compliance_risk: Legal challenges for data leaks or unauthorized use of employee personal information.
penalty: Potential civil liability for damages.
source_reference: Constitución de la República de El Salvador

law_name: Constitution of the Republic of El Salvador
definition: Recognizes the right of employers and workers to organize freely for the defense of their interests by forming associations or unions, and establishes the right to collective bargaining.
compliance_requirements: Respect the freedom of association of workers
Engage in mandatory bargaining processes when initiated by legally constituted unions
Avoid interference in union activities
compliance_risk: Constitutional violation leading to potential lawsuits and international labor complaints.
penalty: Nullification of anti-union acts and possible constitutional amparo proceedings.

definition: Governs the procedure for the negotiation, signature, and approval of Collective Labor Agreements (Contrato Colectivo de Trabajo) between employers and unions.
compliance_requirements: Negotiate in good faith with representative unions
Deposit the signed collective agreement with the Ministry of Labor and Social Welfare
Apply the terms of the agreement to all workers within the scope of the contract
compliance_risk: Fines from the Ministry of Labor and potential strikes if negotiations fail due to bad faith.
penalty: Administrative fines as established by the Labor Code and potential legal liability for damages.
source_reference: Decree No. 15, Labor Code of El Salvador

law_name: Ministry of Labor and Social Welfare Internal Regulations
law_code: Reglamento de Organizacion y Funcionamiento del Ministerio de Trabajo
definition: Sets the administrative framework for the Ministry of Labor to oversee the registry of collective bargaining agreements and mediation processes.
compliance_requirements: Provide required documentation to labor inspectors
Participate in mandatory mediation phases conducted by the Ministry
documentation_required: Communication logs with the labor authority
compliance_risk: Obstruction of labor authority and administrative sanctions.
penalty: Administrative fines up to the maximum legal limit permitted by current Labor Code scales.
source_reference: Ministry of Labor and Social Welfare official website

definition: Governs the formation, operation, and dissolution of labor unions, as well as the protections afforded to union organizers and board members (fuero sindical).
compliance_requirements: Permit employees to join or form unions
Do not discriminate against unionized employees
Comply with requirements for collective bargaining negotiation
Maintain neutrality during union organization processes
documentation_required: Notices of union formation issued by the Ministry of Labor
compliance_risk: Medium to High: Illegal interference (prácticas desleales) leads to heavy administrative fines and potential reinstatement of employees if they are fired without prior legal authorization.
penalty: Administrative fines determined by the Ministry of Labor and Social Welfare; reinstatement of employees with back pay for union-related dismissals.

definition: Sets the procedure for negotiating and executing Collective Labor Agreements (Contrato Colectivo de Trabajo) between employers and unions.
compliance_requirements: Enter into mandatory negotiations if a collective agreement is requested
Negotiate in good faith
Observe the collective agreement terms as mandatory once registered
Provide required information for bargaining
documentation_required: Ministry of Labor registration certificate of the agreement
compliance_risk: High: Failure to negotiate or honor the Collective Agreement leads to legal strikes or administrative sanctions by the Labor Inspectorate.
penalty: Fines per article violation and potential closure or mediation intervention by the Ministry of Labor.

law_name: Special Law for Remote Work (Ley Especial de Teletrabajo)
definition: The law regulating the telework modality in the public and private sectors, ensuring that remote workers maintain the same rights, duties, and benefits as face-to-face workers.
compliance_requirements: Formalize the remote work modality through a written addendum or new contract
Provide necessary technological and connectivity tools to the employee
Guarantee the right to digital disconnection
Ensure occupational health and safety standards in the home office
Maintain equal treatment compared to on-site employees
documentation_required: Occupational risk assessment documents for remote locations
compliance_risk: Legal exposure for breach of contract, discrimination claims, and violations of health and safety standards.
penalty: Administrative fines imposed by the Ministry of Labor and Social Welfare under the Labor Code provisions.

definition: General labor regulations governing individual employment contracts, covering essential terms and conditions that apply to all modalities, including remote work.
compliance_requirements: Document working hours and wage structure
Ensure compliance with minimum wage laws
Respect maximum working hour limitations even when working remotely
compliance_risk: Claims of lack of social protection, violation of minimum wage, or unauthorized overtime.
penalty: Fines ranging from 3 to 15 minimum wages depending on company size and severity of the infraction.

definition: Regulatory framework governing the processing, protection, and security of personal data, critical for remote environments where data leaves the controlled office space.
compliance_requirements: Implement measures to protect employee and company data
Ensure data access is restricted and secured
Provide notification of data processing purposes
compliance_risk: Risk of data breaches, litigation, and regulatory sanctions for mishandling sensitive information.
penalty: Fines related to the severity of the privacy breach and damages caused.

law_name: Constitution of the Republic of El Salvador
definition: Guarantees the right to honor, personal image, and private life. Specifically, it establishes the right to habeas data, allowing individuals to access information held about them by public or private entities.
compliance_requirements: Ensure protection of employee personal information
Grant employees access to their personal records upon request
Maintain confidentiality of sensitive employee data
compliance_risk: Violation of constitutional rights leading to legal actions and constitutional protections (Amparo)
penalty: Constitutional sanctions and potential civil liability
source_reference: Asamblea Legislativa de la República de El Salvador

definition: Establishes the employer's obligation to maintain employee records and treat such information with the necessary confidentiality required by labor management.
compliance_requirements: Maintain strict confidentiality of the employee's personal files
Only collect data necessary for the employment relationship
Ensure data is used solely for labor-related administrative purposes
compliance_risk: Improper disclosure of sensitive employee health or salary information
penalty: Administrative fines imposed by the Ministry of Labor and Social Welfare
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Previsión Social

law_name: Law on Access to Public Information
definition: Regulates the protection of sensitive personal data, defining personal data as any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person.
compliance_requirements: Implement security measures to prevent unauthorized access
Obtain consent before sharing employee data with third parties unless required by law
Categorize and protect sensitive data (health, biometric, political or religious beliefs)
compliance_risk: Legal challenges for data breaches and unauthorized processing of personal information
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 1 to 40 minimum wages depending on the severity of the violation
source_reference: Portal de Transparencia - Gobierno de El Salvador

law_name: Ley General de Prevención de Riesgos en los Lugares de Trabajo
definition: The law mandates that employers must ensure the safety and health of workers in all aspects related to work, including the implementation of preventive measures and risk management systems.
compliance_requirements: Create a Safety and Health Committee
Perform a risk assessment of the workplace
Provide personal protective equipment (PPE) free of charge
Conduct health and safety training programs
compliance_risk: High risk of workplace accidents, workplace inspections by the Ministry of Labor, and potential operational suspension.
penalty: Fines ranging from 1 to 3 minimum wages per worker affected depending on the severity of the infraction.

definition: Establishes the obligation of the employer to provide adequate medical assistance and safety conditions to prevent occupational hazards and illnesses.
compliance_requirements: Maintain clean and hygienic facilities
Provide necessary medical kits
Ensure compliance with occupational hygiene standards
compliance_risk: Legal liability in case of occupational illness or accident and sanctions from the Ministry of Labor.
penalty: Administrative fines as specified by the Ministry of Labor regulations.

law_name: Reglamento General de Prevención de Riesgos en los Lugares de Trabajo
definition: Provides the technical and specific procedures for the implementation of the General Law on Risk Prevention in the workplace.
compliance_requirements: Appointment of a health and safety delegate
Design of emergency evacuation plans
Maintenance of specialized machinery safety guards
compliance_risk: Lack of compliance results in high penalties during Ministry of Labor inspections.
penalty: Monetary fines and temporary closure of the establishment.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Previsión Social

law_name: Ley de Migración y de Extranjería
definition: Regulates the entry, residence, and employment status of foreign nationals within El Salvador, mandating specific permits for remunerated activities.
compliance_requirements: Obtain specific residence permit for remunerated activities
Registration of foreign staff with the General Directorate of Migration and Foreigners
Annual renewal of work authorization for foreign workers
documentation_required: Employment contract approved by the Ministry of Labor
Clean criminal record from country of origin
compliance_risk: Deportation of the employee and significant administrative fines for the employer for employing undocumented workers.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 1 to 5 monthly minimum wages; potential revocation of business permits.
source_reference: General Directorate of Migration and Foreigners (DGME)

definition: Limits the percentage of foreign employees a company can hire (maximum 10% of the workforce) and requires Ministry of Labor oversight for foreign contracts.
compliance_requirements: Maintain minimum 90% Salvadoran staff ratio
Submit foreign employment contracts to the Ministry of Labor for approval
Ensure foreign workers hold valid work permits before onboarding
documentation_required: Ministry of Labor certified employment contract
compliance_risk: Fines for violating the nationality quota rules and potential invalidation of employment contracts.
penalty: Fines per affected employee and mandatory adjustment of workforce composition.
source_reference: Ministry of Labor and Social Welfare (MTPS)

law_name: Constitution of the Republic of El Salvador
definition: Establishes that all persons are equal before the law and prohibits discrimination based on nationality, race, sex, or religion.
compliance_requirements: Ensure equality of treatment in all employment practices
Prohibit arbitrary distinctions in hiring and promotion
compliance_risk: Constitutional violation leading to nullity of internal regulations and potential civil rights lawsuits.
penalty: Administrative sanctions, legal nullity of discriminatory actions, and potential payment of damages.

definition: Prohibits employers from imposing conditions that imply discrimination based on gender, race, religion, or political affiliation within the workplace.
compliance_requirements: Implement non-discriminatory hiring processes
Ensure equal pay for equal work
Maintain objective criteria for promotions and firing
documentation_required: Internal Labor Regulations (Reglamento Interno de Trabajo)
compliance_risk: Lawsuits for unjustified dismissal or discriminatory practices, fines by the Ministry of Labor.
penalty: Fines ranging from daily salary multiples for each violation, ordered reinstatement, or payment of severance.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Prevision Social

law_name: Law for the Prevention, Prohibition and Elimination of Discrimination
law_code: Ley para la Prevencion, Prohibicion y Eliminacion de la Discriminacion
definition: Explicitly prohibits all forms of discrimination, whether public or private, which limits the human rights or equality of opportunity of any individual.
compliance_requirements: Adopt codes of conduct covering discrimination
Provide mechanisms for employees to report discriminatory acts
Refrain from publishing discriminatory job advertisements
documentation_required: Records of training regarding diversity and non-discrimination
compliance_risk: Administrative and civil penalties, public reputation damage, and mandatory mediation or reconciliation processes.
penalty: Fines imposed by the National Council for the Prevention and Elimination of Discrimination, administrative orders to cease discriminatory acts.

definition: The obligation for employers to pay an annual bonus (Aguinaldo) to employees based on their years of service.
compliance_requirements: Pay bonus between December 12th and December 20th
Calculate based on years of service
Proportional payments for service under one year
compliance_risk: Fines by the Ministry of Labor and mandatory retroactive payment
penalty: Monetary fines calculated based on the number of employees and severity of the infringement
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Previsión Social

law_name: Ley del Sistema de Ahorro para Pensiones
definition: Mandatory participation in the pension savings system for private sector employees.
compliance_requirements: Employer contribution of 7.75% of salary
Employee contribution of 7.25% of salary
Monthly transfer to AFPs
compliance_risk: Employer liability for failing to remit deducted employee funds to the pension fund
penalty: Monetary fines and potential criminal liability for misappropriation of funds

definition: Mandatory paid annual leave of 15 business days after one year of continuous service.
compliance_requirements: Grant 15 days of paid vacation
Pay a bonus of at least 30% over the base salary for the vacation period
documentation_required: Payroll receipts showing vacation bonus payment
compliance_risk: Mandatory payment for double time if not granted and labor lawsuit risk
penalty: Fines issued by the Ministry of Labor
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Previsión Social

definition: Regulates the mandatory registration of employers and employees with the Salvadoran Social Security Institute (ISSS) and the obligation to report monthly payroll contributions.
compliance_requirements: Register employer with ISSS
Register employees within 5 days of hiring
File monthly payroll reports (planillas)
Pay employer and employee contributions
compliance_risk: High. Potential for fines, legal interest on arrears, and denial of employee medical benefits.
penalty: Fines ranging from 25% to 50% of the contribution amount owed plus interest surcharges.

law_name: Ley del Sistema de Ahorro para Pensiones
definition: Mandates the deduction and reporting of employee pension contributions to the Pension Fund Administrators (AFP).
compliance_requirements: Calculate correct percentage of pension deduction
Submit timely payroll deduction reports to AFP
Transfer withheld funds within the first 10 days of the following month
compliance_risk: High. Criminal liability may arise if employee withholdings are not transferred to the AFP (appropriation).
