penalty: Fines and potential criminal prosecution for misappropriation of funds.

law_name: Código de Trabajo de El Salvador
definition: Requires employers to maintain accurate payroll records and individual wage records for all employees to verify compliance with minimum wage and payment terms.
compliance_requirements: Maintain current payroll books
Issue proof of payment to employees
Record overtime and deductions
Ensure compliance with minimum wage laws
compliance_risk: Moderate. Failure to produce records during Ministry of Labor inspections results in fines.
penalty: Fines per violation, subject to the inspection authority's assessment.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Previsión Social

law_name: Ley del Impuesto sobre la Renta
definition: Establishes the obligation of employers to act as withholding agents for employee income tax on salaries.
compliance_requirements: Withhold income tax from salary payments
File monthly withholding reports
Issue annual income tax statements to employees
Annual reconciliation report
compliance_risk: High. Tax audits by the Ministry of Finance regarding unpaid or under-reported withholdings.
penalty: Fines, interest, and possible audit tax penalties.

law_name: Código de Trabajo de El Salvador
definition: Requires employers to maintain an up-to-date registry of employees, including hiring date, position, salary, and personal details.
compliance_requirements: Maintain a register of employees
Keep internal labor regulations approved by the Ministry of Labor
compliance_risk: Administrative sanctions and fines by the Ministry of Labor for non-compliance with record-keeping obligations.
penalty: Fines ranging from $57.14 to $57.14 per violation depending on the company size and nature of the infraction.

law_name: Ley del Impuesto sobre la Renta
definition: Obligation to register employees as income tax contributors and manage the deduction of income tax at the source.
compliance_requirements: Register employee with Tax Authority (Ministerio de Hacienda)
Obtain Tax ID (NIT) for the employee
Issue tax retention receipts (Constancias de Retención)
compliance_risk: Tax evasion audits, interest on unpaid taxes, and significant administrative fines.
penalty: Fines determined by the Tax Code (Código Tributario) for failing to act as a withholding agent.

definition: Specific requirement to formalize the remote work modality within the employment contract and notify labor authorities.
compliance_requirements: Addendum to the labor contract specifying remote work conditions
Registration of the remote work arrangement
compliance_risk: Dispute over labor rights if remote work conditions are not clearly registered and documented.
penalty: Standard labor fines as per the Ministry of Labor oversight.

law_name: Constitution of the Republic of Guatemala
definition: Establishes labor as a right and a social obligation, setting the fundamental principles of minimum wage, non-discrimination, and the protection of workers' rights.
compliance_requirements: Ensure fair remuneration
Guarantee equality in employment
Provide rest days and annual leave
compliance_risk: Constitutional violation leading to nullity of contracts
penalty: Legal claims and potential nullification of adverse working conditions
source_reference: Constitucion Politica de la Republica de Guatemala

definition: Defines the individual employment contract and the obligations of employers to formalize employment relationships in writing.
compliance_requirements: Execute written contracts for indefinite or fixed term
Register contracts with the Ministry of Labor
compliance_risk: Presumption of employer's version if contract is missing
penalty: Fines ranging from 2 to 10 minimum wages

law_name: Organic Law of the Guatemalan Social Security Institute (IGSS)
definition: Mandates the registration of all employees with the IGSS to provide coverage for illness, maternity, and accidents.
compliance_requirements: Register employer with IGSS
Register all employees within 3 days of hiring
Pay monthly social security contributions
compliance_risk: High financial liability for medical expenses if employee is not registered
penalty: Fines and mandatory payment of retroactive contributions plus interest

definition: Regulates the withholding of Income Tax (ISR) from employment income.
compliance_requirements: Withhold applicable income tax from employee salaries
File monthly withholding tax returns
Provide annual tax statements to employees
compliance_risk: Tax evasion audits and monetary penalties
penalty: Fines, interest, and surcharges on unpaid tax

definition: Regulates the remote work modality, ensuring workers have the same rights as onsite workers.
compliance_requirements: Explicitly agree to telework in the contract
Define work equipment and expenses covered by employer
Ensure data privacy and health and safety
compliance_risk: Disputes over equipment costs and overtime
penalty: Administrative fines by the Ministry of Labor

definition: Protects the habeas data right regarding the collection and processing of employee personal information.
compliance_requirements: Maintain confidentiality of employee health and personal files
Obtain consent for data usage
compliance_risk: Civil liability for privacy breach
penalty: Damages and potential judicial sanctions

definition: Individual labor contract is the juridical bond whereby a person agrees to submit to another person to perform a personal service or execute a work, under the immediate or delegated direction of the employer, in exchange for remuneration.
compliance_requirements: Contracts must be in writing if the term exceeds one year
Must be executed in three copies
Must specify work duties, duration, and salary
documentation_required: Proof of registration with the Ministry of Labor
compliance_risk: Presumption that the employer's version of events is true if a written contract is missing
penalty: Fines ranging from 3 to 12 monthly minimum wages
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Guatemala

law_name: Ley de Contrato de Trabajo a Tiempo Parcial
definition: Regulates the modality of part-time work, ensuring proportionality in social security and benefits.
compliance_requirements: Must be in writing
Must register with the Ministry of Labor and Social Welfare
Social security contributions calculated on effective hours worked
documentation_required: IGSS registration forms for partial time
compliance_risk: Reclassification of the contract to full-time leading to back pay and social security arrears
penalty: Administrative fines by the Ministry of Labor
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Previsión Social

definition: Regulates the performance of work duties remotely through information and communication technologies.
compliance_requirements: Written amendment or contract specifying remote status
Defining communication and control mechanisms
Employer responsibility for equipment and connectivity costs
documentation_required: Occupational health and safety assessment of the remote site
compliance_risk: Labor inspection for lack of clear remote work policies
penalty: Sanctions according to the Labor Code violations

law_name: Constitution of the Republic of Guatemala
law_code: Constitucion Politica de la Republica de Guatemala
definition: Establishes the maximum duration of the workday and workweek, setting the foundation for labor rights in the country.
compliance_requirements: Limit the effective workday for day shifts to 8 hours daily, not exceeding 44 hours per week.
Limit the effective workday for night shifts to 6 hours daily, not exceeding 36 hours per week.
Provide rest and meal breaks during the workday.
compliance_risk: Unconstitutional labor practices, potential human rights litigation.
penalty: Administrative sanctions by the Ministry of Labor and potential lawsuits for damages.
source_reference: Constitucion Politica de la Republica de Guatemala

definition: Defines ordinary workday, overtime, night shifts, mixed shifts, and the calculation of overtime pay.
compliance_requirements: Pay overtime for work exceeding the legal limits.
Ensure overtime pay is at least 50% above the base hourly wage.
Ensure workday limits for day, night, and mixed shifts are strictly observed.
Maintain accurate attendance records.
Define and record all overtime hours performed.
compliance_risk: Fines by the Ministry of Labor and Social Welfare, mandatory back-pay for unpaid overtime.
penalty: Fines ranging from 2 to 18 minimum wages based on the size of the company and severity.

law_name: Regulation for the Control of Records
definition: Regulates the obligation of employers to keep records of the working hours of their employees to prove compliance.
compliance_requirements: Maintain a registry of working hours for all employees.
Keep records accessible for labor inspections.
Register the control system with the Ministry of Labor if required.
compliance_risk: Inability to prove compliance during a labor audit leads to presumption of guilt regarding overtime claims.
penalty: Administrative fines for failure to maintain legally required books.

law_name: Ley del Instituto Guatemalteco de Seguridad Social
definition: Mandates the creation and administration of the social security system covering maternity, illness, accidents, and old age.
compliance_requirements: Register employer with IGSS
Register employees within three days of hiring
Submit monthly payroll reports
Make monthly employer and employee contribution payments
compliance_risk: Fines, interest on late payments, and potential criminal charges for tax evasion.
penalty: Fines based on the amount owed and potential suspension of services.
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Guatemala

definition: Establishes the rules for wages, mandatory bonuses, and payroll protection.
compliance_requirements: Payment of minimum wage
Payment of Christmas bonus (Aguinaldo)
Payment of Incentive Bonus (Bono 14)
compliance_risk: Lawsuits by employees for unpaid wages and heavy fines by the Ministry of Labor.
penalty: Fines ranging from 3 to 12 minimum monthly wages.
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Guatemala

law_name: Ley Reguladora del Pago del Aguinaldo
definition: Mandates the payment of a 100% monthly salary bonus to all workers as an annual bonus (Aguinaldo).
compliance_requirements: Pay 50% in December and 50% in January
Calculate based on average salary of the previous year
compliance_risk: Ministry of Labor inspections and employee claims.
penalty: Fines up to 18 minimum wages.
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Guatemala

law_name: Ley de Bonificación Anual para Trabajadores del Sector Privado y Público
definition: Mandates the payment of an additional salary as a bonus known as 'Bono 14'.
compliance_requirements: Pay annually during the first 15 days of July
Calculate based on the 12-month period from July 1 to June 30
compliance_risk: Sanctions and penalties for non-payment or late payment.
penalty: Fines up to 18 minimum wages.
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Guatemala

definition: Regulates the withholdings for Income Tax (ISR) on employment income.
compliance_requirements: Calculate withholding for employees earning above the taxable threshold
Declare and pay withheld amounts to SAT monthly
compliance_risk: Tax evasion audits, interest, and penalties from SAT.
penalty: Monetary fines and possible tax audits.

definition: Regulates the Income Tax (ISR) applicable to employment income, specifically regarding withholdings that employers must perform on employee salaries.
compliance_requirements: Calculate and withhold income tax on monthly salary payments
Submit monthly declarations using Form SAT-1311
Issue annual constancy of withholdings to employees
compliance_risk: Financial sanctions, interest charges, and potential criminal liability for tax evasion if withholdings are not declared.
penalty: Monetary fines based on the tax amount unpaid, plus interest and 100% fine on the omitted tax amount in cases of fraud.

law_name: Ley Orgánica del Instituto Guatemalteco de Seguridad Social
definition: Mandates the payment of social security contributions by both employer and employee based on the employee's payroll.
compliance_requirements: Register the company with IGSS
Register employees with IGSS
Calculate and deduct 4.83% from employee salary
Pay the employer's share (10.67%) to IGSS monthly
compliance_risk: Loss of social security benefits for employees, legal suits, and administrative fines from IGSS.
penalty: Administrative fines, interest on late payments, and collection processes that can lead to judicial action.

definition: Governs the mandatory payment of the 'Bono 14' and 'Aguinaldo', which are considered taxable income components for reporting purposes.
compliance_requirements: Pay Aguinaldo (100% of salary in December)
Pay Bono 14 (100% of salary in July)
Maintain accurate ledger records of these payments for tax reporting
compliance_risk: Labor lawsuits for non-payment and potential tax penalties for incorrect payroll reporting.
penalty: Fines from the Ministry of Labor and Labor Courts for non-payment; tax adjustments if reported incorrectly to SAT.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Previsión Social

definition: Regulates the mandatory period of paid vacation for employees after completing one year of continuous service with the same employer.
compliance_requirements: Provide 15 working days of paid vacation per year of service
Payment must be made in advance
Vacation days cannot be compensated with money, except for termination
compliance_risk: High. Failure to grant vacation can lead to administrative fines and legal claims from the Ministry of Labor.
penalty: Fines ranging from 2 to 18 minimum monthly wages depending on the number of affected employees.
source_reference: Congreso de la Republica de Guatemala - Decreto 1441

definition: Mandatory maternity leave for female employees, providing protection during pregnancy and post-birth.
compliance_requirements: Grant 84 days of maternity leave (42 pre-natal and 42 post-natal)
Maintain job security for the mother during pregnancy and up to one year after birth
Ensure IGSS payment coordination
compliance_risk: Very High. Unauthorized dismissal of a pregnant worker or failure to pay salary during leave leads to immediate reinstatement and payment of back wages.
penalty: Reinstatement, payment of unpaid wages, and administrative fines.
source_reference: Congreso de la Republica de Guatemala - Decreto 1441

law_name: Ley Organica del Instituto Guatemalteco de Seguridad Social
definition: Governs the obligation of employers to contribute to social security for health, maternity, and disability coverage.
compliance_requirements: Register the company and employees with IGSS
Pay the employer's portion of social security contributions
Deduct and remit the employee's portion of contributions
documentation_required: Planilla de Seguridad Social (IGSS payroll report)
compliance_risk: High. Non-payment of social security contributions prevents employees from accessing medical benefits, creating severe liability.
penalty: Interest on late payments, administrative fines, and criminal charges for withholding worker contributions.

law_name: Ley de Bonificacion Anual para Trabajadores del Sector Privado y Publico
definition: Mandatory 'Bono 14', an annual bonus equivalent to one month's salary for all private and public sector employees.
compliance_requirements: Pay the bonus in the first 15 days of July
Calculate based on the average salary of the previous 12 months
Apply pro-rata for employees with less than one year of service
compliance_risk: Medium. Non-payment is subject to strict inspections by the Ministry of Labor.
penalty: Administrative fines set by the Ministry of Labor and mandatory payment of the benefit.
source_reference: Congreso de la Republica de Guatemala - Decreto 42-92

definition: The formalization of the employment relationship, defining the contractual agreement between employer and employee, establishing rights and obligations, and setting terms for trial periods.
compliance_requirements: Execution of written employment contract for fixed-term or task-based work
Registration of the contract with the Ministry of Labor and Social Welfare
Specification of salary, hours, place of work, and job description
compliance_risk: Risk of being sanctioned by the Ministry of Labor and presumption of an indefinite employment relationship if the contract is not formalized.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 2 to 10 minimum wages according to Article 272 of the Labor Code.
source_reference: Decreto Numero 1441 del Congreso de la Republica

law_name: Ley Organica del Instituto Guatemalteco de Seguridad Social
definition: The legal obligation to register all employees with the Guatemalan Social Security Institute (IGSS) for health, maternity, and disability coverage.
compliance_requirements: Register the employer with IGSS
Register every new hire with IGSS within 3 days of starting employment
Report monthly payroll for social security contributions
compliance_risk: Fines and responsibility for costs of medical care if an employee is not registered at the time of an accident or illness.
penalty: Fines, surcharges on late payments, and legal liability for all medical expenses incurred by the unregistered worker.
source_reference: Decreto 295 del Congreso de la Republica

definition: Requires the employer to act as a withholding agent for the employee's Income Tax (ISR) regarding labor income.
compliance_requirements: Obtain employee tax identification (NIT)
Calculation and withholding of ISR from salaries
Annual reconciliation of income and taxes withheld
compliance_risk: Tax evasion penalties, interest, and surcharges for failure to withhold or pay collected taxes to the tax administration (SAT).
penalty: Monetary fines based on the amount of tax not withheld or paid, plus criminal liability in cases of significant tax fraud.
source_reference: Decreto 10-2012 del Congreso de la Republica

law_name: Constitucion Politica de la Republica de Guatemala
definition: Establishes that labor rights are non-renounceable and any agreement that reduces, restricts, or diminishes them is void.
compliance_requirements: Adhere to minimum labor standards mandated by the constitution
Avoid discriminatory hiring practices
Ensure equality of opportunity and treatment
documentation_required: Employment contracts compliant with constitutional minimums
compliance_risk: Any contractual clause violating constitutional rights is null and void, exposing the employer to labor litigation.
penalty: Nullity of contract clauses and potential legal action from the Labor Inspectorate.
source_reference: Constitucion Politica de la Republica de Guatemala

definition: The Labor Code establishes the principle of 'Primacia de la Realidad' (Primacy of Reality), where the employment relationship is defined by the existence of subordination, personal service, and remuneration, regardless of the label given to the contract.
compliance_requirements: Ensure that independent contractors do not work under the direct supervision or subordination of the employer
Avoid assigning fixed work schedules or providing tools that imply an employment relationship
Verify that the contractor operates independently and carries their own business risk
documentation_required: Signed service agreement (Contrato de Servicios Profesionales)
Documentation showing the contractor is providing services to multiple clients
compliance_risk: High risk of reclassification of independent contractors as employees, triggering back-pay for social security, severance, and mandatory bonuses.
penalty: Fines imposed by the Ministry of Labor and Social Welfare (MINTRAB) and mandatory payment of all retroactively due social security contributions (IGSS) plus interest and legal fees.
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Guatemala, Decreto 1441

law_name: Ley del Impuesto al Valor Agregado
definition: Defines the rendering of professional services as taxable activities. Contractors must bill for services, distinguishing them from payroll-based wage income which is subject to different tax regimes (ISR on employment).
compliance_requirements: Contractor must be registered as a service provider under the proper tax regime
Contractor must issue compliant invoices for services rendered
Employer must ensure payments are made against valid tax documents
compliance_risk: Tax evasion audits by SAT (Superintendencia de Administración Tributaria) if the service contract is found to be a sham to avoid payroll taxes.
penalty: Fines ranging from 50% to 100% of the omitted tax, plus interest and potential criminal tax fraud charges.
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Guatemala, Decreto 27-92

law_name: Ley Orgánica del Instituto Guatemalteco de Seguridad Social
definition: Establishes that all persons providing services to an employer under a relationship of subordination are subject to compulsory social security affiliation.
compliance_requirements: Affiliate all employees to the IGSS (Instituto Guatemalteco de Seguridad Social)
Verify if the contractor relationship possesses the necessary elements to be deemed an employment relationship subject to mandatory affiliation
compliance_risk: Retroactive assessment of social security quotas and penalties for failing to register employees.
penalty: Fines per affected worker, plus payment of unpaid quotas, and potential civil or criminal liability for the employer.
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Guatemala, Decreto 295

definition: Defines direct dismissal as the termination of the employment contract by the employer without just cause or attributed to the employee's fault.
compliance_requirements: Provide notice of dismissal
Pay accrued benefits (prestaciones laborales)
Provide a certificate of employment
documentation_required: Proof of payment of accrued benefits (vacation, Aguinaldo, Bono 14)
compliance_risk: High risk of labor lawsuits, back pay claims, and administrative fines from the Ministry of Labor.
penalty: Payment of severance (one month salary per year of service), unpaid proportional benefits, and potential court-ordered damages for wrongful dismissal.
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Guatemala

definition: Regulates the consequences of unjustified dismissal, requiring the employer to prove the existence of just cause.
compliance_requirements: Burden of proof lies with the employer
Must substantiate the cause of dismissal in accordance with Article 77
documentation_required: Detailed letter of dismissal indicating legal cause
Evidence of misconduct or poor performance
compliance_risk: Ordering of reinstatement if the court deems the dismissal unjustified, or payment of full severance if the employer cannot prove cause.
penalty: Full severance pay plus damages equivalent to the salary the employee would have earned during the lawsuit period up to a limit.
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Guatemala

definition: Specifies the just causes for which an employer may terminate the employment contract without incurring liability for severance payment.
compliance_requirements: Strict adherence to listed causes (e.g., dishonesty, violence, negligence, repeated unexcused absences)
Formal investigation before dismissal
documentation_required: Evidence of breach of internal rules
compliance_risk: Wrongful dismissal lawsuit if documentation is insufficient to support the claimed cause.
penalty: Loss of the ability to terminate without severance, leading to mandatory payment of all benefits and potentially reinstatement.
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Guatemala

law_name: Ley de Bonificación Anual para Trabajadores del Sector Privado y Público
definition: Mandates the payment of proportional annual bonus (Bono 14) upon termination of employment.
compliance_requirements: Calculate the proportional amount based on the days worked in the current year
Include in the final settlement (liquidación)
compliance_risk: Fines from the Ministry of Labor for incomplete final payments.
penalty: Administrative sanctions and mandatory payment of arrears.
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Guatemala

definition: Mandatory requirement to document individual employment contracts in writing, establishing the terms and conditions of the labor relationship.
compliance_requirements: Must be signed in three copies: one for each party and one for the Ministry of Labor.
Include identity, dates, position, salary, and working conditions.
documentation_required: Record of registration at the Ministry of Labor
compliance_risk: Presumption of the existence of a contract in favor of the employee in case of legal disputes due to lack of documentation.
penalty: Fines ranging from 2 to 10 minimum wages for non-agricultural activities.
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Guatemala

definition: Obligation of the employer to register the company and its employees with the Guatemalan Social Security Institute (IGSS).
compliance_requirements: Registration of the business entity.
Affiliation of every worker within 3 days of hiring.
documentation_required: Social security affiliation records for employees
compliance_risk: Liability for all medical and social security costs incurred by the employee if not registered.
penalty: Administrative fines and mandatory retroactive payment of contributions plus interest.

definition: Regulates the withholding and documentation of Income Tax (ISR) from labor income.
compliance_requirements: Calculate and withhold ISR from employees.
Keep records of income and withholdings for annual tax reporting.
compliance_risk: Tax evasion charges and audits by the Superintendency of Tax Administration (SAT).
penalty: Interest, surcharges, and potential criminal tax fraud charges.

law_name: Ley de Protección de Datos Personales (Proposed Context)
definition: Guarantees the right to privacy and access to information, requiring employers to document and protect sensitive employee data.
compliance_requirements: Maintain confidentiality of employee health and personal files.
Obtain consent for processing sensitive data.
compliance_risk: Civil liability for misuse or disclosure of private employee information.
penalty: Damages and potential criminal prosecution if data exposure leads to identity theft or harm.
source_reference: Constitución Política de la República de Guatemala

law_name: Reglamento de Salud y Seguridad Ocupacional
definition: Mandatory documentation of health and safety conditions in the workplace.
compliance_requirements: Creation of a Health and Safety Committee.
Documentation of risk assessments and training records.
compliance_risk: Fines from the Ministry of Labor and liability in the event of workplace accidents.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 8 to 18 minimum wages.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Previsión Social

definition: The legal relationship between employer and employee defined by subordination and remuneration.
compliance_requirements: Formalize employment contracts in writing
Maintain employment records
Observe maximum working hours
compliance_risk: Lawsuits for unjustified dismissal and claims for unpaid social benefits.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 2 to 18 minimum wages.
source_reference: Congreso de la Republica de Guatemala

law_name: Ley Organica del Instituto Guatemalteco de Seguridad Social
definition: Mandatory contribution system for health, maternity, and disability coverage.
compliance_requirements: Register employer and employees with IGSS
Make timely monthly contributions
Report changes in personnel
compliance_risk: Fines, interest on late payments, and liability for medical costs if employee is not registered.
penalty: Monetary fines based on the amount of evasion and potential criminal charges for fraud.

definition: Regulation of income tax withholding for employees and corporate tax obligations.
compliance_requirements: Calculate and withhold Income Tax (ISR) from employee salaries
File monthly withholding reports to SAT
compliance_risk: Tax evasion audits and mandatory surcharges.
penalty: Administrative fines, tax surcharges, and interest.

law_name: Reglamento de Salud y Seguridad Ocupacional
definition: Norms governing the prevention of occupational hazards and work environments.
compliance_requirements: Appoint a health and safety committee
Provide personal protective equipment
Conduct risk assessments
compliance_risk: Workplace accidents and Ministry of Labor inspections resulting in work stoppages.
penalty: Administrative fines and temporary shutdown of operations.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Prevision Social

law_name: Ley de Proteccion de Datos Personales
definition: Rights of individuals regarding the collection and processing of their personal data.
compliance_requirements: Obtain consent for data processing
Ensure confidentiality of employee sensitive info
Limit use to employment purposes
compliance_risk: Violation of privacy rights leading to civil litigation.
penalty: Damages for civil liability and administrative sanctions.
source_reference: Constitucion Politica de la Republica de Guatemala

law_name: Constitution of the Republic of Guatemala
law_code: Constitucion Politica de la Republica de Guatemala
definition: Establishes that labor rights are minimal, non-renounceable, and mandatory, protecting the right to collective bargaining as a fundamental social right.
compliance_requirements: Recognize the right of workers to negotiate conditions collectively
Refrain from acts that hinder the exercise of trade union rights
compliance_risk: Unconstitutionality claims and potential legal challenges to internal company policies that violate collective labor rights.
penalty: Legal nullity of individual contracts that restrict constitutional labor rights.
source_reference: Congreso de la Republica de Guatemala

definition: Defines the Collective Agreement of Working Conditions as the agreement between one or more unions and one or more employers to regulate conditions for the provision of services.
compliance_requirements: Negotiate in good faith with union representatives
Sign the agreement in three copies
Submit the agreement to the Ministry of Labor for registration
compliance_risk: Declarations of illegal strikes and state-mandated arbitration processes.
penalty: Fines ranging from 2 to 10 minimum wages, plus potential forced mediation.
source_reference: Congreso de la Republica de Guatemala

definition: Procedures for the direct settlement of collective disputes and the requirement of the 'Pliego de Peticiones' (Statement of Demands) in the collective bargaining process.
compliance_requirements: Respond to the statement of demands within the legal timeframe
Participate in the Direct Settlement phase (Arreglo Directo)
Participate in the Conciliation phase if required
compliance_risk: Escalation of labor conflict to legal strike status if negotiation fails.
penalty: Court-ordered intervention and heavy fines if the employer fails to appear at conciliation hearings.
source_reference: Congreso de la Republica de Guatemala

law_name: Constitution of the Republic of Guatemala
definition: Recognizes the right of workers to unionize freely for the defense and promotion of their economic and social interests.
compliance_requirements: Ensure workers are not dismissed for participating in the formation of a union
Refrain from interfering in union internal affairs
compliance_risk: Constitutional violation leading to potential labor lawsuits and international complaints.
penalty: Labor lawsuits, administrative sanctions by the Ministry of Labor, potential criminal charges for obstruction of union rights.
source_reference: Constitución Política de la República de Guatemala

definition: Regulates the formation, operation, and dissolution of labor unions, providing protection for unionized workers.
compliance_requirements: Grant leave of absence to union leaders
Do not hinder unionization processes
Bargain in good faith during collective bargaining
Provide required information to unions as mandated by law
compliance_risk: Fines, labor disputes, and potential nullification of employer actions that violate union protections.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 2 to 10 minimum wages; potential criminal investigation for illegal dismissal of union leaders.

law_name: Agreement on Social and Economic Aspects and Agrarian Situation
definition: Mandates the strengthening of labor rights and the effective exercise of freedom of association in line with ILO conventions.
compliance_requirements: Strict adherence to ILO conventions 87 and 98 concerning freedom of association and protection of the right to organize
documentation_required: Records of compliance with ILO standards
Evidence of non-discrimination against unionized workers
compliance_risk: Reputational damage and potential international trade sanctions or labor grievances.
penalty: Administrative sanctions by Ministry of Labor; external pressure from international labor monitoring bodies.

definition: Regulates the modality of telework as an organizational scheme for the performance of work duties through the use of information and communication technologies.
compliance_requirements: Voluntary agreement between employee and employer
Written amendment to the employment contract
Employer must ensure the provision of tools and equipment or pay a stipend for connectivity/electricity
Must guarantee equal rights and benefits as on-site employees
documentation_required: Proof of payment for internet/electricity expenses if applicable
compliance_risk: Legal challenge regarding the nature of the employment relationship, potential claims for unpaid equipment expenses or utility reimbursement.
penalty: Administrative fines issued by the Ministry of Labor and Social Welfare (MINTRAB) ranging from 2 to 18 monthly minimum wages.
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Guatemala, Decreto 9-2022

definition: Governs the essential elements of an employment contract, requiring subordination and personal service, applicable even in remote work settings.
compliance_requirements: Must maintain the essential elements of the employment contract despite the change in location
Employer retains subordination and management authority
documentation_required: Internal labor regulations updated for remote operations
compliance_risk: Risk of misclassification of employees as independent contractors, resulting in back-pay of social security and labor benefits.
penalty: Fines from 2 to 18 minimum wages for labor law violations.
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Guatemala, Decreto 1441

law_name: Ley de Protección de Datos Personales
law_code: Iniciativa de Ley (Referenced as best practice standard)
definition: Requirement to protect employee sensitive information while accessing systems remotely.
compliance_requirements: Implement cybersecurity protocols for home offices
Protect employee's personal data during remote access
Ensure confidentiality agreements for remote access to company databases
compliance_risk: Legal liability for data breaches caused by inadequate security measures at remote work locations.
penalty: Civil and criminal liability depending on the severity of data leakage.
source_reference: Constitution of Guatemala Art. 24 and 31 (Right to Privacy)

law_name: Constitution of the Republic of Guatemala
law_code: Constitución Política de la República de Guatemala
definition: The right of individuals to access information concerning them contained in archives, records, or any other form of state or private registries, and the right to know its purpose, correction, rectification, or updating.
compliance_requirements: Provide employees access to personal files upon request
Maintain accuracy of personal information records
Allow for correction of incorrect data
compliance_risk: Constitutional violation leading to potential Amparo proceedings.
penalty: Court-ordered compliance via Amparo; potential legal liability for damages.
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Guatemala

definition: Obligation of the employer to maintain registry books and files of employees with confidentiality and proper internal management.
compliance_requirements: Restrict access to payroll and personnel files to authorized personnel only
Ensure HR records are kept secure
Maintain confidentiality of sensitive employee health and identification data
compliance_risk: Fines by the Ministry of Labor and Social Welfare for mishandling private labor information.
penalty: Administrative fines imposed by the General Inspectorate of Labor.

law_name: Law on Access to Public Information
law_code: Ley de Acceso a la Información Pública
definition: Regulations governing sensitive personal data, defined as data that refers to the most intimate sphere of the individual, including health, race, and sexual preference, requiring specific protection.
compliance_requirements: Implementation of strict security measures for sensitive employee data
Prohibition of unauthorized disclosure of sensitive data
Requirement to obtain consent for the processing of sensitive data
documentation_required: Consent forms for sensitive data processing
compliance_risk: Administrative sanctions and potential civil or criminal liability for data breaches.
penalty: Fines from the Ombudsman's office (Procurador de los Derechos Humanos) and potential legal action.

definition: General provisions establishing the employer's obligation to adopt necessary measures to protect the life, safety, and health of workers.
compliance_requirements: Implementation of safety equipment
Proper installation of machinery to prevent accidents
Prevention of occupational diseases
Compliance with technical standards for safety
compliance_risk: Fines, temporary shutdown of facilities, and legal liability in case of workplace accidents.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 2 to 18 minimum wages based on the severity of the violation and company size.
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Guatemala

law_name: Regulations on Health and Safety at Work
definition: Comprehensive set of technical regulations governing occupational health and safety standards for all work environments in Guatemala.
compliance_requirements: Appointment of a Health and Safety Committee
Preparation of an Occupational Risk Assessment
Provision of Personal Protective Equipment (PPE)
Conducting annual safety audits
compliance_risk: Sanctions from the General Labor Inspectorate, potential work stoppage orders.
penalty: Monetary fines and possible criminal liability for employers in cases of gross negligence causing death or permanent injury.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Previsión Social

law_name: Organic Law of the Guatemalan Social Security Institute (IGSS)
definition: Regulates the mandatory registration of employers and employees for social security coverage, including protection against work-related accidents and occupational illnesses.
compliance_requirements: Registration of the company with the IGSS
Payment of employer and employee social security contributions
Reporting workplace accidents to IGSS
Compliance with IGSS medical inspection standards
compliance_risk: Non-payment of contributions, failure to report accidents leading to denial of benefits to workers and legal claims.
penalty: Fines, interest on unpaid contributions, and potential criminal charges for non-payment of deductions.

definition: Regulates the entry, stay, and employment of foreign nationals in Guatemala, establishing requirements for different types of visas and residence permits for labor purposes.
compliance_requirements: Obtain specific authorization from the Guatemalan Migration Institute (IGM) for foreigners intending to work.
Ensure the foreigner has a residence status that allows for work (Temporary or Permanent Resident).
Notify the Ministry of Labor of the employment relationship of foreign personnel.
documentation_required: Proof of job offer or contract
Identification document (Cédula de Vecindad/DPI for residents)
compliance_risk: Expulsion, deportation, and administrative fines for both the employer and the foreign national.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 2 to 10 minimum wages, and potential prohibition from hiring foreign staff for a set period.

definition: Establishes that at least 90% of a company's workforce must be Guatemalan, unless otherwise authorized by the Ministry of Labor, and caps foreign participation in the total payroll at 15%.
compliance_requirements: Maintain a payroll composition that respects the 90/10 ratio.
Keep records verifying the citizenship of all employees.
Request exemption from the Ministry of Labor if the 90% quota cannot be met for technical reasons.
documentation_required: Justification report for technical hiring needs
Ministry of Labor authorization letter for quota exemptions
compliance_risk: Fines, legal orders to terminate excessive foreign personnel, and inability to renew work permits.
penalty: Fines ranging from 2 to 12 minimum wages, plus potential mandatory adjustment of staff composition.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Previsión Social (MINTRAB) - mintrabajo.gob.gt

law_name: Regulation for the Employment of Foreigners
definition: Detailed regulatory framework governing the administrative process by which the Ministry of Labor authorizes the employment of foreign nationals in the country.
compliance_requirements: Registration of the employment contract with the Ministry of Labor.
Verification of the foreigner's migratory status before formalizing the employment contract.
Quarterly reporting on foreign staff if applicable.
documentation_required: Proof of specialized skill or technical necessity
compliance_risk: Administrative sanctions for unauthorized hiring and potential labor inspections leading to fines.
penalty: Fixed administrative fines established by the Ministry of Labor and mandatory regularization.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Previsión Social (MINTRAB)

law_name: Constitution of the Republic of Guatemala
definition: Establishes that in Guatemala all human beings are free and equal in dignity and rights; it prohibits any form of discrimination based on race, sex, religion, or political belief.
compliance_requirements: Implement non-discriminatory hiring practices
Ensure equal pay for equal work regardless of personal characteristics
Maintain a work environment free of harassment and prejudice
compliance_risk: Constitutional violation, potential for lawsuits based on fundamental human rights violations.
penalty: Civil and criminal liability depending on the severity; nullity of discriminatory employment contracts or actions.
source_reference: Constitución Política de la República de Guatemala

definition: Prohibits discriminatory practices in the workplace, specifically regarding the conditions of employment and the treatment of workers during the employment relationship.
compliance_requirements: Grant equal treatment to all employees
Prohibit discrimination based on gender in employment offers and promotions
Ensure maternity protection and equal access to benefits
documentation_required: Internal work regulations (Reglamento Interior de Trabajo)
compliance_risk: Fines by the Ministry of Labor and Social Security, potential labor lawsuits for moral and economic damages.
penalty: Fines ranging from 2 to 18 minimum wages based on the gravity and the number of employees affected.

law_name: Law for the Dignification and Integral Promotion of Women
definition: Provides specific protections against discrimination toward women in the labor market, ensuring equal opportunities for access to work and career development.
compliance_requirements: Ensure non-discrimination in recruitment processes
Guarantee equal compensation for men and women
Prevent sexual harassment in the workplace
documentation_required: Records of training on gender equality
compliance_risk: Legal penalties for gender-based discriminatory practices, reputational damage.
penalty: Administrative fines and court-ordered remediation or compensation to the affected employee.
source_reference: Ley de Dignificación y Promoción Integral de la Mujer

definition: Criminalizes discrimination in the exercise of any right, including employment, when it involves disparagement or exclusion based on language, race, gender, or religion.
compliance_requirements: Establishment of strict internal protocols to avoid any criminal discriminatory behavior
Management accountability to prevent discriminatory conduct in the company
documentation_required: Records of disciplinary actions for discriminatory behavior
compliance_risk: Criminal prosecution of the employer or designated company representative.
penalty: Imprisonment ranging from one to three years and a fine.

definition: The right to the thirteenth-month salary (Aguinaldo), which must be paid at least fifty percent in the first half of December and the rest in January.
compliance_requirements: Payment of 100 percent of the monthly salary as a bonus.
Proportional payment if the employee has worked less than a year.
documentation_required: Payment receipts signed by the employee
compliance_risk: Fines by the Ministry of Labor and labor lawsuits for non-payment.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 2 to 18 minimum monthly wages.
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Guatemala

law_name: Ley Reguladora de la Prestación del Aguinaldo para los Trabajadores del Sector Privado
definition: Regulates the mandatory payment of a bonus equivalent to 100 percent of the basic monthly salary for workers in the private sector.
compliance_requirements: Calculation based on the average salary earned during the year.
Payment must be made within the first 15 days of December.
compliance_risk: Sanctions by the Labor Inspectorate.
penalty: Monetary fines and requirement to pay overdue amounts with legal interest.
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Guatemala

law_name: Ley de Bonificación Anual para Trabajadores del Sector Privado y Público
definition: Commonly known as 'Bono 14', this is a mandatory annual bonus equivalent to one month of salary, paid in July.
compliance_requirements: Payment of one monthly salary.
Pro-rated payment for employees with less than one year of service.
documentation_required: Proof of bank transfer or cash receipt
compliance_risk: Administrative penalties and mandatory payment of back-dated benefits.
penalty: Administrative fine between 2 to 18 minimum wages.
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Guatemala

law_name: Ley de Bonificación Incentivo para Trabajadores del Sector Privado
definition: A mandatory monthly incentive bonus of Q250.00 for all private-sector employees.
compliance_requirements: Monthly payment of Q250.00 alongside the salary.
Must be included in payroll.
compliance_risk: Ministry of Labor audit failure.
penalty: Fine per employee for failure to pay or failure to report.
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Guatemala

law_name: Ley Orgánica del Instituto Guatemalteco de Seguridad Social (IGSS)
definition: Social security contribution requirements that provide health, maternity, and disability coverage.
compliance_requirements: Employer contribution of 10.67% of the payroll.
Employee contribution of 4.83% of the payroll.
Monthly reporting via IGSS portal.
compliance_risk: Accrual of legal interest, fines, and legal action by the IGSS.
penalty: Significant financial penalties and interest charges on unpaid contributions.

definition: Governs the withholding and reporting of Income Tax (ISR) from employment income.
compliance_requirements: Calculate annual income tax projections for employees
Withhold applicable ISR monthly
Submit withholding declarations to SAT (Superintendencia de Administración Tributaria)
Provide annual statements (Constancias de Retención)
compliance_risk: Tax audits, assessment of tax omissions, and interest penalties.
penalty: Fines equivalent to 100% of the omitted tax if detected by tax authorities.

law_name: Reglamento del Registro Electrónico de Planillas
definition: Mandates the registration and electronic reporting of payroll records to the Ministry of Labor (MINTRAB).
compliance_requirements: Upload payroll data to the Ministry of Labor web portal
Update employee information periodically
Maintain digital records of reports filed
documentation_required: Digital file output from payroll systems
compliance_risk: Administrative sanctions and blocking of specific employer processes with the Ministry.
penalty: Fines ranging from 2 to 9 minimum wages.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Previsión Social

definition: The requirement for employers to maintain a written record of employment and, in some cases, register contracts with the Ministry of Labor.
compliance_requirements: Maintain a written contract for employees
Record employee data in the internal payroll registry
Ensure all employment records are available for inspection by the Ministry of Labor
compliance_risk: Fines for lack of formal contracts or records.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 2 to 10 minimum wages.
source_reference: Congreso de la República de Guatemala

law_name: Organic Law of the Guatemalan Social Security Institute (IGSS)
definition: Mandatory registration of all employees with the Guatemalan Social Security Institute (IGSS) within the first 3 days of employment.
compliance_requirements: Enroll employee in IGSS within 72 hours of hiring
Submit monthly payroll reports
Pay employer and employee social security contributions
compliance_risk: Fines, mandatory payment of retroactive contributions, and lack of coverage for employees in case of workplace accident.
penalty: Administrative fines and interest on late contributions.

definition: Requirements for employers to act as withholding agents for Income Tax (ISR) regarding employee earnings and registration in tax systems.
compliance_requirements: Register employee as an income earner in the tax system
Obtain tax identification (NIT) for each employee
Maintain payroll records for tax withholding verification
compliance_risk: Audits from SAT, financial penalties for incorrect withholding, and tax evasion charges.
penalty: Monetary fines based on the amount of tax not withheld or reported, plus interest.

law_name: General Regulation of Occupational Health and Safety
definition: Mandatory registration of the workplace and occupational health protocols with the Ministry of Labor.
compliance_requirements: Register the company's health and safety committee
Maintain registry of accidents and medical records
File the health and safety annual report
compliance_risk: Suspension of work activities and fines for failing to provide safe working conditions.
penalty: Administrative fines determined by the Ministry of Labor Inspectorate.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Previsión Social

definition: Regulates the individual employment contract, establishing it as the agreement where a person binds to provide services to an employer under their direction.
compliance_requirements: Must be in writing if for a fixed term or for a specific project.
Must detail the nature of work, duration, salary, and place of work.
compliance_risk: Imposition of fines and legal presumption that the contract is indefinite in favor of the employee.
penalty: Administrative fines imposed by the Ministry of Labor and Social Security.
source_reference: Secretaría de Trabajo y Seguridad Social

definition: Mandates the registration of employees in the Honduran Social Security Institute (IHSS) for coverage against illness, maternity, disability, old age, and death.
compliance_requirements: Register the company with the IHSS.
Enroll all employees within 3 days of hiring.
Make monthly payroll contributions.
compliance_risk: Fines, surcharges on late payments, and liability for medical costs if the employee was not registered.
penalty: Surcharges of 10% to 20% on unpaid contributions and additional administrative fines.

law_name: Código del Trabajo - Jornada de Trabajo
definition: Defines the standard working hours: day shift (8 hours), night shift (6 hours), and mixed shift (7 hours), and mandates extra pay for overtime.
compliance_requirements: Observe maximum hourly limits.
Pay overtime at specified percentages (25% to 75% depending on time).
Ensure rest periods during shifts.
compliance_risk: Lawsuits for unpaid overtime and labor inspector sanctions.
penalty: Fines ranging from 50 to 500 Lempiras per infraction, subject to adjustment for current inflation.
source_reference: Secretaría de Trabajo y Seguridad Social

law_name: Ley de Protección de Datos Personales
definition: Regulates the handling and protection of personal data collected during the employment relationship.
compliance_requirements: Obtain consent for data processing.
Implement security measures to protect employee sensitive information.
Limit data collection to employment purposes only.
compliance_risk: Administrative and civil liability for unauthorized data exposure.
penalty: Fines according to the gravity of the violation.
source_reference: Instituto de Acceso a la Información Pública (IAIP)

law_name: Ley de Empleo a Tiempo Parcial
definition: Establishes the legal framework for hiring employees on a part-time basis with proportional benefits.
compliance_requirements: Contracts must specify hours.
Proportional payment of salary and benefits based on time worked.
compliance_risk: Reclassification as a full-time employee if requirements are not met.
penalty: Payment of retroactive benefits as if the employee were full-time.
source_reference: Secretaría de Trabajo y Seguridad Social

definition: Regulates the nature, form, and content of individual employment contracts, establishing that a contract exists by the mere fact of providing services under subordination.
compliance_requirements: Contracts must be in writing if for a fixed term or specific project
Must specify the type of work, duration, salary, and place of work
Must be signed in triplicate (employer, employee, Ministry of Labor)
documentation_required: Registration at the Ministry of Labor and Social Security
compliance_risk: Presumption that the employer is responsible for any claim if a written contract is missing
penalty: Fines ranging from 3 to 10 minimum wages depending on the severity and company size
source_reference: Secretaría de Trabajo y Seguridad Social de Honduras

definition: Governs the performance of employment tasks from a remote location using information and communication technologies.
compliance_requirements: Specific addendum or new contract reflecting remote nature
Agreement on equipment provision and expense coverage
Guarantee of privacy and data security standards
compliance_risk: Misclassification of remote workers as contractors and inability to prove compliance with working hours
penalty: Administrative sanctions by the Ministry of Labor and potential labor lawsuits
source_reference: La Gaceta - Diario Oficial de la República de Honduras

law_name: Ley Marco del Sistema de Protección Social
definition: Defines the employer's obligation to register employees with the IHSS (Honduran Social Security Institute) upon contract commencement.
compliance_requirements: Registration of the employee within 3 days of hiring
Payment of mandatory employer contributions
Accurate salary reporting for benefit calculations
compliance_risk: High financial liability for medical expenses if an employee is not registered during an accident
penalty: Surcharges of 5% to 20% on unpaid contributions plus administrative fines

law_name: Ley de Protección de Datos Personales
definition: Regulates the collection and processing of personal data, including employee sensitive information required for payroll and contract management.
compliance_requirements: Obtaining informed consent for data processing
Securing employee sensitive information
Limiting data access to HR and management
compliance_risk: Civil lawsuits and administrative sanctions for data breaches or unauthorized disclosure
penalty: Financial fines and potential criminal liability in extreme cases
source_reference: IAIP (Instituto de Acceso a la Información Pública)

definition: Defines the standard daytime working hours as those performed between 5:00 AM and 7:00 PM, with a maximum duration of 8 hours per day and 44 hours per week.
compliance_requirements: Ensure workday does not exceed 8 hours for daytime work
Maintain a 44-hour weekly limit
Provide a rest period of at least one hour during the shift
compliance_risk: Fines by the Ministry of Labor and potential lawsuits for unpaid overtime
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 3 to 10 minimum wages depending on the severity and company size
source_reference: Secretaría de Trabajo y Seguridad Social de Honduras

definition: Establishes the limits for mixed and night shifts: night shifts are those worked between 7:00 PM and 5:00 AM, with a maximum of 6 hours duration.
compliance_requirements: Limit night shifts to 6 hours
Apply correct surcharges for night work
Maintain proper registration of night shift staff
documentation_required: Payroll records indicating night shift premiums
compliance_risk: Claims for breach of maximum work duration for night shifts
penalty: Administrative sanctions and mandatory back-payment of overtime
source_reference: Secretaría de Trabajo y Seguridad Social de Honduras

definition: Mandates the payment of overtime (horas extraordinarias) at a rate of 25% for daytime, 50% for night-time, and 75% for extra night hours.
compliance_requirements: Pay overtime premiums according to legal rates
Obtain written authorization for overtime where required by internal policy
Separate overtime payments from regular salary in payroll
compliance_risk: High exposure to litigation and labor inspections for systemic failure to pay overtime
penalty: Payment of double or triple time, plus administrative fines from the Ministry of Labor
source_reference: Secretaría de Trabajo y Seguridad Social de Honduras

definition: Regulates the payment of wages, the mandatory 13th and 14th-month bonuses, and the payment frequency.
compliance_requirements: Pay employees at least twice a month or every fifteen days
Payment of Decimo Tercer Mes (13th month bonus)
Payment of Decimo Cuarto Mes (14th month bonus)
compliance_risk: Fines, legal labor disputes, and forced payment of arrears.
penalty: Administrative fines established by the Ministry of Labor and potential payment of legal interests.
source_reference: Secretaria de Trabajo y Seguridad Social

definition: Establishes the mandatory social security contributions for employers and employees to the IHSS (Instituto Hondureno de Seguridad Social).
compliance_requirements: Register the company and employees with IHSS
Withhold and contribute the employer and employee shares monthly
Report changes in salary or workforce status
compliance_risk: Accumulation of debt with IHSS, surcharge interest, and legal action.
penalty: Financial surcharges of 2% per month and potential legal prosecution for failure to remit deductions.

law_name: Ley del Impuesto Sobre la Renta
definition: Obligates employers to act as tax withholding agents for their employees' income tax.
compliance_requirements: Withhold income tax from employee salaries based on the applicable scale
File the annual withholding report (Declaracion Jurada de Retenciones)
Remit withheld taxes to the SAR (Servicio de Administracion de Rentas)
compliance_risk: High tax penalties, tax audits, and criminal liability for withholding tax evasion.
penalty: Fines, interest, and surcharges calculated by the SAR.

definition: Governs the mandatory contributions to the Regime of Contributions for Social Projection (RAP).
compliance_requirements: Mandatory employer and employee contributions to individual retirement and housing accounts
Timely submission of monthly payroll reports to RAP
compliance_risk: Administrative sanctions and debt collection processes by the fund administrator.
penalty: Interest for late payment and administrative fines.

law_name: Income Tax Law (Ley del Impuesto sobre la Renta)
definition: The legal framework governing the taxation of income derived from labor and employment, mandating withholding of income tax (ISR) from employee salaries.
compliance_requirements: Withhold income tax from employee payroll based on progressive brackets
Submit monthly declarations of withheld taxes to the Tax Administration (SAR)
Issue annual income statements to employees for tax filing purposes
compliance_risk: Fines, interest accruals, and potential criminal charges for tax evasion.
penalty: Fines ranging from 10% to 100% of the unpaid tax amount depending on the duration and nature of the delinquency.
source_reference: Servicio de Administración de Rentas (SAR) - Honduras

law_name: Social Security Law (Ley del Seguro Social)
definition: Governs the mandatory contributions to the Honduran Social Security Institute (IHSS) intended for healthcare, disability, and pension coverage.
compliance_requirements: Register employer and employees with IHSS
Calculate and deduct employee contributions from payroll
Submit employer-paid portion of contributions
Submit monthly contribution forms (Planillas)
compliance_risk: Suspension of services for employees, financial penalties, and interest charges.
penalty: Fixed fines for late filings and monthly interest penalties on outstanding contribution balances.

law_name: Law of Educational Contribution (Ley de Aportación al Fondo Educativo)
definition: Mandates an employer contribution to the educational fund based on the total payroll amount paid to permanent employees.
compliance_requirements: Monthly payment of the educational contribution
Maintain accurate records of all personnel subject to the contribution
Submit timely payments to the Treasury
compliance_risk: Financial audit risk and mandatory fine assessments.
penalty: Interest on late payments and a surcharge of 10% on the total due.

definition: Regulates the rights of employees to paid annual vacations based on seniority.
compliance_requirements: Provide 10 days after 1 year of service
Provide 12 days after 2 years of service
Provide 15 days after 3 years of service
Provide 20 days after 4 years of service
compliance_risk: High risk of labor lawsuits and fines by the Ministry of Labor.
penalty: Administrative fines determined by the Ministry of Labor and payment of accrued vacation balance.
source_reference: Código del Trabajo de Honduras, Decreto 189

definition: Mandates the payment of the Thirteenth Month salary (Aguinaldo) and Fourteenth Month salary (Decimocuarto Mes) as mandatory additional benefits.
compliance_requirements: Pay 13th month salary (Aguinaldo) in December
Pay 14th month salary (Decimocuarto Mes) in June
compliance_risk: Significant legal risk for non-payment, potential for labor claims.
penalty: Payment of outstanding benefits plus potential legal interest and administrative fines.
source_reference: Código del Trabajo de Honduras, Decreto 189

definition: Covers mandatory employer and employee contributions for illness, maternity, disability, and old age.
compliance_requirements: Register employer and employees with IHSS
Calculate and withhold payroll contributions
Submit monthly payments to IHSS
compliance_risk: Fines, surcharges, and inability to clear tax status.
penalty: Interest on late payments, administrative fines, and criminal responsibility for withheld but unpaid contributions.

definition: Provides female employees with 10 weeks of paid maternity leave (4 weeks pre-natal, 6 weeks post-natal).
compliance_requirements: Provide 10 weeks of paid leave
Guarantee job security during pregnancy and post-partum
Ensure prenatal and postnatal medical care access via IHSS
compliance_risk: Violation of fundamental constitutional rights and protection against dismissal.
penalty: Reinstatement of the employee and payment of back wages plus statutory damages.
source_reference: Código del Trabajo de Honduras, Decreto 189

definition: Mandates compulsory registration of all employees under the Honduran Social Security Institute (IHSS).
compliance_requirements: Register employer with IHSS
Register employees within 8 days of hiring
Submit monthly payroll reports
compliance_risk: Exposure to medical liabilities and retroactive payment of contributions plus interest and fines.
penalty: Administrative fines and responsibility for medical costs incurred by workers during the unregistered period.

definition: Regulation for the hiring of labor by the hour for specific needs.
compliance_requirements: Must provide social benefits pro-rata
Must register contract with Ministry of Labor
Must not replace permanent positions
documentation_required: Registration seal from Ministry of Labor
compliance_risk: Reclassification of employment status to permanent if not properly documented.
penalty: Payment of full severance and back-pay benefits for reclassified employees.
source_reference: La Gaceta Diario Oficial de Honduras

definition: Fundamental right to work and the protection of labor by the State.
compliance_requirements: Guarantee non-discriminatory hiring
Ensure equal pay for equal work
compliance_risk: Constitutional challenges and lawsuits based on discriminatory practices.
penalty: Judicial sanctions and potential nullification of discriminatory acts.
source_reference: Constitucion de la Republica de Honduras

definition: Establishes the principle of primacia de la realidad (primacy of reality), whereby the nature of the relationship is determined by the actual performance of duties rather than the contract title.
compliance_requirements: Ensure that the relationship does not meet the criteria of subordination, fixed schedule, and remuneration for work performed under the employer's direction.
Avoid using independent contracts for roles that are core to the business operations.
documentation_required: Evidence of the contractor's independent business registration
compliance_risk: High risk of reclassification of independent contractors into employees, leading to back payments for social security, severance, and labor benefits.
penalty: Payment of all unpaid social security contributions (IHSS), back taxes, and statutory severance pay for the entire duration of the relationship plus fines.
source_reference: Decreto 189, Codigo del Trabajo, Congreso Nacional de Honduras

law_name: Ley del Impuesto sobre la Renta
definition: Regulates the withholding and tax obligations for professional services, distinguishing between income from wages (dependent) and professional fees (independent).
compliance_requirements: Register payments made to independent professionals correctly in tax filings.
Apply correct withholding tax rates applicable to non-employees if required by the Tax Administration.
documentation_required: RTN (Registro Tributario Nacional) of the contractor
Fiscal invoices (facturas) issued under the contractor's own tax ID
compliance_risk: Tax audit resulting in the disallowance of expenses if the contractor is found to be an employee in disguise.
penalty: Fines up to 100% of the unpaid tax, plus interest and administrative sanctions from the SAR (Servicio de Administracion de Rentas).
source_reference: Ley del Impuesto sobre la Renta, Diario Oficial La Gaceta

definition: Regulates the conditions and procedures for ending the employment relationship, defining just cause (justificado) versus unjust dismissal (injustificado), and the mandatory payment of severance benefits.
compliance_requirements: Provide formal written notice of termination for just cause
Calculate and pay legal severance (preaviso, cesantía, vacaciones proporcionales)
Process final payment within the statutory timeframe
documentation_required: Signed resignation letter or dismissal notice
compliance_risk: High financial liability for unjust dismissal and litigation costs.
penalty: Payment of statutory severance, potential payment of lost wages up to maximum limits, and administrative fines imposed by the Ministry of Labor.
source_reference: La Gaceta, Diario Oficial de la República de Honduras

law_name: Ley de Justicia Tributaria (Relevant to Salary/Payroll Taxes)
definition: Governs the tax treatment of severance payments and final settlements, requiring proper withholding of income tax (ISR) on applicable components.
compliance_requirements: Withhold income tax on severance payments exceeding legal thresholds
Issue tax certificates for finalized employment
compliance_risk: Tax audits and administrative sanctions for under-withholding.
penalty: Fines calculated as a percentage of unpaid taxes and potential interest.

definition: Mandates the notification of employee separation to the Honduran Social Security Institute (IHSS) to cease employer contributions.
compliance_requirements: Submit 'Aviso de retiro' (notice of withdrawal) to IHSS upon termination
Finalize all outstanding social security contributions for the last payroll period
compliance_risk: Accrual of unnecessary debt and social security penalties.
penalty: Administrative fines and mandatory payment of arrears plus surcharges.

definition: Mandates the requirement of a written employment contract to formalize the employer-employee relationship.
compliance_requirements: The contract must be in writing
It must be drafted in triplicate
It must specify nature of work, duration, salary, and work schedule
documentation_required: Copy for the Ministry of Labor
compliance_risk: Lack of a written contract creates a presumption in favor of the employee regarding the conditions of employment.
penalty: Fines ranging from 3 to 10 minimum wages according to the severity and repetition of the offense.
source_reference: Código del Trabajo de Honduras, Decreto 189-1959

definition: Requires mandatory registration of employees and employers with the IHSS (Instituto Hondureño de Seguridad Social).
compliance_requirements: Register the company with IHSS
Register all employees within 3 days of hiring
Maintain current payroll records
compliance_risk: Fines, interest on unpaid contributions, and liability for benefits if an unregistered employee suffers an accident.
penalty: Fines determined by IHSS regulations and potential civil liability.

law_name: Ley del Impuesto sobre la Renta
definition: Obligates employers to withhold income tax from employee salaries and provide withholding certificates.
compliance_requirements: Calculate withholding tax (ISR)
Submit withholding declarations to SAR (Servicio de Administración de Rentas)
Issue annual income statements (Constancia de Retención)
compliance_risk: Tax evasion penalties, interest, and surcharges for late filings.
penalty: Monetary fines based on a percentage of the omitted tax and potential criminal charges for tax evasion.
source_reference: Ley del Impuesto sobre la Renta, Decreto 120-94

law_name: Ley de Protección de Datos Personales
definition: Regulates the handling of sensitive personal data of employees collected by employers.
compliance_requirements: Obtain consent for data processing
Maintain secure databases
Notify the Data Protection Authority for database registration
documentation_required: Internal security protocols for personnel files
compliance_risk: Unauthorized disclosure of personal information and breach of privacy rights.
penalty: Administrative sanctions ranging from warnings to significant fines per violation.
source_reference: Ley de Protección de Datos Personales, Decreto 11-2013

definition: Regulates the conditions for termination of employment contracts and the employer's obligation to pay severance (Preaviso and Cesantía).
compliance_requirements: Provide formal written notice for termination
Calculate and pay legal severance based on length of service
Payment of outstanding salaries and benefits
compliance_risk: Lawsuits for unfair dismissal and claims for unpaid severance which can lead to significant financial liabilities.
penalty: Payment of double the severance amount, back wages, and legal costs.
source_reference: Secretaría de Trabajo y Seguridad Social

definition: Mandates the registration of all employees with the Honduran Social Security Institute (IHSS) and payment of monthly contributions.
compliance_requirements: Register company and employees with IHSS
Make timely monthly contribution payments for employer and employee shares
compliance_risk: Fines, administrative sanctions, and personal liability for employers who fail to cover medical coverage for workers.
penalty: Monthly surcharges on unpaid balances, administrative fines, and potential suspension of services.

definition: Regulates the maximum duration of the workday and the requirement to pay overtime for work performed beyond legal limits.
compliance_requirements: Maintain accurate time logs
Pay overtime premiums for night shifts and work exceeding 44 hours/week
compliance_risk: Inspections by the Ministry of Labor finding non-payment of mandatory overtime premiums.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 1 to 10 minimum wages per affected employee.
source_reference: Secretaría de Trabajo y Seguridad Social

law_name: Ley de Protección de Datos Personales
definition: Regulates the handling and processing of personal data of individuals, including employee records.
compliance_requirements: Obtain consent for sensitive data processing
Ensure security of personnel files
compliance_risk: Sanctions from the access to public information institute for mishandling employee private data.
penalty: Administrative warnings and monetary fines.

law_name: Constitución de la República de Honduras
definition: The Constitution guarantees the right to collective bargaining and the right to strike as fundamental rights of workers for the protection and improvement of their economic and social conditions.
compliance_requirements: Respect the freedom of association
Engage in good faith negotiations when a trade union is present
Refrain from interfering in union formation or activities
compliance_risk: Violation of constitutional rights can lead to constitutional injunctions (Amparo) and international human rights scrutiny.
penalty: Constitutional liability and potential civil damages.
source_reference: La Gaceta Oficial de la República de Honduras

definition: Sets the legal framework for collective bargaining agreements (Convención Colectiva), requiring employers to negotiate with worker unions to determine working conditions.
compliance_requirements: Must negotiate in good faith upon receiving a collective bargaining request
Agreement must be signed in writing
Must be registered with the Ministry of Labor and Social Security
Apply agreed terms to all employees within the bargaining unit
compliance_risk: Unfair labor practices, strikes, and legal sanctions for refusal to bargain.
penalty: Fines ranging from 500 to 10,000 Lempiras depending on the severity and company size as determined by the Ministry of Labor.

definition: Specific procedural regulations for the submission, registration, and enforcement of collective agreements between employers and labor unions.
compliance_requirements: Submission of the draft agreement to the Ministry
Verification of union legitimacy
Posting of notice regarding the bargaining process
documentation_required: Minutes of assembly approving the agreement
compliance_risk: Invalidity of the collective agreement if procedural steps are not followed.
penalty: Administrative sanctions and inability to enforce contract terms in labor courts.
source_reference: Secretaría de Trabajo y Seguridad Social (STSS) Regulations

law_name: Constitution of the Republic of Honduras
definition: Guarantees the right of workers to unionize and form collective bargaining agreements for the defense of their interests.
compliance_requirements: Recognize the legal right of employees to organize
Refrain from interfering with union formation
Respect the right to strike and collective action
compliance_risk: Constitutional infringement claims and international human rights scrutiny.
penalty: Civil litigation and potential international labor sanctions.
source_reference: Constitución de la República de Honduras

definition: Regulates the formation, operation, and dissolution of labor unions, as well as the mechanisms for collective bargaining and resolution of collective labor disputes.
compliance_requirements: Permit employees to associate freely in unions
Negotiate collective labor agreements in good faith
Ensure no anti-union discrimination occurs during hiring or termination
Provide payroll deductions for union dues if requested by the employee
compliance_risk: Fines from the Ministry of Labor and Social Security, labor lawsuits for unfair labor practices.
penalty: Fines ranging from 500 to 5000 Lempiras per violation, subject to adjustments per Labor Code updates.

law_name: Ministry of Labor and Social Security Regulations
definition: Administrative rules governing the registry of trade unions and the mandatory reporting of collective bargaining status to the Ministry.
compliance_requirements: Submit collective agreements for Ministry registration
Maintain current registry of union representatives
Facilitate inspections by the Labor Inspectorate regarding union activity
compliance_risk: Administrative sanctions and failure to enforce terms of the collective agreement.
penalty: Administrative fines issued by the Ministry of Labor and Social Security.
source_reference: Secretaría de Trabajo y Seguridad Social (STSS)

definition: Regulates the modality of telework as an organizational scheme of service that is performed at the employer's direction, outside the employer's premises, through the use of information and communication technologies.
compliance_requirements: Formalization of telework through a written agreement/addendum
Guarantee of equal rights compared to in-office workers
Provision of equipment and tools by the employer
Reimbursement of additional expenses incurred by the employee (internet, electricity)
Compliance with ergonomic and occupational health and safety standards
Respect for the right to disconnect outside working hours
documentation_required: Written telework agreement signed by both parties
Payroll records reflecting telework specific payments/reimbursements
compliance_risk: Legal reclassification of employment relationship, labor claims regarding health and safety, and non-compliance with the right to disconnect.
penalty: Administrative fines imposed by the Ministry of Labor and Social Security (STSS) ranging from 1 to 10 minimum wages depending on the gravity of the violation.
keywords: Seguridad y salud en el teletrabajo

definition: The overarching legal framework governing the employment relationship, rights, and obligations of employers and employees regardless of the work location.
compliance_requirements: Maintain official payroll records
Ensure payment of social security contributions (IHSS)
Adherence to maximum working hours even when working remotely
compliance_risk: Claims of lack of social security coverage, failure to pay overtime, or violation of fundamental labor rights.
penalty: Administrative sanctions and potential lawsuits for breach of contract or violation of the Labor Code.

law_name: Ley sobre Privacidad de la Información y Protección de Datos Personales
law_code: Ley de Transparencia y Acceso a la Información Pública
