definition: Regulates the protection of personal data and information managed by entities, including employer-employee data interactions in digital environments.
compliance_requirements: Implement technical and organizational measures to secure employee data transmitted remotely
Maintain confidentiality of company information processed outside the office
compliance_risk: Data breaches, loss of confidential business information, and potential lawsuits for privacy violations.
penalty: Administrative sanctions, liability for damages caused to third parties.

law_name: Constitution of the Republic of Honduras
definition: Guarantees the right to individual privacy and the protection of personal honor, image, and data, establishing that access to information is restricted unless authorized by law or judicial authority.
compliance_requirements: Protect employee private information from unauthorized disclosure
Maintain confidentiality of sensitive personal records
Ensure data processing respects constitutional privacy rights
compliance_risk: Constitutional infringement claims and potential civil lawsuits for damages resulting from privacy breaches.
penalty: Civil liability, indemnification, and possible constitutional amparo actions filed by the aggrieved party.
source_reference: Official Gazette (La Gaceta) - Constitution of Honduras

law_name: Law on Transparency and Access to Public Information
law_code: Ley de Transparencia y Acceso a la Información Pública
definition: Defines personal information as information related to an individual that identifies them or makes them identifiable, which is considered confidential and sensitive.
compliance_requirements: Classification of personal data
Implementation of security measures for sensitive records
Restriction of access to unauthorized third parties
compliance_risk: Administrative sanctions for improper handling of sensitive information and potential liability regarding the improper exposure of individual data.
penalty: Administrative fines imposed by the IAIP (Institute for Access to Public Information), civil liability, and disciplinary actions.

definition: Establishes the obligation of the employer to maintain the confidentiality of information related to the employee's life and personal circumstances that are not relevant to the employment relationship.
compliance_requirements: Restrict collection of data strictly to professional needs
Protect medical records and family information
Ensure data is used only for labor-related administrative purposes
documentation_required: Data protection clause in employment contracts
compliance_risk: Labor disputes regarding privacy intrusion, breach of confidentiality, and potential fines from the Ministry of Labor.
penalty: Fines ranging from minimum to maximum salaries depending on the severity of the infraction as defined by the Ministry of Labor.

definition: General provisions mandating that employers provide safe working conditions, equipment, and personal protective gear to prevent workplace accidents and occupational diseases.
compliance_requirements: Implement safety protocols
Provide protective equipment
Conduct regular risk assessments
Maintain clean and hygienic facilities
compliance_risk: Fines, temporary shutdown of facilities, and legal liability in the event of workplace accidents.
penalty: Fines ranging from 50 to 1,000 Lempiras per violation, subject to Labor Ministry discretion, or suspension of operations.
source_reference: Secretaria de Trabajo y Seguridad Social de Honduras

definition: Mandatory social security coverage for occupational hazards, sickness, and disability related to workplace conditions.
compliance_requirements: Register all employees with IHSS
Pay monthly social security contributions
Report all workplace accidents to IHSS
compliance_risk: Litigation for medical costs, government fines, and criminal charges in cases of extreme negligence.
penalty: Surcharges on unpaid contributions and potential criminal liability for failure to provide mandatory social security.

law_name: Reglamento General de Medidas Preventivas de Accidentes de Trabajo y Enfermedades Profesionales
definition: Specific regulatory framework detailing technical measures for accident prevention and hygiene standards in all work environments in Honduras.
compliance_requirements: Establishment of Joint Health and Safety Committees
Regular training for employees on hazards
Standardized signage and emergency exits
Periodic health checks for workers
compliance_risk: Administrative sanctions by the Labor Inspectorate, immediate site closure, and severe financial penalties.
penalty: Administrative fines adjusted by the Labor Ministry per infraction and mandatory corrective orders.
source_reference: La Gaceta, Diario Oficial de la República de Honduras

definition: The legal framework governing the entry, stay, and authorization for foreign nationals to perform remunerated activities in Honduras.
compliance_requirements: Foreigners must obtain a temporary residence permit with the right to work prior to commencing employment.
Employers must sponsor the permit application by demonstrating the need for foreign personnel.
Foreigners must register with the National Institute of Migration (INM) and the Ministry of Labor.
documentation_required: Work contract approved by the Ministry of Labor
Criminal record certificate from country of origin and residence
Sponsorship letter from the Honduran employer
compliance_risk: Deportation of the foreign worker, fines for the employer, and potential blacklisting of the company from sponsoring future visas.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 1,000 to 10,000 Lempiras per violation, plus deportation costs.

definition: Regulation limiting the percentage of foreign workers a company can employ relative to the total workforce.
compliance_requirements: Employers must maintain at least 90% Honduran staff.
Foreigners cannot exceed 10% of the total workforce unless authorized by the Ministry of Labor under special circumstances.
documentation_required: Payroll records showing nationality of employees
Ministry of Labor authorization for foreign hire exceptions
compliance_risk: Fines for violating the national employment quota and legal invalidation of foreign work contracts.
penalty: Fines varying according to the number of affected employees and company size.
source_reference: Secretaría de Trabajo y Seguridad Social

law_name: Reglamento de la Ley de Migración y Extranjería
definition: Detailed procedural rules for the issuance of residence permits for investment and employment purposes.
compliance_requirements: Submission of documentation to the Ministry of Labor for labor certification before applying to the INM.
Renewal of residency permits 30 days prior to expiration.
documentation_required: Bank solvency or proof of financial support
compliance_risk: Irregular migration status resulting in labor fines and potential loss of employment status.
penalty: Administrative sanctions as defined in the Ley de Migración.

law_name: Constitución de la República de Honduras
definition: Establishes that all Hondurans are born free and equal in rights and that no discrimination based on sex, race, class, or any other cause detrimental to human dignity is permitted.
compliance_requirements: Ensure equal treatment for all employees
Prohibit discriminatory hiring and promotion practices
Implement non-discriminatory workplace policies
compliance_risk: Constitutional violation leading to nullity of internal regulations and potential human rights claims.
penalty: Civil and administrative liability; judicial orders to cease discriminatory actions.
source_reference: Biblioteca Virtual del Congreso Nacional de Honduras

definition: Prohibits employers from exerting any form of pressure or influence on workers to join or not join a union, or to participate in any political or religious activity, and prohibits discrimination based on race, sex, age, religion, or political affiliation.
compliance_requirements: Maintain neutral political and religious environment
Apply identical disciplinary rules to all staff
Ensure promotion is based on merit
compliance_risk: Fines by the Ministry of Labor and Social Security; labor lawsuits for unfair treatment.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 1 to 10 minimum wages; mandatory reinstatement if termination was based on discrimination.
source_reference: Secretaría de Trabajo y Seguridad Social

law_name: Ley de Igualdad de Oportunidades para la Mujer
definition: Provides a legal framework to guarantee equal opportunities for women in the labor market, prohibiting sexual harassment and gender-based discrimination.
compliance_requirements: Implement anti-sexual harassment protocols
Ensure equal pay for equal work
Prohibit questioning about pregnancy status during hiring
compliance_risk: Reputational damage and potential litigation regarding gender pay gap or harassment.
penalty: Fines imposed by the National Institute for Women (INAM) and Ministry of Labor; potential criminal charges for harassment.
source_reference: La Gaceta - Diario Oficial de Honduras

definition: The right of every worker to receive a complementary salary equivalent to one month of salary at the end of the year.
compliance_requirements: Pay the equivalent of one month's salary to all employees who have worked a full year.
Pro-rate payment for employees who worked less than a year.
Must be paid between December 10 and December 20.
compliance_risk: Back-pay claims and administrative fines by the Ministry of Labor.
penalty: Fines ranging from 3 to 10 minimum wages per affected employee.
source_reference: Secretaria de Trabajo y Seguridad Social (STSS)

definition: A mandatory bonus equivalent to one month of salary paid to workers in the middle of the year.
compliance_requirements: Payment must be made between June 15 and June 30 of each year.
Apply pro-rata for employees with less than one year of service.
compliance_risk: Litigation and labor inspections resulting in severe penalties.
penalty: Financial fines determined by the labor inspectorate based on the number of employees.
source_reference: Secretaria de Trabajo y Seguridad Social (STSS)

definition: Mandatory contributions to the Honduran Social Security Institute for health and pension coverage.
compliance_requirements: Register employer and employees with IHSS.
Make monthly employer and employee contributions.
Report changes in payroll and workforce status.
compliance_risk: Loss of social security benefits for workers and legal action against the employer for unpaid contributions.
penalty: Surcharges of 5% of unpaid contributions, plus interest and legal enforcement.

law_name: Labor Code - Severance and Pre-Labor Compensation
definition: Indemnification paid to the employee upon termination of the employment contract without just cause.
compliance_requirements: Calculate and pay 'Preaviso' (notice) based on length of service.
Calculate and pay 'Auxilio de Cesantía' (severance) based on years of service.
Settlement must be finalized within the timeframe established by law.
compliance_risk: Lawsuits for unfair dismissal and additional mandatory legal wages.
penalty: Full payment of outstanding benefits plus potential legal interest and lawyer fees.
source_reference: Secretaria de Trabajo y Seguridad Social (STSS)

definition: Entitlement of the employee to paid leave after one year of continuous service.
compliance_requirements: Grant 10 days for 1 year, 12 days for 2 years, 15 days for 3 years, and 20 days for 4 years or more.
Vacation must be paid in advance.
Record vacation dates in the employee file.
documentation_required: Proof of salary payment during vacation period
compliance_risk: Inspection penalties for failure to allow vacation or failure to pay corresponding wages.
penalty: Monetary fines and forced compliance to pay for missed leave.
source_reference: Secretaria de Trabajo y Seguridad Social (STSS)

definition: Regulates the obligation of the employer to keep precise records of wages, hours, and payments for all employees.
compliance_requirements: Maintain updated payroll ledgers
Record total hours worked
Detail all deductions made from salary
compliance_risk: Fines by the Ministry of Labor and potential lawsuits for unpaid wages.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 3 to 10 minimum monthly wages.
source_reference: Secretaría de Trabajo y Seguridad Social

definition: Mandates the registration of employees and the monthly reporting of payroll to the Honduran Social Security Institute (IHSS).
compliance_requirements: Register new hires within 3 days
Submit monthly payroll contribution reports
Pay employer and employee contributions
compliance_risk: Surcharges on unpaid contributions and potential criminal liability for withholding tax evasion.
penalty: Interest surcharges of 2% per month and fines of up to 20% of the total debt.

law_name: Ley de Fortalecimiento de los Ingresos, Equidad Social y Racionalización del Gasto Público
definition: Governs the payroll requirements for social contributions and tax reporting procedures related to professional services and employee compensation.
compliance_requirements: Reporting of non-recurring benefits
Compliance with reporting thresholds for tax purposes
documentation_required: Proof of payments for tax purposes
compliance_risk: Loss of tax deductibility for payroll expenses.
penalty: Administrative fines for non-disclosure.

definition: The obligation of the employer to maintain a register of employees and provide information to labor authorities.
compliance_requirements: Maintain a payroll register
Keep employee personnel files
Maintain records of hours worked and wages paid
compliance_risk: Sanctions by the Ministry of Labor and Social Security and potential legal labor disputes.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 1 to 10 minimum wages.
source_reference: Secretaría de Trabajo y Seguridad Social (STSS)

definition: Mandatory registration of all employees with the Honduran Social Security Institute (IHSS) within the first days of hiring.
compliance_requirements: Register the company with IHSS
Register all employees within 3 days of starting work
Report salary changes
compliance_risk: Fines for lack of coverage and liability for medical costs if an employee is not registered.
penalty: Fines equivalent to the unpaid premiums plus interest and potential legal action.

law_name: Ley de Protección de Datos Personales
definition: Regulates the collection and handling of employee sensitive data during the registration and onboarding process.
compliance_requirements: Obtain consent for data processing
Maintain physical and digital security of employee files
Use data only for labor/payroll purposes
compliance_risk: Privacy lawsuits and administrative sanctions for data breaches.
penalty: Warning, fines, or suspension of data processing activities.
source_reference: Instituto de Acceso a la Información Pública (IAIP)

definition: Regulates the relationship between employers and employees, establishing the mandatory requirements for labor contracts.
compliance_requirements: Formalize employment in written contracts for fixed-term or specific tasks.
Include mandatory clauses: identification of parties, salary, working hours, place of work, and benefits.
compliance_risk: Lack of written contract leads to the presumption that the employer is responsible for conditions stated by the employee.
penalty: Fines ranging from 50 to 5000 times the Unit of Measurement and Update (UMA).

definition: Regulates the mandatory registration of employees in the Mexican Social Security Institute (IMSS).
compliance_requirements: Register all employees with IMSS within 5 business days of hiring.
Report salary modifications.
Calculate and pay monthly social security quotas.
documentation_required: Proof of payment of quotas (EMA and EBA)
compliance_risk: Significant financial liability for healthcare costs and pension deficits in case of non-registration or under-reporting of salary.
penalty: Fines from 20 to 350 UMAs, plus surcharge interest and potential criminal liability for fraud.

definition: Governs employment where the majority of activities are performed at a location other than the employer's premises.
compliance_requirements: Provide equipment and tools to employees.
Pay proportional costs of internet and electricity.
Ensure the right to disconnect is respected.
documentation_required: Remote work annex to labor contract
compliance_risk: Reclassification of remote work as onsite work if documentation is missing; claims regarding work-life balance violations.
penalty: Administrative fines determined by the Labor Inspectorate.

law_name: Ley Federal de Protección de Datos Personales en Posesión de los Particulares
definition: Protects personal data collected by employers from employees (payroll, health records, etc.).
compliance_requirements: Draft and obtain employee signature for Privacy Notices.
Implement security measures to protect sensitive data.
Appoint a data privacy officer.
compliance_risk: High administrative fines and reputational damage due to data leaks or unauthorized data processing.
penalty: Fines up to 320,000 UMAs depending on the severity and nature of the data involved.
source_reference: INAI (Instituto Nacional de Transparencia, Acceso a la Información y Protección de Datos Personales)

definition: Establishes requirements for identifying, analyzing, and preventing psychosocial risk factors at work.
compliance_requirements: Conduct periodic evaluations of the workplace environment.
Establish mechanisms for reporting violent acts.
Implement policies for prevention of workplace violence.
compliance_risk: Labor inspection audits and penalties for failing to provide a safe work environment regarding mental health.
penalty: Fines ranging from 50 to 5000 UMAs per infraction.
source_reference: Secretaría del Trabajo y Previsión Social

law_name: Federal Labor Law (Ley Federal del Trabajo)
definition: Governs the existence of an employment relationship, defining it as the provision of a personal subordinate service to a person, through the payment of a salary. Mandates written contracts for all employment.
compliance_requirements: Maintain written employment contracts for all employees
Include identity details of parties
Specify work duration, service type, workplace, work hours, and salary
Include training and benefits details
compliance_risk: Presumption that the employer's statements are true in case of a dispute if a written contract is missing.
penalty: Fines ranging from 50 to 5000 times the Unit of Measurement and Update (UMA), depending on the severity and number of affected employees.

law_name: Federal Labor Law - Remote Work Provisions
definition: Regulates telework where more than 40% of the work is performed outside the employer's facilities using information and communication technologies.
compliance_requirements: Include telework conditions in the employment contract or addendum
Cover proportional costs of electricity and internet
Provide ergonomic furniture and computer equipment
Respect the right to disconnect
compliance_risk: Non-compliance with equipment provision and cost reimbursement can lead to labor inspections and lawsuits.
penalty: Fines per affected employee as established in Title Seventeen of the Federal Labor Law.

law_name: Federal Law on the Protection of Personal Data Held by Private Parties
definition: Governs the processing of personal data collected during the hiring and employment process, requiring consent and transparency.
compliance_requirements: Obtain signed Privacy Notice (Aviso de Privacidad)
Implement technical and administrative security measures for employee data
Limit data collection to necessary information
compliance_risk: Significant legal and reputational risk, including data breach exposure.
penalty: Fines based on the severity of the violation, potentially reaching millions of pesos.

law_name: Social Security Law (Ley del Seguro Social)
definition: Mandates the registration of employees with the Mexican Social Security Institute (IMSS) and payment of social security contributions.
compliance_requirements: Register employees with IMSS within 5 days of hiring
Report salary modifications
Pay contributions based on the Integrated Daily Wage (SBC)
compliance_risk: Fiscal audit by IMSS and liability for medical costs in case of unreported employees.
penalty: Fines, tax credits, and surcharge payments on outstanding contributions.

law_name: Constitución Política de los Estados Unidos Mexicanos
definition: Sets the maximum duration of the working day at eight hours.
compliance_requirements: Ensure workday does not exceed 8 hours for day shifts.
Ensure night shift does not exceed 7 hours.
Ensure mixed shift does not exceed 7.5 hours.
compliance_risk: Constitutional violation leading to potential lawsuits and administrative sanctions.
penalty: Administrative fines determined by the Ministry of Labor and Social Welfare.

definition: Defines the workday, provides limits for overtime, and mandates premium payments for extended hours.
compliance_requirements: Do not exceed 3 hours of overtime per day, maximum 3 times per week.
Pay 100% extra for overtime hours within the legal limit.
Pay 200% extra for overtime hours beyond the 9-hour weekly limit.
Mandate at least one rest day for every six days of work.
documentation_required: Proof of payment for extra hours
compliance_risk: Claims for unpaid wages, potential labor inspections, and fines for non-payment of overtime premiums.
penalty: Fines ranging from 50 to 5000 times the Unit of Measure and Update (UMA) per affected worker.

law_name: Reglamento Federal de Seguridad y Salud en el Trabajo
definition: Establishes provisions regarding working hours in relation to health, fatigue, and occupational safety.
compliance_requirements: Monitor working hours to prevent worker fatigue that could cause industrial accidents.
Adjust work shifts in hazardous environments.
compliance_risk: Increased accident liability, safety inspection failures, and potential closure of work sites.
penalty: Administrative fines and mandatory safety compliance orders.

definition: Regulates the mandatory social security system, covering healthcare, pension, and disability insurance contributions.
compliance_requirements: Register employees with IMSS within 5 days of hiring
Calculate and pay employer and employee contributions monthly/bimonthly
Report salary modifications (SBC changes)
compliance_risk: Imposition of fiscal credits, surcharges, and update fines.
penalty: Fines ranging from 20 to 350 times the UMA and potential criminal charges for tax evasion.

law_name: Ley del Instituto del Fondo Nacional de la Vivienda para los Trabajadores
definition: Mandatory employer contributions to the National Housing Fund for employees.
compliance_requirements: Register employer with INFONAVIT
Pay 5 percent of the base salary (SBC) as an employer contribution
Withhold and remit employee loan repayments
compliance_risk: Fiscal audit and tax liens on corporate assets.
penalty: Fines of 50 percent to 100 percent of the omitted contribution plus updates and surcharges.

law_name: Ley del Impuesto sobre la Renta
definition: Regulates the withholding and reporting of income tax for employees.
compliance_requirements: Withhold income tax from employee salaries
Issue CFDI digital tax receipts for payroll
Annual reconciliation of withheld taxes
compliance_risk: Non-deductibility of payroll expenses and tax penalties.
penalty: Fines for non-issuance of CFDI and interest/surcharges for delayed tax payments.

definition: General labor provisions establishing the requirement to maintain payroll documentation and records.
compliance_requirements: Maintain current payroll records
Provide pay stubs showing all deductions and contributions
compliance_risk: Reversal of the burden of proof in labor lawsuits.
penalty: Fines from 50 to 5000 times the UMA based on the severity of the omission.

law_name: Ley del Impuesto sobre la Renta
definition: The obligation of legal entities that make payments for independent personal services or salary/wages to withhold and pay the Income Tax (ISR) corresponding to their employees.
compliance_requirements: Calculate the tax due on every payment cycle
Withhold the calculated tax from employee wages
Deposit the withheld amounts to the Tax Administration Service (SAT)
Issue digital tax receipts (CFDI) for payroll
compliance_risk: High, including tax evasion audits and fiscal debt assessments by SAT.
penalty: Fines ranging from 55% to 75% of the omitted tax amount, plus surcharges and potential criminal charges for tax fraud.

definition: Obligation to register employees with the IMSS and report salary modifications to ensure contributions for medical care, disability, and retirement.
compliance_requirements: Register the company and employees with IMSS
Report current wages (SBC - Salario Base de Cotización)
Pay monthly employer/employee contributions
Update salary information within 5 business days of change
compliance_risk: High, leads to credit assessments (cedulas de liquidacion) and potential denial of public services for employees.
penalty: Fixed fines between 20 to 350 UMAs depending on the violation, plus capital constitutive payments if an accident occurs while unregistered.

definition: Employees are entitled to a paid annual vacation period after one year of service, which increases with seniority.
compliance_requirements: Provide minimum 12 days of vacation after the first year of service
Increase vacation days by 2 days per year until reaching 20 days
Grant vacation days within 6 months of the anniversary date
documentation_required: Payroll records proving vacation pay payment
compliance_risk: Failure to grant mandatory vacation days leads to fines and labor claims.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 50 to 5000 times the daily measure unit (UMA).

definition: Mandatory annual bonus equivalent to at least 15 days of salary, payable before December 20th.
compliance_requirements: Pay 15 days of salary as a minimum
Pay pro-rata for employees with less than one year of service
Ensure payment is made before December 20th
compliance_risk: Non-payment is a direct violation of labor rights subject to inspection.
penalty: Fines from 50 to 5000 UMAs per affected employee.

definition: Entitlement to a 12-week paid maternity leave (subsidized by IMSS) to protect the mother and child.
compliance_requirements: Register employee with IMSS correctly
Facilitate the issuance of the medical certificate by IMSS
Respect employment security during leave
documentation_required: Payroll records indicating social security contributions
compliance_risk: Discrimination or failure to support leave benefits can trigger heavy legal and administrative penalties.
penalty: Fines from 20 to 350 UMAs plus potential lawsuits for discrimination.

definition: Additional compensation paid to employees during their vacation period to cover extra expenses.
compliance_requirements: Pay minimum 25% of the salary corresponding to the vacation days granted
documentation_required: Payroll receipts showing vacation premium breakdown
compliance_risk: Underpayment of benefits is subject to Labor Ministry audit.
penalty: Administrative fines up to 5000 UMAs.

definition: Mandatory paid days off recognized by the law for all workers.
compliance_requirements: Provide leave on official holidays
Pay triple salary if an employee is required to work on an official holiday
documentation_required: Payroll records for holiday work payments
compliance_risk: Failing to pay triple time for worked holidays constitutes a wage violation.
penalty: Administrative fines.

definition: Mandates that employment conditions be documented in writing and specifies the minimum content required in labor contracts.
compliance_requirements: Provide written contract in Spanish
Specify duration of employment
Outline job duties, salary, benefits, and workplace location
Include training details
compliance_risk: Lack of written contract is attributed to the employer, leading to a presumption that all employee claims regarding conditions are true.
penalty: Fines ranging from 50 to 2500 times the daily UMA (Unit of Measurement and Update).

definition: Obligates employers to register employees with the Mexican Social Security Institute (IMSS) from the start of employment.
compliance_requirements: Register employee within 5 days of hiring
Report salary accurately including all integrated variables
Notify changes in salary or termination
compliance_risk: Financial liability for medical costs if employee is injured while unregistered and potential lawsuits.
penalty: Administrative fines, surcharges, and back-payment of social security contributions with penalties.

law_name: Federal Law for the Protection of Personal Data Held by Private Parties
law_code: Ley Federal de Protección de Datos Personales en Posesión de los Particulares
definition: Regulates the collection and processing of personal data of employees during the hiring and retention process.
compliance_requirements: Provide privacy notice (Aviso de Privacidad) to candidates
Obtain express consent for sensitive data
Implement technical security measures for physical and digital files
compliance_risk: Heavy fines and reputational damage due to data leaks or unauthorized processing of sensitive information.
penalty: Fines up to 320 million MXN depending on the severity and nature of the breach.

law_name: Federal Labor Law - Remote Work Regulation
definition: Regulates employment relationships where work is performed primarily outside the employer's premises using ICTs.
compliance_requirements: Include remote work clauses in the contract
Provide necessary equipment and ergonomic tools
Cover proportional costs related to internet and electricity
Respect the right to disconnect
documentation_required: Policy on digital communication and disconnect hours
compliance_risk: Claims of lack of equipment, unrecognized overtime due to improper digital time tracking, and costs reimbursement disputes.
penalty: Fines for breach of labor conditions established in the contract and LFT.

definition: Establishes identification, analysis, and prevention elements for psychosocial risk factors in the workplace.
compliance_requirements: Identify psychosocial risks during hiring and tenure
Create a policy for prevention of psychosocial risks
Conduct periodic evaluations
Keep records of assessments
compliance_risk: Inspections by the Ministry of Labor (STPS) leading to fines.
penalty: Fines from 250 to 5000 times the daily UMA.
source_reference: Secretaría del Trabajo y Previsión Social

law_name: Federal Labor Law (Ley Federal del Trabajo)
definition: Prohibits labor subcontracting for core business activities (subcontratación de personal). Subcontracting is only permitted for specialized services or execution of specialized works that are not part of the corporate purpose or the main economic activity of the beneficiary company.
compliance_requirements: Ensure specialized services do not overlap with the main corporate activity
Obtain REPSE (Registry of Providers of Specialized Services and Specialized Works) registration from the service provider
Verify that the provider is up to date with social security and tax obligations
documentation_required: Service agreement detailing the specialized nature of work
Proof of payment of social security contributions by the contractor
compliance_risk: High: Risk of being declared the joint employer (employer substitution) with full liability for labor and social security debts.
penalty: Fines ranging from 2,000 to 50,000 UMAs, potential criminal charges for tax evasion, and non-deductibility of expenses for tax purposes.

law_name: Social Security Law (Ley del Seguro Social)
definition: Establishes joint liability regarding social security contributions when specialized services are contracted, requiring the beneficiary to verify compliance by the service provider.
compliance_requirements: Provide quarterly information to the IMSS regarding subcontracting agreements
Maintain evidence of the contractor's compliance with IMSS payments
documentation_required: Quarterly reports filed via the IMSS ICSOE platform
Copies of tax receipts (CFDI) covering payroll and social security payments
compliance_risk: Joint liability for the payment of social security contributions for employees of the contractor.
penalty: Fines and credit assessments for unpaid social security quotas, surcharges, and penalties.

law_name: Federal Tax Code (Código Fiscal de la Federación)
definition: Sets forth the requirements for the tax deductibility and crediting of expenses derived from specialized service agreements, prohibiting the tax deduction of subcontracting costs that do not meet the legal requirements.
compliance_requirements: Ensure invoices include the specific data of the REPSE permit
Ensure the service rendered is clearly defined as specialized and not part of the beneficiary's main activity
documentation_required: Proof of payment for the specialized services
Tax compliance opinion (Opinión de Cumplimiento) of the contractor
compliance_risk: Non-deductibility of expenses for Income Tax purposes and inability to credit Value Added Tax (IVA).
penalty: Rejection of tax deductions, potential accusation of tax fraud for simulating specialized services.

definition: Specifies valid causes for immediate dismissal without employer liability, such as providing false information or repeated acts of dishonesty.
compliance_requirements: Provide a written notice of termination to the employee
Clearly state the cause of termination
Deliver notice within the legal timeline to the employee or to the labor court
documentation_required: Proof of delivery of the notice
Personnel file documenting the causes of dismissal
compliance_risk: High; failure to provide the notice correctly invalidates the dismissal and can lead to claims of unjustified dismissal.
penalty: Payment of three months' salary (indemnification), back pay for up to 12 months, and potential reinstatement if the employee requests it.
source_reference: Secretaría del Trabajo y Previsión Social (STPS)

definition: Governs the consequences of unjustified termination, entitling the employee to reinstatement or a severance package.
compliance_requirements: Provide three months' salary compensation
Pay accrued seniority premiums
Pay proportional benefits (vacation, bonus, aguinaldo)
documentation_required: Finiquito document (Itemized calculation of final payments)
Receipt of payment signed by the employee
compliance_risk: Extremely high; can lead to long-term litigation and mounting back-pay penalties.
penalty: Three months salary + 20 days per year of service + unpaid back pay (salarios caídos) up to one year plus interest.
source_reference: Secretaría del Trabajo y Previsión Social (STPS)

definition: Establishes the right of employees to receive a seniority premium upon separation from the company.
compliance_requirements: Calculate premium at 12 days of salary per year of service
Ensure the salary base for the calculation does not exceed two minimum wages if the standard salary is higher
compliance_risk: Medium; financial liability during audit or litigation.
penalty: Payment of the outstanding premium amount plus potential interest penalties determined by the labor court.
source_reference: Secretaría del Trabajo y Previsión Social (STPS)

law_name: Ley del Impuesto sobre la Renta
definition: Governs the tax treatment (withholding) of severance payments and gratuities paid to employees upon termination.
compliance_requirements: Calculate income tax on the portion of severance that exceeds the tax-exempt threshold
Issue tax receipts (CFDI) for the total severance amount
compliance_risk: Medium; potential tax audit issues regarding the classification of payments (income vs. compensation).
penalty: Tax fines, penalties for underreporting, and interest charges.

definition: Requirement for written employment contracts in Spanish to establish terms of employment.
compliance_requirements: Drafting individual employment contracts
Ensuring contracts are in writing
Including mandatory clauses regarding job functions and duration
compliance_risk: Lack of written contract shifts the burden of proof to the employer.
penalty: Fines ranging from 50 to 5000 times the daily minimum wage.
source_reference: Cámara de Diputados del H. Congreso de la Unión

law_name: Ley Federal de Protección de Datos Personales en Posesión de los Particulares
definition: Regulation of the processing and storage of employee personal data.
compliance_requirements: Maintain a privacy notice (Aviso de Privacidad)
Obtain explicit consent for sensitive data
Implement security measures for files
compliance_risk: Regulatory audits by INAI and significant economic sanctions.
penalty: Fines of up to 32 million MXN depending on the severity.

definition: Specific obligations for documenting remote work arrangements.
compliance_requirements: Include remote work terms in the employment contract
Maintain a health and safety checklist for remote workstations
compliance_risk: Reclassification of employment terms and labor lawsuits.
penalty: Labor inspections and administrative sanctions for non-compliance.
source_reference: Cámara de Diputados del H. Congreso de la Unión

law_name: Ley del Impuesto sobre la Renta
definition: Employer obligation to withhold and report income tax for employees.
compliance_requirements: Calculate annual tax adjustments
Issue digital tax receipts (CFDI) for payroll
Submit monthly tax declarations
compliance_risk: Disallowance of payroll as a business expense and tax penalties.
penalty: Fines from 55% to 75% of the omitted contributions plus interest.

definition: Regulates the relationship between employers and employees, establishing the minimum conditions for employment contracts.
compliance_requirements: Execution of written employment contracts
Registration of employees with IMSS
Maintenance of payroll records
compliance_risk: Reclassification of employment status, claims of seniority, and unjustified dismissal lawsuits.
penalty: Fines ranging from 50 to 5000 UMAs, plus payment of retroactive wages and severance.

definition: Mandates employer and employee contributions to the social security system covering healthcare, pension, and disability.
compliance_requirements: Register employer with IMSS
Register employees within 5 business days
Pay monthly social security quotas
compliance_risk: Capital Constitutivo (fines for not registering employees or reporting lower salaries).
penalty: Fines calculated based on the omission of quotas plus surcharges and indexation.

law_code: Ley del Impuesto sobre la Renta
definition: Regulates the withholding and reporting of Income Tax (ISR) from employee salaries.
compliance_requirements: Withhold ISR from employee wages
Issue digital tax receipts (CFDI)
Submit monthly and annual declarations
compliance_risk: Tax evasion allegations and fiscal audits.
penalty: Fines, tax credits, and potential criminal tax fraud charges.

law_name: Federal Law on Protection of Personal Data Held by Private Parties
definition: Regulates the processing and protection of personal data of employees collected by employers.
compliance_requirements: Provide privacy notice to employees
Obtain consent for sensitive data
Implement physical and technical security measures
compliance_risk: Data breaches and investigations by INAI.
penalty: Fines up to 320,000 UMAs depending on the severity of the violation.

definition: Establishes safety and health conditions for teleworkers, ensuring ergonomic and operational requirements.
compliance_requirements: Policy on telework
Assessment of workstations
Provision of equipment and ergonomics
compliance_risk: Non-compliance with STPS occupational health inspections.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 50 to 5000 UMAs per worker.
source_reference: Secretaría del Trabajo y Previsión Social

law_name: Political Constitution of the United Mexican States
law_code: Constitución Política de los Estados Unidos Mexicanos
definition: Recognizes the right to strike and the right of association for workers and employers.
compliance_requirements: Ensure workers right to form unions
Guarantee freedom of association
Recognize the right to collective bargaining
compliance_risk: Constitutional violation leading to nullification of employment practices.
penalty: Constitutional sanctions and potential international labor treaty disputes.

definition: Governs the collective bargaining agreement (CCT), which is the agreement between one or several unions and one or several employers to establish conditions under which work is performed.
compliance_requirements: Negotiate and sign a Collective Bargaining Agreement
Register the CCT with the Federal Center for Conciliation and Labor Registration
Ensure union democratic legitimacy via personal, free, secret, and direct voting
Distribute copies of the CCT to all workers
compliance_risk: Loss of CCT validity, union strikes, and fines for lack of transparency.
penalty: Fines ranging from 50 to 5000 times the daily wage unit (UMA) for non-compliance with collective rights.

definition: Requires the legitimation of existing collective bargaining agreements through a secret ballot vote by workers.
compliance_requirements: Conduct a legitimation process for existing agreements
Submit the voting results to the Federal Center for Conciliation and Labor Registration
compliance_risk: Termination of the collective bargaining agreement if not legitimated by the deadline.
penalty: Invalidation of the collective contract.

definition: Governs the right of workers and employers to form unions, the protection of union autonomy, and the internal regulation of union activities.
compliance_requirements: Recognize the right of employees to associate freely.
Refrain from interference in union internal affairs.
Provide unionized employees with rights granted under collective agreements.
Conduct union voting by personal, free, direct, and secret ballot.
compliance_risk: Risk of being found guilty of unfair labor practices, including union interference or blacklisting.
penalty: Fines ranging from 50 to 5000 times the daily minimum wage per affected worker, plus potential nullification of employer actions.

definition: Requires the legitimation of collective bargaining agreements through a personal, free, direct, and secret vote of the workers.
compliance_requirements: Submit collective agreements for worker validation.
Ensure the Center for Federal Conciliation and Labor Registration (CFCRL) oversees voting.
Ensure transparency in the dissemination of agreement terms to employees.
documentation_required: Constancia de Legitimación issued by CFCRL.
Verification report from the voting process.
Proof of distribution of the collective agreement to all employees.
compliance_risk: Loss of the collective bargaining agreement's validity and legal recognition.
penalty: Termination of the collective agreement and potential labor conflict exposure.
source_reference: Centro Federal de Conciliación y Registro Laboral (CFCRL)

law_name: Political Constitution of the United Mexican States
law_code: Constitución Política de los Estados Unidos Mexicanos
definition: Establishes the fundamental constitutional right of workers and employers to associate for the defense of their respective interests by forming unions or professional associations.
compliance_requirements: Guarantee the constitutional right to strike.
Ensure no worker is forced to join or abstain from joining a union.
Allow collective bargaining without coercion.
documentation_required: Bylaws of the enterprise confirming adherence to constitutional labor rights.
compliance_risk: Constitutional violations leading to lawsuits in federal courts.
penalty: Potential constitutional injunctions (Amparo) and government mandated corrective actions.
source_reference: Constitución Política de los Estados Unidos Mexicanos

law_name: Federal Labor Law (Ley Federal del Trabajo)
definition: Regulates telework where more than 40% of the working time is performed at a non-employer location, requiring a formal agreement.
compliance_requirements: Formalize a written telework agreement as part of the employment contract
Provide, install and maintain necessary equipment (computers, ergonomic chairs, etc.)
Assume responsibility for proportional costs of internet and electricity
Ensure the right to disconnect
Maintain a registry of teleworkers
Implement ergonomic, psychosocial, and health and safety policies
compliance_risk: Risk of being classified as regular employment without the necessary safety and cost-reimbursement protections, resulting in labor lawsuits and back-pay claims.
penalty: Fines ranging from 50 to 5000 UMAs (Unit of Measurement and Accommodation) depending on the violation severity and number of affected employees.

definition: Establishes safety and health conditions in telework to prevent accidents and occupational diseases.
compliance_requirements: Perform a verification list of safety and health conditions at the remote workplace
Establish a policy for telework
Provide training on health and safety for teleworkers
Maintain current records of telework safety assessments
compliance_risk: Fines from the Ministry of Labor for failing to ensure employee safety in remote environments.
penalty: Administrative fines up to 5000 UMAs per violation.
keywords: Seguridad y Salud en el Trabajo
source_reference: Secretaría del Trabajo y Previsión Social (STPS)

law_name: Federal Law on Protection of Personal Data Held by Private Parties
definition: Governs the processing of personal data in remote work environments to ensure confidentiality and security.
compliance_requirements: Update privacy notices to reflect remote processing
Implement technical security measures for remote access
Ensure data protection during remote communication
compliance_risk: Heavy sanctions for data breaches resulting from insecure remote work practices.
penalty: Fines up to 320 million MXN for serious breaches, or criminal charges in cases of data misuse.
source_reference: Instituto Nacional de Transparencia, Acceso a la Información y Protección de Datos Personales (INAI)

law_name: Ley Federal de Protección de Datos Personales en Posesión de los Particulares (LFPDPPP)
definition: Federal law regulating the processing of personal data by private entities to guarantee the right to informational self-determination, privacy, and protection of personal data.
compliance_requirements: Obtain express consent for sensitive data
Provide a Privacy Notice (Aviso de Privacidad) to employees
Implement technical, physical, and administrative security measures
Appoint a data privacy officer or department
Limit data collection to necessary purposes only
documentation_required: Contracts with third-party service providers (Data Processors)
compliance_risk: Improper handling of employee sensitive data, unauthorized data disclosure, or lack of proper privacy disclosures.
penalty: Fines ranging from 100 to 320,000 times the daily minimum wage, depending on the severity of the violation and potential recurrence, or imprisonment in cases of intentional illicit processing.

law_name: Reglamento de la Ley Federal de Protección de Datos Personales en Posesión de los Particulares
definition: The regulatory framework providing specific procedures for the implementation of the LFPDPPP, including requirements for data protection impact assessments and specific privacy notice contents.
compliance_requirements: Establish internal procedures for ARCO rights (Access, Rectification, Cancellation, Opposition)
Maintain a personal data filing system
Ensure security measures are proportional to the sensitivity of the data
documentation_required: Training records for staff handling employee data
compliance_risk: Failure to provide a mechanism for employees to exercise their data rights (ARCO).
penalty: Administrative sanctions, fines, and mandatory corrective actions imposed by the INAI (National Institute for Transparency, Access to Information and Personal Data Protection).

definition: Sets the fundamental obligations for employers to provide a safe and healthy working environment, including accident prevention and necessary medical kits.
compliance_requirements: Provide training on accident prevention
Maintain appropriate health and safety equipment
Establish mixed health and safety commissions
documentation_required: Minutes from Joint Health and Safety Commission meetings
compliance_risk: High, including civil liability and potential criminal negligence for fatal accidents.
penalty: Fines ranging from 50 to 5000 times the Unit of Measurement and Update (UMA).

law_name: General Regulation on Workplace Safety and Health
law_code: Reglamento Federal de Seguridad y Salud en el Trabajo
definition: Detailed regulatory framework establishing the technical requirements for prevention, protection, and safety in all Mexican workplaces.
compliance_requirements: Conduct hazard identification and risk assessment
Implement internal safety programs
Provide personal protective equipment (PPE)
documentation_required: Preventive program for safety and health
compliance_risk: Fines from the Secretariat of Labor and Social Welfare (STPS) during audits.
penalty: Fines up to 5000 UMA per violation.

definition: Regulation focused on identifying, analyzing, and preventing psychosocial risk factors in the workplace, including stress and workplace violence.
compliance_requirements: Identify and analyze psychosocial risk factors
Establish a policy for the prevention of psychosocial risks
Promote an organizational culture of respect
compliance_risk: Non-compliance during STPS inspections.
penalty: Fines based on the number of non-compliant employees.

definition: Establishes safety and health conditions specifically for employees performing telework to prevent accidents and occupational diseases at home.
compliance_requirements: Conduct verification checklists for the telework station
Ensure ergonomic conditions
Establish a policy on telework
compliance_risk: Legal inability to defend against workplace injury claims occurring at the home office.
penalty: Administrative fines under the Federal Labor Law.

definition: The legal framework regulating the entry, exit, transit, and stay of foreigners in Mexico, specifically governing the authorization for foreign nationals to perform remunerated activities.
compliance_requirements: Ensure foreign employees hold a temporary resident permit with work authorization (Residente Temporal con permiso para trabajar).
Registration of the employer with the National Migration Institute (INM) via the Constancia de Inscripción de Empleador (CIE).
Update INM records within 15 working days if there are changes in employment status or contract termination.
documentation_required: Offer of employment (Oferta de empleo) stamped by INM.
compliance_risk: High. Unauthorized employment can lead to immediate sanctions for the employer and deportation for the employee.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 20 to 100 times the Unit of Measurement and Update (UMA), plus potential suspension of employer registration.

law_name: Regulations of the Migration Law (Reglamento de la Ley de Migración)
law_code: Reglamento de la Ley de Migración
definition: Provides specific procedural details for the application and maintenance of work permits for foreign nationals under the supervision of the Ministry of the Interior (SEGOB).
compliance_requirements: Submit notifications for any changes in the employee's personal data or professional activity.
Comply with reporting requirements regarding foreign staff headcounts.
documentation_required: Format for the request of visa/permit update.
Legal representation documents for the employer entity.
Certificate of tax compliance (Opinión de cumplimiento).
compliance_risk: Moderate. Failure to report administrative changes leads to loss of good standing with INM.
penalty: Fines indexed to UMA and potential denial of future work authorization requests.

law_name: Federal Labor Law (Ley Federal del Trabajo)
definition: Limits the percentage of foreign workers an employer can hire, ensuring a preference for Mexican nationals in the workforce.
compliance_requirements: Ensure foreign workers do not exceed 10% of the total staff in any given company.
Exclude specialized or technical staff from the percentage calculation as defined by the Federal Labor Law.
documentation_required: Documentation proving the technical necessity of foreign staff to justify exceptions to the 10% limit.
compliance_risk: Moderate. Failure to comply with national quotas can lead to labor inspections.
penalty: Fines from 50 to 2,500 times the UMA.

law_name: Political Constitution of the United Mexican States
law_code: Constitucion Politica de los Estados Unidos Mexicanos
definition: Prohibits all forms of discrimination based on ethnic or national origin, gender, age, disabilities, social condition, health status, religion, opinions, sexual preferences, marital status or any other that violates human dignity.
compliance_requirements: Ensure non-discriminatory hiring practices
Implement internal policies that guarantee equal treatment
Avoid criteria that exclude individuals based on protected characteristics
compliance_risk: High, risk of human rights litigation and constitutional challenges
penalty: Nullification of discriminatory acts, potential civil and human rights liability

definition: Establishes that labor relations must be based on respect for human dignity and that employers are forbidden from discriminating in hiring or conditions of work.
compliance_requirements: Prohibition of asking for pregnancy tests
Prohibition of age or gender requirements in job postings
Equal pay for equal work
compliance_risk: High, subject to inspections by the Ministry of Labor (STPS)
penalty: Fines ranging from 50 to 5000 units of measurement and updating (UMA)

law_name: Federal Law to Prevent and Eliminate Discrimination
law_code: Ley Federal para Prevenir y Eliminar la Discriminacion
definition: Framework law aimed at preventing and eliminating all forms of discrimination carried out by individuals or private entities, including workplace settings.
compliance_requirements: Implementation of mechanisms to identify and eliminate discriminatory practices
Duty to report and investigate internal claims of discrimination
compliance_risk: Medium to High, risk of investigation by CONAPRED
penalty: Public reprimands, administrative fines, or mediation/reparation requirements

law_name: NOM-025-STPS-2015 on Labor Equality and Non-Discrimination
definition: A standard that provides the requirements for the integration and implementation of a labor equality and non-discrimination management system in workplaces.
compliance_requirements: Maintain a certification system for labor equality
Conduct periodic audits of labor policies
Provide equal access to training and development
documentation_required: Labor equality and non-discrimination management system documentation
compliance_risk: Medium, loss of certification and reputational damage
penalty: Administrative sanctions by labor authorities for non-compliance with STPS standards

definition: The right of employees to receive an annual bonus payment equivalent to at least 15 days of salary, payable before December 20th.
compliance_requirements: Calculate bonus based on at least 15 days of salary
Ensure payment is made no later than December 20th
Pro-rate payment for employees who worked less than one full year
compliance_risk: High
penalty: Fine ranging from 50 to 5000 times the Unit of Measure and Update (UMA).

definition: The right to annual paid vacation time, which increases based on years of service, with a mandatory vacation premium.
compliance_requirements: Grant minimum 12 days after the first year
Provide a minimum 25% salary premium on top of vacation pay
Schedule vacation time within six months following the anniversary of employment
compliance_risk: Medium
penalty: Fines ranging from 50 to 2500 times the UMA per affected employee.

definition: Employer obligation to register employees with the Mexican Social Security Institute (IMSS) and contribute to their social security, including health, disability, and retirement.
compliance_requirements: Register employees with IMSS within 5 days of hiring
Submit monthly contributions (SBC)
Maintain current payroll data for contributions
documentation_required: Payroll reports for INFONAVIT and IMSS
compliance_risk: Extreme
penalty: Fixed and proportional fines, potential criminal liability for fraud, and back-payment of premiums with interest.

law_name: Ley del Instituto del Fondo Nacional de la Vivienda para los Trabajadores (INFONAVIT)
definition: The obligation for employers to contribute 5% of the employee's base salary into an individual housing account administered by INFONAVIT.
compliance_requirements: Submit 5% employer contribution bi-monthly
Monitor employee housing credit deductions if applicable
compliance_risk: High
penalty: Fines, tax credit collection procedures, and legal interest on unpaid contributions.

definition: The right of employees to participate in a percentage of the company's annual profits (PTU - Participación de los Trabajadores en las Utilidades).
compliance_requirements: Distribute 10% of taxable income as calculated by the tax authority
Form a joint commission to determine distribution
Pay within 60 days after the annual tax declaration is due
compliance_risk: High
penalty: Fines up to 5000 times the UMA per worker, potential inspection audits by the Ministry of Labor.

definition: Obligation of the employer to register employees and report salaries to the Mexican Social Security Institute (IMSS).
compliance_requirements: Register employees within 5 days of hiring
Report salary modifications within 5 days
Calculate and pay monthly employer-employee social security quotas (SUA/IDSE)
compliance_risk: High; audit risk for underreporting salary (SBC), penalties, and loss of tax deductible status for social security payments.
penalty: Fines ranging from 20 to 350 UMAs depending on the severity of the omission, plus surcharges and update fees.

law_name: Ley del Impuesto sobre la Renta (LISR)
definition: Obligation to withhold and report income tax (ISR) from employee salaries and provide digital tax receipts (CFDI).
compliance_requirements: Calculate ISR withholding
Issue CFDI payroll receipts (CFDI de Nómina 4.0)
Submit annual payroll declarations
Provide annual tax statements to employees
compliance_risk: Severe; improper CFDIs make payroll expenses non-deductible for corporate income tax purposes.
penalty: Fines, fiscal non-deductibility, and potential criminal tax fraud charges for deliberate evasion.

law_name: Ley del Instituto del Fondo Nacional de la Vivienda para los Trabajadores (INFONAVIT)
definition: Mandatory employer contribution to the employee's housing fund, reported via the same channels as IMSS.
compliance_requirements: Report employee wages for contribution calculation
Withhold and remit employee housing loan repayments
compliance_risk: Moderate to High; interest and late payment surcharges apply to unpaid contributions.
penalty: Fines from 3 to 350 UMAs, plus legal interest on unpaid housing fund contributions.

definition: General obligation of the employer to provide proof of payment and maintain payroll records in accordance with the law.
compliance_requirements: Provide receipt of payment for wages
Maintain accurate payroll accounting
Comply with PTU (Profit Sharing) reporting requirements
compliance_risk: Moderate; violation may lead to inspection by the Ministry of Labor (STPS) and individual labor lawsuits.
penalty: Fines ranging from 50 to 5,000 UMAs depending on the specific violation.

definition: Mandates the registration and formalization of employment relationships including obligations for subcontracting and direct employment.
compliance_requirements: Formalize employment contracts in writing
Register employees with the relevant labor authorities
Ensure subcontracting services (REPSE) are registered if applicable
compliance_risk: High; labor authorities may presume an employment relationship exists under unfavorable terms for the employer if contracts are not formalized.
penalty: Fines ranging from 250 to 5000 times the daily UMA (Unit of Measurement and Update).

law_name: Ley Federal de Protección de Datos Personales en Posesión de los Particulares
definition: Mandates the handling of sensitive personal data collected during the employee registration and onboarding process.
compliance_requirements: Provide privacy notice to employees
Obtain consent for sensitive data processing
Maintain internal data security controls
compliance_risk: Moderate; risk of administrative fines and reputational damage.
penalty: Fines up to 320,000 times the UMA for severe violations.
source_reference: INAI (Instituto Nacional de Transparencia, Acceso a la Información y Protección de Datos Personales)

definition: The primary legal framework governing employer-employee relationships, working hours, contracts, and termination in Nicaragua.
compliance_requirements: Formalization of written employment contracts
Adherence to maximum working hours (48 hours per week)
Payment of legal minimum wage
Provision of legally mandated benefits
compliance_risk: Fines, mandatory payment of back wages, and potential lawsuits for unfair dismissal.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 1 to 20 minimum wages depending on the gravity of the infraction.

definition: Mandatory social insurance scheme providing coverage for health, maternity, disability, old age, and death.
compliance_requirements: Registration of the company and all employees with the INSS
Timely monthly payment of employer and employee contributions
Updating employee status changes (hiring/termination)
compliance_risk: Accumulation of debt, interest, and surcharges; inability to process retirement or medical claims for employees.
penalty: Fines, surcharges on late payments, and potential freezing of business bank accounts.

definition: Governs the taxation of income generated by labor, including withholding requirements for employers.
compliance_requirements: Withholding income tax (IR) from employee salaries based on established brackets
Monthly submission of tax withholdings to the DGI
Annual reconciliation reporting
compliance_risk: Tax evasion penalties, audit scrutiny, and heavy financial fines.
penalty: Fines of 10% to 100% of the omitted tax, plus interest.

law_name: Constitución Política de la República de Nicaragua
definition: Establishes labor as a right and social obligation, protecting the freedom to choose work and the right to collective bargaining.
compliance_requirements: Guarantee equal opportunity for all employees
Respecting the right to organize and form unions
Ensuring a safe working environment
compliance_risk: Constitutional claims and labor lawsuits alleging violations of fundamental human rights.
penalty: Constitutional protection suits (Recursos de Amparo) and damages.

law_name: Reglamento de Higiene y Seguridad del Trabajo
definition: Regulates the conditions under which work must be performed to protect the health and physical integrity of the worker.
compliance_requirements: Implementation of a safety committee
Risk assessment at the workplace
Provision of safety equipment (EPP)
Training on occupational hazards
compliance_risk: Temporary closure of facilities, heavy fines, and civil/criminal liability in case of workplace accidents.
penalty: Fines ranging from 5 to 50 minimum wages for severe safety violations.

definition: The labor contract is the agreement whereby a person commits to provide personal services to an employer under subordination in exchange for remuneration.
compliance_requirements: Must be in writing for contracts with a duration greater than one month
Must include employer/employee identification, duration, place of work, salary, and job description
Must be signed in triplicate
documentation_required: Three original copies of the signed employment contract
compliance_risk: Existence of verbal contracts deemed to follow employer declarations; potential lawsuits claiming indefinite contract status.
penalty: Fines calculated based on the Minimum Wage scale; nullity of illegal contract clauses.

law_name: Political Constitution of the Republic of Nicaragua
definition: Establishes that workers have the right to stable employment and protection against arbitrary dismissal.
compliance_requirements: Ensuring contracts align with constitutional protections against forced labor and discrimination
compliance_risk: Constitutional appeal (Recurso de Amparo) by the employee for violation of fundamental labor rights.
penalty: Reinstatement or full payment of severance indemnification.

definition: Provides administrative guidance for the registration and formality of employment contracts.
compliance_requirements: Mandatory filing of contract copies with the Ministry of Labor (MITRAB) in certain sectors
compliance_risk: Administrative sanctions by labor inspectors during audits.
penalty: Monetary fines for administrative non-compliance.

law_name: Código del Trabajo de la República de Nicaragua
definition: Establishes the maximum duration of the ordinary workday as 8 hours per day and 48 hours per week for day shifts.
compliance_requirements: Ensure workday does not exceed 8 hours for day shifts
Monitor total weekly hours not to exceed 48
Maintain accurate time logs for all employees
compliance_risk: Fines by the Ministry of Labor and payment of unpaid overtime claims
penalty: Administrative fines determined by the Ministry of Labor based on the severity and number of affected employees
source_reference: Ley No. 185, Código del Trabajo de Nicaragua, La Gaceta No. 205

law_name: Código del Trabajo de la República de Nicaragua
definition: Defines the day shift (jornada diurna) as between 6:00 AM and 8:00 PM; night shift (jornada nocturna) as between 8:00 PM and 6:00 AM.
compliance_requirements: Apply correct shift classification for night work
Pay legal surcharges for night work hours
documentation_required: Payroll receipts indicating night shift surcharges
compliance_risk: Lawsuits for unpaid night shift differentials
penalty: Payment of retroactive differentials plus interest and administrative fines
source_reference: Ley No. 185, Código del Trabajo de Nicaragua

law_name: Código del Trabajo de la República de Nicaragua
definition: Regulates the mandatory surcharge for overtime hours worked, requiring a minimum of 100% additional pay for extra hours.
compliance_requirements: Obtain employee consent for overtime
Ensure overtime does not exceed legal daily limits
Calculate overtime with the 100% surcharge on base hourly rate
compliance_risk: High risk of labor claims and inspection penalties from MITRAB
penalty: Double pay for hours not compensated at the correct rate plus potential labor court sanctions
source_reference: Ley No. 185, Código del Trabajo de Nicaragua

definition: Governs the compulsory affiliation and contribution system for workers and employers to the Nicaraguan Institute of Social Security (INSS) covering illness, maternity, disability, old age, and death.
compliance_requirements: Mandatory registration of the employer with the INSS
Mandatory enrollment of employees within 3 days of hiring
Timely payment of monthly employer and employee contributions
Reporting of salary adjustments and staff movements
compliance_risk: Financial liability for unpaid benefits, administrative sanctions, and legal claims from employees.
penalty: Fines calculated as a percentage of the debt, interest on late payments, and potential legal prosecution for embezzlement of worker contributions.

definition: Regulates the fundamental rights and obligations between employers and workers, specifically regarding the payment of salaries, the 13th month bonus, and vacation pay.
compliance_requirements: Payment of salary within the agreed timeframe
Payment of the 13th month salary (Aguinaldo) in December
Provision of legally mandated vacation time and payment
Adherence to the national minimum wage schedule
compliance_risk: Labor lawsuits, Ministry of Labor inspections, and forced payment of accrued benefits plus interest.
penalty: Administrative fines imposed by the Ministry of Labor (MITRAB) based on the number of employees affected.

definition: Establishes the income tax (IR) obligations for individuals (employees) which the employer must withhold and pay to the tax authority (DGI).
compliance_requirements: Withholding of Income Tax (IR) from employee salaries based on the progressive tax table
Monthly submission of withholding declarations
Annual reconciliation of withheld taxes
Issuance of withholding certificates to employees
documentation_required: Annual income tax report for employees
compliance_risk: Tax audits, penalties, interest on delayed payments, and criminal charges for tax evasion.
penalty: Monetary fines based on the amount of unpaid tax, interest, and surcharges.

definition: Regulates the Impuesto sobre la Renta (IR) applicable to labor income (Rentades del Trabajo), establishing progressive tax brackets for employee salaries.
compliance_requirements: Withhold income tax from employee salaries monthly
Submit monthly declarations using Form 401
Provide annual tax withholding certificates to employees
Maintain updated payroll records for fiscal audit purposes
documentation_required: Proof of withholding payments to the DGI
compliance_risk: High risk of fiscal audits, imposition of surcharges, and potential criminal liability for tax evasion.
penalty: Fines calculated based on the tax due plus interest (per diem), and administrative sanctions for failure to file or pay.

definition: Mandates compulsory contributions from both the employer and the employee to the Nicaraguan Social Security Institute (INSS) to fund health, disability, old age, and death benefits.
compliance_requirements: Register the company and employees with the INSS
Calculate and deduct employee contributions (INSS Laboral)
Pay employer contributions (INSS Patronal)
Submit monthly payroll reports via the VUI platform
compliance_risk: Interruption of employee social security coverage and severe financial penalties.
penalty: Fixed fines per employee, administrative surcharges, and prohibition to participate in public bidding processes.

definition: Governs the obligation of employers to maintain accurate wage payment records and provides the legal basis for payroll deductions allowed by law.
compliance_requirements: Maintain accurate wage records
Ensure timely payment of salaries and benefits
Deduct only legally authorized amounts from the payroll
compliance_risk: Labor lawsuits, claims for unpaid benefits, and administrative fines by the Ministry of Labor (MITRAB).
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from specific wage units and potential orders to pay arrears with interest.

definition: The right of the employee to enjoy a paid rest period after completing six months of continuous service with the same employer.
compliance_requirements: Provide 15 days of paid leave for every 6 months of continuous work
Grant leave within 6 months of the entitlement date
Pay leave salary based on the average ordinary and extraordinary wages earned in the last 6 months
documentation_required: Proof of payment for vacation days
compliance_risk: Fines by the Ministry of Labor (MITRAB) and risk of legal claims for accumulated or unpaid vacation
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 500 to 5,000 Cordobas depending on the severity and number of affected employees
source_reference: Codigo del Trabajo de Nicaragua, Ley 185

definition: Protection and paid leave granted to pregnant employees during the pre-natal and post-natal period.
compliance_requirements: Grant 4 weeks pre-natal and 8 weeks post-natal leave
Ensure the employee receives the full salary through Social Security (INSS) and employer contribution
Prohibit dismissal during pregnancy and the maternity leave period
documentation_required: Medical certificate confirming pregnancy and expected delivery date
Payroll records showing maternity leave status
compliance_risk: Heavy legal penalties for wrongful termination during maternity protection period
penalty: Payment of salaries for the entire duration of protected period plus potential reinstatement costs
source_reference: Codigo del Trabajo de Nicaragua, Ley 185

definition: Compensation for temporary incapacity to work due to common illness or non-occupational accident.
compliance_requirements: Facilitate the submission of INSS medical leave certificates
Pay the employer's portion (20%) of the salary during the medical leave period
Guarantee reinstatement upon recovery
compliance_risk: Litigation for breach of social security obligations or failure to rehire
penalty: Payment of back wages and potential fines for social security non-compliance

definition: Mandatory paid non-working days designated by the state.
compliance_requirements: Grant paid leave on specified national holidays
Pay double wages if the employee is required to work during a mandatory public holiday
documentation_required: Payroll records reflecting overtime or holiday pay
compliance_risk: Labor inspection sanctions for non-payment of holiday premiums
penalty: Payment of unpaid wages plus interest and administrative fines
source_reference: Codigo del Trabajo de Nicaragua, Ley 185

definition: Mandatory additional month of salary payable to employees who have worked a minimum of 3 months in the year.
compliance_requirements: Pay the thirteenth month salary in the first 10 days of December
Proportional payment for employees who worked less than a full year
Must be tax-exempt up to a certain threshold
documentation_required: Payroll proof of payment for the aguinaldo
compliance_risk: Legal claims for unpaid benefits and significant fines by the Ministry of Labor
penalty: Payment of the bonus plus interest and potential double payment in case of litigation
source_reference: Ley No. 62, Ley del Decimo Tercer Mes

definition: Governs the formalization of the employment relationship, defining the requirements and characteristics of employment contracts.
compliance_requirements: Execution of a written employment contract in triplicate
Registration of the contract with the Ministry of Labor (MITRAB)
Provision of a copy of the contract to the employee
Verification of legal age for employment
compliance_risk: Risk of presumption of an indefinite employment contract if written documentation is lacking or defective.
penalty: Fines imposed by the Ministry of Labor and potential payment of back-pay for undeclared conditions.
source_reference: La Gaceta, Diario Oficial No. 205 del 30 de octubre de 1996

law_name: Law of the Nicaraguan Social Security Institute (INSS)
definition: Mandatory enrollment requirement for every employee hired in the territory to guarantee social security coverage.
compliance_requirements: Register the employer with the INSS
Enroll the employee in the system within the first three days of hiring
Deduct and remit social security contributions
compliance_risk: Financial liability for medical expenses if an accident occurs and the employee is not registered.
penalty: Monetary fines, interest on unpaid contributions, and criminal liability in cases of extreme negligence.

definition: Regulates the collection, processing, and storage of personal information of employees during the hiring process.
compliance_requirements: Obtain informed consent from candidates
Limit data collection to necessary employment purposes
Implement security measures to protect sensitive employee data
compliance_risk: Legal action for unauthorized disclosure or misuse of candidate/employee personal information.
penalty: Administrative sanctions and potential civil lawsuits for damages.
source_reference: La Gaceta, Diario Oficial No. 58 del 26 de marzo de 2012

law_name: Political Constitution of the Republic of Nicaragua
definition: Establishes the right to work as a fundamental right and prohibits discrimination in the hiring process.
compliance_requirements: Prohibition of discrimination based on birth, nationality, political opinion, race, gender, language, religion, or economic position
Guaranteeing equal opportunity in employment
documentation_required: Internal labor regulations (Reglamento Interno de Trabajo)
compliance_risk: Constitutional protection claims (Recurso de Amparo) if hiring practices are found discriminatory.
penalty: Nullification of discriminatory hiring practices and potential damages.

definition: Establishes the principle of 'Primacy of Reality', where the labor relationship is determined by the actual performance of work rather than the name given to the contract by the parties.
compliance_requirements: Verify if the worker is subject to schedules and employer control
Ensure contractor independence is maintained in practice
Avoid signing civil service contracts for roles that constitute permanent employment
compliance_risk: High risk of reclassification of independent contractors as employees (labor subordination)
penalty: Back payment of social security contributions (INSS), payment of all labor benefits (aguinaldo, vacations, seniority indemnity), and administrative fines by MITRAB
source_reference: Ley No. 185, Código del Trabajo de la República de Nicaragua

definition: Defines the scope of compulsory affiliation, mandating that all persons providing services under subordination must be registered with the INSS.
compliance_requirements: Ensure that individuals classified as contractors are not performing duties inherent to the employer's business
Avoid using contractor labels to evade social security contributions
compliance_risk: Fiscal liability for unpaid social security contributions plus interest and surcharges
penalty: Fines from the Nicaraguan Social Security Institute (INSS) and forced retroactive affiliation of workers
source_reference: Decreto No. 975, Ley de Seguridad Social

definition: Regulates the withholding taxes (IR) applicable to independent professionals versus employees.
compliance_requirements: Correct withholding of income tax (IR) for service providers
Proper classification for VAT (IVA) compliance
compliance_risk: Tax evasion audits and potential reclassification of labor costs
penalty: Fiscal penalties, interest, and fines based on the DGI (Dirección General de Ingresos) tax code
source_reference: Ley No. 822, Ley de Concertación Tributaria

law_name: Labor Code of the Republic of Nicaragua
definition: Regulates the termination of an indefinite employment contract by the employer without just cause, requiring the payment of severance (antigüedad).
compliance_requirements: Payment of severance pay based on years of service
Payment of pro-rated 13th month
Payment of pro-rated vacation
Notification to the Ministry of Labor
documentation_required: Approval from the Ministry of Labor
compliance_risk: High risk of labor lawsuits and reinstatement orders.
penalty: Payment of indemnity equivalent to one month of salary per year of service up to a maximum of five months, plus back wages if the employee wins a reinstatement suit.

law_name: Labor Code of the Republic of Nicaragua
definition: Defines just causes for termination of employment contract without employer liability (despido con causa justificada).
