compliance_risk: Interruption of medical coverage for employees, lawsuits, and high financial penalties for unpaid contributions.
penalty: Surcharges of 1.5% per month of delay, plus administrative fines and potential closure of business premises for persistent non-compliance.

definition: The right of the worker to a mandatory period of paid rest after one year of continuous service.
compliance_requirements: Grant 12 working days of paid leave for up to 5 years of service
Grant 18 working days for 5 to 10 years of service
Grant 30 working days for more than 10 years of service
documentation_required: Payroll records proving salary payment during leave
compliance_risk: Litigation for denied leave and administrative fines by the Ministry of Labor.
penalty: Fines ranging from 10 to 30 minimum daily wages per affected employee.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo, Empleo y Seguridad Social

definition: Mandatory leave for pregnant employees consisting of 18 weeks (126 days) of paid rest.
compliance_requirements: Ensure 18 weeks of rest
Ensure the employee receives compensation equivalent to her salary during the leave period
documentation_required: IPS (Social Security) registration for subsidy payments
compliance_risk: Discriminatory firing lawsuits and substantial labor fines.
penalty: Fines and mandatory reinstatement of the employee if dismissed without cause.

law_name: Carta Orgánica del Instituto de Previsión Social
definition: Economic protection provided by the IPS to workers during periods of illness or temporary disability.
compliance_requirements: Register employee with IPS
Pay monthly social security contributions
Facilitate employee access to medical certification from IPS
compliance_risk: Fines for failing to report the employee, forcing the employer to pay medical expenses directly.
penalty: Administrative sanctions and interest on delayed payments.

law_name: Ley de Protección a la Maternidad y Apoyo a la Lactancia Materna
definition: Right to breastfeeding breaks and the provision of appropriate facilities.
compliance_requirements: Provide 90 minutes of daily break for breastfeeding until the child is 24 months old
Ensure access to a clean and safe area for lactation
compliance_risk: Violation of fundamental rights and health safety regulations.
penalty: Significant administrative fines and potential labor lawsuits.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo, Empleo y Seguridad Social

definition: Regulates the essential requirements for the validity of an employment contract, establishing that contracts can be written or verbal, but detailing the necessity of written form for specific types of contracts.
compliance_requirements: Provide a written contract for fixed-term or specific work
Specify duration, salary, and job description
Maintain signed copies by both parties
compliance_risk: Risk of the contract being deemed indefinite in duration if formal requirements are missing for temporary roles.
penalty: Fines ranging from 10 to 30 minimum daily wages per affected employee.
source_reference: Biblioteca y Archivo Central del Congreso de la Nacion

definition: Mandates the compulsory registration of all employees with the Instituto de Prevision Social (IPS) from the first day of work.
compliance_requirements: Register employer with IPS
Register employee with IPS within 15 days of hiring
Pay monthly social security contributions
compliance_risk: Heavy financial liability for workplace accidents or illnesses if the employee is not registered.
penalty: Fines, surcharges, and potential criminal liability for evasion of social security contributions.

definition: Governs the legal framework for remote work, requiring specific clauses in the employment contract regarding the mode of work.
compliance_requirements: Define remote status in the written contract
Specify tools and equipment provided by employer
Ensure digital disconnection rights
documentation_required: Addendum to the labor contract regarding remote location
Safety and hygiene assessment of home workspace
compliance_risk: Disputes over work hours and equipment liability if not clearly codified.
penalty: Administrative sanctions by the Ministry of Labor, Employment and Social Security (MTESS).
source_reference: Gaceta Oficial de la Republica del Paraguay

law_name: Ley de Proteccion de Datos Personales
definition: Regulates the collection, storage, and processing of personal data of employees.
compliance_requirements: Obtain consent for data processing
Ensure data security protocols
Limit data access to authorized personnel only
compliance_risk: Civil lawsuits for privacy breaches and unauthorized disclosure of employee medical or financial information.
penalty: Legal claims for damages and administrative warnings.
source_reference: Biblioteca y Archivo Central del Congreso de la Nacion

law_name: Constitucion Nacional de la Republica del Paraguay
definition: Guarantees the rights to work, fair wages, organization in unions, and social security as fundamental human rights.
compliance_requirements: Prohibit discrimination in hiring
Ensure equal pay for equal work
Respect the right to join unions
compliance_risk: Constitutional challenges and human rights lawsuits.
penalty: Invalidity of discriminatory labor practices and potential constitutional litigation.

definition: Defines the employment relationship as one where a person provides personal services to another under dependence and subordination, in exchange for remuneration.
compliance_requirements: Assess the existence of subordination and dependence to determine if a worker is an employee or independent contractor
Avoid using independent contractor agreements to mask an employment relationship
documentation_required: Proof of payment of professional fees
Records demonstrating independence and lack of schedule control
compliance_risk: Reclassification of an independent contractor as an employee by the Ministry of Labor (MTESS) or courts.
penalty: Payment of backdated social security contributions, unpaid overtime, vacation pay, bonuses (aguinaldo), and severance pay (indemnizacion), plus fines and interest.

definition: Establishes that all persons providing services under a subordinate relationship are mandatorily subject to the Instituto de Prevision Social (IPS).
compliance_requirements: Register all employees with IPS
Correctly identify workers who meet the 'subordinate' criteria for mandatory registration
documentation_required: Contracts showing the nature of the engagement
compliance_risk: Evasion of social security contributions and potential administrative sanctions by IPS inspectors.
penalty: Fines calculated based on the number of non-registered workers, plus interest and surcharge on unpaid contributions.

law_name: Constitucion Nacional de la Republica del Paraguay
definition: Guarantees the right to work and sets the foundation for labor protection, emphasizing the protection of the worker against disguised employment.
compliance_requirements: Adhere to the principle of 'primacy of reality' (primacia de la realidad) where the actual nature of the work supersedes the contract title
compliance_risk: Constitutional labor rights protect the worker; if substance shows employment, legal protections apply regardless of contract name.
penalty: Labor court rulings favoring the employee for benefits and legal costs.
source_reference: Constitucion Nacional de la Republica del Paraguay

definition: Regulates the termination of the employment contract, covering causes, notice periods, and the calculation of severance pay (indemnification).
compliance_requirements: Provide formal written notice of termination for cause
Payment of outstanding wages and proportional benefits (vacation, aguinaldo)
Payment of seniority indemnity if the contract is terminated without just cause
Issuance of the certificate of employment
documentation_required: Proof of social security contribution clearance (IPS settlement)
compliance_risk: High risk of labor litigation, demands for reinstatement, and payment of back wages or punitive damages.
penalty: Payment of statutory severance up to 15 years of service, plus potential back wages and legal interest costs.
source_reference: Biblioteca y Archivo Central del Congreso de la Nación

law_name: Constitución Nacional de la República del Paraguay
definition: Establishes the fundamental rights of workers, including protection against arbitrary dismissal and the right to fair severance compensation.
compliance_requirements: Guarantee stability in employment as prescribed by law
Ensure equality and non-discrimination in dismissal procedures
compliance_risk: Constitutional challenges regarding violation of labor rights, discrimination claims.
penalty: Court ordered reinstatement, payment of damages for moral and material injury.
source_reference: Constitución Nacional de la República del Paraguay

law_name: Reglamento del Instituto de Previsión Social (IPS)
definition: Mandatory administrative requirements for notifying the Social Security Institute (IPS) of employee separation to finalize contributions.
compliance_requirements: Notify IPS within the established timeframe after the end of employment
Complete the 'Aviso de Salida' (Notice of Exit)
documentation_required: Formulario de Aviso de Salida de IPS
compliance_risk: Fines for late notification or continued contribution obligations after employment termination.
penalty: Administrative fines (multas) determined by IPS and potential accrual of debt.

definition: Requirement that every individual employment contract must be made in writing in two copies.
compliance_requirements: Draft written contracts for all employees
Sign in duplicate
Maintain file for at least 5 years after contract termination
compliance_risk: Presumption that contract terms favor the employee in disputes and possible administrative fines.
penalty: Administrative fines imposed by the Ministry of Labor, Employment and Social Security (MTESS).

definition: Mandatory record-keeping of employee data, wages, and hours worked.
compliance_requirements: Maintain a registry of all employees
Record hours worked
Record wages paid
Submit digital records via MTESS platforms
compliance_risk: MTESS audit sanctions and inability to prove compliance with minimum wage or working hour laws.
penalty: Fines ranging from 10 to 30 minimum wages.

definition: Obligation to register all employees with the Social Security Institute (IPS) and maintain proof of contribution.
compliance_requirements: Register employee with IPS within 48 hours of hiring
Monthly submission of payroll contribution reports
Payment of social security contributions
compliance_risk: Fines, interest, and employer liability for employee medical expenses during period of non-registration.
penalty: Fines and mandatory back-payment of social security contributions with interest.

law_name: Ley de Proteccion de Datos Personales
definition: Requirement to protect sensitive personal and employment data of workers.
compliance_requirements: Implement data storage security
Obtain consent for processing sensitive data
Limit data access to authorized personnel
documentation_required: Internal security protocols for digital HR files
compliance_risk: Legal action for data breach or unauthorized disclosure of employee private records.
penalty: Civil liability and potential damages for data privacy violations.

definition: Specific requirements for remote work agreements and digital documentation.
compliance_requirements: Incorporate telework modality into employment contract
Specify equipment and costs in writing
Define communication and reporting channels
compliance_risk: Risk of being classified as standard in-person employment with full operational cost claims by employees.
penalty: Fines and potential reimbursement of employee equipment and utility costs.

definition: Regulates the maximum duration of the workday, mandatory rest periods, and remuneration for extraordinary hours worked.
compliance_requirements: Maintain accurate records of hours worked
Pay overtime premiums of 50% for standard overtime and 100% for Sundays and holidays
Ensure workday does not exceed 8 hours daily or 48 hours weekly
compliance_risk: Litigation regarding unpaid overtime and regulatory fines from the Ministry of Labor.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 10 to 30 minimum wages, and back-pay claims.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo, Empleo y Seguridad Social

definition: Mandates compulsory registration of all employees with the Instituto de Previsión Social (IPS).
compliance_requirements: Register employees within 48 hours of hiring
Make monthly employer contributions of 16.5% of the gross salary
Withhold and remit employee contributions of 9%
compliance_risk: Liability for medical expenses of employees and heavy fines for evasion.
penalty: Fines, interest on late payments, and potential criminal charges for tax evasion.

law_name: Ley de Protección de Datos Personales
definition: Governs the collection, processing, and storage of personal employee data.
compliance_requirements: Obtain employee consent for data collection
Restrict access to personnel files to authorized personnel
Ensure data accuracy and security
compliance_risk: Civil lawsuits for unauthorized disclosure of private information.
penalty: Compensation for damages and potential administrative sanctions.
source_reference: Ley de Información de Carácter Personal

definition: Legal framework for remote work, emphasizing the voluntary nature and equal treatment of teleworkers.
compliance_requirements: Signed addendum to employment contract specifying remote work conditions
Provide equipment and tools or reimburse costs
Guarantee the right to disconnect
documentation_required: Occupational health and safety risk assessment
compliance_risk: Misclassification of remote workers and breach of right to disconnect.
penalty: Fines by the Ministry of Labor for non-compliance with the written agreement requirements.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo, Empleo y Seguridad Social

law_name: Constitution of the Republic of Paraguay
definition: Guarantees the freedom of association and the right of workers and employers to form unions or associations without prior authorization for the defense of their interests and the execution of collective labor agreements.
compliance_requirements: Recognize the right of employees to organize
Refrain from interfering with union formation
Engage in good faith negotiations
documentation_required: Records of recognized collective bargaining agents
compliance_risk: Violation of constitutional rights leading to legal challenges and international oversight.
penalty: Constitutional nullity of discriminatory acts; potential constitutional protection claims (Amparo).

definition: Establishes the collective labor agreement (Convención Colectiva de Trabajo) as a written agreement between one or more unions and one or more employers to regulate conditions of labor and employment.
compliance_requirements: Submit signed agreements to the Ministry of Labor for registration
Apply agreed-upon conditions to all workers in the sector
Negotiate in good faith with representative unions
documentation_required: Original copy of the Collective Labor Agreement
compliance_risk: Invalidation of labor conditions, labor strikes, and administrative fines.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 10 to 30 minimum wages depending on the severity of the breach.

definition: Regulates the administrative procedures for the filing and enforcement of collective bargaining agreements before the Ministry of Labor.
compliance_requirements: Follow formal procedural steps for collective conflict mediation
Mandatory participation in conciliation hearings
documentation_required: Proof of notification for collective bargaining meetings
compliance_risk: Procedural failures may lead to illegal strike declarations or unenforceable agreements.
penalty: Administrative sanctions for non-attendance to mandated mediation hearings.
source_reference: Code of Labor Procedure (Código Procesal del Trabajo)

law_name: Constitution of the Republic of Paraguay
definition: Recognizes the right of workers to form unions freely and without prior authorization for the defense of their interests.
compliance_requirements: Respect the freedom of association
Refrain from interfering in the internal activities of labor unions
Avoid discriminatory practices against unionized employees
documentation_required: Proof of absence of anti-union practices
compliance_risk: Constitutional infringement, lawsuits based on fundamental rights violation, and potential international labor complaints.
penalty: Compensation for damages, administrative sanctions by the Ministry of Labor, and potential nullity of acts performed in violation of the right.
source_reference: National Constitution of the Republic of Paraguay (1992)

definition: Governs the formation, registration, functioning, and dissolution of labor unions, as well as the protection of union leaders (Fuero Sindical).
compliance_requirements: Recognition of the union's legal personality
Deduction of union dues from employee payroll when authorized
Negotiation in good faith with representative unions
Respect the 'Fuero Sindical' (union immunity) for leaders
documentation_required: Payroll records showing union dues deductions
compliance_risk: Unfair labor practices fines, legal challenges to terminations of protected leaders, and labor unrest.
penalty: Fines ranging from 10 to 30 times the minimum wage per violation, possible reinstatement of employees, and indemnification.
source_reference: Ley 213/1993 que establece el Código del Trabajo

definition: Framework for the negotiation, signing, and registration of collective labor agreements between employers and unions.
compliance_requirements: Mandatory participation in collective bargaining processes if requested by the union
Submission of collective bargaining agreements to the Ministry of Labor for registration
Application of agreed-upon benefits to all covered employees
documentation_required: Proof of registration with the Ministry of Labor
compliance_risk: Non-compliance with established collective agreements, breach of contract, and labor disputes.
penalty: Administrative fines, potential liability for breach of contractual obligations, and court-ordered compliance.
source_reference: Ley 213/1993 que establece el Código del Trabajo

definition: Regulates the modality of telework as a form of labor performance where the employee provides services partially or totally outside the employer's facilities using information and communication technologies.
compliance_requirements: Voluntary agreement between employer and employee
Written contract or amendment to the existing employment contract
Guarantee of equal rights and benefits as on-site employees
Employer responsibility for providing, installing, and maintaining telework equipment and software
Employer coverage of electricity, internet, and communication costs
Respect for the right to disconnect
Compliance with health and safety standards in the home office environment
documentation_required: Health and Safety Assessment of the remote workplace
Receipt of equipment and tools provided by the employer
compliance_risk: Reclassification of the work relationship to on-site if regulations are not met, labor lawsuits for overtime payment, and administrative fines.
penalty: Administrative fines imposed by the Ministry of Labor, Employment and Social Security (MTESS), ranging from 10 to 30 minimum wages per violation.
source_reference: Biblioteca y Archivo Central del Congreso de la Nación

definition: Defines the existence of an employment contract and the general obligations of the employer, applicable to all workers regardless of location.
compliance_requirements: Formalization of the employment relationship
Mandatory enrollment in the Social Security Institute (IPS)
Payment of minimum wage and benefits
Maintenance of payroll records and digital employment registration
compliance_risk: Fines for lack of registration with IPS, inability to prove compliance with labor rights, and potential back-pay claims.
penalty: Fines per affected worker, risk of temporary business suspension by the Ministry of Labor.
source_reference: Ministry of Labor, Employment and Social Security (MTESS)

definition: Governs the treatment of private information, requiring strict confidentiality and protection of personal data handled in professional environments, including remote monitoring.
compliance_requirements: Ensure data security for remote systems
Maintain confidentiality of employee and company data
Obtain consent for data processing
Ensure data protection measures for remote access
compliance_risk: Civil liability for data leaks, judicial penalties for breach of privacy.
penalty: Fines determined by court and mandatory compensation for damages.

law_name: Ley de Protección de Datos de Carácter Personal
definition: Regulates the treatment of personal data in databases to protect the right to privacy and the right to information self-determination.
compliance_requirements: Obtain express and informed consent from employees before processing sensitive data.
Implement technical and organizational security measures to protect data.
Establish protocols for data access, rectification, and deletion requests.
Appoint a data protection officer or responsible person for the database.
documentation_required: Consent forms for sensitive data collection.
Internal security policies for data handling.
compliance_risk: Unauthorized disclosure of employee information, data leaks, and administrative sanctions.
penalty: Fines ranging from 10 to 500 monthly minimum wages, depending on the severity of the infringement.
source_reference: Biblioteca y Archivo Central del Congreso Nacional del Paraguay

definition: Article 68 mandates that employers maintain strict confidentiality regarding professional, commercial, and technical secrets disclosed by the employee.
compliance_requirements: Maintain confidentiality of employee medical records and personal files.
Restrict access to employee personal data to only authorized personnel.
Ensure data is used strictly for employment-related purposes.
documentation_required: Employee personnel files with access logs.
compliance_risk: Legal claims for breach of confidentiality and damages for privacy violation.
penalty: Fines and potential liability for damages caused to the worker.

law_name: Constitución Nacional de la República del Paraguay
definition: Guarantees the right to privacy, family life, and the protection of honor and reputation of individuals.
compliance_requirements: Ensure that the collection of employee data does not infringe upon constitutional privacy rights.
Limit data collection to information essential for the employment relationship.
compliance_risk: Unconstitutional violation of fundamental rights, leading to potential civil or constitutional lawsuits.
penalty: Compensation for damages and potential injunctions against the employer.

definition: Sets the general framework for occupational health, safety, and hygiene in the workplace, requiring employers to take all necessary measures to protect the life and health of workers.
compliance_requirements: Implement necessary safety measures
Provide protective equipment
Maintain hygienic facilities
Conduct periodic medical exams
compliance_risk: Fines, temporary or permanent closure of business, and legal liability in case of workplace accidents.
penalty: Fines ranging from 10 to 30 minimum wages depending on the severity of the violation.
source_reference: Biblioteca y Archivo Central del Congreso de la Nación

definition: Detailed technical regulations governing the physical conditions, lighting, ventilation, and machinery safety within industrial and commercial workplaces.
compliance_requirements: Maintain safe electrical installations
Proper marking of emergency exits
Fire prevention systems
Noise and vibration control
compliance_risk: High administrative risk including heavy fines and potential criminal liability for management if negligence leads to fatalities.
penalty: Administrative fines up to 100 minimum wages and operational shutdown.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo, Empleo y Seguridad Social

definition: Requires companies to establish an internal occupational health and safety committee and assign specific roles for hazard prevention.
compliance_requirements: Form an internal safety committee
Conduct risk mapping
Provide annual safety training
Appoint a safety supervisor
compliance_risk: Non-compliance with administrative reporting requirements and lack of internal oversight structures.
penalty: Administrative sanctions ranging from 5 to 20 minimum wages.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo, Empleo y Seguridad Social

definition: Regulates the entry, residence, and employment of foreigners within Paraguayan territory, establishing the requirement for specific residence permits that authorize labor activities.
compliance_requirements: Employers must verify the foreign employee holds a valid residence permit that explicitly authorizes paid employment (Temporary or Permanent Residency).
Maintain current records of the employee's migration status.
Notify the General Directorate of Migration of any changes in the labor relationship.
Ensure foreign workers do not engage in work outside the scope of their permit category.
documentation_required: Work authorization certificate issued by the General Directorate of Migration
Cedula de Identidad (Paraguayan ID for foreigners)
compliance_risk: High. Hiring a foreigner without a valid work permit can lead to heavy administrative fines and potential deportation of the employee, as well as legal sanctions against the employer.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 100 to 500 minimum wages, depending on the severity and number of undocumented workers.

definition: Establishes that at least 90% of a company's workforce must be Paraguayan, and specifies that the technical and administrative positions occupied by foreigners must not exceed 10% of the total, with specific exemptions for specialized roles.
compliance_requirements: Employers must maintain the 90/10 ratio of Paraguayan to foreign workers.
Provide justification to the Ministry of Labor if foreign experts or specialized technicians are needed beyond the 10% limit.
Ensure the foreign worker has obtained the necessary migration authorization to work.
compliance_risk: Medium. Breach of the 90/10 rule can lead to inspections by the Ministry of Labor and Employment (MTESS) and administrative fines.
penalty: Fines ranging from 10 to 30 minimum daily wages per violation.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo, Empleo y Seguridad Social (MTESS)

law_name: Constitution of the Republic of Paraguay
definition: The Constitution prohibits all forms of discrimination against workers in the workplace, ensuring equality of opportunities and treatment for all employees regardless of their personal characteristics.
compliance_requirements: Implement non-discriminatory hiring practices
Ensure equal pay for equal work
Maintain a work environment free of harassment and bias
compliance_risk: High: Legal challenges based on constitutional violations and human rights abuses.
penalty: Nullity of discriminatory acts and possible civil liability for damages.
source_reference: Constitución Nacional de la República del Paraguay

definition: Prohibits discrimination based on race, color, sex, age, religion, political opinion, social origin, or any other status that does not constitute a valid occupational requirement.
compliance_requirements: Prohibit discriminatory practices in employment offers
Prohibit discrimination in promotion and training
Ensure equal conditions for men and women
compliance_risk: Medium to High: Potential labor lawsuits, fines from the Ministry of Labor, and administrative sanctions.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 10 to 30 minimum daily wages per affected employee.
source_reference: Código del Trabajo de Paraguay, Ley 213/93

law_name: Law on Integral Protection for Women against All Forms of Violence
definition: Establishes measures to prevent, punish, and eradicate violence against women in the workplace, including workplace harassment (mobbing) and sexual harassment.
compliance_requirements: Implement mandatory protocols to prevent workplace harassment
Establish internal complaint channels
Provide mandatory training for employees on gender-based violence
compliance_risk: High: Reputational damage, significant legal liability, and mandatory administrative intervention.
penalty: Administrative sanctions, duty to compensate, and potential criminal liability depending on the severity of the harassment.
source_reference: Ley 5777/16, Protección Integral a las Mujeres

definition: The legal requirement for employers to pay the 'Aguinaldo' or 13th-month salary, which must be paid before December 31st of each year.
compliance_requirements: Payment must be made no later than December 31st
Calculation is 1/12 of the total income earned during the calendar year
Must be paid to all employees regardless of the contract type
documentation_required: Payment receipt signed by the employee
compliance_risk: Fines by the Ministry of Labor and mandatory payment of interest and potential labor lawsuits for breach of contract.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 10 to 30 minimum wages depending on the number of affected employees.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo, Empleo y Seguridad Social (MTESS)

law_name: Ley de creación del Instituto de Previsión Social
definition: Mandatory social security coverage for all workers in the private sector to provide health, retirement, and pension benefits.
compliance_requirements: Employer must register the employee within the first 48 hours of starting
Employer must contribute 16.5% of the gross salary
Employee must contribute 9% of the gross salary
Monthly reporting via the IPS 'REPYSO' system
compliance_risk: High risk of investigation, heavy fines, and personal liability for the employer for unpaid medical expenses in case of work-related accidents.
penalty: Fines ranging from 50% to 100% of the unpaid contributions plus interest, and administrative sanctions.

definition: Mandatory benefit for employees with minor children under their guardianship, paid as a percentage of the minimum wage.
compliance_requirements: Verify children's birth certificates
Include the benefit in the payroll monthly
5% of the minimum wage for each child
compliance_risk: Labor inspection non-compliance findings, back payment orders.
penalty: Administrative fine by the Ministry of Labor.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo, Empleo y Seguridad Social (MTESS)

definition: Mandatory annual paid leave based on the seniority of the employee within the company.
compliance_requirements: Provide 12 days for 1-5 years, 18 days for 5-10 years, 30 days for 10+ years
Must be taken within 6 months of the entitlement year
Must be paid before the start of the leave
documentation_required: Receipt of payment for vacation days
compliance_risk: Labor claim for compensatory damages and administrative sanctions.
penalty: Fines from the Ministry of Labor and potential payment of double wages if leave is not granted.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo, Empleo y Seguridad Social (MTESS)

law_name: Ley N° 98/92 que establece el Régimen Legal del Seguro Social
definition: Regulates the compulsory affiliation and payment of contributions to the Social Security Institute (IPS) for all employees in both the public and private sectors.
compliance_requirements: Register employer with IPS
Register all employees within 3 days of hiring
Submit monthly payroll reports (Planilla de IPS)
Pay employer and employee contributions (IPS quotas)
compliance_risk: Fines, surcharges for late payment, legal disputes with workers, and restriction to bid in public tenders.
penalty: Monetary fines calculated on minimum wage units, plus interest on arrears.

definition: Establishes the requirement for employers to maintain official payroll records and report wage data to the Ministry of Labor, Employment and Social Security (MTESS).
compliance_requirements: Maintain the 'Libro de Salarios' (Wage Book) duly rubricated
Record all payments, overtime, and deductions
Report annual payroll summaries to the MTESS
compliance_risk: Sanctions by the Labor Inspectorate, inability to prove payment in case of labor litigation.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 10 to 30 minimum wages.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo, Empleo y Seguridad Social (MTESS)

law_name: Ley N° 6380/2019 de Modernización y Simplificación del Sistema Tributario Nacional
definition: Governs the tax obligations of the employer as a withholding agent for the Personal Income Tax (IRP) of employees.
compliance_requirements: Calculate and withhold income tax on employee salaries exceeding legal thresholds
File monthly withholding declarations (Formularios Marangatu)
Issue annual withholding certificates
compliance_risk: Tax evasion penalties, interest on unpaid taxes, and criminal charges in severe cases of tax fraud.
penalty: Fines ranging from 50% to 300% of the unpaid tax, plus interest charges.

definition: Mandates the requirement for employers to maintain a personnel registry that details the employment terms and conditions for all workers.
compliance_requirements: Maintain an updated registry of all personnel
Record employee start dates, salary, and job description
Keep records available for labor inspection
compliance_risk: Fines, administrative sanctions, and potential legal disadvantage during labor litigation.
penalty: Monetary fines ranging from 10 to 30 minimum wages depending on the severity and number of employees affected.
source_reference: Biblioteca y Archivo Central del Congreso Nacional

definition: Mandatory registration of all employees with the Instituto de Previsión Social (IPS) within the first 48 hours of employment.
compliance_requirements: Register employee with IPS before or within 48 hours of starting work
Submit notice of employee entry
Maintain current employer IPS account
compliance_risk: High risk of legal liability for work-related accidents without coverage, fines, and mandatory payment of omitted contributions plus interest.
penalty: Significant administrative fines and liability for 100% of medical costs and indemnities if an accident occurs while unregistered.

law_name: Ministry of Labor Resolution on Electronic Registry
definition: Establishes the mandatory requirement to register employment contracts and personnel records through the electronic platform 'REOP' (Registro Electrónico de Obligaciones Patronales).
compliance_requirements: Register all labor contracts electronically
Submit monthly payroll reports online
Keep digital records up to date
compliance_risk: Inability to pass labor inspections and accumulation of daily administrative fines.
penalty: Administrative fines up to 50 minimum wages for recurrent non-compliance.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo, Empleo y Seguridad Social (MTESS)

definition: Establishes the fundamental rights of workers including fair remuneration, maximum work hours, and the right to unionize.
compliance_requirements: Ensure fair pay
Respect the 8-hour workday limit
Guarantee freedom of association
compliance_risk: Constitutional rights violation leading to lawsuits and heavy fines from SUNAFIL.
penalty: Administrative sanctions, civil liability, and possible judicial mandates.

definition: Regulates the private sector labor regime, including types of contracts, trial periods, and dismissal procedures.
compliance_requirements: Execute written contracts for fixed-term employees
Register employees in the T-Registro system
Maintain valid payroll records
compliance_risk: Reclassification of temporary contracts to indefinite, fines for informal hiring.
penalty: Fines ranging from small to extremely high depending on number of employees affected.

definition: Regulates the conditions and obligations for remote work in the private and public sectors.
compliance_requirements: Agreement on remote work in the contract or addendum
Provision of equipment and internet
Respect for disconnection time
compliance_risk: Claim of unpaid overtime if digital disconnection rights are violated.
penalty: Administrative fines by SUNAFIL.

definition: Regulates the processing of personal data, including employee records maintained by employers.
compliance_requirements: Obtain employee consent for data processing
Implement security measures for database storage
Register databases with the Ministry of Justice
compliance_risk: Fines from the National Authority for the Protection of Personal Data.
penalty: Multas coercitivas and administrative fines.

law_name: Health and Safety at Work Law
definition: Obligates employers to prevent accidents and occupational diseases at the workplace.
compliance_requirements: Establish a Health and Safety Committee
Provide mandatory training
Conduct periodic medical examinations
compliance_risk: Criminal liability in case of fatal workplace accidents.
penalty: Fines up to 52.55 tax units (UIT) depending on company size and severity.

definition: Regulates employer mandatory contributions to the social health system (EsSalud).
compliance_requirements: Pay 9% of payroll as health contribution
Ensure timely enrollment of all employees
compliance_risk: Interest charges and tax penalties from SUNAT.
penalty: Fines plus default interest.

law_name: Ley de Productividad y Competitividad Laboral
definition: Regulates the types of labor contracts, including indefinite, fixed-term, and part-time agreements, establishing that the indefinite contract is the general rule.
compliance_requirements: Provide written contracts for fixed-term employees
Justify the cause for fixed-term contracts
Register contracts in the T-Registro system
Respect maximum working hours
compliance_risk: Reclassification of fixed-term contracts to indefinite, leading to labor stability claims and potential back-pay for benefits.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 0.26 to 52.53 UIT depending on the company size and number of affected workers.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Promoción del Empleo (MTPE)

definition: Governs the conditions under which an employment contract is performed outside the employer's premises using technology.
compliance_requirements: Consent of the worker to change to remote work
Specific contract addendum detailing tools and equipment provision
Ensuring the right to disconnect
Occupational health and safety assessment
compliance_risk: Fines for lack of equipment provision, privacy violations, or breach of the right to disconnect.
penalty: Administrative fines by SUNAFIL up to 52.53 UIT.

law_name: Ley de Protección de Datos Personales
definition: Regulates the collection and processing of personal data of employees during the hiring process and throughout the labor relationship.
compliance_requirements: Obtain informed, express, and written consent for data processing
Implement security measures for employee sensitive data
Define the purposes of data collection in hiring
documentation_required: Consent forms for sensitive personal data
compliance_risk: Severe sanctions for mishandling sensitive employee personal or medical records.
penalty: Fines ranging from 0.5 UIT to 100 UIT based on the severity of the infringement.
source_reference: Autoridad Nacional de Protección de Datos Personales (ANPDP)

law_name: Ley de Jornada de Trabajo, Horario y Trabajo en Sobretiempo
definition: Regulates the maximum duration of the workday (8 hours daily or 48 hours weekly) and the requirements and compensation for overtime work.
compliance_requirements: Ensure workday does not exceed 8 hours per day or 48 per week
Authorize overtime in advance
Pay overtime premiums of 25% for first two hours and 35% thereafter
compliance_risk: Fines by SUNAFIL for improper recording of hours and lack of payment for work performed beyond the regular shift.
penalty: Administrative fines ranging from 0.26 to 52.53 UIT depending on the number of affected workers and company size.

law_name: Texto Único Ordenado de la Ley de Productividad y Competitividad Laboral
definition: Defines working time and establishes the legal framework for the employer's management powers regarding shifts and schedules.
compliance_requirements: Establish fair schedules consistent with company operations
Respect mandatory rest periods
compliance_risk: Litigation for constructive dismissal if working hours are unilaterally altered without technical justification.
penalty: Indemnity for arbitrary dismissal equivalent to 1.5 monthly salaries per year of service.

definition: Regulates the right to digital disconnection for employees working remotely, ensuring they are not obligated to respond to communications outside working hours.
compliance_requirements: Respect the digital disconnection period
Define work hours in the remote work agreement
compliance_risk: Fines by labor authorities for infringing on the right to digital disconnection.
penalty: Fines categorized as serious labor infractions under SUNAFIL standards.

law_name: Ley de Productividad y Competitividad Laboral
definition: Governs the private sector labor regime, defining rights to remuneration, social benefits (CTS, Gratification), and working conditions.
compliance_requirements: Payment of monthly salary
Payment of CTS (Compensación por Tiempo de Servicios)
Payment of Gratifications (July and December)
Payment of Family Allowance
compliance_risk: High; potential labor litigation, payment of fines, and requirement to pay backdated benefits with interest.
penalty: Administrative fines imposed by SUNAFIL ranging from 0.26 to 52.53 UIT depending on company size and number of affected workers.

law_name: Ley de Modernización de la Seguridad Social en Salud
definition: Establishes the obligation of employers to contribute to EsSalud for the healthcare coverage of their employees.
compliance_requirements: Register employees in T-Registro
Make monthly contributions of 9% of the payroll
Declare contributions via PLAME
compliance_risk: High; tax evasion charges and loss of employee health benefits coverage.
penalty: Interest, surcharges, and fines from SUNAT for failure to declare or pay contributions on time.

law_name: Ley del Sistema Privado de Administración de Fondos de Pensiones
law_code: Texto Único Ordenado de la Ley N° 27419
definition: Regulates the mandatory withholding of pension contributions from employee salaries to the AFP system.
compliance_requirements: Withhold pension contributions from salary
Transfer withheld amounts to the respective AFP
Report contributions via PLAME
documentation_required: Proof of transfer to the AFP
compliance_risk: Extreme; misappropriation of funds carries criminal liability for the employer.
penalty: Administrative fines, daily interest, and potential criminal charges for retention of pension funds.
source_reference: SBS (Superintendencia de Banca, Seguros y AFP)

law_name: Ley que regula la planilla electrónica
definition: Mandates the use of the electronic payroll (PLAME) for all formal employment activities to report taxes and social security contributions.
compliance_requirements: Maintain current T-Registro status
Submit monthly PLAME by the established SUNAT deadline
Ensure data accuracy between payroll and government records
compliance_risk: Medium; discrepancies trigger audits from SUNAT and SUNAFIL.
penalty: Monetary fines for late submission or inaccurate information.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Promoción del Empleo

law_name: Ley del Impuesto a la Renta
definition: Regulates the taxation of income generated by individuals and entities, including employment income (Fifth Category Income) and its withholding obligations by the employer.
compliance_requirements: Calculate, withhold, and pay the Income Tax on employees' salaries (Fifth Category)
Maintain current payroll records
Submit withholding declarations to SUNAT via PLAME
documentation_required: Employee income statements (Certificado de Rentas de Quinta Categoría)
compliance_risk: Fines, interest, and surcharges for under-withholding or failure to remit taxes to the tax authority.
penalty: Monetary fines calculated as a percentage of the omitted tax (typically 50% of the tax due) or based on the Tax Unit (UIT).
source_reference: SUNAT (Superintendencia Nacional de Aduanas y de Administración Tributaria)

law_name: Ley de Creación del Impuesto Temporal a los Activos Netos
definition: Tax levied on the net assets of companies, which has implications for employers in terms of accounting and fiscal reporting related to labor costs reflected in company assets.
compliance_requirements: Include all labor-related liabilities in the net asset calculation for tax declarations
Annual filing with SUNAT
compliance_risk: Audit discrepancies if labor-related assets or liabilities are misstated in financial reports.
penalty: Penalty for late declaration or failure to pay, including default interest.

law_name: Ley de Creación del Seguro Social de Salud
definition: Mandates the payment of contributions (EsSalud) which are tax-like social security levies applied to the gross payroll of the employer.
compliance_requirements: Employer must pay a 9% contribution on the total payroll
Monthly payment through SUNAT payment platform
Regularize payments in the electronic payroll
compliance_risk: Liability for health expenses of employees if contributions are unpaid, plus administrative fines and surcharges.
penalty: 100% of the unpaid contribution if detected in an audit, plus interest.

law_name: Legislative Decree No. 713, Consolidation of Legislation on Compensatory Rest for Workers Subject to the Private Labor Regime
definition: Regulates the right of employees in the private sector to 30 calendar days of paid annual vacation after completing one year of service and a minimum number of worked days.
compliance_requirements: Grant 30 days of paid vacation per year
Ensure vacation is taken within the year following the accrual
Pay vacation pay before the start of the rest period
compliance_risk: Fines by SUNAFIL and legal claims for accrued vacation pay.
penalty: Administrative fines categorized as serious or very serious ranging from 0.26 to 52.53 UIT depending on the number of affected workers.

law_name: Law No. 26644, Law that grants pregnancy leave to the working woman
definition: Grants 98 days of maternity leave to working women (49 prenatal and 49 postnatal).
compliance_requirements: Grant the leave period
Ensure the employee receives the maternity subsidy from ESSALUD
compliance_risk: Violation of reproductive rights and labor discrimination penalties.
penalty: Severe administrative fines and potential lawsuits for reinstatement if dismissed.

law_name: Law No. 29409, Law that grants paternity leave to workers in the public and private sectors
definition: Grants 10 consecutive calendar days of paid paternity leave for the birth or adoption of a child.
compliance_requirements: Grant 10 days of leave
Pay the salary corresponding to the leave days
compliance_risk: Administrative fines for failure to grant statutory leave.
penalty: Fines imposed by SUNAFIL for breach of labor regulations.

law_name: Law No. 27360, Law of Promotion of the Agrarian Sector (and subsequent modifications)
definition: Sets specific benefits for agricultural workers including gratuities and CTS (Compensatory Service Time) at a different rate.
compliance_requirements: Calculate and pay Gratuities and CTS according to the specific agricultural regime percentages
compliance_risk: Underpayment of social benefits leading to retroactive claims.
penalty: Fines from SUNAFIL and mandatory payment of social benefits plus legal interest.

law_name: Law No. 27735, Law that regulates the right of workers in the private sector to receive gratuities for July 28 and December 25
definition: Mandates the payment of two gratuities per year equivalent to one month's salary each.
compliance_requirements: Pay full gratuity in the first half of July and December
Include non-contributory bonuses in the calculation
compliance_risk: Non-payment results in fines and interest penalties.
penalty: Fines by SUNAFIL and legal recovery actions by the employee.

law_name: Ley de Productividad y Competitividad Laboral
definition: Regulates the types of employment contracts, focusing on the principle of indefinite-term contracts and specific requirements for fixed-term contracts.
compliance_requirements: Ensure the existence of a valid objective cause for fixed-term contracts
Put contracts in writing
Register the contract with the Ministry of Labor if required
Adhere to maximum duration limits for fixed-term contracts
compliance_risk: High risk of reclassification of fixed-term contracts to indefinite-term contracts if legal formalities or objective causes are not met.
penalty: Payment of back pay, potential payment of severance for unfair dismissal, and administrative fines by SUNAFIL.

definition: Establishes the mandatory requirements and formalities for the validity of fixed-term labor contracts.
compliance_requirements: Detail the specific objective cause for the fixed-term hiring
Provide a copy of the contract to the employee
Maintain the physical or digital contract on file
documentation_required: Employment contract signed by both parties
compliance_risk: Invalidation of the fixed-term nature of the contract leading to permanent employment status.
penalty: Administrative fines imposed by the Labor Inspection Authority (SUNAFIL).
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Promoción del Empleo

law_name: Ley de Transparencia, Derecho a la Información y Obligaciones de los Empleadores
definition: Mandates that employers must inform employees in writing of the health and safety risks and job requirements at the time of hiring.
compliance_requirements: Deliver the Internal Rules of Conduct (RIS)
Provide health and safety training upon onboarding
Inform about salary and benefits structure
documentation_required: Evidence of receipt of internal regulations
compliance_risk: Administrative sanctions for lack of information and potential liability in work accidents.
penalty: Fines based on the number of affected employees and company size.
keywords: Seguridad y Salud en el Trabajo
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Promoción del Empleo

law_name: Ley de Protección de Datos Personales
definition: Regulates the collection and processing of personal data of employees for hiring and payroll purposes.
compliance_requirements: Obtain written consent from candidates for data processing
Ensure data security measures are in place
Limit data collection to necessary employment purposes
documentation_required: Privacy notice signed by the employee
compliance_risk: Heavy fines from the Personal Data Protection Authority (ANPDP).
penalty: Tiered fines ranging from minor to very serious infractions.
source_reference: Ministerio de Justicia y Derechos Humanos

law_name: Ley que regula el Registro de Información Laboral
definition: Obligation to register all employees in the T-Registro system (part of SUNAT's PLAME) before the start of the employment relationship.
compliance_requirements: Register the employee in T-Registro no later than the day of hiring
Update employee information periodically
compliance_risk: Unregistered employment leads to heavy fines and labor inspection intervention.
penalty: Administrative fines based on the scale of the company and number of employees.

law_name: Ley de Productividad y Competitividad Laboral
definition: Establishes the principle of primacy of reality, stating that in the event of discordance between what occurs in practice and what is stated in documents, the facts prevail over the written contract.
compliance_requirements: Ensure that independent contractors do not work under subordination, fixed schedules, or receive instructions from a supervisor.
Avoid providing tools or workspace that resemble an employer-employee relationship.
Verify that the individual maintains autonomy over how the service is performed.
compliance_risk: Reclassification of independent contractor to employee status leading to retroactive payment of social benefits, bonuses, and severance.
penalty: Fines imposed by SUNAFIL ranging from 0.23 to 52.53 UIT depending on the number of affected employees and company size.
source_reference: Decreto Legislativo 728, Texto Único Ordenado de la Ley de Productividad y Competitividad Laboral

law_name: Ley General de Inspección del Trabajo
definition: Regulates the inspection powers of the labor authority to verify the actual nature of the work relationship regardless of the civil contract signed.
compliance_requirements: Cooperate with SUNAFIL inspections.
Maintain clear distinctions between service providers and subordinate workers.
Ensure professional services are not masked employment contracts.
compliance_risk: High probability of reclassification if indicators of subordination (schedules, tools, hierarchical structure) are present during inspection.
penalty: Administrative fines determined by the seriousness of the violation and number of workers affected.
source_reference: Ley 28806, Ley General de Inspección del Trabajo

law_name: Ley de Productividad y Competitividad Laboral
definition: Regulates the causes for termination of the employment contract, including justified causes related to the worker's conduct or capacity, and objective causes like economic or structural reasons.
compliance_requirements: Provide formal written notice of termination citing the specific cause
Grant the employee a legal term to defend themselves against charges of misconduct
Provide severance pay (CTS) and other social benefits within 48 hours of termination
Obtain authorization from the Ministry of Labor for collective dismissals due to objective causes
documentation_required: Proof of delivery of the dismissal letter
Record of social benefit settlements (Liquidación de beneficios sociales)
compliance_risk: High risk of wrongful dismissal claims leading to reinstatement or indemnity payments.
penalty: Indemnity for arbitrary dismissal (despido arbitrario) equal to 1.5 monthly salaries per year of service, capped at 12 salaries.

definition: Establishes the right to protection against arbitrary dismissal, granting the worker a right to restitution or indemnity.
compliance_requirements: Ensure termination procedures do not violate the constitutional protection against arbitrary acts
Uphold due process during internal disciplinary proceedings
compliance_risk: Constitutional litigation and potential court-ordered reinstatement.
penalty: Potential court-ordered reinstatement with payment of back wages (salarios caídos).

law_name: Ley de Compensación por Tiempo de Servicios
definition: Regulates the mandatory social benefit (CTS) meant to act as a buffer for the worker upon employment termination.
compliance_requirements: Calculate and deposit the CTS proportional to time worked upon final settlement
Provide the letter of release for the CTS fund within 48 hours of termination
Settle outstanding payments including vacations and bonus fractions
compliance_risk: Administrative fines by the National Superintendency of Labor Inspection (SUNAFIL) for non-payment.
penalty: Fines calculated based on the number of affected workers and the gravity of the infraction, plus interest.

law_name: Ley de Productividad y Competitividad Laboral
definition: Regulates the written form requirements for fixed-term employment contracts to be valid and enforceable.
compliance_requirements: Ensure all employment contracts are in writing
Specify the objective cause for hiring
Register the contract with the Ministry of Labor if applicable
compliance_risk: High risk of contract being declared as indefinite-term by labor authorities if formalities are not met.
penalty: Administrative fines issued by SUNAFIL based on company size and number of affected workers.

definition: Mandates the registration of all workers, pensioners, and service providers in the electronic payroll system (T-Registro) managed by SUNAT.
compliance_requirements: Register workers on the day they start work
Update payroll information monthly (PLAME)
Maintain current personal and labor data
compliance_risk: High risk of heavy fines from SUNAFIL for non-registration or late registration of workers.
penalty: Progressive fines depending on the number of workers affected and the severity of the infraction.

law_name: Ley de Seguridad y Salud en el Trabajo
definition: Obligates employers to maintain a specific set of records related to occupational health and safety.
compliance_requirements: Keep records of accidents and occupational diseases
Maintain records of safety inspections
Maintain records of training and inductions
documentation_required: Internal regulations of safety and health
Proof of personal protective equipment delivery
compliance_risk: Significant legal risk, including criminal liability for employers in case of fatal accidents.
penalty: Administrative fines and potential criminal prosecution.

law_name: Ley de Protección de Datos Personales
definition: Governs the collection and processing of personal data, including employee information.
compliance_requirements: Obtain consent for processing sensitive data
Register data banks with the National Authority for Personal Data Protection
Implement security measures for stored data
compliance_risk: Reputational and financial risk due to leaks or unauthorized processing.
penalty: Fines ranging from light to very serious infractions.

law_name: Reglamento de la Ley de Teletrabajo
definition: Requires specific documentation for the voluntary agreement of remote work between employer and employee.
compliance_requirements: Execution of written addendum for remote work
Documentation of equipment provided
Record of the established working hours
documentation_required: Policy on data security for remote environment
compliance_risk: Reclassification of remote work to standard employment if documentation is absent, creating liability for overtime or equipment costs.
penalty: Administrative sanctions by SUNAFIL.

law_name: Ley de Productividad y Competitividad Laboral
definition: Governs the rules for individual employment contracts, trial periods, and valid reasons for dismissal.
compliance_requirements: Provide written justification for dismissal
Grant the worker time to present defense
Payment of legal severance (CTS) and settlement (Liquidación) within 48 hours
compliance_risk: High risk of unfair dismissal claims and payment of indemnity for arbitrary dismissal.
penalty: Payment of indemnity equivalent to 1.5 monthly salaries per year of service (capped) plus potential court-ordered reinstatement.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Promoción del Empleo

law_name: Ley de Seguridad y Salud en el Trabajo
definition: Sets the obligation of the employer to guarantee the safety and health of workers in the workplace.
compliance_requirements: Implement a health and safety management system
Conduct periodic medical exams
Form a health and safety committee
Train employees
compliance_risk: Criminal liability for the employer in case of fatal accidents or serious bodily harm.
penalty: Administrative fines up to 52.53 UIT depending on the number of affected workers and company size.

law_name: Ley de Protección de Datos Personales
definition: Regulates the processing of personal data, including employee payroll and biographical data.
compliance_requirements: Obtain consent for data processing
Register databases with the Ministry of Justice
Implement security measures for digital files
compliance_risk: Regulatory fines by the Personal Data Protection Authority.
penalty: Fines ranging from 0.5 UIT to 100 UIT based on the severity of the breach.
source_reference: Autoridad Nacional de Protección de Datos Personales

law_name: Ley que regula el Trabajo Remoto
definition: Regulations governing the provision of services remotely outside of the employer's premises.
compliance_requirements: Include remote work clause in contract
Respect the right to digital disconnection
Provide necessary equipment or compensation
compliance_risk: Claims regarding overtime and lack of equipment reimbursement.
penalty: Fines from SUNAFIL for infringing labor rights or health and safety standards.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Promoción del Empleo

definition: Recognizes the right to unionization, collective bargaining, and strike, ensuring the state guarantees these rights to promote social harmony.
compliance_requirements: Ensure freedom of association for employees
Engage in good-faith negotiations
Prevent anti-union practices
compliance_risk: Violation of constitutional rights leading to legal injunctions and international scrutiny.
penalty: Constitutional protection actions (Acción de Amparo) and nullification of retaliatory acts.

law_code: Ley de Relaciones Colectivas de Trabajo
article_number: Texto Único Ordenado del Decreto Ley 25593, Art. 41-70
definition: The legal framework governing the initiation, negotiation, and settlement of collective bargaining agreements between employers and labor unions.
compliance_requirements: Receive and respond to the list of claims (Pliego de Reclamos) within 10 days
Engage in direct negotiation sessions
Provide information necessary for negotiations
Adhere to the mandatory arbitration procedure if direct negotiation fails
compliance_risk: Unfair labor practice claims, administrative fines by the Ministry of Labor (MTPE), and potential forced collective bargaining outcomes.
penalty: Administrative fines imposed by SUNAFIL ranging from light to very serious infractions.

law_name: Regulation of the Collective Labor Relations Act
law_code: Reglamento de la Ley de Relaciones Colectivas de Trabajo
definition: Specifies the administrative procedures for the registration of unions and the filing and processing of collective bargaining lists.
compliance_requirements: Submit the agreement to the Labor Authority for registration within 15 days of signing
Ensure the composition of the negotiating committee follows legal ratios
Maintain neutrality during union activity
documentation_required: Certified copy of the collective agreement
Evidence of the negotiating committee's authority
Registration forms for the labor authority
compliance_risk: Invalidation of the collective agreement due to improper registration or failure to comply with administrative timelines.
penalty: Administrative sanctions, fines, and potential invalidation of the bargaining instrument.

definition: Guarantees the rights of unionization, collective bargaining, and the right to strike, ensuring state protection for worker representative organizations.
compliance_requirements: Respect the freedom of association of employees
Refrain from interfering in the internal management of unions
Negotiate collective bargaining agreements in good faith
compliance_risk: Violation of fundamental constitutional rights leading to judicial intervention and nullity of anti-union acts.
penalty: Possible legal actions for constitutional protection (Amparo), fines by the labor inspection authority (SUNAFIL), and potential criminal liability for restricting freedom of association.

law_code: Texto Único Ordenado de la Ley de Relaciones Colectivas de Trabajo (D.S. N° 010-2003-TR)
definition: The primary regulatory framework governing the formation, operation, and dissolution of unions, as well as the collective bargaining process in Peru.
compliance_requirements: Recognize duly registered unions
Provide union leave for leaders
Provide check-off facilities for union dues
Engage in collective bargaining negotiations when requested by the union
documentation_required: Registration certificate from the Ministry of Labor (MTPE)
compliance_risk: High risk of labor inspections, fines, and legal claims for unfair labor practices.
penalty: Administrative fines categorized as serious or very serious ranging up to 52.53 tax units (UIT) depending on the number of affected workers.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Promoción del Empleo (MTPE)

law_name: General Regulation of the Collective Labor Relations Act
definition: Detailed procedural rules for the enforcement of the Collective Labor Relations Act, including registration procedures and strike formalities.
compliance_requirements: Adherence to procedures for declaring a legal strike
Registration of union board members at the MTPE
Submission of collective bargaining agendas within legal timelines
compliance_risk: Procedural errors in strike handling leading to illegal strike declarations and potential termination of strikers.
penalty: Fines from the Labor Inspection Authority (SUNAFIL) for procedural non-compliance.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Promoción del Empleo (MTPE)

definition: Regulates the remote work modality in both public and private sectors, defining it as a professional activity performed without the employee's physical presence at the workplace.
compliance_requirements: Change to remote work must be by mutual agreement or unilateral decision due to extraordinary circumstances
Employer must provide digital equipment or pay for connectivity/power expenses
Ensure the right to digital disconnection
Maintain the same working conditions as in-person work
Protect personal data and privacy
documentation_required: Communication of the change of work location
Registry of remote work equipment delivery
Internal work policy on remote work
compliance_risk: Risk of legal claims for salary adjustments, non-compliance with digital disconnection, and administrative fines by SUNAFIL.
penalty: Fines ranging from 0.26 to 52.53 UIT depending on the severity and number of affected employees.

law_name: Reglamento de la Ley de Trabajo Remoto
definition: Detailed regulatory framework providing operational guidelines for the implementation, equipment provisioning, and security standards for remote work in Peru.
compliance_requirements: Specify the nature of the tasks to be performed
Define the digital monitoring tools used
Ensure occupational health and safety (SST) assessments are conducted
Ensure proper training for remote work tools
documentation_required: SST Risk Assessment Form for remote workplace
compliance_risk: Failing to conduct health and safety assessments results in employer liability for work-related illnesses or accidents.
penalty: Administrative fines issued by SUNAFIL for breach of SST standards.
keywords: Seguridad y Salud en el Trabajo

law_name: Ley de Protección de Datos Personales
definition: Establishes obligations for the protection of personal data, applicable to employees working remotely who handle sensitive corporate or customer information.
compliance_requirements: Ensure secure processing of personal data in home environments
Implement technical and organizational measures for data security
Obtain consent for monitoring employees via software
documentation_required: IT security manual for remote access
compliance_risk: Data leakage or unauthorized access to sensitive information leading to heavy fines from the Ministry of Justice (MINJUS).
penalty: Fines ranging from 0.5 to 100 UIT depending on the nature of the infringement.
source_reference: Ministerio de Justicia y Derechos Humanos

law_name: Ley de Protección de Datos Personales
definition: Regulates the processing of personal data, establishing principles and rights for individuals (including employees) regarding their personal information held by data controllers.
compliance_requirements: Obtain informed, express, and prior consent from employees for data processing
Implement technical, organizational, and legal security measures
Register personal data banks with the National Authority for the Protection of Personal Data (ANPDP)
Ensure data accuracy and update information when necessary
Appoint a data protection officer if required
compliance_risk: Legal sanctions, administrative fines, reputational damage, and civil liability claims from employees regarding privacy violations.
penalty: Fines categorized as minor (up to 0.5 UIT), serious (up to 10 UIT), and very serious (up to 100 UIT), where UIT is the Tax Unit.

law_name: Reglamento de la Ley de Protección de Datos Personales
definition: Provides detailed procedures and regulatory framework for the application of Law N° 29733, specifying roles for data controllers and processors in the employment context.
compliance_requirements: Maintain an inventory of personal data banks
Handle and respond to ARCO rights requests (Access, Rectification, Cancellation, Opposition)
Ensure secure storage and disposal of sensitive employee data
Manage third-party access to employee payroll/benefits data via secure contracts
compliance_risk: Administrative proceedings for failure to manage employee data securely or ignoring rights requests.
penalty: Financial fines ranging from 0.5 to 100 UIT depending on the severity of the infringement.

law_name: Ley de Seguridad y Salud en el Trabajo
definition: Sets the minimum standards for the prevention of occupational hazards, establishing the duty of the employer to guarantee a safe and healthy work environment.
compliance_requirements: Implement a Management System for Safety and Health at Work (SST)
Conduct an initial baseline assessment
Establish a Health and Safety Committee or elect a Supervisor
Develop an annual training program
Perform medical exams for employees
compliance_risk: Civil, administrative, and criminal liability in case of accidents or death of workers due to negligence.
penalty: Fines ranging from 0.23 to 52.53 UIT (Unidad Impositiva Tributaria) depending on the seriousness and number of affected workers.
keywords: Seguridad y Salud en el Trabajo

law_name: Reglamento de la Ley de Seguridad y Salud en el Trabajo
definition: Provides specific operational procedures and technical guidelines for the implementation of the Safety and Health at Work Law.
compliance_requirements: Maintain the Internal Regulations for Safety and Health at Work (RISST)
Ensure workplace safety signs are installed
Conduct periodic occupational health monitoring
Report occupational accidents to the Ministry of Labor
documentation_required: Evidence of PPE (Personal Protective Equipment) delivery
compliance_risk: Administrative fines for lack of documentation or non-functional safety committees.
penalty: Fines assessed by SUNAFIL based on severity and firm size.

law_name: Norma Técnica de Salud que establece el Listado de Enfermedades Profesionales
definition: Defines the list of occupational diseases recognized in Peru, requiring employers to mitigate specific risks related to these conditions.
compliance_requirements: Identify specific work-related health risks for employees
Implement technical controls to prevent listed occupational diseases
Maintain health surveillance for staff exposed to identified hazards
compliance_risk: Lawsuits related to occupational health and compensation claims from employees.
penalty: Administrative sanctions by SUSALUD and Ministry of Labor.

definition: Regulates the entry and residency status of foreign nationals, specifically defining the 'Working Visa' (Calidad Migratoria de Trabajador) for those entering into an employment contract under the Peruvian Labor Regime.
compliance_requirements: Register the employment contract with the Ministry of Labor (MINTRA)
Obtain specific immigration status (Calidad Migratoria) before starting work
Maintain current records in the Foreigners' Registry
Notify Migraciones of any changes in employment conditions
documentation_required: Valid employment contract approved by MINTRA
Copy of passport and residency permit
compliance_risk: Imposition of heavy fines, deportation of the foreign worker, and potential blacklisting of the company from future sponsorship.
penalty: Fines ranging from 1 to 50 UIT (Unidad Impositiva Tributaria) depending on the severity of the infraction.
source_reference: Diario Oficial El Peruano / Superintendencia Nacional de Migraciones

law_name: Reglamento de la Ley de Migraciones
definition: Provides detailed administrative procedures for the processing of temporary work permits and the change of immigration status for foreign workers in Peru.
compliance_requirements: Submit the electronic application via the Migraciones platform
Verify the authenticity of educational credentials and professional titles
Maintain employer registry in the National Superintendency of Migrations
documentation_required: Copy of RUC (Tax ID) of the employer
Evidence of the professional qualification of the foreign worker
compliance_risk: Rejection of residency application or temporary work authorization, leading to unauthorized working status.
penalty: Administrative fines based on UIT; suspension of the ability to hire foreign personnel.

definition: Specific statute regulating the hiring of foreign workers in Peru, establishing limitations on the number of foreign employees (20% of workforce) and payroll percentage (30% of total payroll).
compliance_requirements: Ensure foreign staff does not exceed 20% of the total headcount
Ensure foreign staff does not exceed 30% of total payroll expenses
Maintain mandatory written employment contracts
Approval of contracts by the Ministry of Labor
documentation_required: Affidavit of compliance with workforce limits
Corporate documentation proving the company is legally established in Peru
compliance_risk: Contracts declared null and void by labor authorities; fines for violating quota limitations.
penalty: Fines determined by SUNAFIL based on the number of affected employees and the nature of the violation.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Promoción del Empleo (MTPE)

definition: Guarantees the right of every person to equality before the law. No one shall be discriminated against on the basis of origin, race, sex, language, religion, opinion, economic status, or any other grounds.
compliance_requirements: Implement non-discriminatory hiring practices
Ensure equal pay for equal work
Provide equal access to promotion and training
compliance_risk: Constitutional protection claims, Amparo lawsuits, and reputational damage.
penalty: Nullification of discriminatory acts, compensation for moral and economic damages.
source_reference: Congreso de la Republica del Peru

law_name: Law on Equality of Opportunities between Women and Men
definition: Establishes the legal framework to ensure equality of opportunities for women and men in the public and private spheres, including the workplace.
compliance_requirements: Eliminate gender-based barriers in recruitment
Implement policies to close the gender wage gap
Ensure gender equality in professional development
compliance_risk: Administrative fines by SUNAFIL and discrimination claims.
penalty: Administrative fines graded as serious or very serious.

law_name: General Labor Inspection Law and Regulations
definition: Defines discrimination as a serious breach of labor regulations, subjecting employers to administrative inspection and sanction.
compliance_requirements: Cooperate with SUNAFIL inspections regarding discrimination claims
Maintain non-discriminatory personnel records
compliance_risk: High administrative fines issued by SUNAFIL.
penalty: Monetary fines calculated based on the number of employees affected and the severity of the offense.
source_reference: Ministerio de Trabajo y Promocion del Empleo

law_name: Law that prohibits discrimination in the workplace
definition: Prohibits any discriminatory requirement in job offers and access to employment, such as age, sex, race, or physical appearance, unless justified by the nature of the job.
compliance_requirements: Remove discriminatory language from job postings
Ensure selection processes are merit-based
Avoid profiling based on personal characteristics
compliance_risk: Fines for illegal job postings and discriminatory recruitment practices.
penalty: Administrative fines determined by the Ministry of Labor.

definition: Mandatory annual bonuses paid twice a year (July and December), equivalent to one month's salary each.
compliance_requirements: Payment must be made during the first 15 days of July and December
Employee must have worked at least one month in the qualifying semester
compliance_risk: Fines by SUNAFIL and mandatory interest payments
penalty: Tiered fines based on the number of affected employees and company size
source_reference: Congreso de la Republica del Peru

definition: A mandatory social benefit intended to provide financial security for workers upon the termination of employment.
compliance_requirements: Deposit 50% of the half-year value in May and 50% in November into a bank account chosen by the employee
Calculate based on salary and 1/6 of the most recent Gratificación
compliance_risk: High administrative fines and legal labor complaints for unpaid social benefits
penalty: Administrative fines indexed to the number of affected employees and non-compliance duration

definition: A monthly benefit equivalent to 10% of the minimum wage (RMV) for employees with children under 18 or under 24 if enrolled in higher education.
compliance_requirements: Validate birth certificates or student registration
Include in monthly payroll
Report in PLAME
compliance_risk: Underpayment of salary components and payroll errors
penalty: Administrative fines by SUNAFIL

definition: A mandatory group life insurance policy provided by the employer for employees after 4 years of service, though currently expanded to start from the first day.
compliance_requirements: Provide insurance coverage to all eligible employees
Cover death and permanent disability
compliance_risk: Employer becomes liable for the insurance amount if the policy was not contracted
penalty: Financial liability for the employer equivalent to the insurance payout, plus administrative fines

law_name: Ley de creación del Registro de Información Laboral (T-Registro) y Planilla Electrónica (PLAME)
definition: Mandatory electronic system where employers register labor data, security information, and financial payments for employees, pensioners, and service providers.
compliance_requirements: Register all employees within the T-Registro system on their start date
Submit monthly PLAME (Planilla Mensual de Pagos) through SUNAT portal
Report all social security contributions and income tax withholdings
Ensure data matches payroll accounting records
compliance_risk: Fines by SUNAT and labor inspections by SUNAFIL for incomplete or inaccurate reporting.
penalty: Fines ranging from 0.25 to 50 UIT based on the number of affected workers and severity of the infraction.
source_reference: SUNAT Official Portal / Ministry of Labor and Employment Promotion

law_name: Ley del Impuesto a la Renta
definition: Regulations governing the withholding of income tax (Fifth Category) on employee salaries and mandatory reporting to tax authorities.
compliance_requirements: Calculate Fifth Category Income Tax for all employees
Withhold taxes from monthly payroll
Declare and pay withholdings to SUNAT
Issue annual summary of earnings (Certificado de Rentas)
compliance_risk: Tax evasion, late payment interest, and penalties for failing to report withheld taxes.
penalty: Up to 100% of the unpaid amount depending on the type of infringement and prior history.
source_reference: SUNAT (Superintendencia Nacional de Aduanas y de Administración Tributaria)
